En: That machine is a Kvalsund 10-20 HK.
Zh: 那是一台库瓦桑德10-20HK起重机。

En: This is *your* cloak. You can feel it.
Zh: 这是*你的*斗篷。你能感觉到。

En: Lieutenant... (Point to the cloak.) "I think that's mine."
Zh: “警督……”（指向斗篷。）“我觉得那是我的。”

En: Yes. It does bear the RCM insignia. And we're the only detectives in Martinaise.
Zh: “是的。上面确实有RCM的标志。而且我们是马丁内斯唯一的警探。”

En: He turns back to the sad piece of fabric flapping in the wind.
Zh: 他再次看向在风中飞舞的那件可怜的衣物。

En: Yes... we were in the middle of something -- your cloak...
Zh: “是的……我们正忙着处理——你的斗篷呢……”

En: Then he turns back to the sad piece of fabric flapping in the wind.
Zh: 然后他又转回到风中翻飞的那块悲惨的布料。

En: But I digress. We were focusing on your *cloak* here. He looks at the sad piece of fabric flapping in the wind.
Zh: “但是我有点跑题了。我们要专注你的*斗篷*。”他看着在风中飞舞的那件可怜的衣物。

En: Well, it doesn't seem too dangerous -- two metres tops. Whenever you're ready to do it, I'll be right behind you.
Zh: “呃，看起来也不是太危险——最多两米。等你什么时候准备好了，我会在你身后。”

En: The necktie lets go a little, its vicious grip easing around your neck. Colours return to the world around you.
Zh: 领带稍微放开了一些，你脖子周围恶意的紧扼感放松了不少。你周围的世界又恢复了颜色。

En: Good. We can always come back when you're feeling better. It's just a cloak after all. He looks around.
Zh: “很好。等你感觉好一点之后，我们随时可以回来。毕竟这只是一件斗篷。”他看了看周围。

En: The tarpaulin cloak swings alone for a while there in the wind. No one's coming for it anymore.
Zh: 防水斗篷在风中独自摇摆。不会再有人去管它了。

En: Man down! A trooper has been left behind the enemy lines!
Zh: 有人倒下了！一名骑兵被留在了敌人后方！

En: Looks like someone left his tarpaulin cloak hanging on the railing here.
Zh: 看来某人把他的防水斗篷落在栏杆上了。

En: What are we doing? We're awfully close to breaking into the industrial harbour -- they're bound to have information for us. I thought that was our intention...
Zh: “我们在做什么？我们离闯入工业港口只有咫尺之遥——他们一定会为我们提供信息。我觉得那也是我们的目的……”

En: Or it could be that we're just *exploring*. He looks around, wind rustling his hair.
Zh: 他看了看周围，头发被风吹地沙沙作响。“或者我们只是在*探索*。”

En: I was under the assumption we could ask the leader of this Union to help us get this body down. This is why we're here, right? He doesn't wait for you to answer.
Zh: “我原以为我们可以去找工会老大，让他帮我们把尸体弄下来。这就是我们来这里的原因，不是吗？”他并没有等待你的回答。

En: When the wind ruffles the cloak you can most definitely see a white rectangle on its back.
Zh: 当风吹起斗篷的时候，你几乎可以肯定自己在它的背面看到一个白色的矩形。

En: You could swear it's more of an instinct than an actual sight, but you *know* there's the RCM's signature rectangle on the cloak. It's a cop's cloak.\n
Zh: 你可以发誓，相比真切的视觉，这更像是一种本能，但是你*知道*斗篷上有RCM的标志矩形。这是一件警察的斗篷。

En: You could swear it's more of an instinct than anything else, but you *know* it's a cop's cloak.
Zh: 你可以发誓这更像是一种本能，但是你*知道*这是一件警察的斗篷。

En: Look, lieutenant! (Point to the flapping cloak.) Someone left his cloak behind.
Zh: “看，警督！（指向那件翻飞的斗篷。）有人把斗篷落下了。”

En: Ignore the cloak. [Leave.]
Zh: 无视这件斗篷。[离开。]

En: I guess it's probably nothing. [Leave.]
Zh: 我猜可能没什么重要的吧。[离开。]

En: Yes. It's probably yours. It bears the RCM insignia and you have a habit of being careless with your equipment... He judges the drop.
Zh: “是的，有可能是你的。那上面有RCM的标志，而且你有对自己的装备粗心大意的习惯——”他在判断落差。

En: Yes. It is probably yours. It does bear the RCM insignia after all. Now -- how to retrieve it? He judges the drop.
Zh: “是的，有可能是你的。毕竟上面有RCM的标志。现在——该怎么把它拿回来呢？”他在判断落差。

En: You could probably make the jump. You look like you've done some track and field in your day.
Zh: “你也许能跳过去。看起来你以前好像玩过田径。”

En: You son of a gun, it's a cop's cloak!
Zh: “你个王八蛋的，那是件警察的斗篷！”

En: Indeed it is.
Zh: 确实是的。

En: Considering the limited police presence in Martinaise -- you think we might take a leap of logic and say it's yours?
Zh: 考虑到马丁内斯有限的警力——你觉得我们是不是可以来点逻辑上的飞跃，说它是你的呢？

En: You son of a gun, it's my cloak!
Zh: 你个王八蛋的，那是我的斗篷！

En: The white rectangle, now clearly visible in the wind, seems to confirm your hypothesis. But who's the cop?
Zh: 白色矩形，现在在风中清晰可见，好像证实了你的假设。但是这个警察是谁？

En: You are the cop. It's your cloak. Don't make things complicated.
Zh: 你是警察。这是你的斗篷。别把事情搞复杂了。

En: Nailed it!
Zh: 搞定了！

En: A cop's cloak! It's a cop's cloak!!!
Zh: “一件警察的斗篷！那是一件警察的斗篷！！！”

En: Unaware of your existence the cloak continues its helpless flapping.
Zh: 没有注意到你的存在，斗篷依然无助地上下翻飞着。

En: Go for the cloak -- jump over the ledge!
Zh: 为了你的斗篷——跳过这个平台！

En: Backed by Physical Instrument.
Zh: 有人体机器的支持。

En: Amber Die with a Mosquito.
Zh: 镶嵌着一只蚊子的琥珀骰子。

En: Not in these pants.
Zh: 穿着这条裤子可不行。

En: What exactly are we doing up here?
Zh: “我们到底在这里干什么？”

En: Look around.
Zh: 看看四周。

En: Do you really think this cloak is mine? Should I go for it? Jump?
Zh: “你真觉得那件斗篷是我的吗？我是不是该把它拿回来？跳过去？”

En: Yeah, not right now. [Leave.]
Zh: “是啊，现在不行。”[离开。]

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Nope! No! That's still too high. What were you thinking, you're not a gymnast, you're a boxer -- and you've climbed WAY too high up here...\n
Zh: 不！不可以！还是太高了。不管你在想什么，你不是一个体操运动员，你是个拳击手——而且你爬的实在有点太高了……

En: Holy wow, that's *high*! 'Doesn't seem dangerous...' The lieutenant must be crazy to suggest that! This is certain death. (Vertigo almost pushes you over the edge.)\n
Zh: 我滴个乖乖，真是有够*高*的！‘看起来好像不危险啊……’警督这么建议一定是疯了！这样肯定会没命的。（眩晕几乎要把你推向崩溃的边缘。）

En: Holy wow, that's *high*! You'd have to be a total madman to jump. This is certain death!
Zh: 我滴个乖乖，真是有够*高*的！要是跳过去你绝对是疯了。这肯定会没命的！

En: As you leap in the air, a chilly breeze engulfs you, sharpening you senses.
Zh: 你跳向空中，一阵冷风将你吞没，让你的感官也变得敏锐起来。

En: Close your eyes, let your senses take in the world around you.
Zh: 闭上眼睛，让你的感官去体会周围的世界。

En: Ankles tingling from the tension, blood roaring in your ears -- you are ready for your rendezvous with the concrete pavement below.
Zh: 紧绷的脚踝微微刺痛，血液在你的耳中轰鸣——你已经准备好与下面的混凝土路面亲密接触了。

En: Martinaise goes about its daily routine as you soar through the air. The loud voices of protesters mix with the engine sounds from the traffic jam; waves crash against the pier and dense, salty sea air fills your lungs.
Zh: 当你在空中翱翔的时候，马丁内斯还在进行着它的日常。抗议者响亮的呼喊声跟交通堵塞的引擎声混杂在一起；海浪拍打着码头，浓密微咸的海风充满你的肺部。

En: Don't let go of the moment yet.
Zh: 先不要放走这个时刻。

En: The corpse is dominating the yard and the stench is nauseating, even so far from the epicentre. It brings tears to your eyes.
Zh: 尸体俯视着整个院子，就算如此远离震中，那股恶臭还是让人作呕，让你眼泪盈眶。

En: We'll take care of that body too. We'll take care of all of it!
Zh: 我们会处理好那具尸体的。我们全都会处理好的！

En: As the concrete floor welcomes you, you realize it's been a while since you felt so alive, alert, capable. Must be the adrenaline.
Zh: 混凝土路面欢迎着你，你意识到自己已经有一阵子没感觉到如此地活跃、机敏和能干。一定是肾上腺素的原因。

En: The little backyard, Kingdom of Cuno, looks almost idyllic when it's not dominated by a decomposing human being.
Zh: 这个小后院，坤诺的王国，当它不再被一具腐烂的尸体支配的时候，看起来几乎如田园诗一般。

En: Flying f****t... winged pig... shit is airborne... These are the only words the wind coming in from the Insulindian ocean carries to you.
Zh: “会飞的混球……长翅膀的猪头……屎都会飞了……”风只从伊苏林迪海洋那里捎过来这几个字眼。

En: And its de-facto ruler, Cuno the Gremlin King, is pointing at *you*, talking to his friend. What is he saying?
Zh: 还有它实际上的统治者，小鬼国王坤诺，正指着*你*，跟他的朋友说话。他在说什么？

En: His words remain indiscernible. No matter -- it's time to start thinking about the landing.
Zh: 你听不见他的话。不要紧——是时候开始考虑一下着陆的事了。

En: Continue the voyage through the salty air.
Zh: 继续在咸腥的空气中航行。

En: Hell yeah! The lieutenant exclaims. "My climbing might not have been as disco as your jump, but can we still get an Ace's High?"
Zh: “我去！”警督大声呼喊。“我的攀爬技巧可能不如你的跳跃一样迪斯科，不过我们还能来下前扑吗？”

En: FOR THE PEOPLE!!!
Zh: “为了人民！！！”

En: REVACHOL!!!
TORQUE!!!
TARE!!!
FRANCONEGRO!!!
Zh: “弗兰考格斯！！！”

En: Not bad, not too bad at all.
Zh: “还不赖，一点也不赖。”

En: The cloak? I *do* think it's yours, yes. As to whether you should go for it... He looks over the ledge, at the cold pavement below.
Zh: “斗篷？我*的确*认为它是你的，没错。至于你应不应该去拿……”他看着平台对面，还有下面冰冷的人行道。

En: The cloak looks like a bag of goodies floating in the wind. Who knows what its pockets may hide?!
Zh: 斗篷看起来像是一袋在风中漂浮的巧克力糖。谁知道里面可能隐藏着什么？！

En: You could have died there! Shit, shit, shit, shit... I'm failing you. This wasn't part of our deal!
Zh: 你可能会在那里死掉的！该死，该死，该死，该死……我让你失望了。我们可不是这么约定的！

En: I got you, I got you, *bratan*. Let me just adjust your breathing a little bit... There, isn't that better?
Zh: 我懂你的，我懂你，*兄弟*。让我稍微帮你调整一下呼吸……怎么样，感觉好多了吗？

En: (Tug on your tie.) God, now I can't breathe at all... Stop doing this to me, necktie, you were supposed to be a *friend*!
Zh: （扯一扯你的领带。）天啦，我快不能呼吸了……别这么做了，领带，你不应该是我的*朋友*吗！

En: Hey, hey... what happened? He sounds genuinely worried.
Zh: “喂，喂……出什么事了？”他听起来真的很担心。

En: The tears just come. From nowhere. Like a sudden guerrilla attack from the bushes, they overwhelm you.
Zh: 眼泪流了下来。不知道从何而来。就像一支游击队从灌木丛中发起突袭，瞬间将你淹没。

En: Hey, hey... what's happening? You okay? He sounds genuinely worried.
Zh: “喂，喂……出什么事了？你还好吗？”他听起来真的很担心。

En: I'm alright, Kim... I... I just... (Keep tugging on your tie.) "I can't *breathe*."
Zh: “我很好，金……我……我只是……”（继续拉扯你的领带。）“我不能*呼吸*了。”

En: It's okay, you're just having a *little* panic attack. Try to breathe as slowly as you can, alright? He shakes you gently by the shoulder.
Zh: 他轻轻摇了摇你的肩膀：“没事的，你只是稍微*有点*惊恐发作。试着慢慢呼吸，好吗？”

En: Thanks, I think it's working. (Let go of your tie.)
Zh: “谢谢，我觉得好像起作用了。”（松开你的领带。）

En: After a while your sobbing stops. Up here no one saw the little meltdown you just had and your heart actually feels lighter now. Time to proceed.
Zh: 过了一会儿，你停止了啜泣。这里没人看见你刚才崩溃的样子，你的心情确实感觉放松了不少。是时候继续前进了。

En: Tears run down your face. You're clutching your chest and mumbling incoherently. How much worse can it get?
Zh: 眼泪从你的脸颊上滑落。你捂住胸口，语无伦次地喃喃自语。情况还能比现在更糟糕吗？

En: Just let me fucking *breathe*, Horrific Necktie.
Zh: 我他妈的要*呼吸*，你这条可怕的领带。

En: Feel the squeeze, baby!
Zh: 感觉这种窒息感，宝贝！

En: Fine, be boring, breathe! -- Get high on some of that sweet-sweet O, baby!
Zh: 好吧，那就无聊一点，呼吸！因为那份甜蜜兴奋起来吧，宝贝！

En: Just let it all out, let it go, let it happen.
Zh: 把一切都释放出来，放手吧，让它发生。

En: As you let go a tidal wave of tears breaks through the dam and washes away everything.
Zh: 当你放手的那一瞬间，眼泪如浪潮一般冲破大坝，把一切全都冲走了。

En: Right, I can't do it right now -- I'll make the jump next time.
Zh: “没错，现在不行——我下次再跳过去。”

En: Sure. He looks at you, worried. "Just be careful, okay? Looks like you almost strained a muscle there."
Zh: “当然。”他担忧地看着你。“小心一点，好吗，看起来你差点拉伤肌肉了。”

En: I was under the impression we're on our way to meet the king of this *castle under siege* -- Evrart -- one of the people we're interviewing. This way we may be able to get around his henchmen.
Zh: “我记得我们正打算去见这座*被围困城堡*的国王——艾弗拉特——我们走访名单上的一个人。这样我们也许就能说服他的手下。”

En: The wind is aggressive up here. The lieutenant looks at the enormous crane towering in the distance, over the container yard.
Zh: 这里的风变得很有侵略性。警督看着远方集装箱堆场里那台巨大的起重机。

En: The look in his eyes is a mix of the engineer-like interest and the wonder of a six year old seeing a horse for the first time.
Zh: 他的眼神里混杂着工程师般的兴趣，还有第一次看见马的六岁孩童的惊叹。

En: Kvalsund 10-20 HK... (Point at the crane.)
Zh: “库瓦桑德10-20HK……”（指着那台起重机。）

En: Motherfucker... (Let out a whistle.) "Now that's a huge crane!"
Zh: “我去……”（吹一声口哨。）那台起重机可真是够大的！”

En: It's just a machine, lieutenant. Like a giant hammer or a pair of pliers. Nothing to see here.
Zh: “它只是一台机器，警督。像是一个巨大的铁锤，或者是老虎钳。没什么好看的。”

En: Let him have this moment of wonder.
Zh: 让他享受这惊奇的一刻。

En: His eyes trace the crane's contour as if trying to memorize its majestic shape. Then he notices your gaze.
Zh: 他的眼睛勾画着起重机的轮廓，似乎想要记住它那宏伟的形状。然后他注意到你的视线。

En: I think there's a certain beauty in this giant pair of pliers. But you're right, we were focusing on your *cloak*...
Zh: “我觉得这个巨大的老虎钳有一种特别的美。不过你说得对，我们要专注在你的*斗篷*上……”

En: Yes, that's one way of putting it. He turns to you with a faint smile, then looks back at the crane. "It's certainly an impressive achievement of engineering."
Zh: “是的，的确可以这么形容它。”他转向你，脸上带着一丝微笑，然后又把目光转移到起重机身上。“它的确是一项令人惊叹的工程学成就。”

En: Is it? He looks at you, impressed. "Kvalsund makes lot of heavy equipment, but this is phenomenal even for them."
Zh: “是吗？”他看着你，有些惊讶。“库瓦桑德出产很多重型设备，不过就算是对于他们来说，这也是很叹为观止的。”

En: The tarpaulin cloak is still hanging on the railing. The white rectangle of the Revachol Citizens Militia is clearly visible on its back.\n
Zh: 防水斗篷还挂在栏杆上。代表瑞瓦肖公民武装的白色矩形在它的背面清晰可见。

En: Looks like someone left his tarpaulin cloak hanging on the railing here. The white rectangle of the Revachol Citizens Militia is clearly visible on its back.\n
Zh: 看来某人把他的防水斗篷落在栏杆上了。你能清晰地看见它的背面有一个白色的矩形。

En: Looks like someone left his tarpaulin cloak hanging on the railing here. There is a white rectangle clearly visible on its back.
Zh: 看来某人把他的防水斗篷落在栏杆上了。你能清晰地看见它的背面有一个白色的矩形。

En: Grab the cloak. [Leave.]
Zh: 抓住斗篷。[离开。]

En: Yes. It was probably you. He looks at you. "It does bear the RCM insignia after all."
Zh: “是啊，它可能是你的。”他看着你。“毕竟上面确实有RCM的标记。”

En: Look, lieutenant. (Point to the cloak.) Someone left their cloak behind.
Zh: “看，警督！（指向斗篷。）有人把他的斗篷落下了。”

En: You think I should get it?
Zh: “你觉得我应该把它拿回来？”

En: The service cloak issued to you by your station? Yes, yes I do.
Zh: “单位发给你的警用斗篷？是的，没错，我觉得要拿回来。”

En: As your fingers touch the tarpaulin it almost feels like the cloak wants to deliver a message of comfort through your fingertips...
Zh: 你的手指碰触到斗篷，几乎感觉它想要通过你的指尖传递一条安慰的信息……

En: Just a cop's cloak... I don't want to deal with this right now. [Leave.]
Zh: 只是一件警察的斗篷……我现在不想处理它的事。[离开。]

En: The cloak does not concern me at the moment. [Leave.]
Zh: 这件斗篷现在不关我的事。[离开。]

En: Also, you should be having a drink right now -- you should have a bottle of alcohol in your hand *right now*.
Zh: 而且，你现在就应该去喝一杯——你的手里*现在*就应该拿着一瓶酒。

En: What was that about a drink? Wouldn't that ruin the moment?
Zh: 喝一杯是想干嘛？那不就毁掉这个时刻了？

En: *I will shield you from the elements and give my life for yours* -- that's what the cloak is relaying.
Zh: *它会为你挡风遮雨，为你舍弃自己的生命*——斗篷传递出这条信息。

En: I knew you could do it! The lieutenant exclaims. "My climbing down might not have been as disco as your jump, but at least we can explore the harbour now."
Zh: “我就知道你能做到的！”警督呼喊着。“我这样爬下去可能不如你的纵身一跃那么迪斯科，但至少我们可以探索港口了。”

En: A tarpaulin cloak with possible RCM markings is still caught on the railing.
Zh: 一件很可能带有RCM标志的防水斗篷仍然挂在栏杆上。

En: A tarpaulin cloak is still caught on the railing. No one has claimed it for their own.
Zh: 一件防水斗篷仍然挂在栏杆上。没人前来认领。

En: Don't be ridiculous, of course not.
Zh: 别说这些荒唐话，当然不是了。

En: No, a drink *would* ruin the pristine beauty of this moment of ultimate sobriety.
Zh: 不，一杯酒*会*毁掉这极致清醒一刻的原始之美。

En: Damn, you're right! Beer gushing into my face would really *nail it* right now.
Zh: 该死。你说得对！如果现在有啤酒在我脸上喷涌，那就真是太*成功*了。

En: I have a bottle in hand right now -- I could go for it!
Zh: 我现在手里就有一瓶酒——我可以试试！

En: Don't really know why you haven't yet.
Zh: 真不知道你为什么还没动手。

En: That would be incredibly unsound at this moment.
Zh: 在这种时刻会是非常不合理的。

En: Time is running out -- continue the voyage and celebrate later.
Zh: 时间不多了——继续航行吧，稍后再庆祝。

En: Yeah. You'd be like the ultimate rock and roll mega star. A flying legend.
Zh: 是啊。你会像个超级摇滚巨星一样。一个飞翔的传说。

En: What have you done to my precious alcoholic friend? You're no fun anymore.
Zh: 你对我亲爱的酒鬼朋友做了什么？你一点都不好玩了。

En: No one hears your shouts, but it makes you feel powerful inside. Now, with your feet firmly planted on the concrete, the noise of the harbour rushes back in.
Zh: 没人听见你的叫喊声，不过这让你感觉内心充满力量。现在，你的双脚牢牢踩在混凝土上面，港口的喧闹声又冲了回来。

En: No one is listening to your words, but it doesn't matter. You feel pride. Now, with your feet firmly planted on the concrete, the noise of the harbour rushes back in.
Zh: 没人听到你的话语，不过不要紧。你感觉很骄傲。现在，你的双脚牢牢踩在混凝土上面，港口的喧闹声又冲了回来。

En: With your feet firmly planted on the concrete, the noise of the harbour rushes back in.
Zh: 你的双脚牢牢踩在混凝土上面，港口的喧闹声又冲了回来。

En: Will you? Because it feels like you strained a muscle there. Or two.
Zh: 你会吗？因为感觉像是有一块肌肉拉伤了。或者是两块。

En: The necktie is guiding you through this. It's your spirit animal, both your nemesis and friend. Suddenly a feeling of ease brushes through you -- you're fine again.
Zh: 领带会引导你度过这一关。它是你的灵兽，既是你的敌人，又是朋友。突然间，一种轻松的感觉掠过你的全身——你又没事了。

En: The sadness, the stress, the failures of the past and present and the weight of being you. It would wash you away, too, if it weren't for the firm grip of your necktie.
Zh: 悲伤，压力，过去和现在的失败，还有做你自己的负担。如果不是因为领带的牢牢把握，你也可能会被冲走的。

En: I'm alright, Kim... I... I just can't do it right now.
Zh: “我没事，金……我……我只是现在做不到。”

En: I understand. We can always come back when you're feeling better. It's just a cloak after all.
Zh: “我理解。等你感觉好一点之后，我们随时可以回来。毕竟这只是一件斗篷。”

En: Interesting. Why would someone leave a cloak there, and how can I get to it?
Zh: 有意思。为什么有人会把斗篷落在这里，我怎么才能拿到它呢？

En: The cloak flaps in the wind, invitingly. It's almost within your reach, if you only just... just go for it.
Zh: 斗篷在风中动人地飞舞。几乎就在你伸手可及的地方，如果你可以……放手去做。

En: He's thinking you've forgotten where you are again.
Zh: 他以为你又忘记自己在哪了。

En: He's so on the money, *bratan*. Just imagine yourself dual-wielding a bottle and a flaming cigarette! Whilst airborne!
Zh: 这可真是浑然天成啊，*哥们*。想象一下自己一手抓着酒瓶，一手夹着点燃的香烟！而且还是在半空中！

En: Telling the gardener you know her name might throw her off? Perhaps something to consider, later...
Zh: 告诉园丁你知道她的名字，也许能迷惑她？也许之后可以考虑一下……

En: The accent is so thick it's impossible not to notice he's Ubi. From the vanishing peninsula of Ubi Sunt? (sic) on Mundi.
Zh: 他的口音实在太重，很难不注意到他是尤比人。来自正在消失的尤比萨特半岛？（属于蒙迪）

En: You're Ubi, right?
Zh: “你是尤比人，对吧？”

En: Oh yes, born and raised in Iraesh, mister. Mum had to leave my dad after he got a bit violent, took us here to the New New World. I was about ten then, too old to lose my accent then...
Zh: “是的，生长在艾瑞斯，先生。我父亲变得有些过于暴力之后，母亲就无奈地离开他了，把我们带到这里，这个新新世界。那时我大概10岁，已经很大了，很难丢掉家乡口音……”

En: People say us Ubis are up to all sorts of trouble with sheep and other animals and what not -- I just want you to know there was *never* any of that where I come from. No sir. Those are just nasty rumours.
Zh: “大家都说我们尤比人一直在找山羊和其他动物的麻烦，等等之类的——我只想让你知道，我的家乡*根本没有*这种情况。没有的，先生。那些只是恶意的谣言。”

En: Thank you for clarifying that, sir.
Zh: “感谢你的澄清，先生。”

En: He probably doesn't know anything anyway. Who in their right mind would tell *him*?
Zh: 反正他可能什么也不知道。哪个心智正常的人会告诉*他*呢？

En: Okay, so Evrart has a brother Edgar. That's something -- but the rest of it is taking *too* long my friend...
Zh: 好吧，艾弗拉特有个兄弟叫埃德加。这还有点价值——不过其他部分的时间真是*太长*了，我的朋友……

En: South Safre? A lot of *bulk* chemical manufacturing going on there. A lot of cargo shipments being made too.
Zh: 南萨弗里？那里生产了*大量*的化学制品。还有很多货物运输。

En: The trade must've been lucrative for the trip to be so long.
Zh: 那么长的行程，这笔交易肯定获利良多。

En: Honestly. I don't know *what* he does for us, but it must be important because everybody likes him. Yes, they do. I think that's what he does, he makes everyone feel a little better.
Zh: “老实说。我不知道他为我们做了*什么*，不过一定很重要，因为大家都喜欢他。是的，没错。我想这就是他的工作，让大家感觉更好一些。”

En: Oil for the wheels. Much needed in stressful times like these.
Zh: 给事情加点润滑油。在这种紧张的时刻很有必要。

En: Once he said he's a *dragon* to this mob fellow who came picking a fight with some Union men. Heh, I think he really believed Jean-Luc *was* a dragon, because he ran right off. Another time he almost killed another guy, but... I shouldn't talk about that.
Zh: “他曾经说过，他是这些暴民眼里的*恶龙*，专门来找工会的一些人打架。嗨，我觉得他真心相信让·卢克*曾经是*一头恶龙，因为他马上就跑了。还有一次，他差点杀了个人，不过……我不该提这个的。”

En: That's precisely what he is. Evrart's dragon.
Zh: 他就是如此。艾弗拉特的恶龙。

En: Mr. Martin -- Gaston also taught History and Human Studies to the Claire brothers.
Zh: 马丁先生——加斯顿也是克莱尔兄弟的历史和人类学老师。

En: Thank you, mister... Heh! Almost forgot! Mr. Evrart is usually in that container over there. Leo points to his left. "But he already left for the day. He never stays after 22."
Zh: “谢谢你，先生……啊！我差点忘了！艾弗拉特先生一般会在那边的集装箱里。”利奥指向左边。“但是他今天已经离开了。他从来不会待到22点之后。”

En: Thank you... Heh! Almost forgot! Mr. Evrart is usually in that container over there. Leo points to his left. "But he usually leaves around 22, so you gotta be quick if you wanna catch him."
Zh: “谢谢你……啊！我差点忘了！艾弗拉特先生一般会在那边的集装箱里。”利奥指向左边。“不过他一般会在22点左右离开，所以如果你想找他的话最好动作快点。”

En: Oh, Mr. Evrart is usually in his office, of course. He points to the two joint containers on your right. "But he already left for the day. He always leaves around 22."
Zh: “哦，当然了，艾弗拉特先生一般都待在自己的办公室里。”他指向你右侧那两个连在一起的集装箱。“但是他今天已经离开了。他总是在22点左右离开。”

En: Oh, Mr. Evrart is usually in his office, of course. He points to the two joint containers on your right. "But you gotta be quick if you wanna see him. He leaves around 22."
Zh: “哦，当然了，艾弗拉特先生一般都待在自己的办公室里。”他指向你右侧那两个连在一起的集装箱。“你想见他的话动作要快一点。他大概22点左右离开。“

En: Now he's talking about some drunk sawmill owner who... no, he already switched to a prized fishing rod he apparently owned at some point... You know what -- just cut in there with your questions.
Zh: 现在他说的是一个醉酒的锯木厂老板……不，他已经换了个话题，说到自己曾经拥有的一根价格不菲的钓杆……你知道吗——直接问问题，打断他吧。

En: Thanks, Leo. Another question though?
Zh: “谢谢你，利奥。还能再问个问题吗？”

En: The night guard? He was a peculiar fellow. Headstrong and proud. We didn't really talk all that much. The little guy looks you in the eye. "He died recently. Somethink with his ticker..."
Zh: “守夜人？哦，他是个奇怪的家伙。顽固又自负。我们真没怎么说过话。”小个子看着你的眼睛。“他最近死了。心脏问题……”

En: I mean, I don't personally mind, folks is just folks, you know -- and folks gotta eat... He doesn't seem to be waiting for you to answer.
Zh: “我的意思是，我个人并不介意，乡亲就是乡亲，你知道的——而且乡亲要吃饭的……”他好像没有在等待你的答案。

En: What are you doing with the containers?
Zh: “你在对集装箱做什么？”

En: Do you work here?
Zh: “你在这里工作吗？”

En: Okay, I'm off. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我走了。”[离开。]

En: Bye bye now! He returns to his work.
Zh: “再见了！”他继续回去工作了。

En: I'm looking for the leader of the Dockworkers' Union.
Zh: “我正在找码头工人工会的会长。”

En: Where is everyone? The harbour is empty.
Zh: “大家都到哪里去了？这个港口都空了。”

En: Evrart, Evrart, Evrart, he looks after everyone. Huh... well, hey there! He smiles. "How can I help you, mister?"
Zh: “艾弗拉特，艾弗拉特，艾弗拉特，他照顾所有人。啊……你好！”他笑了。“有什么能帮你的吗，先生？”

En: The look in his deep blue eyes is as sincere as you've ever seen. Kind of makes you feel like an asshole for no apparent reason.
Zh: 他深蓝色的眼睛里流露出你前所未见的真诚。让你莫名其妙地觉得自己就是个混蛋。

En: Container, container, used to be Wild Pines. Container, container now belongs to Evrart! The tiny man is so engaged in his work he doesn't notice you.
Zh: “集装箱，集装箱，以前是野松公司的。集装箱，集装箱，现在属于艾弗拉特！”小个子男人工作地很投入，根本没有注意到你。

En: I see you are not a Union man, mister. Did you get lost? You're not one of them scabs, are you? A shadow passes over his kind face.
Zh: “我看你不是工会的人，先生。你迷路了吗？你不是工贼，对吧？”一道阴影掠过他友善的脸庞。

En: Oh, most of the guys are down at the gates, keeping the scabs from coming in... He leans in with a confidential look...
Zh: “哦，大部分人都在门下面，不让那些工贼进来……”他靠近了一些，脸上带着信任的表情……

En: Oh I'm just making some covers for them containers here, yes I am, yes I am... So it's easier for the crane operators to spot them. He waves at the containers towering behind him.
Zh: “哦，我只是给集装箱刷漆，是的，是的……这样起重机操作员就更容易找到它们的位置了。”他朝着背后的集装箱挥了挥手。

En: Yes-yes, everybody needs a job and this is mine. I'm Leonard, by the way, Leonard Bellec, but everyone calls me Leo. The little man raises his hand in a welcoming gesture.
Zh: “是的，是的，大家都需要工作，这就是我的工作。话说，我是利奥纳德，利奥纳德·贝莱克，不过大家都叫我利奥。”小个子举起手表示欢迎。

En: Oh, you want Mr. Evrart, then. He's an awfully nice fellow, he is. Him and his brother are both nice fellows -- they've lived their entire lives in this here neighbourhood. He coughs, then continues immediately...
Zh: “哦，你要找艾弗拉特先生啊。他是个特别好的人，没错。他和他的兄弟都是很好的人——他们一辈子都住在附近的街区。”他咳了几声，然后立马继续……

En: Guys like Mr. Evrart and Mr. Edgar -- his brother -- are real good guys, made Martinaise what it is today... Mr. Evrart and Mr. Edgar and I went to the same school, we did, when we were boys...
Zh: “像艾弗拉特先生，还有他的兄弟埃德加先生——都是很好的人，让马丁内斯能够变成现在的样子……艾弗拉特先生和埃德加先生，还有我上的是同一所学校，没错，当我们还是小男孩的时候……”

En: Easy now, Leo -- I just want to know where can I find this man.
Zh: “放松一些，利奥——我只想知道能在哪找到这个人。”

En: Tell me about this Edgar guy you keep mentioning?
Zh: “跟我说说你一直提的这个埃德加先生？”

En: Do not interrupt Leonard.
Zh: 不要打断利奥纳德。

En: Mr. Edgar is Mr. Evrart's brother. He's looks a bit younger, he does, but a very smart fellow, very smart fellow indeed. He's away on some Union business... Not even in Revachol, they say...
Zh: “埃德加先生是艾弗拉特先生的兄弟。他看起来稍微年轻一些，也确实是这样，不过他是个非常聪明的人，确实很聪明。他出去办工会的事了……他们说，好像都不在瑞瓦肖……”

En: All kinds of places he visits. Him and his brother both do when they're on a vacation. Right now it's Mr. Evrart's turn to look after the Union, but last year he spent a whole winter in South Safre. He chuckles.
Zh: “他去过各种各样的地方。他还有他的兄弟度假的时候去的。现在轮到艾弗拉特先生照顾工会，不过去年他整个冬天都待在南萨弗里。”他轻笑出声。

En: Let me stop you there, Leo, I had another question.
Zh: “我要打断你一下，利奥，我还有个别的问题。”

En: Don't interrupt Leo.
Zh: 不要打断利奥。

En: Uh... sure, mister, he seems a bit surprised by your abruptness. "What is it?"
Zh: “呃……当然可以，先生，”他好像对你的唐突感到有些吃惊。“什么事？”

En: Oh, Mr. Evrart is where he always is, in his office, of course. He points to the two joint containers on your right.
Zh: “哦，艾弗拉特先生一直待在那儿。当然了，我是说在他的办公室里。”他指着你右边两个连起来的集装箱。

En: ... had an arithmetics teacher, Miss Bellows. Leo let's out a little chuckle. "Her real name was Miss Bellams, she was a real pretty lady, but when she got mad..." He starts laughing.
Zh: “……有一个算数老师，贝洛斯小姐，”利奥轻轻笑出了声。“她的真名就叫贝洛斯小姐，她是个很漂亮的女士，不过她生气的时候……”他开始笑了。

En: All the boys liked her if you know what I mean, mister... He winks at you. "We used to sneak in her yard in the dark and peek through the window. One time we saw Miss Bellows with a fellow. Yes we did, yes we did, mister." He looks for signs of disbelief in your eyes.
Zh: “所有男孩子都喜欢她，如果你知道我是什么意思，先生……”他朝你眨眨眼。“我们以前会在晚上溜进她的院子，然后从窗户偷窥。有一次我们看见贝洛斯小姐跟一个家伙在一起。没错，是这样的，先生。”他在你的眼睛里看到一丝怀疑的迹象。

En: Evrart, Leo, where can I find Evrart? (Interrupt him.)
Zh: “艾弗拉特，利奥，我去哪找艾弗拉特？”（打断他）

En: Don't say anything.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: No you fucking didn't!
Zh: “不，你不是吧！”

En: He keeps nodding and looking at you with a smile that's too sincere to be clever. Finally he seems to loose some internal struggle and adds: "Them was naked too, that's all I got to say about that."
Zh: 他一直点着头，面带微笑的看着你，那种笑容太过真诚，感觉不太聪明的样子。最后他好像放松了一些内心的挣扎，然后补充到：“他们都没穿衣服，这就是我要说的。”

En: Very interesting, very interesting. Thank you for telling me the whole thing.
Zh: “很有意思，真的很有意思。感谢你告诉我整个故事。”

En: Yes we did, we did, mister. He looks at you and shakes his head. "Indeed we did. Him was a big, big fellow, used to drive the *troyka* near our'ses school and everything."
Zh: “是的我们看见了，是的，先生。”他看着你，摇摇头。“我们确实看到了。他是个大高个，以前在我们的学校附近开*三套車*。”

En: Leo leans in and whispers: "Them was all naked too. That's all I got to say about that."
Zh: 利奥靠近了一些，低语到：“他们都没穿衣服，这就是我要说的。”

En: I'm like Mr. Evrart's right-hand man, when Mr. Edgar is out of town -- and Mr. *Edgar's* right hand man when Mr. Evrart is away! He chuckles.
Zh: “当埃德加先生出城的时候，我就像艾弗拉特先生的得力助手——艾弗拉特先生不在的时候，我就是*埃德加*先生的得力助手！”他咯咯地笑了。

En: But I can't listen to the radio all the time. There's so much to do around here and I'm always busy keeping things running here. Yes I am, yes I am.
Zh: “但是我不能一直听广播。这里有很多可以做的事，我总是在忙着打理这里的事务。没错，这就是我，没错。”

En: Kim asked you who this Miss Beaufort is.
Zh: “金问你这位博福特小姐事谁。”

En: Hold on, who's this Miss Beaufort you mentioned?
Zh: “等等，你提到的这个博福特小姐是谁？”

En: Oh, Lizzy? She is a real sharp tool. Mr. Evrart put her through some fancy school and everything, east of the river. Four years she was gone and when she came back she was all fancy and *law-yerly*.
Zh: “哦，莉齐啊？她可是个厉害的人儿。艾弗拉特先生让她上了一所很好的学校什么的，就在河东。她离开了4年，等回来之后，她就变得很时髦，还有很*律师范*。

En: He respects that word -- that's obvious.
Zh: 他很尊重那个词——这很明显。

En: But she's a real nice girl, grew up in this here neighbourhood, knows everybody and gets along with everyone, real pillar of the community one day, I'm sure.
Zh: “但她真的是个好姑娘，在这片街区长大，认识所有人，跟大家相处都很好，我肯定她总有一天会变成社区的支柱。”

En: Doctor Lemaitre said so, and she knows about such things. Been a doctor for almost fifty years, she has... He sighs and falls silent, watching you meekly with his blue, blue eyes.
Zh: “勒迈特医生也这么说，而且她很懂这些东西。她当了差不多50年的医生，她……”他叹了口气，然后陷入沉默，用那双蓝色的眼睛温顺地看着你。

En: Leo, you seem to know everyone around here, I want to ask about someone.
Zh: “利奥，你好像认识这里的所有人，我想跟你打听个人。”

En: Mr. Evrart doesn't really want me to talk to people about Union guys... He thinks for a moment. "But who did you want to talk about?"
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生不想让我跟别人说工会的人……”他想了一会儿。“但是你想问谁？”

En: Tell me about Measurehead.
Zh: “跟我说说测颅先生。”

En: Tell me about the guy in the container.
Zh: “跟我说说集装箱里那个人。”

En: Tell me about Evrart.
Zh: “跟我说说艾弗拉特。”

En: Tell me about Mañana.
Zh: “跟我说说马列拉。”

En: Tell me about René.
Zh: “跟我说说雷内。”

En: ...but Titus and his boys got into some drunken trouble and Evrart sent them on a nice vacation. For a week or so... He stops -- but seems eager to tell you more.
Zh: “……但是提图斯和他的兄弟喝醉酒惹了麻烦，艾弗拉特送他们愉快地度了个假。大概有一个多星期吧……”他停了下来——不过很想再多跟你说一些的样子。

En: What kind of trouble did this... Titus and his friends get into?
Zh: “提图斯和他的朋友……惹上什么麻烦了？”

En: What trouble did Titus and his friends get into?
Zh: “提图斯和他的兄弟惹上什么麻烦了？”

En: Got it. But there were other things I needed to discuss...
Zh: “明白了。不过我还有别的事想和你讨论……”

En: Like what, mister? His wide smile is truly disarming.
Zh: “比如说什么呢，先生？”他那张灿烂的笑脸真的很容易让人消除戒备。

En: Oh... I'm not really supposed to talk about that. That's Union business. He smiles and leans closer.
Zh: “哦……我真的不应该说这个。这是工会的事。”他笑了笑，靠的更近了。

En: Him and his boys stirred something up in town. Probably drank too much and got into a fight or something... I heard Mr. Evrart telling them to take some time off...
Zh: “他和他的兄弟在城里惹了一些麻烦。可能是喝太多，然后打了一架之类的……我听说艾弗拉特先生让他们休息一段时间……”

En: I guess the boys got a bit too rowdy and had to let out some steam. I don't really know the details. That's just how boys are you know... Another chuckle. "I haven't been in a fight since I was in middle-school..."
Zh: “我猜那些家伙可能是太暴力了，也需要发泄一下。细节我真的不太清楚。男孩子嘛，就是这样的……”又是一声轻笑。“我自从上中学之后就没打过架了……”

En: I remember I was the runt of the class. He laughs merrily. "The bigger boys always used to pick on me. You see, I had a bit of a temper back in the day, flew off the handle like nobody's business..."
Zh: “我记得自己是班里最矮的。”他高兴地笑了。“那些大个子男孩总是喜欢欺负我。你看，有一天我刚好有些脾气，然后就大发雷霆……”

En: But Mr. Evrart and his brother always came to help. Once they beat old Noel Becker so bad he needed stitches on his head... He chuckles again. "Noel never started another fight with anyone after this."
Zh: “不过艾弗拉特先生和他的兄弟总是来帮忙。有一次他们狠狠地揍了一顿老诺埃尔·贝克，结果他的头上都缝了几针……”他又笑了起来。“在那之后，诺埃尔再也没有和任何人打过架了。”

En: Mr. Evrart and Mr. Edgar are real nice guys, mister. You should go talk to Mr. Evrart -- I'm sure you'll be good friends. He's friends with everyone around here. The little guy starts coughing.
Zh: “艾弗拉特先生和埃德加先生都是好人，先生。你应该跟艾弗拉特先生聊聊——我肯定你们能成为好朋友的。他是这里每一个人的朋友。”小个子开始咳嗽起来。

En: He's a Union man through and through. Good guy. He falls silent, hesitating. "He's very calm... laid back. Doesn't do much. Talks to Evrart sometimes."
Zh: “他是个完完全全的工会人。一个很好的人。”他陷入沉默，有些犹豫。“他很平静……闲散。做的事不多。偶尔会跟艾弗拉特聊聊。”

En: Ohh, he's really something... The little man starts laughing. "He doesn't talk much to me usually, but when he does... I don't really understand most of what he's saying..." He suddenly falls quiet. "Actually, I don't think he would like me running my mouth about him like that."
Zh: “哦哦哦，他真的很了不起……”小个子男人笑了起来。“他一般话不多，不过每次说的时候……大部分我真的听不太明白……”他突然陷入沉默。“其实，我觉得他不会喜欢我这样说他的。”

En: Tell me about Titus.
Zh: “跟我说说提图斯。”

En: Who do you mean, mister? He's rubbing his nape and looking at you with childlike innocence.
Zh: “你指的是谁，先生？”他摩挲着颈背，就像个天真的孩子一样看着你。

En: You do realize he might be just a figment of your imagination, right?
Zh: 你应该意识到他可能只是你凭空想象出来的吧？

En: Container, container, I'll turn you nice and red. Container, container, put the logos on...
Zh: “集装箱，集装箱，我会让你变得又红又漂亮。集装箱，集装箱，把标签都贴上……”

En: The lyrics to this container-song are being made up as he goes along.
Zh: 这首集装箱之歌的歌词是他一边干活一边编出来的。

En: The fancy guy in a suit -- in that container over there. (Point to it.)
Zh: “穿西装的那个时髦家伙——就在那边的集装箱里。”（指向它。）

En: I don't know anyone like this, mister -- maybe he's one of Mr. Evrart's fancy friends. He knows all kinds of fancy people, with suits and perdy carriages. Leo falls silent.
Zh: “我不认识这种人，先生——也许他是艾弗拉特先生的朋友吧。他认识各种各样的人，穿着西装，开着漂亮汽車。”利奥陷入沉默。

En: He really feels very uncomfortable discussing matters related to Evrart with you.
Zh: 跟你讨论艾弗拉特有关的事情真的让他觉得不太舒服。

En: Oh, Titus is a longshoreman through and through, he was born on a boat, they say. The little man rubs a patch on his elbow. "His veins are probably filled with saltwater I tell you, hehehe -- nice friendly sort old Titus is."
Zh: “哦，提图斯是个彻头彻尾的港口工人，大家说他是在一条船上出生的。”小个子男人摸了摸手肘上的药贴。“我告诉你，他的血管里大概流的是海水吧，哈哈哈——老提图斯是个很友好的人。”

En: He's probably one of those rare specimens who are born when two drunk seamen stumble on top of each other on the deck amidst a storm so violent it flings whales around.
Zh: 他很可能是一个稀有样本：暴风雨来临的时候，两个醉酒的海员互相绊倒在甲板上，动作猛烈到把周围的鲸鱼都惊动了，而他也诞生了。

En: Uh... I'd best not... I mean I could, but I don't think Mr. Evrart would like it very much, you better ask him yourself, mister. If anything the ever present smile on Leo's face gets even warmer.
Zh: “呃……最好还是不要了……我的意思是我可以，不过我觉得艾弗拉特先生不会喜欢的，你最好自己去问他，先生。”如果说有什么区别的话，那就是利奥脸上永不消失的笑容变得甚至更加温暖了。

En: The night guard? Oh, he's a peculiar fellow, Leo looks at the guard booth on the wall. "He's the strong silent type you could say."
Zh: “守夜人？哦，他是个很奇怪的家伙，”利奥看着墙上的保安亭。“你可以说他是那种沉默寡言的人。”

En: We talk all the time, but I don't really know much about him... He plays pétanque with my old human studies teacher, Mr. Martin down at the plaza. I think he's the only fellow who actually knows old René.
Zh: “我们一直在说话，但是我对他真的了解不多……他跟我以前的人类学老师——马丁先生，一起在广场下边玩地掷球。我觉得他是唯一真正了解老雷内的人。”

En: Actually I wanna talk about something else.
Zh: “其实我还想知道些别的。”

En: Uh... sure, mister, what can Leo do for you? The expression on his wrinkled face says *I really want to help you*.
Zh: “呃……当然可以，先生，利奥能为你做些什么？”他满是皱纹的脸上露出了*我真的很想帮你*的表情。

En: Oh hey, mister! I knew you'd be back to talk with old Leo here, yes I did, yes I did. It's like Lady Leriche said when she opened a bath house in the basement of my apartment building: *They can only get so far before they're aching to get back*. And lot of folks really did keep coming back...
Zh: “你好啊，先生！我知道你会回来找老利奥说话的，我知道的，我知道的。莱利彻夫人在我家公寓楼下的地下室开了一间澡堂，那时候她说：*在他们渴望回来之前，就只能走这么远*。而且确实有很多家伙真的回来了……”

En: Leo, Leo... in the future, can we keep this greeting shorter?
Zh: “利奥，利奥……以后我们见面的时候，打招呼的时间能不能短一些？”

En: Oh hey, Mr. Harry! The little fellow looks happy to see you. "It sure is nice of you to help out Mr. Evrart like you've been doing. He's so awfully busy most days -- he doesn't even eat the turnip porridge me missus sends him every day. She makes it with lots of butter and sliced sausage, she does... It's delicious."
Zh: “你好啊，哈里先生！”小个子很高兴看见你。“你能像以前一样帮艾弗拉特先生真是太好了。他大部分时间都非常忙——他甚至都没吃我太太每天送他的萝卜粥。她做的时候放了很多黄油和切片香肠，没错……真的很美味。”

En: Are you the Leo, who wrote the note to make more banners?
Zh: “你就是利奥吧，那个写纸条说要做更多横幅的人？”

En: Yeah, I'm pretty sure this place would totally fall apart if you didn't shine the bossman's shoes, Leo.
Zh: “是啊，我很肯定如果你没把老板的鞋擦得油光锃亮的话，这个地方肯定会彻底崩溃的，利奥。”

En: I think you're doing a great job around here, Leo.
Zh: “我觉得在这里你做得很好，利奥。”

En: Oh, mister... I do a lot more than that. I really do most everything around here. Mr. Evrart said many times I'm irreplaceable. He's still smiling, but his voice trembles ever so slightly.
Zh: “哦，先生……我做的可不止这些。我在这里几乎什么都做。艾弗拉特先生讲过很多次，说我是不可替代的。”他还在笑，不过他的声音有些轻微地颤抖。

En: He really takes great pride in being in Evrart's good graces.
Zh: 他真的为赢得艾弗拉特的青睐感到很骄傲。

En: You see disdain in the lieutenant's dark eyes when they meet yours for a moment. He does not approve of you ridiculing this little man.
Zh: 你跟警督的视线交汇的时候，你在他的黑眼睛里看到了一丝鄙夷的意味。他不赞同你奚落这个小个子。

En: Yes, this place really seems to run like clockwork. Keep it up, Leo. The lieutenant smiles at the little man.
Zh: “是的，这个地方一切进展地都很顺利。继续坚持，利奥。”警督朝小个子男人笑了笑。

En: Well thanks a lot, coming from you it means a lot, really. You didn't think it was possible, but the smile becomes even wider.
Zh: “谢谢你，能听到你这句话对我来说很有意义，真心的。”你觉得有些不可能，但他的微笑变得更灿烂了。

En: The white rectangle on your clothes might not mean an awful lot in Martinaise, but the recognition from an authority figure made Leo's day.
Zh: 你衣服上的白色矩形在马丁内斯可能没有多大的意义，但是获得权威人士的认可真的让利奥很开心。

En: Sure. Let me ask you about something else?
Zh: “当然。我能问你点别的吗？”

En: Sure, mister, about what?
Zh: “当然可以，先生，什么事？”

En: What is underneath these red covers?
Zh: “那些红色外漆下面是什么？”

En: Oh I don't know, mister. They say it's some *chee-mikals*. Most of them have labels on them I think.
Zh: “哦，我不知道，先生，大概是*化学制品*吧。我想大部分上面都有标签。”

En: Leo, has anyone told you *why* you need to change the covers?
Zh: “利奥，有没有人告诉过你*为什么*要改变外漆呢？”

En: Thanks, Leo. You've been very helpful! (Conclude.)
Zh: “谢谢你，利奥。你真的帮上大忙了！”（总结。）

En: Oh no trouble at all, mister, noooo trouble at all, the little guy affirms merrily.
Zh: “哦，一点都不麻烦，先生，一点都不麻烦，”小个子男人愉快地肯定着。

En: No, not really. He shrugs and continues merrily: "Mr. Evrart doesn't tell me all the big things. Says I go and tell them to everyone."
Zh: “不，没说过。”他耸耸肩，愉快地继续说道：“艾弗拉特先生不会告诉我大事情的。他说我会告诉所有人的。”

En: Hi!
Do not interrupt the little man in his joyous activity. [Leave.]
Zh: 不要打断这个小个子男人快乐的工作。[离开。]

En: What is it with you people and scabs?\n
Zh: “你们的人跟工贼是怎么回事？”

En: No, of course not!
Zh: “不，当然不是了！”

En: Actually I am, yes. I even said so to Mañana.
Zh: “其实我是的，没错。我甚至跟马列拉这么说了。”

En: Actually I am, yes.
Zh: “其实我是的，没错。”

En: What's going on here? (Look at the mountains of containers rising behind him.)
Zh: “这里出什么事了？”（看着他身后堆得山一样高的集装箱。）

En: Dresscode: Modus Mullen.
Zh: 着装要求：模式马伦。

En: Heard Evrart's plan.
Zh: 听闻艾弗拉特的计划。

En: The containers in the yard are green in Wild Pines livery and the mountain rising behind Leo is all red in Union colours.
Zh: 院子里的集装箱是绿色的，代表野松公司，利奥背后山一样高的集装箱是红色的，那是工会的颜色。

En: Everything is so pretty and red -- you and Leo look like brothers as you glance around with similar childlike wonder.
Zh: 一切都那么地美，那么地红——你和利奥像兄弟一样，带着相同的孩子般的好奇心四处张望。

En: Keep the amazement to yourself.
Zh: 把这种惊愕藏在心底。

En: Wow, red is so much prettier than drab old green.
Zh: “哇哦，红色看起来比那些单调的旧绿色要漂亮多了。”

En: This looks like a massive redecorating operation, Kim.
Zh: “这像是一个大规模的翻新工程啊，金。”

En: Yes. They're hiding it from the inside. All the red containers have the Débardeurs' Union logo on them.
Zh: “是的。他们在里面把它藏起来了。所有红色集装箱上都有装卸工会的标志。”

En: I hate to break this bonding moment, but the red containers mean they're replacing the company livery with the Union livery. Which means this strike isn't gonna stop any time soon.
Zh: “我很不想打断这样的时刻，不过红色集装箱意味着他们把公司的专用色彩换成了工会的。这就代表这次罢工不会很快结束。”

En: But what did they actually do?
Zh: “但是他们到底做了什么？”

En: Okay, Leo, let's hear about this fight you got into.
Zh: “好吧，利奥，跟我们说说你那次打架的事。”

En: Sometimes I feel some of the guys don't really get how much I bust my ass for them here, but you guys are all right.
Zh: “有时候我觉得有些人真的不理解我为他们到底付出了多少汗水，不过你们挺好的。”

En: For a fraction of a second there's sadness in his eyes.
Zh: 有那么一瞬间，他的眼睛里闪过一丝悲伤。

En: He goes on: "If me missus and me was to have a child I'd be real happy if she turned out like her... But she can't have kids."
Zh: 他继续说道：“如果我太太和我有个孩子，而且孩子跟她很像的话，我一定会很高兴的……但是她没有办法生育。”

En: Patience. Deep down, you have the mental power to keep listening. Not many would -- but you do.
Zh: 耐心一点。实际上，你拥有继续倾听的精神力量。不是很多人能做到——但你可以。

En: Actually, Miss Beaufort is the right-hand man, but she's a lay-dee, a goodhearted chuckle again.
Zh: “其实呢，博福特小姐是他的得力助手，不过她是个女士，”这个好心人又笑了。

En: A real pretty lady with a skin like those 'Doux & Sucre' candy bars my missus likes so much. Them are real nice to suckle on once the dinner is done and me and the missus sit down besides the radio.
Zh: “一个真的很漂亮的女士，皮肤像我太太特别喜欢的‘甜香槟&苏克雷’糖果一样。晚饭结束之后，我和太太坐在收音机旁来上一颗，感觉真是棒极了。”

En: We're on a *strike* -- the whole union is! You don't have to work when you're on strike. Ha -- we haven't worked for two months now.
Zh: “我们在*罢工*——整个工会都是！罢工的时候你就不用工作了。哈——现在我们已经有两个月没有工作了。”

En: Not everyone is down there of course, he chuckles, "Mr. Evrart is in his office, where he always is, and Jean-Luc is guarding the gate..." He pauses to think.
Zh: “当然了，不是所有人都在下面，”他笑着说道，“艾弗拉特先生在办公室，他总是在那里，让·卢克在看守大门……”他停下来想了一会儿。

En: Too rowdy? Leo, what kind of a fight did they get into? Did they kill a mercenary?
Zh: “太暴力？利奥，他们是怎么个打架法？他们杀了一个雇佣兵吗？”

En: Too late! Leo's mouth is still moving and words are spewing forth... words, words and look -- even more words -- this guy could go on till the end of days.
Zh: 太迟了！利奥的嘴巴还在蠕动，言语滔滔不绝……说话，说话，看啦——还在说话——这家伙可以一直说到世界末日。

En: It's very-very good. Makes a man feel so warm and happy. He shakes his head with a wide smile. "I feel like I could take on Mr. Renaudin's boar-dogs every time the lunch is done."
Zh: “它真的非常非常好。让人感到温暖和快乐。”他摇晃着脑袋，灿烂地笑着。“每次吃完午餐之后，我都感觉自己可以跟勒诺丹先生的猎犬较量一番。”

En: Power-borscht, huh? Never heard of a borscht that turns little guys into dogfighters. Alcohol, however...
Zh: 能提供能量的罗宋汤，是吗？我从没听说过罗宋汤能让小个子变成猛汉。不过酒精就……

En: What do you mean by 'taking this soup to the men'? Is it for striking?
Zh: “你说‘给人送汤’是什么意思？是为了罢工吗？”

En: Oh, absolutely, mister. He nods along. "What do you want to know?"
Zh: “哦，当然了，先生。”他点点头。“你想知道什么？”

En: Yes, yes, I'm taking it to them. The borscht keeps them happy and in fighting spirits. Makes you all warm inside. They brew it in the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “是的，是的，我给他们送东西。罗宋汤能让他们感到快乐，斗志昂扬。能让你身体里面热乎乎的。汤是在褴褛飞旋里做的。”

En: Sure is mister, sure is! Leo looks around merrily. "Really livens up the place. Yes it does, yes it does."
Zh: “当然了，先生，当然！”利奥愉快地看着四周。“真的让这个地方活跃起来了。没错，就是这样，是这样没错。”

En: Law school? Could he be talking about the Union fixer, AKA the gardener.
Zh: 法律学校？这个他说的会不会是工会的调停人，又称园丁。

En: Just, some of the other guys don't look too kindly on the scabbing-kind, if you know what I mean, mister.
Zh: “只是，其他一些人看起来对工贼之类的人不太友好，如果你明白我的意思，先生。”

En: Sure, mister, absolutely -- I'm always willing to help out nice fellows such as yourself. He smiles his disarming smile and looks you deep in the eye.
Zh: “当然了，先生，绝对的——我一直很想帮助像你这样的好人。”他看着你的眼睛，露出了愉快的笑容。

En: I had some questions for you, if that's not too much trouble?
Zh: “我有些问题要问你，不会给你添太多麻烦吧？”

En: Oh hey, mister! I'm not gonna bother you with a long greeting just like we talked about before. I know you're probably a busy-busy man, being an important police officer and all, and personally I think the more people keeping the peace the better.
Zh: “你好啊，先生！像之前说的，我不会再用那些长长的问候语来打扰你了。我知道你可能是个很忙的人，是个很重要的警官等等之类的，而且我个人觉得维持和平的人越多越好。”

En: What's in that container over there? (Point to the container suspended from the crane arm.)
Zh: “那个集装箱里面有什么？”（指着悬挂在起重机臂上的集装箱。）

En: No trouble at all, mister. No trouble at all. It's like that old saying goes -- *wisdom withers if not shared* -- and old Leo is always up for sharing.
Zh: “一点也不麻烦，先生。一点也不麻烦。就好像那句老话说的——*智慧若不分享就会枯萎*——而且老利奥一直都很乐意分享。”

En: Mr. Harry! Boy, old Leo sure was right about you! I knew you was a Union man at heart when I first saw you. He takes a quick breath. "Right as I turned around and looked at you, I said to myself: 'Now that fellow is a union man right there. Through and through.' And I was right, yes I was, yes I was."
Zh: “哈里先生！天啦，老利奥没有看错你！当我第一眼看到你的时候，就知道你打心底就是我们工会的人。”他飞快地吸了一口气。“就在我转身看到你的时候，我就对自己说：‘那家伙一看就是工会的人。彻头彻尾。’而且我是对的，没错就是这样，是这样没错。”

En: I guess you just *know* people, Leo. It's like a special ability you got.
Zh: “我猜你真的很*懂*别人，利奥。这就像是你的特殊技能。”

En: Leo, I only tolerate the Union to as long as it furthers my own goals. Nothing more.
Zh: “利奥，我能容忍工会只不过是因为它能推动我向目标前进。仅此而已。”

En: Sure, sure, Leo... go easy now and answer some questions please.
Zh: “当然，当然，利奥……现在放松一点，请回答我的问题。”

En: It's true, it's true. he nods. "I've had an eye for helpful people for as long as I remember. I like helpful people, and I like helping helpful people, so what do you need, mister?"
Zh: “是真的，是真的。”他点点头。“从我记事开始，我就很会分辨乐于助人的人。我喜欢乐于助人的人，而且我喜欢帮助乐于助人的人，所以你有什么需要吗，先生？”

En: Oh no, mister, you've already done so much for the Union. The Union is in your debt, for sure. He nods with a happy smile. "So how can I help you, mister?"
Zh: “哦不，先生，你已经为工会做的够多了。工会欠了你的，这一点很肯定。”他点点头，脸上挂着开心的笑容。“所以我能为你做什么，先生？”

En: Sure thing, mister. I'm always ready to help out a helpful person such as yourself. Folks have got to help each other out, right?
Zh: “当然了，先生。我总是随时准备帮助你这种乐于助人的人。大家都应该互相帮助，对吧？”

En: There appear to be cisterns underneath the Union container covers.
Zh: 工会集装箱外漆下面好像有水箱。

En: Really? Did they kill someone?
Zh: “真的吗？他们杀人了吗？”

En: I have another question.
Zh: “我还有别的问题。”

En: Keep it together, no need to show your wonder.
Zh: 镇定点，没必要表现出一副惊讶的样子。

En: It's like some red infection was spreading outwards from the container yard's core.
Zh: 看起来就像某种红色的传染病从集装箱堆场的中心向外面扩散。

En: This looks like a massive redecorating operation.
Zh: 这看起来像是个大规模的翻新工程。

En: Yes, moving from inwards to outwards. By the looks of it soon everything will be in Union colours.
Zh: 是的，从里向外移动。这么看来，很快一切都会变成工会的颜色。

En: So *no one* is working?
Zh: “所以*没有人*在工作了？”

En: So *no one's* working?
Zh: “所以*没有人*在工作了？”

En: Don't go all *bad cop* on this simple friendly fellow.
Zh: 不要对这个单纯友好的人玩*坏警察*那一套。

En: Sure, sure. The small man nods along. "How can I help you?"
Zh: “当然，当然。”小个子男人点点头。“有什么能帮你的？”

En: No, I don't think they killed anyone. The little guy shies away from you. "Let's better talk about something else. Titus and his boys do good work, I don't wanna get them in trouble over a little drinking..."
Zh: “不，我觉得他们没有杀人。”小个子男人躲开了你。“我们最好聊些别的吧。提图斯和他的兄弟们工作做得很好，我不想让他们因为喝了一点小酒就惹上麻烦……”

En: Easy, Leo, let's keep this on the Hardies. He looks to you for assistance.
Zh: “放松点，利奥，我们继续聊聊哈迪兄弟。”他看向你寻求帮助。

En: You're never getting anything useful out of him. It's good enough you found out Evrart has a brother called Edgar and he helped Leo.
Zh: 你永远不能从他这里问出有用信息的。能问出艾弗拉特有个叫埃德加的兄弟，而且他帮过利奥就已经不错了。

En: Who is this Miss Beaufort? The lieutenant looks up at Leo.
Zh: “博福特小姐是谁？”警督抬头看着利奥。

En: Who's Miss Beaufort?
Zh: “博福特小姐是谁？”

En: Stay on this *Miss Beaufort* topic.
Zh: 继续聊*博福特小姐*的话题。

En: So Evrart trained a lawyer named Miss Beaufort. Interesting.
Zh: 所以艾弗拉特训练了一个名叫博福特小姐的律师，有点意思。

En: Okay so this is the Union fixer, the gardener?
Zh: “好吧，所以这个工会调停人，是园丁吗？”

En: Another question, Leo.
Zh: “还有个问题，利奥。”

En: Yes sure, I'm glad to answer. His benevolent eyes are looking into yours.
Zh: “当然可以，我很乐意回答。”他亲切的眼睛直直地看着你。

En: I'm not sure what a fixer is, but she is a real nice girl. Smart as a whip too. The little guy smiles a disarming smile at you.
Zh: “我不确定调停人是什么，不过她真的是个好女孩，而且聪明绝顶。”小个子男人朝你露出让人毫无戒心的笑容。

En: Looks like it.
Zh: “看起来就是了。”

En: Thank you... Heh! Almost forgot! Mr. Evrart is in that container over there. Leo points to his left. "I got distracted telling the story, but he's in there."
Zh: “谢谢你……啊！差点忘了！艾弗拉特先生就在那边的集装箱里。”利奥指向左边。“讲故事让我分心了，不过他就在那里。”

En: They lived on the same street their entire lives -- even dated the same girl on and off for as long I can remember." He chuckles again. "Strange fellows, but mister Martin was always real nice to me in school... I remember once..."
Zh: “他们这一辈子都住在同一条街上——在我断断续续的记忆中，甚至还和同一个女孩约会过。”他又轻笑起来。“奇怪的家伙，不过马丁先生在学校的时候对我一直很好……我记得有一次……”

En: Hold up, Gaston was your human studies teacher?
Zh: “等一下，加斯顿是你的人类学老师？”

En: I have another person in mind.
Zh: “我还有个其他问题。”

En: Suuuuure, he stretches the word. "Who did you have in mind?"
Zh: “当……然，”他拖长了声音。“你在想什么呢？”

En: Mr. Martin, yes. Leo nods. "Don't really remember much about him. I was just a boy back then."
Zh: “马丁先生，是的。”利奥点点头。“他的事我记得的不多。那时候我只是个小男孩。”

En: Left with the first autumn rains and didn't come back before the trees where green again. The little guy chuckles again.
Zh: “随着第一场秋雨离开了，树叶再次变绿之前也没有回来。”小个子男人又笑了起来。

En: Oh that one... He looks at the container. "That should be empty as far as I know. Lots of containers here have nothing in them. They're just waiting to be loaded up."
Zh: “哦，那个啊……”他看着集装箱。“据我所知，那个应该是空的。很多集装箱里什么也没有。它们还在等着装货呢。”

En: Told you.
Zh: “我说过的吧。”

En: Oh yes, yes, he replies excitedly. "I leave all kinds of notes for myself. That old head of mine ain't so good at keeping things in no more. I almost forgot about the borscht."
Zh: “啊，是的，是的，”他激动地回复着。“我会给自己留各种各样的纸条。我这个脑袋生锈了，不太擅长记东西咯。我差点连罗宋汤都忘了。”

En: What was that about the borscht?
Zh: “罗宋汤是怎么回事？”

En: Cool. Another question then.
Zh: “很好。那还有个问题。”

En: Of course, mister. I'm more than happy to be helpful.
Zh: “当然可以，先生。我非常乐意帮忙。”

En: Oh yes, I've been taking special Whirling borscht to the men every day since the strike started. The little guy chuckles merrily.
Zh: “哦，是的，自从罢工开始之后，我每天都会从飞旋旅社取来特制的罗宋汤送给大家。”小个子男人开心地笑了。

En: Hold up, who makes it at the Whirling?
Zh: “等一下，那飞旋旅社是谁在做呢？”

En: Oh, the Whirling's cook. He makes it. Them is always talking with Mr. Mañana in that weird language and laughing together. He doesn't speak what we speak, he's from Graad.
Zh: “哦，是飞旋旅社的厨师，他做的。他们总是用很奇怪的语言跟马列拉先生说话，然后还笑个不停。他说的话跟我们不一样，他是从格拉德来的。”

En: Looks like the borscht is spiked, I'm gonna look into it. (Accept task)
Zh: “看来这个罗宋汤有点问题，我要调查一下。”（接受任务）

En: Something is off about this borscht... I'm going to look into it. (Accept task.)
Zh: “总觉得这个罗宋汤有点问题……我要调查一下。”（接受任务。）

En: That's it. I just wanted to know who wrote the note. (Refuse task.)
Zh: “我只想知道纸条是谁写的。”（拒绝任务。）

En: Oh, I did, mister! He chuckles again. "Yes I did, yes I did."
Zh: “哦，是我啊，先生！”他又笑了。“没错是我，是我没错。”

En: Oh sure, mister... sure. The little guy nods. "You do that, yes sir."
Zh: “哦，当然了，先生……当然。”小个子男人点点头。“你去吧，先生。”

En: He didn't actually understand what you meant and now he's just nodding along.
Zh: 他其实并没有理解你的意思，只是跟着点头而已。

En: Look at him, it's not gonna be anything useful anyway. Don't fight it. Better to go with the flow.
Zh: 看看他，反正也不会有什么用。别挣扎了。最好还是顺其自然吧。

En: The old man whistles and hums a jaunty tune to himself.
Zh: 老男人吹着口哨，自己哼唱着愉快的小调。

En: 'Found god knows where'? The big man exhales loudly. "That *Acele* is the daughter of Mico the Kebab -- a man who once killed a guy with a kebab."
Zh: “‘天知道哪里’？”大块头男人大声地呼出一口气。“那个*阿西尔*是烤肉店老板米科的女儿——那个男人曾经用一串烤肉杀过人。”

En: Mr. Du Bois! I hope time is on your side this time... Please, take a seat! He smiles broadly, apparently unconcerned about the breakdown that interrupted your last conversation.
Zh: “杜博阿先生！希望这次时间能站在你那边……快请坐下吧！”他笑的很灿烂，显然并不在意上次硬生生中断的对话。

En: I'm told the Union is involved in the local drug trade.
Zh: “有人告诉我工会参与了本地的毒品交易。”

En: What?! He smacks his forehead, completely flabbergasted. "Harry... how could you say that to me? You know I appreciate a joke as much as any jolly fat guy, but I can't take *slander*. Are you actually investigating this?"
Zh: “什么？！”他啪的一下打在脑门上，彻底惊呆了。“哈里……你怎么能这么跟我说话呢？你知道我跟所有乐呵呵的大胖子一样很喜欢笑话，但是*诽谤*就不能接受了。你真的在调查这件事嘛？”

En: No comment.
Zh: “无可奉告。”

En: I am.
Zh: “是的。”

En: I am. (Lie.)
Zh: “是的。”（撒谎。）

En: No, I'm not. (Lie.)
Zh: “不，我没有。”（撒谎。）

En: No, I'm not.
Zh: “不，我没有。”

En: You've hurt me, Harry -- me! A friend! The man rubs his temple and closes his eyes, in pain. "But you know what?" He perks up.
Zh: 男人揉了揉太阳穴，痛苦地闭起双眼。“你伤到我了，哈里——我！你的朋友！但是你知道吗？”他振作精神。

En: I trust you, like I trust all my friends. And I know you'll never talk to me about this again, because you don't want to *wound* me. So do what you want -- and let's change the subject.
Zh: “我相信你，就像相信所有朋友一样。而且我知道你永远不会再跟我说这种话了，因为你不想*伤害*我。所以你有什么需要吗——还有，我们换个话题吧。”

En: Harry... He sighs. "You wound me, Harry. In the heart. But I trust you to put this to bed. Do what you must and let's change the subject, shall we?"
Zh: “哈里……”他叹了口气。“你伤到我了，哈里。伤了我的心。但是我相信你不会再提了。做你该做的事吧，还有，我们换个话题，好吗？”

En: I've just finished investigating the local drug trade.
Zh: “我刚刚完成了本地毒品贸易的调查。”

En: You've got a lotta spirit, clearing up the drug problem alongside this murder. He adjusts his glasses. "I'll talk to the mayor and see if I can get you the key to the city, Harry. Now let's talk real business."
Zh: 他调整了一下眼镜。“你精神可嘉，不仅要处理毒品问题，还要破获凶杀案。我会跟市长谈谈，看能不能给你颁发一把城市钥匙，哈里。现在我们还是聊聊正经事吧。”

En: Amblyopia. A childhood condition.
Zh: 弱视。童年时期留下来的问题。

En: Yet he's unperturbed. Holding his own.
Zh: 然而他还是面不改色，镇定自若。

En: With a mixture of expectation and impatience, well bottled.
Zh: 他带着一种混杂着期待和不耐烦的情绪，他控制得很好。

En: Grotesquely magnified by his +6 glasses.
Zh: 被他那副+6的眼镜奇异地放大了。

En: He looks extremely comfortable. The tiny folding chair, on the other hand, looks like a torture device.
Zh: 看起来他坐得很舒服。而另一方面呢，那张小折叠椅看起来就像是一件刑具。

En: The folding chair looks like a torture device. Extremely uncomfortable.
Zh: 那张小折叠椅看起来就像是一件刑具，非常地不舒服。

En: Let's get straight to business. There's been a murder.
Zh: “我们直接谈正事吧。这里发生了一起凶杀案。”

En: Let's get straight to business. There's a dead body in a tree.
Zh: “我们直接谈正事吧。树上挂着一具尸体。”

En: Damn... that's a nasty play. He's got you -- a titan of Volition -- in a fork. Guess you just have to sit or leave.
Zh: 该死……真是一场恶心的游戏。他让你——一个意志坚强的巨人——进退两难。我猜你要么只能坐下，要么就离开。

En: You did it! This might help against whatever comes next...
Zh: 你做到了！这样也许对接下来的事能有帮助……

En: Yes, but you'll *owe* him for it.
Zh: 是的，但是你会*欠*下他的人情。

En: Wow, that's 25 reál! That's good money. You *need* it.
Zh: 哇哦，那可是25雷亚尔！这么多钱。你很*需要*它。

En: Hey, that's 25 reál. That's good money. Think of all the *stuff* you could...
Zh: 喂，那可是25雷亚尔！这么多钱。想想你可以用它弄到的多少*东西*……

En: ...*owe* him for? This is a trap.
Zh: ……*欠*他人情？这是个陷阱。

En: No.
Zh: 不。

En: You've just got to grin and bear it. Like a champ. A champ with a rod up his ass!
Zh: 你只能默默忍受了。像个冠军一样。一个屁股上插了跟棍子的冠军！

En: Fuck yeah! Remember when you tried *The Expression* on that little minx in the silver jumpsuit? That was *you*.
Zh: 是啊！还记得你在那个穿银色连体裤的小妞身上试验过的那个*表情*吗？那个才是*你*。

En: Wait, that's it? That's my point of pride here?
Zh: 等等，就这样？你以为这就能打击到我的自尊吗？

En: Give him *The Expression* -- *Click-click*.
Zh: 快对他做那个*表情*——*咔哒**咔哒*

En: Listen, Evrart -- pal, we both know what makes the wheels of the world turn.
Zh: “听着，艾弗拉特——老兄，我们都知道是什么让世界的車轮转动的。”

En: Try it on Evrart!
Zh: 对艾弗拉特试试！

En: Give him *The Expression* -- and the finger pistols!
Zh: 快对他做那个*表情*——还有手指手枪！

En: Relax, loosen up -- get *comfortable*.
Zh: 镇定，放松下来——让自己*舒服*起来。

En: Sink deep into the folding chair. Smile and cross your hands behind your back.
Zh: 让自己深陷入折叠椅。笑着把手交叉放在身后。

En: Fuck yes! You're part of the old guard, the *ancient* guard -- the most corrupt, unholy cops in the land. Chill out like the *black dragon* you are.
Zh: 当然是了！你是守旧者的一部分，*古老*的守卫者——这片土地上最腐败、最罪恶的警察。像*黑龙*一样冷静下来吧。

En: Summon a smile on your face. A big shit eating grin like his.
Zh: 让你自己面露微笑。跟他那张大臭嘴露出的龇牙咧嘴的笑容一样。

En: *Snap, snap!* The fat man shoots those finger pistols right back at you. He seems to be enjoying the display.
Zh: *噼啪，噼啪*！胖男人用手指手枪回敬了你几下。他好像很享受这种行为。

En: The fat man seems to be enjoying your rigor mortis grin -- he shoots one just like it right back at you.
Zh: 胖男人好像很享受你那张有如死后僵直般龇牙咧嘴的笑容——他也有模有样地回敬了你一个。

En: The fat man does the same -- sinking deeper into his chair than one would think is physically possible. He seems to be enjoying your little display.
Zh: 胖男子也做了同样的动作——深陷入他的椅子里，直到大家以为肉体可能达到的极限。他好像很享受你小小的炫耀行为。

En: You cross your legs and yawn. The fat man does the same -- sinking deeper into his chair than one would think is physically possible. He seems to be enjoying your little display.
Zh: 你双腿交叉，打了个哈欠。胖男人也做了同样的动作——深陷入他的椅子里，直到大家以为肉体可能达到的极限。他好像很享受你小小的炫耀行为。

En: Mhm. *This* fighter only sits on a throne.
Zh: “嗯。*这个*战士只会坐在王座上。”

En: That's it -- now kick back and add a final flourish. For *dignity*.
Zh: 就是这样——现在给他一个回踢，然后再加一个花哨的完结动作。为了你的*尊严*。

En: Oh fuck, it was loaded... There were two bullets in it. You always keep at least two barrels loaded.
Zh: 卧槽，它上膛了……里面有两颗子弹。你总是会保留至少两发子弹。

En: Is he?
Zh: 他在开玩笑？

En: Good. Now lean in with some *corruption*.
Zh: 很好。现在向前倾身，带着一点*腐败*的感觉。

En: The reaction appears to be sincere, but... it's impossible to tell with this guy, honestly.
Zh: 反应似乎很真诚，但是……说真的，这家伙还真是不好说。

En: And gets over it in two seconds? Seems like it didn't really hurt him.
Zh: 而且两秒钟就恢复过来了？看来并没有真的伤到他。

En: He's hiding his real reaction beneath courtesy.
Zh: 他把真正的反应都隐藏在礼貌之下了。

En: Thank you for understanding, the lieutenant looks him in the eye. "We will continue to do what we must."
Zh: “感谢你的理解，”警督看着他的眼睛。“我们会继续做我们该做的事。”

En: You too, lieutenant -- heh! He chuckles, suddenly. "You know, I like you, but you never were my favourite. I'm a Harry-guy. I'm Team Harry."
Zh: “你也是，警督——哈！”他突然笑出了声。“你知道吗，我喜欢你，但你永远不会是我最喜欢的那个。我喜欢哈里。我站哈里。”

En: None taken, the lieutenant quickly replies and then turns to you: "Did we have anything else to do here, Harry?"
Zh: “没关系，”警督飞快地回复到，然后转向你：“我们在这里还有什么事吗，哈里？”

En: He refuses to discuss it further. It's probably just a small nuisance to him.
Zh: 他拒绝进一步讨论。对于他来说，这可能是一件小麻烦事。

En: Not even a speck of anger in his voice. That's that then.
Zh: 他的声音里甚至连一丝愤怒的痕迹都没有。那就这样吧。

En: Actually, Revachol doesn't have a mayor...
Zh: 其实吧，瑞瓦肖没有市长……

En: Ah yes, your side-investigation! Thank you. He adjusts his glasses. "You've got some spirit, clearing up phony drug accusations alongside this murder. I'll talk to the mayor and see if I can get you the key to the city, Harry. Now let's talk real business."
Zh: “啊，是的，你的侧面调查！谢谢你。”他调整了一下眼镜。“你真是精神可嘉，不仅要处理虚假的毒品指控，还要破获凶杀案。我会跟市长谈谈，看能不能给你颁发一把城市钥匙，哈里。现在我们还是聊聊正经事吧。”

En: In there, under pillows of winter fat, his stress-ridden heart still beats furiously.
Zh: 在那里，在冬季脂肪的枕头下，他那颗倍感压力的心脏还在猛烈地跳动着。

En: Hmm... it *was* interesting. No arguing against that.
Zh: 嗯……这*的确*很有意思。这是无可争议的。

En: It saddens him -- that she can. Sours it, but only a tiny bit.
Zh: 她可以这一点——让他有些难过。让它再酸些，不过只要一点点。

En: If that's what it takes, I'll do it -- for the working class.
Zh: “如果真有这个必要，我会做的——为了工人阶级。”

En: And you've already lost for so long.
Zh: 而且你已经失败这么久了。

En: Hmm...
And safer, too. You never know who you can trust.
Zh: 而且也更安全。你永远不知道能相信谁。

En: Okay.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: So what now?
Zh: “现在怎么办？”

En: So what the fuck now? (Spread your arms.)
Zh: “他妈的现在怎么办？”（摊开双臂。）

En: Now there's a whole lot of work to do! For me *and* for you. I suppose there was a reason you came here, so let's get to it. What can I help my best friend and comrade with today?
Zh: “现在有很多工作要做啊！对于你*和*我来说都一样。我猜你来这里是有原因的，所以就直说吧。我最好的朋友和同志，今天我能帮你什么呢？”

En: Oooooookay! He swings his fist through the air and shakes his head in agreeance. "Hell yes, it is."
Zh: “好吧！”他在空中挥舞着拳头，赞同地点点头。“当然是了，没错。”

En: Now we let bygones be bygones because there's work to do. For me *and* for you. I suppose there was a reason you came here, so let's have it! What can I help my best friend and comrade with today?
Zh: “现在我们就既往不咎吧，因为还有工作要做。对于你*和*我来说都一样。我猜你来这里是有原因的，所以就直说吧。我最好的朋友和同志，今天我能帮你什么呢？”

En: (Lie.) "I opened the door to your weasel's den."
Zh: （撒谎。）“我打开黄鼠狼巢穴的大门了。”

En: It's done. I mailed the signatures you asked me to mail.
Zh: “完事了。你让我寄的签名，我已经寄出去了。”

En: I mailed the signatures you asked me to get. (Lie.)
Zh: “你让我寄的签名，我已经寄出去了。”（撒谎。）

En: I may have changed my mind about getting those signatures.
Zh: “弄那些签名的事，我可能改变主意了。”

En: No, you didn't. He waves you off with a chuckle. "I know the mail-man, Harry. I know everyone and everything that happens in this town and I *know* there's no letter in that mailbox yet."
Zh: “不，你没有。”他笑着朝你摆摆手。“我认识那个邮递员，哈里。我知道这里的每一个人，这里发生的每一件事，而且我还*知道*邮箱里现在一封信也没有。”

En: It's not gonna be as easy this time.
Zh: 这次就不会那么简单了。

En: Just like I *know* you'll get it done. Once you stop horsing around. He nods reassuringly. "Let me know when it's done."
Zh: “就像我*知道*你会告诉我已经完事了一样。就在你胡闹完之后。”他鼓励地点点头。“事情办完之后记得告诉我。”

En: I'm ready for my gun now.
Zh: “我已经准备好拿枪了。”

En: Remember the container I asked you about? Turns out there's a mega rich light-bending guy inside.
Zh: “还记得我问你的那个集装箱吗？原来里面有一个超级有钱，还能扭曲光线的家伙。”

En: Do you know there's a mega rich light-bending guy inside one of the containers outside?
Zh: “你知道有外边的某个集装箱里一个超级有钱，还能扭曲光线的家伙吗？”

En: Yes, it sort of did look like that. A bacteria. Growing smaller and smaller.
Zh: “是的，看起来确实有点像。一个细菌。变得越来越小。”

En: I don't understand. What do you mean?
Zh: “我不明白。你什么意思？”

En: Don't worry, Harry. He does. He nods towards the lieutenant. "At least I think he does."
Zh: “别担心，哈里。他明白。”他朝着警督点点头。“至少我觉得他明白。”

En: You *wanted* me to deliver that message to her...
Zh: “你*想*让我给她带个信……”

En: That this is a takeover, that I want a war? He nods, forcefully. "God, I hope you also told her about the drug trade... They absolutely hate getting their hands dirty with that."
Zh: “说这是一次接管行动，我想要的是一场战争？”他用力点点头。“天啦，我真希望你把贩毒的事跟她说了……他们肯定很讨厌被那种东西弄脏自己的手。”

En: You can kill a million people, but if you do something the *po-lice* doesn't like... well, then you're out of the yacht-club. A vein on his forehead is pulsating. "Goodbye, you inhuman fuck..."
Zh: “你可以杀掉一百万人，不过如果你做了什么*警察*不喜欢的事……那么，你就要被踢出游艇俱乐部了。”他前额上的一条血管在颤动。“再见了，你这个惨无人道的贱种……”

En: There's a mean little light in his eyes.
Zh: 他的眼睛里闪烁着一丝凶狠的光芒。

En: His tone is totally different now. The jolly man of the people is gone and so is the smile.
Zh: 现在他的口吻完全不一样了。大家口中快活的男人不见了，笑容也消失了。

En: His jaw is clenched and his quadruple-chin is quivering with hatred. This is *decades* of hatred speaking right now.
Zh: 他的下颌缩紧，四层下巴因为憎恶而颤抖着。现在是*数十年*的怨恨在说话。

En: You get a sinking feeling...
Zh: 你有一种不祥的预感……

En: Oh my god, you've been played!
Zh: 天啦，你被耍了！

En: You wanted us to relay all this information to her...
Zh: “你想让我们把所有信息都传达给她……”

En: It's okay, Harry. I'm just thankful you delivered my message.
Zh: “没事的，哈里。很感激你能为我送信。”

En: What message?
Zh: “什么信息？”

En: You're right, Harry. There's is no need for that. He laughs, but his laughter is void of joy. "I guess what matters is that my message got across."
Zh: “你说得对，哈里。没必要这样。”他笑了，不过笑声里却没有欢乐的气息。“我猜重要的是我的消息已经传出去了。”

En: I bet it did, I bet it did. He laughs, but his laughter is void of joy. "I guess what matters is that my message got across."
Zh: “我敢打赌，我绝对敢打赌。”他笑了，不过笑声里却没有欢乐的气息。“我猜重要的是我的消息已经传出去了。”

En: You wanted us to relay all this information to her... the lieutenant cuts in.
Zh: “你想让我们把所有信息都传达给她……”警督插了进来。

En: What message?
Zh: “什么信息？”

En: What message?
Zh: “什么信息？”

En: So this was your plan all along. For me to relay this information to her.
Zh: “所以这就是你一直以来的计划。为了让我把这个信息传给她。”

En: How do you know she left?
Zh: “你怎么知道她离开了？”

En: You used me. (Proceed.)
Zh: “你利用了我。”（总结。）

En: Harry... He exhales slowly. "I can't see into the future. We are all playing *by ear* on this planet. I had no idea she'd react so strongly."
Zh: “哈里……”他慢慢呼出一口气。“我无法看到未来。在这个星球上，我们都在*见机*行事。我不知道她会有这么激烈的反应。”

En: But you did want me to relay info to her?
Zh: “但是你确实是想让我把信息传达给她？”

En: I did. I knew the negotiations would go better with police officers telling her horror stories from inside the harbour. It's scarier this way. Turns out it was a magnificent strategy. I never thought it'd so fundamentally fuck her up.
Zh: “是的。我以为如果她能从一个警察口里听到港口里面的恐怖故事，那谈判就会变得轻松很多。这样会吓人很多。结果这确实是一个伟大的战略。我从没想过会让她变成那副样子。”

En: Harry, I bugged her cabin. I bugged her whole boat. I had cameras surveying her boat. Hell I even wanted to bug that thermal cup, but my boys advised against it.
Zh: “哈里，我监听了她的船舱。我监听了她的整艘船。我用摄像机监视她的船。我甚至想监听那个保温杯，不过被我的手下劝下来了。”

En: They must have done it while Joyce was busy questioning the locals.
Zh: 他们肯定是在乔伊斯忙着询问本地人的时候做的。

En: So you've been listening to our conversations all the time?
Zh: “所以一直以为来你都在监听我们的对话？”

En: Not me personally... he stretches his arms like a discus thrower. "I had guys recording and processing this information for me."
Zh: “不是我个人，”他像个铁饼运动员一样展开双臂。“我手下有人专门负责录音和分析这些信息。”

En: Where are these boys?
Zh: “这些人在哪？”

En: The Hardie boys?
Zh: “哈迪兄弟？”

En: Hell no! he exclaims. "They'd fuck it up. They can't do anything right. I mean my *real* boys. My special task force boys."
Zh: “当然不是了！”他大喊一声。“他们会搞砸的。他们什么事都做不好。我是说*真正*的手下。我的特别行动小组。”

En: Where are these boys now?
Zh: “现在这些人在哪？”

En: They sure as hell aren't hanging out in the open with beers in their hands for the cops to question. He bursts out laughing. "They're pros, Harry."
Zh: “他们当然不会握着啤酒瓶在外面瞎晃，等着警察去盘问他们了。”他突然大笑出来。“他们是专业的，哈里。”

En: They sure as hell aren't hanging out in the open with beers in their hands for the cops to question. He bursts out laughing. "They're pros, Mr. Kitsuragi."
Zh: “他们当然不会握着啤酒瓶在外面瞎晃，等着警察去盘问他们了。”他突然大笑出来。“他们是专业的，曷城先生。”

En: Harry, you made a conscious decision to relay that information to her. You could have kept it for yourself.
Zh: “哈里，把信息传递给她是你自己的决定。你本来可以自己保密的。”

En: Now I know why you've been so forthcoming.
Zh: “现在我知道你为什么这么热心了。”

En: No, no, Harry, what we have is real. We're working men. This here... He taps his chest, then points to you. "... is *real*."
Zh: “不不不，哈里，我们之间是真的。我们都是工人。这里……”他拍拍胸口，然后指向你。“……是*真的*。”

En: You used my intellectual curiosity against me.
Zh: “你利用我的求知欲来对付我。”

En: But she told me a beautiful story about the discovery of the Insulinde.
Zh: “但是她给我讲了一个发现伊苏林迪的美丽故事。”

En: But she said she's insane. Like me.
Zh: “但是她说她疯了。就像我一样。”

En: So what's going to happen now? (Conclude.)
Zh: “所以现在会怎么样？”（总结。）

En: Against you? He's taken aback. "Oh, Harry, I'd like to think I used it *for* you."
Zh: “对付你？”他有些吃惊。“哦，哈里，我更愿意觉得我这么做是*为了*你。”

En: Of course she did. Rich people have the best stories. About all the interesting things they've done and seen, all the beautiful places they've been to. It's just sentimentalism. She can afford to be sentimental -- and she can afford to lose as well.
Zh: “她当然会了。有钱人最会讲故事了。都是他们干过和见到的那些趣事，还有他们见过的那些美丽的地方。这只不过是在多愁善感。她能经得起多愁善感——而且也能经得起失败。”

En: She's not insane. What did she tell you? His expression betrays extreme scepticism.
Zh: “她没有疯。她跟你说什么了？”他的表情流露出极度的猜疑。

En: She told me she's over-radiated from pale transit.
Zh: “她告诉我说她穿过灰域的时候被过度辐射了。”

En: She has this sentimentality and longing about her...
Zh: “她很多愁善感，而且还有一种渴望……”

En: Heh, he scoffs. "She's a sentimental alcoholic. They all are, Harry. Never take a drop and you'll be eight laps ahead of the upper-class winos, just like old Mr. Claire here. Try it. You'll be a real superdetective."
Zh: “哈，”他嗤笑到。“她是个感情用事的酒鬼。他们全都是，哈里。只要滴酒不沾，你就能比上流社会的那些酒鬼领先八圈，就像我老克莱尔先生一样。试试看吧。你会成为一个真正的超级警探。”

En: Oh, fuck off... I don't drink more than everyone else!
Zh: “哦，见鬼去吧……我喝的不比其他人多！”

En: That's actually... good advice. I might.
Zh: “其实吧……这的确是个好建议。我可能会的。”

En: Don't just say it, Harry. He wags his finger at you. "Do it. We gotta be more disciplined than they are, or we'll *lose*."
Zh: “不要只是说，哈里。”他朝你挥舞着手指。“去做。我们比他们更自律，不然我们就会*失败*。”

En: What was always going to happen. We take the harbour and she fucks off to Ozonne, uncorks a bottle of wine, calls her partners and says they need to distance themselves from this nasty business before the big shit spinner splashes everyone.
Zh: “这种事总会发生的。我们接管港口，她滚回欧佐纳尔，开一瓶酒，打给她的合伙人，说在那个大混球把一切都搅得乱七八糟之前，他们需要赶紧保持距离。”

En: Only difference is the Union doesn't have to lose 2,000 men to machine gun fire.
Zh: “唯一的差别就是，工会没必要在机关枪的火力下损失2000条生命。”

En: I've actually walked the path of sobriety for a while now.
Zh: “其实我已经在清醒的道路上走了好一阵子了。”

En: You could have just told me, I would have been on board.
Zh: “你可以直接告诉我的，我本来可以答应的。”

En: Damn, Evrart, I was hoping I'd somehow get to fuck you over in the end. Not the other way around.
Zh: “该死，艾弗拉特，我还希望最后想办法利用你的。而不是反过来。”

En: But I swore fealty to her. She was my beautiful laede.
Zh: “但是我发誓要对她敬忠了。她是我美丽的女王。”

En: I have a bad taste in my mouth. You better make sure this ends up saving lives.
Zh: “我感觉很不愉快。你最好保证最后的结果能挽救生命。”

En: Look, I just want to do police work.
Zh: “你看，我只想做好警察分内的工作。”

En: Yes, of course, Harry, he says, smiling like a fox. "That's why you went to her and relayed every little thing I told you."
Zh: “当然会了，哈里，”他说着，笑的像只狐狸。“所以你要过去找她，把我告诉你的每一件事都讲给她听。”

En: I can see that, Harry. He studies your face. "Alcohol has left its marks, but you're doing better than when I first saw you. Rich-man is shitting himself -- the working class, sober!"
Zh: “我能看出来，哈里。”他端详着你的脸。“酒精留下了它的印记，不过相比第一次看见你，你的状态确实已经好多了。有钱人自己在乱搞——工人阶级，非常清醒！”

En: See, this is what they want us to do -- suck on that sugar-and-wine teat until we're insane, like they are. You gotta be smarter than that, Harry, or you'll *lose*.
Zh: “看见没，他们就想我们这么做——吸食着糖果和美酒的乳房，直到我们全部疯掉，就像他们一样。你最好放聪明一点，哈里，不然你会*失败*的。”

En: No, she's not, he scoffs. "She's a sentimental alcoholic, they all are. Never take a drop and you'll be eight laps ahead of those upper-class winos, just like old Mr. Claire here. Try it, you'll be a real superdetective."
Zh: “不，她不会的，”他嘲笑到。“她是个感情用事的酒鬼。他们全都是，哈里。只要滴酒不沾，你就能比上流社会的那些酒鬼领先八圈，就像我老克莱尔先生一样。试试看吧。你会成为一个真正的超级警探。”

En: You knew something. Something *big*. And you wanted to see what happens when you tell someone. So you told her. Anyone who's ever been close to power will tell you: inside information is the sweetest thing in the world.
Zh: “你知道一些事。一些*大事*。而且你想看看其他人发现的时候会发生什么。所以你告诉她了。每一个曾经无限接近过权力的人都会告诉你：内部信息是这个世界上最甜美的事情。”

En: It's better than money, it's better than pussy. Money only makes you special for some salesman. Pussy only makes you special for yourself. Information makes you special for all mankind. It's the ticket to history.
Zh: “它比钱好，比美女好。钱只能让你在一个商人那里变得很特别。美女只会让你觉得自己很特别。信息会让人在所有人类面前变得特别。它是一张通往历史的車票。”

En: My thoughts exactly.
Zh: “我就是这么想的。”

En: No-no-no-no-no...
Zh: “不不不不不不……”

En: It does make sense when you word it like that.
Zh: “你这么说好像确实有些道理。”

En: Don't beat yourself up, Harry. What you did was participate in history. When history calls, you *have* to pick up. You had no choice. None of us ever do. A hard disco cop like you -- I knew you weren't one to resist temptation.
Zh: “不要自责，哈里。你所做的就是参与历史。每当历史召唤，你*必须*回应。你别无他选。所有人都一样。特别是像你这种硬派迪斯科警察——我知道你不是一个能抗拒诱惑的人。”

En: But look at me going on and on, like some kind of a father figure. He laughs. "And to think we're about the same age..."
Zh: “看看我，一直絮絮叨叨个不停，搞的像当爹一样。”他笑了。“而且我们俩年龄还差不多……”

En: Right on, Harry. What you did was participate in history. When history calls, you *have* to pick up. You had no choice. None of us ever do. A hard disco cop like you -- I knew you weren't one to resist temptation.
Zh: “没错，哈里。每当历史召唤，你就必须回应。你别无他选。我们所有人都一样。特别是像你这种硬派迪斯科警察。我知道你不是一个能抗拒诱惑的人。”

En: Damn right it does, Harry. What you did was participate in history. When history calls, you *have* to pick up. You had no choice. None of us ever do. A hard disco cop like you -- I knew you weren't one to resist temptation.
Zh: “没错，就是这样，哈里。你所做的就是参与历史。每当历史召唤，你*必须*回应。你别无他选。所有人都没有。特别是像你这种硬派迪斯科警察——我知道你不是一个能抗拒诱惑的人。”

En: You know what, detective? I am going to leave this out of my report, and I suggest you leave it out of yours too.
Zh: “你知道吗，警探？我要把这部分从报告中去掉，而且我建议你也要这么做。”

En: Heh, He chuckles. "Just look at the three of us. The three careless boiadeiros. Good times, good times..."
Zh: “哈，”他轻笑出声。“看看我们三个。三个无忧无虑的牛仔。好时光啊，好时光……”

En: That it will, Harry. That it will. A sincere smile crosses his face. "As to the *bad taste* -- please. You're not a sommelier, you're a cop."
Zh: “会的，哈里。会的。”他脸上的笑容很真诚。“至于*糟糕的品味*——拜托。你不是侍酒师，你是警察。”

En: Fealty-swealty, Harry. You knew something -- something big. And you wanted to see what happens when you tell someone. So you told her. Anyone who's ever been close to power will tell you: inside information is the sweetest thing in the world.
Zh: “汗水淋漓的忠诚，哈里。你知道一些事——*大事*。而且你想看看其他人发现的时候会发生什么。所以你告诉她了。每一个曾经无限接近过权力的人都会告诉你：内部信息是这个世界上最甜美的事情。”\n”

En: Damn, Harry... He shakes his head. "I was hoping that too, but the information I gave you proved too powerful."
Zh: “该死，哈里……”他摇摇头。“我也希望这样，但是我给你的信息份量太重了。”

En: Harry, when I need to *tell* people, I might as well do it myself! That's why I like initiative -- and *inspiring* initiative. It's more *natural* that way.
Zh: “哈里，当我需要*告诉*别人的时候，我还不如自己来呢！所以我喜欢主动权——而且是*鼓舞人心的主动权*。这样的话会更加*自然*一些。”

En: Mega rich light-bending guy? Oh my god! How did that get in there?
Zh: “能扭曲光线的超级有钱人？天啦，那他是怎么进去的？”

En: He's so rich he could get in anywhere.
Zh: “他太有钱了，想去什么地方都可以。”

En: He must have been in the container when someone accidentally closed the door.
Zh: “肯定是他在集装箱里的时候，有人不小心把门给关上了。”

En: I don't know how he got in there.
Zh: “我不知道他是怎么进去的。”

En: Slime like him? Must've oozed in through the cracks.
Zh: “像他那么黏糊？肯定是从裂缝里渗进去的。”

En: Damn it to hell, Harry! He slams his fist on the table. "I specifically told my guys to check all the containers for mega rich light-bending guys."
Zh: “活他妈见鬼了，哈里！”他用拳头狠狠地砸向桌子。“我还特别嘱咐我的人去检查所有集装箱，去找那个能扭曲光线的超级有钱人。”

En: Nothing can stop an innovative mind.
Zh: “没有什么能够阻挡创新思维。”

En: I think he wants to take down the proletariat, Evrart.
Zh: “我觉得他想打倒工人阶级，艾弗拉特。”

En: Kim, tell him he was there.
Zh: “金，告诉他那人就在那里。”

En: You know, some of him *may* have been a figment of my imagination.
Zh: “你知道的，有些人*也许*只是我凭空想象出来的。”

En: There was a guy in the container, the lieutenant says slowly. "But he didn't bend any light. That was in the detective's head."
Zh: “集装箱里有个人，”警督慢慢说道。“不过他没有扭曲任何光线。那是警探脑子里想出来的。”

En: Honestly, Harry, we might be moving all kinds of suspicious things through this harbour, but I won't be caught transporting the light-bending mega-rich. He shakes his head. "I have a reputation to protect."\n
Zh: “老实说，哈里，我们可能会通过这个港口运送各种各样可疑的东西，但我是不会因为运送能扭曲光线的超级有钱人被抓到的。”他摇摇头。“我还要保护自己的声誉。”

En: Honestly, Harry, we might be moving a spot of drugs through this harbour, but I won't be caught transporting the light-bending mega-rich. He shakes his head. "I have a reputation to protect."
Zh: “老实说，哈里，我们可能会通过这个港口运送毒品，但我是不会因为运送能扭曲光线的超级有钱人被抓到的。”他摇摇头。“我还要保护自己的声誉。”

En: Honestly, guys, we might be moving all kinds of suspicious things through this harbour, but I won't be caught transporting the light-bending mega-rich. He shakes his head. "I have a reputation to protect."\n
Zh: “老实说，伙计们，我们可能会通过这个港口运送各种各样可疑的东西，但我是不会因为运送能扭曲光线的超级有钱人被抓到的。”他摇摇头。“我还要保护自己的声誉。”

En: Honestly, guys, we might be moving a spot of drugs through this harbour, but I won't be caught transporting the light-bending mega-rich. He shakes his head. "I have a reputation to protect."
Zh: “老实说，伙计们，我们可能会通过这个港口运送毒品，但我是不会因为运送能扭曲光线的超级有钱人被抓到的。”他摇摇头。“我还要保护自己的声誉。”

En: Yes, for god's sake, you're a socialist.
Zh: “是的，看在老天爷的份上，你是个工人主义者。”

En: The mega rich are people too. He was a nice man. Gave me stock tips.
Zh: “超级有钱人也是人。他是个挺不错的家伙。还给了我买股票的建议呢。”

En: Yes, the transportation of the mega rich should be more tightly regulated. Didn't seem like he was safe.
Zh: “是的，运输超级有钱人更应该严格监管。他在里面看起来并不是很安全。”

En: You should be honoured by the presence of this magus. He was of half-Revacholian blood and amassed his wealth using the mysterious bond of nationhood.
Zh: “你应该为这个魔术师的存在而感到荣幸。他身上流淌着半个瑞瓦肖人的血液，依靠神秘的国家债券积累财富。”

En: You're right, Harry. I *am* a socialist. His face turns serious. "I'm going to catch the mega rich guy inside the container and harvest his energy to power the harbour's fog lights."
Zh: “你说得对，哈里。我*是*个工人主义者。”他的脸变得很严肃。“我要找到那个集装箱里超级有钱的家伙，然后用他的能量来给港口的雾灯提供能源。”

En: He bursts out laughing. "I shudder to think what you're going to tell me next, Harry."
Zh: 他大笑出来。“一想到你接下来要告诉我的事情，我就直打哆嗦，哈里。”

En: No, they're not. He smiles brightly. "They're vermin and one just found a way inside my container. Soon he'll bring the others. All three of them. Thank you for telling me. I'll see to this immediately."
Zh: “不，他们没有。”他笑的很灿烂。“他们是害虫，有一只还想办法钻到我的集装箱里面去了。很快他就会把其他人都招来。所有三个人。谢谢你告诉我。我马上处理这件事。”

En: You're a fucking idiot, Harry. I love it! Thank you for sharing this deep, idiotic part of yourself with me. A hallucinating market liberal. I love it. So poetic.
Zh: “你他妈是个白痴吧，哈里。我爱死你了！感谢你跟我分享内心深处这个傻乎乎的自己。一个让人产生幻觉的自由市场。我爱死它了。多么富有诗意啊。”

En: Harry, it's beginning to dawn on me that you're a real fascist. A mega-fascist who imagines mega rich light-bending fascists. I love it! Thank you for sharing this facet of yourself with me.
Zh: “哈里，我慢慢开始明白，你是个真正的法西斯主义者。一个能想像出扭曲光线的超级有钱人的杰出法西斯主义者。我喜欢！感谢你跟我分享自己的这一面。”

En: Not for one second did he believe there's an actual mega rich person somewhere in his containertown.
Zh: 他从没相信过这个集装箱小镇的某个地方其实真有一个超级有钱人。

En: Look... my gun. (Show it to him.)
Zh: “看……我的枪。”（展示给他看。）

En: My, my... He lets out an appreciative whistle. "She's quite the looker, Harry. You can't imagine how pleased I am the two of you are reunited."
Zh: “天啦……”他吹响一声赞叹的口哨声。“她可真是个美人啊，哈里。你绝对想象不到看到你们俩团聚的我有多开心。”

En: Tell me, was it difficult to convince... The big man pauses to tap on his chin. "... The Pigs to give it up?"
Zh: “告诉我，要说服她很难吗……”大块头男人停下来，拍了拍自己的下巴。“……说服猪猪女侠放弃？”

En: You could say that. She tried to kill me.
Zh: “你可以这么说。她想杀了我。”

En: Not at all.
Zh: “一点也不。”

En: The important thing is I got my gun back.
Zh: “重要的是我把枪拿回来了。”

En: I guess you're right, Harry. He chuckles. "I appreciate you coming back to report on your progress. Now, what can Evrart Claire do for you?"
Zh: “我猜你说得对，哈里。”他笑了。“我很感激你能回来报告自己的进程。现在，艾弗拉特·克莱尔能为你做些什么？”

En: I told you she was a piece of work, Harry. He nods gravely. "But I knew you could handle it. I know my special policeman. Anyway, I'm glad you're alright and armed again."
Zh: 他郑重地点点头。“我告诉过你她很难搞，哈里。但是我知道你可以搞定的。我很了解我这位特别的警官。不管怎么说，很高兴你没事，而且又有武器防身了。”

En: I knew you could handle it. I know my special policeman. Anyway, I'm glad you're alright and armed again, Harry. Now, what can Evrart Claire do for you?
Zh: “我就知道你能搞定的。我很了解我这位特别的警官。不管怎么说，很高兴你没事，而且又有武器防身了，哈里。现在，艾弗拉特·克莱尔能为你做些什么？”

En: How do you know my name?
Zh: “你怎么知道我的名字？”

En: Evrart, about the weasel...
Zh: “艾弗拉特，关于黄鼠狼……”

En: He's playing it cool, but he knows his ploy didn't work.
Zh: 他在假装镇静，但是他知道自己的策略行不通。

En: You're no titan of Volition, buddy. He's got you in a fork. Sit down or leave.
Zh: 你不是意志力的巨人，老兄。他让你进退两难。要么坐下，要么离开。

En: Damn, this chair is uncomfortable...
Zh: 见鬼，这张椅子真不舒服……

En: *Mr. Du Bois*, you don't look so good...
Zh: “*杜博阿先生*，你看起来不太好……”

En: Have you heard about the horseback monument being renovated?
Zh: “你听说过马背纪念碑整修的事吗？”

En: Ah yes, I was informed. He clasps his hands together and rests them on his desk. "It seems to be a big undertaking. What are you hoping to achieve with it exactly?"
Zh: “啊，是的，我听说了。”他双手合十，放在桌子上。“看来这是个大工程。你到底想借它达到什么目的？”

En: How did you know I was behind it?
Zh: “你怎么知道我是幕后主使？”

En: I want the people of this town to understand my net worth. (Show him the stock certificate.)
Zh: 我想让这个城市的人了解我的净资产。 (把股票凭证给他看。)

En: Indeed. He turns back to you. "I'm glad to see you making headway with your endeavours, Mr. Du Bois. I look forward to seeing the end result. Is there anything else on your mind?"
Zh: “确实如此。”他转向你。“我很高兴看到你的努力有了进展，杜博阿先生。我期待着看到最终的结果。你还有别的事情吗？”

En: I'm giving the ordinary folk a hero they can believe in -- *me*.
Zh: “我要给普通人一个他们可以信任的英雄——就是*我*。”

En: A vanity project? Or is this your way of inciting *rebellion*? Tut-tut, I thought you lawmen were supposed to keep the peace?
Zh: “形象工程？还是说这是你煽动*叛乱*的方式？啧啧，我还以为你们这些执法者应该以维持治安为己任呢？”

En: Mr. Du Bois, please, it is clearly the work of a great businessman. And I know *I* didn't order it.
Zh: “杜博阿先生，拜托，这显然是一名伟大商人的杰作。而且我知道不是*我*下令的。”

En: We are, the lieutenant says firmly as he meets Evrart's gaze. "And we will do our best to ensure that's what happens here."
Zh: “我们是。”警督迎着艾弗拉特的目光，坚定地说。“而且我们会尽最大努力确保这里的情况一样和平。”

En: Interesting. This is a small fortune waiting to be claimed. He turns his large face back to you. "Always exciting to see promising businessmen rise up through the chaff."
Zh: “有意思。这是一笔小小的财富，等待着我们去索取。”他把那张大脸转向你。“看到有前途的商人从零开始奋斗，总是令人兴奋。”

En: It's business etiquette to take compliments even if they aren't sincere. Like this one.
Zh: 接受恭维是商业礼仪，即使这些赞美并不真诚。就像这句话一样。

En: In any case, I'm glad to see you making headway with your endeavours. I look forward to seeing the end result. Is there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “无论如何，我很高兴看到你的努力有了进展。我很期待看到最终的结果。你还有什么事要说吗？”

En: Oh right. Thanks. Glad you think that.
Zh: “哦。谢谢你。很高兴你这么想。”

En: Anyway, I'm glad to see you making such headway with your endeavours. I look forward to seeing the end result. Is there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “不管怎么说，我很高兴看到你的努力取得了这样的进展。我期待着看到最终的结果。你还有什么事要说吗？”

En: Remain quiet.
Zh: 保持沉默。

En: He grins slightly wider than usual, pleased to know that you follow the rules of business etiquette.
Zh: 他的笑容比平时更加灿烂，了，显然他很满意你能遵守商业礼仪的规则。

En: He narrows his eyes at you for a brief moment. 'Why didn't he follow the etiquette?', he seems to ask himself.
Zh: 他眯着眼睛看了你一小会儿。“他为什么不按规矩来？”，他似乎在问自己。

En: I'm glad to see you making headway with your endeavours, Mr. Du Bois. I look forward to seeing the end result. Is there anything else on your mind?
Zh: “我很高兴看到你的努力有了进展。杜博阿先生。我很期待看到最终的结果。你还有什么事要说吗？”

En: Who told him it was you?!
Zh: 谁告诉他是你干的？！

En: Smooth-talking? Is that what it takes to open that container? Don't you worry, Savvy has got this -- they *have* to let a VIP in.
Zh: 巧舌如簧？这就是打开那个集装箱的代价吗？你别担心，鬼鬼有办法--他们*必须*让一个贵宾进来。

En: That's *wild* how you got me to do all that stuff. (Proceed.)
Zh: ”你让我做的那些事真是太*疯狂*了。“（继续。）

En: Thanks for letting me play a part, Evrart. (Proceed.)
Zh: “感谢你能让我参与其中，艾弗拉特。”（继续。）

En: Aren't you going to ask me how I got in?
Zh: “你不打算问我是怎么进来的吗？”

En: Thank you, Evrart. (Take the comically large cheque.)
Zh: “谢谢你，艾弗拉特。”（接过滑稽的大支票。）

En: Keep it, I'm good. (Leave the cheque.)
Zh: “你留着吧，我很好。”（不拿支票。）

En: It should be sufficient to cover your expenses for a few days and patch over your differences with the cafeteria manager. Go ahead, take it. He points at it again.
Zh: “这应该足够你支付几天的费用，顺便修补一下你跟餐厅经理之间的分歧。来吧，收下它。”他的手又指了过去。

En: Wait, you know Garte?
Zh: “等等，你认识加尔特？”

En: *This* should take care of that nonsense. He points to a giant novelty cheque on his desk -- it's absolutely, comically huge.
Zh: “*这个*应该能让他闭嘴。”他指向桌子上那张巨额的兑奖支票——大到有些荒唐。
