En: Oh, by the way, I heard you got a rather rude reception from a certain... Lawrence *Garte*. Some people have no manners, it pains me to say.
Zh: “哦，顺便说一句，我听说你受到了很粗鲁的接待，好像是一个叫……劳伦斯·*加尔特*的人。虽然这么说会让我很痛苦，不过有些人真的很没礼貌。”

En: Don't panic.
Zh: 别慌张。

En: Took the novelty cheque.
Zh: 拿走兑奖支票

En: Didn't say thanks for it.
Zh: 不要说谢谢。

En: Gun was a surprise.
Zh: 枪是个惊喜。

En: At least you're not sitting.
Zh: 至少你没坐下。

En: The chair is killing you.
Zh: 这把椅子想杀了你。

En: But your ass is iron.
Zh: 但你的屁股是铁打的。

En: Side-effect spooks.
Zh: 因失枪造成的恐惧。

En: While Evrart is distracted by your odd behaviour, the lieutenant's eyes are mapping everything around you. The folder, desk, papers on the wall...
Zh: 艾弗拉特因为你古怪的行为分心的时候，警督的眼睛扫视着你周围的一切。文件夹，书桌，墙上的报纸……

En: God, you're sweating... your knee is jerking... you're about to cry, aren't you? You're about to cry, because you lost your gun and those children are going to shoot themselves with it.
Zh: 天，你在流汗……你的膝盖在颤抖……你有点想哭，是不是？你就快要哭出来了，因为你弄丢了自己的警枪，那些小孩会用它把自己打伤的。

En: Who does this guy think you are -- Ronnie the Rookie? You ain't worried about no lost gun or unpaid bill or forgotten name! You're the *bad cop*, you're probably more corrupt than him!
Zh: 这家伙以为你是谁——菜鸟罗尼吗？你不用担心失枪，或者是未支付的账单，或者是忘记自己的名字！你是个*坏警察*，你可能比他还要腐败！

En: Thanks for your hospitality. Feel free to visit me down at the station any time.
Zh: “感谢你的招待。有空随时欢迎你来警局找我坐坐。”

En: Why, thank you, it's always nice when a fellow professional appreciates your work.
Zh: “啊，谢谢，有同专业的人欣赏你的工作感觉总是很好的。”

En: Nice bit with the chair, by the way... (Chuckle.) "A good way to keep your guests on edge."
Zh: “话说，你这把椅子不错……”（轻笑一声。）“真是让你的客人坐立不安的好方法。”

En: That we do, Harry. Let them say what they will about you and me, we're both born fighters.
Zh: “是的，哈里。你我之间随便他们爱怎么说就怎么说吧，我们俩生来就是战士。”

En: Could you help me get a dead body down from a tree?
Zh: “你能帮我把尸体从树上弄下来吗？”

En: What is this *Mr. Du Bois* he keeps repeating? *What* is he trying to pull here? You need to cool the fuck down, CHILL.
Zh: 他一直念叨的*杜博阿先生*是什么？他到底想干*什么*？你他妈的需要冷静下来，镇定。

En: Mr. Du Bois... Mr. Du Bois! Harry!!! The large man snaps his fingers, but to no effect -- you're in some stupor.
Zh: “杜博阿先生……杜博阿先生！哈里！！！”大个头男人打着响指，但是毫无效果——你陷入某种恍惚状态了。

En: Should you find yourself more amenable in the future, I'd gladly resume our conversation. But until then, I'm afraid I must ask you to leave. He turns back to his typewriter.
Zh: “如果你发现自己以后变得更好说话了，我很乐意继续我们之间的谈话。不过在那之前，我恐怕要先请你离开了。”他又转回到自己的打字机前。

En: I don't *sit*. It's kind of my thing.
Zh: “我不喜欢*坐下*。这是我的个人风格。”

En: Very well, Mr. Du Bois. I respect a man with strong convictions... As he nods, his multiple chins move like ocean waves.
Zh: “很好，杜博阿先生。我尊敬拥有强大信念的人……”他点点头，下巴上一层层的肉像海浪般一波一波地翻滚着。

En: The chair you're sitting on has got to be the most uncomfortable chair in the world. It's *violating* your backside.
Zh: 你身下的椅子一定是这个世界上最不舒服的那张。它*违反*了你臀部的人体工学。

En: Don't sit. [Leave.]
Zh: 别坐下。[离开。]

En: Sit.
Zh: 坐下。

En: So tell me, how can the head of the Débardeurs' Union help a representative of the Revachol Citizens Militia today?
Zh: “告诉我，你这个瑞瓦肖公民武装的代表，今天需要我这个装卸工会的会长帮你什么忙呢？”

En: Actually I was just leaving. [Leave.]
Zh: “其实我正打算离开。”[离开。]

En: Please, Mr. Du Bois, let us converse in a civilized manner, as equals. Take a seat -- I insist.
Zh: “麻烦你，杜博阿先生，让我们以一种文明的方式平等交流。请坐——我坚持。”

En: Say nothing. Look him dead in the eye.
Zh: 什么也不说。看着他的眼睛。

En: He's trying to throw you off your game with this *Du Bois* nonsense -- don't give him the pleasure.
Zh: 他想用这个*杜博阿*的鬼话把你从这个游戏里踢出局——别让他高兴。

En: Have a good day, Mr. Du Bois, I'm sure I'll be seeing you around. He waves and returns to typing.
Zh: “祝你度过愉快的一天，杜博阿先生，我肯定我们还会见面的。”他挥挥手，又继续回去打字了。

En: Excellent, Mr. Du Bois, I can see that you're a reasonable man, and reasonable men... Reasonable men can be of great use to one other. He gives you a sly wink.
Zh: “太棒了，杜博阿先生，我能看出你是一个讲道理的人，而且讲道理的人……讲道理的人对同样讲道理的人来说可能大有帮助。”他狡猾地朝你眨眨眼。

En: Welcome, Mr. Du Bois, Mr. Kitsuragi. It's good of you two to stop by. Please, have a seat. He gestures to a *tiny* chair opposite to his giant desk.
Zh: “欢迎，杜博阿先生，曷城先生。很高兴你们俩能来拜访。请坐。”他朝着自己大书桌对面的*小*椅子比了个手势。

En: Mr. Du Bois! I take it you have reconsidered your stance on the etiquette of verbal communication? He points to the *tiny* chair.
Zh: “杜博阿先生！我猜你已经重新考虑过自己在口头交流礼仪方面的立场了？”他指向那把*小*椅子。

En: Take a seat.
Zh: 坐下。

En: I'd rather stand.
Zh: “我宁愿站着。”

En: Actually, I'm in a bit of a hurry right now. [Leave.]
Zh: “其实，我现在有点忙。”[离开。]

En: Why are you calling me 'Mr. Du Bois'?
Zh: “你为什么叫我‘杜博阿先生’？”

En: Welcome, Mr. Du Bois. It's good of you to stop by. Please, have a seat. He gestures to a *tiny* chair opposite to his giant desk.
Zh: “欢迎，杜博阿先生。很高兴你能来拜访。请坐。”他朝着自己大书桌对面的*小*椅子比了个手势。

En: The one good eye of this man feels you up without even flickering. The other -- his lazy eye -- is constantly moving like a goldfish in a tank.
Zh: 男人那只好眼睛紧盯着你，甚至一眨没眨。另外一只——弱视的眼球——像水缸里的金鱼一样一直动个不停。

En: Mr. Du Bois, a pleasure as always -- you don't have to sit down this time, since you've already sat on that chair.
Zh: “杜博阿先生，总是很高兴见到你——这次你不用坐下了，因为你坐过了。”

En: Mr. Du Bois! I hope time is on your side this time... Please, take a seat! He gestures to the *miniscule* folding chair opposite his huge desk.
Zh: “杜博阿先生！希望这次时间能站在你那边……快请坐下吧！”他朝着自己大书桌对面的那把*迷你*折叠椅比了个手势。

En: Whatever he has in store for you, it can't be good, he thinks. I'll do my best.
Zh: 不管他为你准备了什么，一定不是什么好事，他想道。我会尽一切努力的。

En: You go ahead, detective. The lieutenant nods at you, then the chair.
Zh: “你坐吧，警探。”警督先朝你点点头，然后望向椅子。

En: Have a good day, Mr. Du Bois, I'm sure I'll be seeing you around. He waves and returns to his typing.
Zh: “祝你度过愉快的一天，杜博阿先生，我肯定我们还会见面的。”他挥挥手，又继续回去打字了。

En: Keep staring.
Zh: 继续盯。

En: Before you is a walrus of a man seated behind a large desk. He looks up from his work, not the least bit surprised to see you...
Zh: 在你面前，一个宛如海象般的男人坐在一张巨大的书桌后面。他停下手中的工作抬起头，看见你一点都不觉得惊讶……

En: Please, have a seat. He gestures to a *tiny* folding chair -- opposite to his giant desk.
Zh: “请坐。”他朝着一把*迷你*折叠椅比了个手势——就在他的大书桌对面。

En: ...Mr. Du Bois, Mr. Kitsuragi, how nice that you found a moment to pay a visit to the Débardeurs' Union. I'm Evrart Claire, head of this little operation here.
Zh: “……杜博阿先生，曷城先生，你们能抽空拜访装卸工会真是太好了。我是艾弗拉特·克莱尔，这个小地方的会长。”

En: ...Mr. Du Bois, how nice that you found a moment to pay a visit to the Débardeurs' Union. I'm Evrart Claire, head of this little operation here.
Zh: “……杜博阿先生，你能抽空拜访装卸工会真是太好了。我是艾弗拉特·克莱尔，这个小地方的会长。”

En: Are you in charge of the dockworkers?
Zh: “你掌管所有码头工人吗？”

En: Have it your way, Mr. Du Bois. You'll be back...
Zh: “随你便吧，杜博阿先生。你会回来的……”

En: Soooo... He begins to speak, albeit very slowly -- purposefully leaving a pause after his opener.
Zh: “所以……”他开了口，尽管有些缓慢——故意在开场之后留下一个停顿。

En: Like a general over his maps, he plots his moves. Judging by the way he's licking his chops, it's going to be a good one.
Zh: 他就像一个将军，正站在地图前部署自己的行动。从他兴奋的样子判断，应该很不错的样子。

En: A typical power play. Wait for him to speak first -- show him you've got a backbone.
Zh: 典型的权力游戏。先等他说完——然后展示一下自己的骨气。

En: I'd offer you my hand, but unfortunately my health prevents me from getting up. You understand.
Zh: “我很想跟你握握手，不过很可惜，我的健康条件不允许我站起来。请理解。”

En: Leave without comment. [Leave.]
Zh: 不做任何评论的离开。[离开。]

En: At first, nothing happens. His face wears a wide and self-satisfied smile. Every now and then, he smacks his big lips.
Zh: 一开始，什么也没发生。他的脸上挂着一副灿烂又洋洋自得的笑容。时不时的，他还会砸吧着那肥厚的嘴唇。

En: I'm Evrart, Evrart Claire, head of the Débardeurs' Union here in Martinaise. The man relaxes into his chair and continues:
Zh: “我是艾弗拉特，艾弗拉特·克莱尔，马丁内斯装卸工会的会长。”男人放松地坐在椅子里，继续说道。

En: You called me 'Mr. Du Bois.' Why?
Zh: “你叫我‘杜博阿先生’。为什么？”

En: Stop. This is getting too weird.
Zh: 打住。这样有点太诡异了。

En: His look goes from steely to inquisitive. A broad, self-satisfied grin crosses his wide face. You hear the wet smack of his lips.
Zh: 他的神情从严肃克制变成了好奇。脸上绽放出灿烂又洋洋自得的笑容。你听见他砸吧嘴的声音。

En: Hello. I have, uh, law enforcement business with you.
Zh: “你好。我，呃，有执法方面的事情要跟你讨论一下。”

En: Try to wink back.
Zh: 尝试眨眼回应。

En: Remain serious.
Zh: 保持严肃。

En: (Take the comically large cheque but don't say anything.)
Zh: （拿走滑稽的大支票，但是什么也不说。）

En: Is there anything you'd like to say to me, or...?
Zh: “你还有什么要跟我说的吗，还是……”

En: He's not.
Zh: 不，他没开玩笑。

En: Don't mention it. But also don't forget it. Another wink. "I'm just kidding, of course."
Zh: “别提了。不过也别忘记了。”又眨了下眼。“当然了，我只是在开玩笑。”

En: Yeah, thanks!
Zh: “是啊，谢谢！”

En: No, it's cool. (You've got integrity.)
Zh: “不，这挺酷的。”（你很正直。）

En: Okay, okay... I respect a man with principles. No handouts, then. He crosses his arms on his ample midsection -- and sinks further into his chair.
Zh: “好吧，好吧……我尊重有原则的男人。那就不要施舍了。”他交叉双臂，停放在宽大的肚子上——往椅子里陷得更深了。

En: Yes, I know Lawrence. He's a real character -- no Union man in him. A real piece of work, that boy is.
Zh: “是的，我认识劳伦斯。他真的很有性格——不像工会的人。那小子，是个人物。”

En: With a grin he points to the cheque again. It's like you're on a game show... *At least* don't thank him for it.
Zh: 他咧嘴笑了笑，手又指向了那张支票。你感觉自己像是在参加一个游戏节目……*至少*别因此感谢它。

En: *Cool*? I wouldn't go that far. I'm sure there are cooler things than delivering a comically oversized novelty cheque to a cafeteria manager, but, sure... if that's what's cool nowadays.
Zh: “*酷*？那还不至于吧。相比给餐厅经理递一张滑稽的大兑奖支票，我肯定还会有远比这更酷的事，但是，当然了……如果现在酷就是这种意思的话。”

En: Now, I'd like to set your mind at ease about one other matter: your *lost gun*. Let me assure you, Union people are on it as we speak. I've got my best hounds looking for that *lost gun*.
Zh: “现在，我想让你对另一件事放心：你的*失枪*。我向你保证，我们说话这会儿，工会的人已经去找了。我安排了手下最棒的猎犬去寻找这把*失枪*。”

En: When he said "don't worry" he actually meant "be very worried."
Zh: 当他说*别担心*的时候，其实意思是*应该很担心*。

En: You don't need a gun. We can still have fun without the gun. The fun doesn't need to stop. HAVE SOME RIGHT NOW.
Zh: 你不需要枪。没枪我们还是能自己找乐子。欢乐是不需要停止的。不如现在就开始吧。

En: Fuck love! This is your *GUN*, this is serious.
Zh: 去他妈的爱情吧！这是你的*枪*，这很严肃。

En: Oh god, why didn't you think of this before -- COPS HAVE GUNS! Where's yours?!
Zh: 天啦，你为什么之前没想过——警察都是有枪的！你的哪里去了？！

En: It's gone. Your gun is gone. There's nothing in your pockets.
Zh: 它没了。你的枪没了。你的口袋里什么也没有。

En: I'm not worried -- I got this.
Zh: “我不担心——我自己能搞定。”

En: I would appreciate any help you could provide.
Zh: “如果你能提供任何帮助，我都会不胜感激。”

En: No, I'm *not* about to cry! (Try to stay cool.)
Zh: 不，我*不会*哭的！（试着保持冷酷。）

En: So what. Men can cry too.
Zh: 那又怎么了。男人也能哭。

En: You *want* to cry? God, you're weak. Whatever you do, don't cry. He'll think you're disgusting.
Zh: 你*想*哭？老天，你也太弱了吧。不管做什么，不要哭。他会觉得你很恶心的。

En: Let's talk about my lost gun.
Zh: “我们聊聊我那把失枪的事吧。”

En: I'm looking into your shady brew.
Zh: “我正在调查你那个见不得人的阴谋。”

En: Evrart, I'm going to leave now, but we might talk again later. [Leave.]
Zh: “艾弗拉特，我现在要先离开，不过也许我们可以之后再聊。”[离开。]

En: Wait... He leans reaches into his drawer and pulls out a plastic card. "You need this to get in and out through the gate."
Zh: “等等……”他把手伸进抽屉，拿出一张塑料卡。“你需要它进出大门。”

En: See you soon, débardeur! The big man raises his hand in farewell. "Just kidding. But not too much."
Zh: “回见，装卸工！”大个子男人挥手向你告别。“只是开个玩笑。不过最好别太过了。”

En: Here -- you're one of us now. A real red and white Union man. Take care, Harry.
Zh: “给你——现在你是我们的一员了。一个真正的红白工会成员。保重，哈里。”

En: You wanted us to relay all this information to her... the lieutenant cuts in.
Zh: “你想让我们把所有信息都传达给她……”警督插了进来。

En: Let's drop the funny stuff. I'm trying to make contact with my revolutionary brethren.
Zh: “我们谈点有趣的事情。我正在设法联系我的革命同志们。”

En: He's got you there. You've got to be 10 or 12 metres up, at least.
Zh: 他说的没错，你起码要爬上10或者12米高才行。

En: If you haven't spoken with my man Mañana yet, I'd suggest you start there. He's more in touch with what's happening at the ground level, so to speak...
Zh: “如果你还没有和我的伙计马列拉说过话，那我建议你先从他开始。可以说，他比我更熟悉下面发生的事情……”

En: That's all you're going to get out of him, it appears.
Zh: 看来你只能从他嘴里套出这些了。

En: Sure thing, Harry. We're *all business* now. The answer is still 'no'. I'm a busy man, as you can see. I don't block off time on my schedule for 'underground' types...
Zh: “当然，哈里。我们现在都在*谈正事*。答案还是‘不’。你也知道，我是个大忙人。我不会在日程表上为‘地下’这种人划出时间来……”

En: I'm looking for a cell of underground communists. Can you help me?
Zh: “我在找一个康米地下小组。你能帮我吗？”

En: That's a non sequitur if you've ever heard one!
Zh: 如果你听过这句话的话，那就是不当推论！

En: Mr. Du Bois, really. Do I look like a man who has time for *underground* communists? I'm as aboveground as it gets!
Zh: “杜博阿先生，你是认真的吗？我看起来像一个有时间找康米*地下小组*的人吗？我已经在地面上了！”

En: That note of contempt in his voice is sincere, sssire.
Zh: 他语气里的那点轻蔑是真诚的，大人。

En: Now, was there anything else you wished to discuss today?
Zh: “好了，今天你还有什么想讨论的吗？”

En: You've already spoken with Mañana, as I understand. I'm afraid I don't have anything else for you on *this subject*...
Zh: “据我所知，你已经和马列拉谈过了。关于*这个话题*，恐怕我没有别的事情能说了……”

En: What's in the container that's outside your office?
Zh: “你办公室外面的集装箱里是什么？”

En: My dear Harry, there are literally millions of containers in this harbour. I couldn't possibly remember what's in all of them.
Zh: “亲爱的哈里，毫不夸张，这个港口有几百万个集装箱。我不可能记得里面所有的东西。”

En: I met a girl named Acele who said she's working with you.
Zh: “我遇到一个叫阿西尔的女孩，她说自己和你一起工作。”

En: Funny... The big man lets out a lazy yawn. "That doesn't ring any bells, Harry."
Zh: “有意思……”大个子男人懒洋洋地打了个哈欠。“听起来不太耳熟啊，哈里。”

En: With great effort, he straightens himself up in his chair, yet says nothing. He simply stares at you.
Zh: 他费了好大劲才从椅子上坐直，不过还是什么也没说，就只是默默地盯着你。

En: Don't give up -- but don't leave either. You're going to have this conversation on your terms. You just need to remain headstrong.
Zh: 别放弃——不过也别离开。你要按照自己的方式进行这次对话。你只需要保持这种顽固的势头。

En: Pat your pockets.
Zh: 拍拍你的口袋。

En: This doesn't worry me at all. Who cares if I've lost my gun *and* the love of my life?
Zh: 我一点都不在乎。谁会在乎我有没有丢枪，*还有*我的此生最爱呢？

En: The world is swallowed by a black hole of fear. Only two words escape its gravitational pull: *lost* and *gun*.
Zh: 这个世界被一个恐惧的黑洞吞噬了。只有两个字逃脱了它的万有引力：*失*和*枪*。

En: His slug-like lips move, but all you hear is an echo: lost gun, lost gun, lost gun...
Zh: 他那张鼻涕虫一般的嘴唇蠕动着，但你只听到一个回声：失枪，失枪，失枪……

En: You can take that comically large cheque and shove it up your ass! (Leave it.)
Zh: “你可以把滑稽的大支票拿走，然后塞进你自己的屁股里！”（丢下它。）

En: How do *you* know about my lost gun?!
Zh: “*你*是怎么知道我的枪不见了？”

En: It's not like you left it loaded. You didn't lose a *loaded gun*. Local children aren't out there playing with it right now, pointing it into their own mouths... It's in a safe place. I just know it. I have a feeling everything's going to be alright.
Zh: “我并没有说它上了膛。你没有丢掉一支*上膛的枪*。本地的那些小孩现在也没有拿着它瞎摆弄，把枪口塞进自己的嘴巴里……这是个安全的地方。我很清楚。我有一种感觉，一切都会没事的。”

En: Are you alright, Harry? You seem anxious. Don't be. Everything's going to be alright.
Zh: “你还好吧，哈里？你看起来很紧张。不用这样。一切都会没事的。”

En: Are you alright, Harry? You say you got this, but you seem a little *anxious* to me. Don't be. Everything's going to be alright.
Zh: “你还好吧，哈里？你说自己能搞定，不过在我看来你好像有一点*紧张*。不用这样。一切都会没事的。”

En: It was loaded. There were two bullets in it. You always keep at least two barrels  loaded.
Zh: 它上膛了。里面有两颗子弹。你总是会保留至少两发子弹。

En: I know *everything*, Harry. Right now I know that you're worried. Don't be worried. Everything's going to be alright.
Zh: “我*什么*都知道，哈里。现在我知道你在担心。不用这样。一切都会没事的。”

En: The chair is *incredibly* uncomfortable. Fortunately, your ass is made of *iron* and the chair is made of wood. Iron beats wood. You manage not to shift around too much...
Zh: 这张椅子*极其*不舒服。幸运的是，你的皮肤是*铁打的*，这把椅子是木头的。铁能打败木头。你想办法尽量不挪动位置……

En: Okay, police officers have weapons they use to shoot people with, right? It's gotta be on you. Check your pockets.
Zh: 好吧，警察的武器是拿来射击坏人的，对吧？它肯定在你身上。检查一下自己的口袋。

En: Pat your pockets.
Zh: 拍拍你的口袋。

En: This doesn't worry me at all. I don't care if I lost my gun and the love of my life.
Zh: 我一点都不担心。谁担心我有没有丢枪，还有我的此生最爱？

En: It's gone. It's not there. Your gun is gone.
Zh: 它没了。你的枪没了。你的口袋里什么也没有。

En: To hell with love! This is your *GUN*, it's pretty bad if you lost it...
Zh: 爱情什么的见鬼去吧！这可是你的*枪*啊，如果弄丢，那就真的糟糕了……

En: Kim, uhm, Kim... (Turn to Kim.)
Zh: “金，呃，金……”（转向金。）

En: Lieutenant Kitsuragi doesn't seem to be here at the moment. You came alone.
Zh: “曷城警督好像现在不在这儿。你一个人来的。”

En: Officer, we will deal with this later. We don't need Mr. Claire's help with this.
Zh: “警官，我们稍后再处理这个问题。我们不需要克莱尔先生的帮助。”

En: I wouldn't be so sure about that, he says, as though to himself.
Zh: “这我就不是很肯定了，”他好像是在自言自语。

En: Mr. Du Bois! I hear the meeting with Titus was a glowing success. His grin widens. "That's such a relief. Titus can be a handful sometimes."
Zh: “杜博阿先生！我听说你跟提图斯的会面非常成功。”他的笑容更灿烂了。“这还真是让人松了一口气。提图斯有时候真的挺难对付的。”

En: Mr. Du Bois! The word in Martinaise is *a certain police officer* is once again happily reunited with his service weapon. He winks at you. "Congratulations, my friend!"
Zh: “杜博阿先生！马丁内斯有传言说*某个警官*再次跟他的配枪愉快地团聚了。”他朝你眨眨眼。“恭喜你，我的朋友！”

En: Mr. Du Bois... He looks at you with surprise. "Why on earth are you dressed like my recently deceased security guard?"
Zh: “杜博阿先生……”他吃惊地看着你。“你为什么穿的跟我那个最近刚过世的保安一样？”

En: Mr. Du Bois, 'Every worker...'? He leans toward you, waiting for your to complete his sentence.
Zh: “杜博阿先生，‘每一个工人……？’”他向你靠近，等待你完成他的句子。

En: I'm honouring a great man.
Zh: “我在向一个伟大的人致敬。”

En: It's a political statement. Revachol is for Revacholians.
Zh: “这是一种政治立场。瑞瓦肖属于瑞瓦肖人。”

En: Well... look at it! I mean, cool, right?
Zh: “呃……看看它！我是说，很酷，对吗？”

En: Oops... maybe this isn't entirely appropriate...
Zh: “哎呀……也许这个并不算完成合适……”

En: Mr. Du Bois! Every worker -- member of the board! He smiles merrily. "How can I help you today?"
Zh: “杜博阿先生！每一个工人——都是董事会成员！”他愉快地笑了。“今天能怎么帮你？”

En: ... a member of the board?
Zh: “‘……都是董事会成员？’”

En: ... is a living god!
Zh: “……都是活生生的神！”

En: ... is an organ in the great state organism of war! Also, looks just like *me*.
Zh: “……都是伟大战争机体中的一个器官！而且，看起来就像*我*一样。”

En: ... for himself!
Zh: “……都要为了自己！”

En: So, the ship has sailed... did you see it? I know you did. He leans toward you. "Tell me, what did it look like as it grew smaller and smaller? When that mainsail dropped behind the horizon..."
Zh: “所以，船已经开了……你看见了吗？我知道你看见了。”他靠向你的方向。“告诉我，看着它越变越小，感觉是什么样子的？当那面主帆消失在地平线以后……”

En: ... did it look like a germ? Did it look like a piece of *bacteria*?
Zh: “……它看起来像一个细菌吗？像不像*菌群*的一部分？”

En: Hahaha... Mr. Du Bois... His amusement seems genuine. "So be it! How can I help you today?"
Zh: “哈哈哈哈……杜博阿先生……”他似乎是真被你逗乐了。“那就这样吧！今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: That's right, Mr. Du Bois! He nods in approval. "I see the socialist-democratic fervour now burns in your heart too. How can I help you today?"
Zh: “没错，杜博阿先生！”他赞同地点点头。“我可以看见工人主义的热情在你心中熊熊燃烧。今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Eeeh... why the hell not? He chuckles merrily. "Now, how can I help you today?"
Zh: “呃……为什么不呢？”他愉快地轻笑出声。“所以，今天有什么可以帮你的呢？”

En: ... and for his fellow man! That's right, Mr. Du Bois! You're turning out to be quite the individualist there... The last sentence is spoken more to himself than anybody else. "So, you had business with me?"
Zh: “……还有为了他手下的人！没错，杜博阿先生！你正在向一个个人主义者转变……”最后一句话不像是对着别人，更像是对他自己说的。“所以，你有事要跟我商量吗？”

En: From where I'm sitting, it looks more like you've robbed a dead man, Mr. Du Bois. He leans back. "But as this matter is far below my pay grade, I'm just going to ask: how can I help you today?"
Zh: “从我现在的这个位置，看起来更像是你掠夺了一个死人的东西，杜博阿先生。”他向后靠了靠。“不过碍于这件事远远低于我的薪金等级，我只会问一句：今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Very powerful, Mr. Du Bois. This is a very powerful statement. He grins far and wide like he doesn't actually think your statement is powerful at all. "Now, how can I help you today, Mr. Political Statement?"
Zh: “很强大，杜博阿先生。这是一份很有力量的陈述。”他微笑的嘴唇咧得更宽了，好像他完全不觉得你的陈述很有份量。“所以，今天有什么能帮你的吗，政治立场先生？”

En: A deceased security guard's uniform? Oh yeah, in the extreme. He covers his mouth with a sweaty hand. "Now how can I help a cool guy such as yourself today, Harry?"
Zh: “一套亡故安保的制服？是啊，非常极端。”他用汗涔涔的手遮住嘴巴。“所以，我今天能怎么帮到一个像你这么酷的人呢，哈里？”

En: Stealing the clothes of a security guard who'd only just died of old age? He seems to ponder it for a moment. "Well, maybe not *entirely*. But -- you had business to discuss with me, Harry?"
Zh: “从一个刚刚老死的安保人员那里偷衣服？”他好像沉思了一会儿。“呃，也许并不是*太彻底*。不过——你有什么事要跟我商量吗，哈里？”

En: No, it looked like a boat sailing away from the coast. No need to be overly dramatic.
Zh: “不，看起来就像是一艘驶离海岸的船。没必要搞得过分戏剧化。”

En: Now, what can Evrart Claire do for you today?
Zh: “所以，今天艾弗拉特·克莱尔能为你做些什么呢？”

En: Mr. Du Bois! Every worker is a god, right? He chuckles. "What can I do for you today?"
Zh: “杜博阿先生！每一个工人都是神明，对吧？”他轻笑到。“今天能怎么帮你？”

En: Mr. Du Bois, every worker... He looks around, searching for something in his room. "To hell with it... Every worker is white! Now, what can I do for you today?"
Zh: “杜博阿先生，每一个工人……”他四下看了看，在房间里寻找某种东西。“见鬼去吧……每一个工人都是纯洁的！所以，今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Mr. Du Bois! Every worker for himself, right? He smiles a wide sly smile. "What can I do for you today?"
Zh: “杜博阿先生！每一个工人都是为了自己工作，对吧？”他狡猾的笑容十分灿烂。“今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: You strike me as a reasonable man, Harry -- I like that in a *lawman*. He smiles. "Let's cut to the chase, shall we? What can Evrart Claire do for you?"
Zh: “我觉得你是个通情达理的人，哈里——我喜欢*执法人员*这一点。”他笑了。“不如我们切入正题吧，怎么样？艾弗拉特·克莱尔能为你做些什么？”

En: *Mr. Du Bois*, you don't look so good...
Zh: “*杜博阿先生*，你看起来不太好……”

En: Keep standing there, wobbly like a man-shaped jello shot.
Zh: 继续站在那儿，像人形果冻一样摇摆不定。

En: Keep sliding down the chair like a jello shot.
Zh: 像果冻一样从椅子上滑下去。

En: There are no *Harries*. Let your mind go to your safe place.
Zh: 根本没有什么*哈里*。让你的思绪回到安全的地方。

En: What an odd demonstration of... huh, you got me, Harry... I don't even know *what*. As entertaining as it was, I'm afraid we're wasting our time, and I'm an extremely busy man, as I'm sure you are too.
Zh: “真是个古怪的示范……啊，你骗到我了，哈里……我甚至都不知道那是*什么*。虽然确实让人感觉很愉快，不过恐怕这只是在浪费我们的时间，而且我是个特别忙的人，我肯定你也一样。”

En: Mr. Du Bois... Are you okay? Can I get you a glass of water or something? Are you having some kind of medical emergency?
Zh: “杜博阿先生……你没事吧？要不要来杯水或者什么的？你需要看急诊吗？”

En: Don't be dramatic. I can see your condition isn't terminal.
Zh: “别搞得这么戏剧化。我能看到你的病情还没有到末期。”

En: I'm melting...
Zh: “我在融化……”

En: Nah, I'm as good as it gets, *Mr. Du Bois*. (Vaguely gesture with your hands above your head.)
Zh: “不，我已经再好不过了，*杜博阿先生*。”（双手举过头顶，做个含糊的手势。）

En: Maybe you could use your hands somehow? In a kind of throwing motion? Like you're *throwing* that Mr. Du Bois act right back at him. *He's* Mr. Du Bois!
Zh: 也许你可以想办法用用你的手？做出一种扔东西的动作？就好像你把杜博阿先生那一套*扔回到*他那里。*他*才是杜博阿先生！

En: First, you can call me Harry, because that's my name.
Zh: “首先，你可以叫我哈里，因为这是我的名字。”

En: Excellent, *Harry*. The smile widens. "Of course, that's your name. What else could it be? Now, please, have a seat."
Zh: “很好，*哈里*。”他的嘴角咧的更开了。“当然，这是你的名字。不然还有可能是什么呢？现在，请坐下来。”

En: Questions will show him who's boss.
Zh: 问题会让他明白，谁才是这里的老大。

En: Somehow you managed to get yourself out of this one. Now quick, keep the momentum up, ask questions!
Zh: 不知怎么的，你想办法让自己恢复了过来。现在快一点，保持这种势头，问他问题！

En: Whatever you do, don't ask him about the gun first. You don't want him thinking you're scared.
Zh: 不管你做什么，先不要问他枪的事。你不想让他以为你在害怕。

En: This is a würm's lair and we both know why I'm here -- to help grow your hoard.
Zh: “这是蠕虫的老巢，而且我们都知道我来这里的原因——为了帮你增加财富。”

En: I'm not saying I'm corrupt. I'm *rational*.
Zh: “我没说我很腐败。我可是很*理性*的。”

En: I think we'd like to ask you a few questions. Don't you think so, detective? The lieutenant looks quite fed up with the situation.
Zh: “我们想问你一些问题。你不觉得吗，警探？”警督看来已经受够这种状态了。

En: I prefer my police officers old-fashioned like that... with a gun. You can do so many things with a gun that you can't do without one. Now, what can I help you with?
Zh: “我比较喜欢老派的警官……带枪的那种。有了枪，你就能做很多没它不能做的事。现在，有什么可以帮你的吗？”

En: He's trying to throw you off your game, thinking you don't know your name -- but you do! So the joke's on him, ha ha!
Zh: 他想把你从这个游戏里踢出局，觉得你不知道自己的名字——不过你知道！所以他才是个笑话，哈哈！

En: Remain standing and don't go anywhere.
Zh: 继续站着，哪里也不去。

En: Leave quietly. [Leave.]
Zh: 安静地离开。[离开。]

En: Evrart starts whistling a little worker song. He really is trying to ignore you... or *will* you out of existence.
Zh: 艾弗拉特开始吹起一曲工人小调。他真的打算无视你……或者你是*真的*并不存在。

En: Stand strong.
Zh: 站稳了。

En: The lieutenant stands right next to you, not showing any signs of impatience or boredom.
Zh: 警督就站在你旁边，没有显露出一丝不耐烦或者厌倦的迹象。

En: I see you are an extremely stubborn man, Mr. Du Bois... That ain't necessarily a bad thing. Finally, the big man looks you in the eye and speaks.
Zh: “看来你还真是个特别顽固的男人，杜博阿先生……其实这未必是件坏事。”大个子男人终于还是看着你的眼睛，开了口。

En: Oh yeah, man -- I'm fucking great... (Keep wobbling.)
Zh: “是啊，老兄——我他妈最棒了……”（继续摇晃。）

En: Oh yeah, man -- I'm fucking great... (Keep sliding.)
Zh: “是啊，老兄——我他妈最棒了……”（继续滑。）

En: Actually, I really could use that glass of water. (Straighten out.)\n
Zh: “其实吧，我真的想喝杯水。”（坐直。）

En: Actually, this chair is uncomfortable. I could use that glass of water. (Sit upright.)
Zh: “其实吧，这张椅子很不舒服。我想喝杯水。”（坐直。）

En: Is that a Union card? I already have one.
Zh: “这是工会卡吗？我已经有一张了。”

En: It's wonderful that you've stolen a membership card, but this is a *key card*. You can't get out of the harbour without it.
Zh: “你偷了一张会员卡其实挺棒的，不过这一张是*钥匙卡*。要是没有它，你就不能离开港口了。”

En: I, too, have convictions, one of which is that I will not engage any man who won't face me at *eye level*.
Zh: “我，一样，也有自己的信念，比如说我不会和任何不跟我*平视*的人打交道。”

En: Quick, here's your window! Get yourself together and ask him questions! Police officer questions!
Zh: 快，你的突破口来了！振作起来，问他问题！警方事务相关的问题！

En: Okay, enough! We are here to ask you some questions pertaining to a murder investigation.
Zh: “好吧，够了！我们来这里是为了询问跟一起凶杀案有关的问题。”

En: It is about a time to stop embarrassing yourself. Questions will help you regain some of your lost dignity.
Zh: 是时候停止让自己难堪了。问问题能帮你恢复一些丢失的尊严。

En: This fighter could've used a more comfortable *chair*.
Zh: “这个战士应该坐一把更舒服的*椅子*。”

En: I do want you to feel at home, Harry, truly. But you'll have to excuse the humble accommodations.
Zh: “我真心希望能给你一种宾至如归的感觉，哈里，真的。但是请你务必体谅一下我这里简陋的条件。”

En: For a moment you don't know where to look. It is unbearably humid in the trailer. Beads of sweat slide down the man's forehead.
Zh: 有那么一会儿，你都不知道该看什么地方。拖車里潮湿地让人难以忍受。汗珠从那人的额头上滚落下来。

En: Forget about that. What's with this Du Bois stuff? You're getting some seriously bad vibes from that name...
Zh: 忘了吧。这个杜博阿什么的是怎么回事？这个名字真的给了你一种很糟糕的感觉……

En: Please, Mr. Du Bois, let us converse in a civilized manner, as equals. Take a seat -- I insist.
Zh: “麻烦你，杜博阿先生，让我们以一种文明的方式平等交流。请坐——我坚持。”

En: It's nothing. Yes, that's *probably* right. It's nothing. Forget about it, filter it out.
Zh: 没什么。是的，那*可能*是对的。真的没什么。忘了吧，把它过滤掉。

En: Titus sent Elizabeth away and said her services were no longer needed.
Zh: “提图斯把伊丽莎白送走了，说不再需要她的服务。”

En: A few more questions about the harbour.
Zh: “还有些关于港口的问题。”

En: Can we go over a few details concerning the murder again?
Zh: “我们能再复习一遍凶杀案有关的细节吗？”

En: I wanted to go over a couple of things about me.
Zh: “我想再复习一些跟我有关的事情。”

En: I want to talk about the hanging.
Zh: “我想聊聊吊死的那个人。”

En: I opened the door you asked me. Can we discuss the murder now?
Zh: “我已经按你说的把门打开了。现在可以讨论凶杀案的事了吗？”

En: I'm reconsidering opening that door you asked me to open. Perhaps it will help me somehow...
Zh: “我正在重新考虑你让我开门的那件事。也许这样能有所帮助……”

En: I did it, Evrart. I turned it off.
Zh: “我下手了，艾弗拉特。我把它关掉了。”

En: I did it, Evrart -- I made it even shadier.
Zh: “我下手了，艾弗拉特——我让它变得更加可疑了。”

En: It turns out the strikers were being served an alcoholic brew. I stopped this madness.
Zh: “原来有人请那些罢工的人喝了酒。我阻止了这件疯狂的事。”

En: It turns out the strikers are being served an alcoholic brew. I made it even stronger.
Zh: “原来有人请那些罢工的人喝了酒。我让酒变得更烈了。”

En: A few more things regarding Joyce...
Zh: “还有一些关于乔伊斯的事……”

En: I met Joyce, the company representative.
Zh: “我见过乔伊斯了，那个公司代表。”

En: I rarely do, Harry. I have people for that. He chuckles. "Now, you were saying...?"
Zh: “我很少这么做，哈里。我手下有人专门干这个的。”他咯咯笑着。“所以，你刚才想说……？”

En: There' something *special* about it. It was attached to the Kvalsund crane.
Zh: “它有些*特殊*。它是系在库瓦桑德起重机上的。”

En: Never mind the container.
Zh: “集装箱的事不重要。”

En: Harry, you smooth-talking son of a bitch, he says with the fondest of smiles. "Time is a precious resource and I don't have enough of it to count containers with you."
Zh: “哈里，你这个能言善道的鬼灵精，”他说笑着，脸上带着喜爱的神情。“时间是一种宝贵的资源，所以我没那个时间跟你一起去数那些集装箱。”

En: Smooth-talking? Maybe *that's* the way to go about opening the container! You should at least *try* convincing it.
Zh: 能言善道？也许*那就是*能把集装箱打开的办法！你至少应该*试着*去说服。

En: Oh yes. She came to see me. Said Titus Hardie is the most stubborn man she's ever met and you... He shakes his finger at you. "You are on a whole new level of *difficult*."
Zh: “是啊。她来见我了，说提图斯·哈迪是她见过的最顽固的男人，还有你……”他朝你晃了晃手指。“你的*难搞*程度完全是一个全新的档次。”

En: Honestly, Harry, he says with a chuckle. "Jamming a wedge between a man and his legal counsel is no small achievement. Sounds like you're handling this like a supercop."
Zh: “说实在的，哈里，”他轻笑着说道。“再一个人和他的法律顾问之间制造隔阂可不是一件小成就。听起来你像个超级警探一样把这件事处理好了。”

En: This is how I operate.
Zh: “我就是这么运作的。”

En: She was trying to help Titus, but the man just didn't want to listen.
Zh: “她是想帮提图斯，不过那家伙就是不愿意听。”

En: I needed to talk to Titus man to man and she was getting in the way.
Zh: “我需要跟提图斯来一场男人之间的谈话，结果她跑出来挡道。”

En: Oh, I can imagine that. Lizzie is tenacious like a little pitbull. Underneath the law degree and sophistication, she's still a Martinaisian through and through.
Zh: “哦，我能想象到。莉齐就像只小斗牛犬一样坚韧。在法律学位和老于世故的外表之下，她还是个彻头彻尾的马丁内斯人。”

En: That's Titus Hardie alright. He starts laughing. "They say no man is an island, but Titus Hardie is definitely an island. And a big one at that. At least he thinks he is."
Zh: “那是提图斯·哈迪没错了。”他笑了出来。“他们说没人是一座孤岛，不过提图斯·哈迪恰恰就是一座孤岛，而且还挺大一座的。至少他觉得自己是。”

En: To tell you the truth, I'm actually quite interested to see how this whole thing between you and Titus is gonna play out. You guys really deserve each other.
Zh: “跟你说实话，其实我很有兴趣，想看看你和提图斯之间这整件事会怎么发展。你们俩真的算得上是旗鼓相当。”

En: Goddamnit, Harry... Please warn me first if I ever make it to your shit-list. And I promise to extend you the same courtesy. He laughs.
Zh: “该死，哈里……如果我真的上了你的个人黑名单，记得首先要警告我。我保证也会对你表现出同等的礼貌。”他笑了。

En: Funny, the lieutenant says, looking up from his notebook. "She was trying to set up a narcotics operation in the old church on the coast."
Zh: “有意思，”警督抬起埋在笔记本里的脑袋，开口说道。“她想在海岸的老教堂建立一个贩毒窝点。”

En: Funny, she was trying to set up a narcotics operation in the old church on the coast.
Zh: “有意思，她想在海岸的老教堂建立一个贩毒窝点。”

En: Oh, that Acele! He taps his temple. "Yes, I do seem to remember sending a pretty young thing down there to liven up the place. Get some anodic music in there."
Zh: “哦，那个阿西尔啊！”他拍拍自己的太阳穴。“是的，我好像记得派过一个年轻漂亮的小东西去那里活跃一下气氛。在那边玩阳极音乐什么的。”

En: Did you also tell her to start the amphetamine lab?
Zh: “你还告诉她开一间安非他命实验室吗？”

En: Anodic music? Liven up the place? Yes, that checks out. Let's change the subject.
Zh: “阳极音乐？活跃气氛？是啊，那个已经检查过了。我们换个话题吧。”

En: Yes, Harry. The big man wriggles in his chair like a giant smiling caterpillar. "Let's talk about what's *really* important to you."
Zh: “是的，哈里。”这个大块头像一只巨大的微笑毛毛虫，在自己的椅子里蠕动。不如我们来聊些对你来说*真正*重要的事情吧。”

En: Amphetamine lab? He seems taken aback. "That sound very immoral and debauched. Frankly a health risk."
Zh: “安非他命实验室？”他似乎吃了一惊。“听起来非常不道德，而且很堕落。老实说，还可能危害健康。”

En: But what do I know about kids these days, the music they listen to, the drugs they do while they listen to that music... He shakes his head with a melancholy smile.
Zh: “但是我又不太了解现在的孩子，不知道他们听什么音乐，听音乐的时候吸什么毒品……”他摇摇头，脸上带着悲哀的笑容。

En: It sounds to me like you're trying to start an amphetamine lab in the district, the lieutenant says with an uncharacteristic note of contempt in his voice. "And you're using some delinquents you found god knows where to set it up and run it for you."
Zh: “在我听来，像是你想在那片区域开一间安非他命实验室，”警督说着，声音里透露出一种不同寻常的轻蔑意味。“而且你利用这些天知道从哪里找来的小流氓为你创立和运营。”

En: Are you using some random kids to start an amphetamine lab?
Zh: “你打算利用一群随便找来的孩子创立一间安非他命实验室吗？”

En: 'Random kids'? The big man exhales loudly. "That there is the daughter of Mico the Kebab -- a man who once killed a guy with a kebab."
Zh: “‘随便找来的孩子’？”大个子男人大声地呼了一口气。“那个是烤肉店老板米科的女儿——那个男人曾经用一串烤肉杀过人。”

En: I think a daughter of a man who killed a man with a kebab can handle running a little nightclub, don't you?
Zh: “我觉得一个能用烤串杀人的男人的女儿，运营一家小夜店应该不成问题，不是吗？”

En: Mico the Kebab? Really?
Zh: “烤肉店的米科？真的吗？”

En: Maybe it was Bogowir? Or Jakub? He stares off into the distance, seemingly trying to remember something. "I think it might have been Konrad..."
Zh: “也许是博戈威尔？还是雅各布？”他凝视着远方，好像试图回忆起什么。“我觉得可能是康拉德..”

En: Anyway, I shut down the amphetamine production, but let them go on with the nightclub plan.
Zh: “总之，我关闭了安非他命生产线，不过没破坏他们开夜店的计划。”

En: I let them go on with their operation.
Zh: “我让他们继续运营了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: A nightclub... He taps on his chin. "Harry, I'm an old fashioned guy. After work I like to listen to some rock and roll music and have a non-alcoholic lager. Nightclubs don't interest me."
Zh: “一间夜店……”他拍拍自己的下巴。“哈里，我是个老派的家伙。下班之后，我喜欢听听摇滚乐，来杯不含酒精的淡啤酒。夜店我没兴趣。”

En: The lieutenant tilts his head but makes no comment.
Zh: 警督歪着头，但是没有发表评论。

En: I got it, Harry! It was Radogost. Radogost the Kebab. He smiles smugly. "But enough of Kojkos and kebabs. What else can I do for you?"
Zh: “我想起来了，哈里！是拉多戈斯特。拉多戈斯特烤肉店。”他有些沾沾自喜。“不过关于克吉克和烤肉店的话题就到这里吧。还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Well, you know how it is, Harry. Kids will be kids. He glances at the photo on his desk. "At her age, me and Edgar were getting drunk, sneaking out at night, throwing rocks at motor carriages..."
Zh: “呃，你知道是怎么回事，哈里。孩子就是孩子。”他瞥了一眼桌子上的相片。“在她这个年纪，我和埃德加会喝得醉醺醺的，晚上偷溜出去，朝汽車扔石子……”

En: Throwing rocks at motor carriages isn't really the same thing as running a drug lab, is it?
Zh: 朝汽車扔石子跟运营一间毒品实验室并不是一回事，对吧？

En: But anyway, don't let me drift away to memory lane. He adjusts himself in the chair. "Tell me how can I help you, Harry."
Zh: “不管怎么说，还是不要让我再感怀往事了。”他重新调整了一下坐姿。“告诉我，有什么需要帮忙的吗，哈里。”

En: But here I am, talking about myself, when you have much more important things going on. His expression becomes serious. "Tell me, Harry, how can I help you?"
Zh: “不过现在我在这里，聊着自己，而你还有更重要的事情要做。”他的表情变得很严肃。“告诉我，哈里，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Mr. Du Bois, a pleasure as always. He grins as if nothing in the world bothers him.
Zh: “杜博阿先生，你总是很让人高兴。”他咧嘴笑了，好像这个世界上没什么事能让他烦恼。

En: Oh, I bet he's still fuming internally due to your *no-sitting* escapades. He doesn't like to lose a battle of will.
Zh: 哦，我敢打赌他心里还在为你*不坐下*的越轨行为生气。他看起来可不像是会输掉意志之战的人。

En: Um, thanks. I was wondering how I'm supposed to get out.
Zh: “嗯，谢谢。我刚还在想自己该怎么出去。”

En: Great! Wouldn't want to get stuck in here.
Zh: “很好！我也不想被困在这里。”

En: He sincerely has no idea what you were talking about -- and he doesn't care either.
Zh: 他是真的不知道您在说什么——而且他也不在乎。

En: So do I. That's one tasty brew, you should drink some right now if you can!
Zh: 我也是。那酒的味道真的很不错，如果可以的话，你现在就应该喝上几口！

En: You can find Jean-Luc down at the gates, but... He smirks. "... you already knew that. Anyway, he's going to help you."
Zh: “让·卢克就在大门下面，不过……”他傻笑起来。“……你已经知道了。不管怎么说，他会帮你的。”

En: He turns back to you: "You can find Luc down at the gates, but..." He smirks. "You already knew that. Anyway, he's going to help you -- now that he's back on his feet."\n
Zh: 他又转向你。“让·卢克就在大门下面，不过……”他傻笑起来。“……你已经知道了。不管怎么说，他会帮你的——现在他已经又重新站起来了。”

En: You can find Jean-Luc down at the gates. He turns back to you. "He's the big, impressive one. You know: tattoos, muscles -- ethnic looking. Can't miss him. Great guy."
Zh: “让·卢克就在大门下面。”他又转向了你。“他个头很大，让人印象很深刻。你知道的：纹身啊，肌肉啊——看起来很有民族特色。肯定不会看漏的。他是个很不错的家伙。”

En: But of course, all joking aside, I *am* going to help you. He picks up the handset of a radio-phone to his right -- then clicks a button.
Zh: “不过当然了，玩笑归玩笑，我*的确*会帮你。”他抓起右边的无线电话听筒——然后按下一个按钮。

En: I can certainly see how having him up there might start affecting *some* real estate values. He licks his fat lips and smiles.
Zh: “我确实能看出，他在那里可能会开始影响*某些*房地产的价值。”他舔了一下肥厚的嘴唇，笑了。

En: He gives you a quizzical look, then smiles pleasantly. "Don't take this question personally, but *why* would I get involved in this matter, Harry?"
Zh: 他露出一个探寻的表情，然后开心地笑了。“不要以为我是在针对某个人，不过我*为什么*要卷入这种事情呢，哈里？”

En: Mr. Claire. The man was hanged with a cargo belt. A steel reinforced cargo belt, like the ones you have lying around here. With 'VERMILLION' on the side. It's safe to assume you had *something* to do with the murder.
Zh: “克莱尔先生。那个男人是被一条货物带吊起来的。一条钢骨货物带，就像你旁边的这种一样。侧面还有“弗米利恩”的标志。可以这么推测，你跟这起凶杀案有着*某种*关联的。”

En: Mr. Claire. The man was hanged with a cargo belt. A steel reinforced cargo belt. It's safe to assume the Union had *something* to do with the murder.

Zh: “没人会知道你对种族做了什么，哈里。你的种族机遇论在我这里很安全。它有多*原滋原味*的消息永远也不会离开马丁内斯。”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: You can see the map of Revachol in your head. Dozens of red arrows are pointing outwards from Martinaise. Moving all across the city.
Zh: 你可以看见脑海中马丁内斯的地图。数十只红色箭头从马丁内斯指向了外面的方向。在整个城市里穿梭。

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: Unfortunately, I can confirm this. My partner has some decidedly outmoded views.
Zh: “很可惜，我可疑确认这一点。我的搭档无疑有一些过时的观念。”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: Evrart, that's just giving orders.
Zh: “艾弗拉特，那只是在发号施令。”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “我们聊聊别的事吧。”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: But of course, Harry.
Zh: “当然可以了，哈里。”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: Oh, you're so clever, Harry. And technically you're right. Now... what else can I do for you?
Zh: “哦，你真的很聪明，哈里。而且从技术的角度来看，你说的没错。现在……还有什么能帮你的吗？”

ConvTitle: CONTAINERYARD / EVRART HOW I GOT HERE
En: Would *you* ask a man like that how he got into *your* container yard?
Zh: “*你*会问这样一个人说他是怎么跑进*你的*集装箱堆场的吗？”

En: Somewhere in Mirova a beautiful woman sees the bright glow on the horizon and says to herself: "Oh my god, I shouldn't have left him..."
Zh: 在米诺瓦的某个地方，一个美丽的女人看着地平线上那明亮的闪光，自言自语到：“我的天啦，我不应该离开他的……”

En: That means he doesn't *really* know anything about you.
Zh: 那就意味着他不是*真的*什么都知道。

En: A pity. The mystery of *you* will have to remain a mystery for the time being.
Zh: 很遗憾。关于*你*的谜题暂时还只能是个谜了。

En: So the Census Bureau says my name is Harry Du Bois?
Zh: “所以人口统计局说我的名字叫哈里·杜博阿？”

En: Yes, that's what I said. Try to keep up, okay? Let's move on.
Zh: “是的，我刚才就是这么说的。努力跟上，好吗？我们继续吧。”

En: Oh, I know everything about you, Harry. He taps on the folder in front of him. "I have a big fat folder on you. Very juicy stuff."
Zh: “哦，我知道你的一切，哈里。”他拍了拍面前的文件夹。“这个厚厚的大文件里都是你的信息。非常有意思的东西。”

En: And the word on the street is your memory is a bit... hazy. He looks at you questioningly. "If that's true, maybe I can help you out? Do you a little favour so to speak."
Zh: “街上有消息说你的记忆有一点……模糊。”他怀疑地看着你。“如果是真的，也许我能帮到你？比如说，帮你一点小忙。”

En: My memory is fine. Don't worry about it.
Zh: “我的记忆很好。不用担心。”

En: It's true. My memory *is* a bit hazy.
Zh: “是真的。我的记忆*确实*有点模糊。”

En: So good to hear that, Harry. Apparently my sources were wrong. He pats the brown folder again.
Zh: “很高兴听你这么说，哈里。显然我的消息来源错了。”他又拍了拍面前的棕色文件夹。

En: I assure you, there's nothing to be ashamed of, Harry. You're among friends and the good news is... He taps on the folder again.
Zh: “我向你保证，没有什么好羞愧的，哈里。你是我的朋友，而且好消息是……”他拍了拍面前的文件夹。

En: However if you *would* have a spot of memory trouble -- well, let's just say a fat juicy folder could really fill in the blanks. He winks at you.
Zh: “不过，如果你*真的*遇到一点记忆问题的话——这么说吧，一大本厚厚的文件夹确实能帮助填补不少空白。”他朝你眨眨眼。

En: I guess word has already reached him. No matter, no harm done.
Zh: 我猜消息已经传到他耳朵里了。不要紧，反正也没造成什么伤害。

En: Words flow like a river of honey from his lips.
Zh: 言语仿佛一条甜蜜的河流，从他的嘴唇里流淌出来。

En: It might be a good idea to hide your *confusion*... I mean, see what his game is first.
Zh: 隐藏你的*困惑*可能是个好主意……我的意思是，先看看他在玩什么把戏。

En: My big fat folder is ready to serve. I'm sure you have a lot of questions -- maybe my fat folder and I can help you out? Do you a little favour of sorts?
Zh: “我的大厚文件夹随时待命。我肯定你有很多问题——也许这个厚文件夹和我能帮到你？比如说帮你点小忙？”

En: It's just a brown folder. You can't make out what's written on it.
Zh: 它只是个棕色的文件夹。你看不出里面写的是什么。

En: Mr. Kitsuragi, would you mind? His eyes never leave yours. "Harry has some questions he needs answers to."
Zh: “曷城先生，你介不介意？”他的眼睛从来没有离开过你。“哈里有些问题需要得到答案。”

En: That's what the hanged corpse called you -- Harry.
Zh: 那具吊着的尸体是这么叫你的——哈里。

En: Where did you get that folder?
Zh: “你从哪里弄到那个文件夹的？”

En: Let's hear it, Harry. He sinks deeper into his chair.
Zh: “说来听听，哈里。”他深深陷入到自己的椅子里。

En: Ah, this? He closes the folder. "My friends in your organization gave it to me, Harry."
Zh: “啊，这个？”他合上文件夹。“我在你们组织里有个朋友，他给我的，哈里。”

En: It's Harry... He glances at the folder. "Harry Du Bois."
Zh: “是哈里……”他瞥了一眼文件夹。“哈里·杜博阿。”

En: But of course, Harry. He nods. "Your precinct is the 41st and you live in Jamrock. You're a Jamrock boy. A long way from home, but that's okay."
Zh: “当然了，哈里。”他点点头。“你隶属41分局，家住加姆洛克。你是个加姆洛克人。虽然离家很远，不过也没什么。”

En: Family? Harry, you're *not* a family man. There's not one peep of family in here. Unless you *think* you're a family man? Do you strike yourself as a family man, Harry?
Zh: “家？哈里，你*不是*那种居家型男人。这里看不出任何家庭有关的信息。除非你*觉得*自己是个居家型男人？你觉得自己是个居家型男人吗，哈里？”

En: Of course, Harry, of course! Let's not linger on personal details and amnesia. You wanted something from me?
Zh: “当然了，哈里，当然！我们还是不要纠结个人信息和失忆症了。你有什么需要吗？”

En: Let's talk of other matters for a moment.
Zh: “我们聊聊别的事吧。”

En: Let's get this straight... what is my full name?
Zh: “我们直接点吧……我的全名叫什么？”

En: What kind of a cop does it say I am ?
Zh: “那上面说我是一个什么样的警察？”

En: Do you know anything about my family? Do I have a wife or kids or...?
Zh: “你知道我的家庭状况吗？我有没有妻子，或者是孩子……？”

En: Do you know where I live?
Zh: “你知道我住哪吗？”

En: This translates into: "Ha-ha, you guys are so corrupt."
Zh: 这就可以翻译成：“哈哈，你们这些家伙真是太腐败了。”

En: Mr. Kitsuragi, would you mind? His eyes never leave yours. "Me and Harry are talking about his lost identity right now."
Zh: “曷城先生，你介不介意？”他的眼睛从来没有离开过你。“我和哈里现在要聊聊他遗忘的身份问题。”

En: It's just a brown folder. You can't make out what's written on it.
Zh: 它只是个棕色的文件夹。你看不出里面写的是什么。

En: Of course, let us dispatch with the formalities. You call me Evrart, I call you Harry!
Zh: “当然了，我们先从礼节开始吧。你可以叫我艾弗拉特，我叫你哈里！”

En: Are you trying to tell me you've gotten hold of some of our documents? The lieutenant inspects Evrart over his spectacles.
Zh: “你是想告诉我，你已经掌握了我们的一些资料？”警督透过自己的眼镜观察着艾弗拉特。

En: It's true. My memory is a bit hazy.
Zh: “是真的。我的记忆是有点模糊。”

En: My god so it's true! I didn't want to believe it, but you *are* a fantastic science-fiction amnesiac cop, aren't you? He shrugs with an amazed expression. "What are the odds of that?"
Zh: “我的天啦，居然是真的！我不想相信，不过你*确实*是科幻小说里那种患了失忆症的神奇警察，对吗？”他耸耸肩，表情很是惊讶。“这种几率能有多少？”

En: So that's really my name?
Zh: “所以那个真是我的名字吗？”

En: Is that my name?
Zh: “那是我的名字吗？”

En: So good to hear that, Harry. Apparently my sources were wrong. He pats the brown folder in front of him. "However if you *did* have a spot of memory trouble I could help you out -- with my big fat folder."
Zh: “很高兴听你这么说，哈里。显然我的消息来源错了。”他拍了拍面前的棕色文件夹。“不过，如果你*真的*遇到了一点记忆问题，我可以帮你——用我这本厚厚的大文件夹。”

En: My memory is fine -- I'm just testing you.
Zh: “我的记忆没问题——我只是在测试你。”

En: I assure you, there's nothing to be ashamed of, Harry. You're among friends and the good news is... He taps on the folder in front of him.
Zh: “我向你保证，没有什么好羞愧的，哈里。你是我的朋友，而且好消息是……”他拍了拍面前的文件夹。

En: I'm sure you had some concerns you thought I might be able to address, he pauses to look down on a brown folder on his desk. "And you were probably right -- I can."
Zh: “我肯定你有一些担忧，而且你觉得我也许能帮忙解决，”他停下来，看了一眼桌子上的棕色文件夹。“你也许是对的——我确实可以。”

En: I guess word has already reached him. No matter, no harm done.
Zh: 我想他已经知道了。不要紧，反正也没造成什么伤害。

En: It might be a good idea to hide your *confusion*... I mean, see what his game is first.
Zh: 隐藏你的*困惑*可能是个好主意……我的意思是，先看看他在玩什么把戏。

En: Don't trust him. For all you know *Du Bois* might be his name. You need to confirm this.
Zh: 不要相信他。你现在只知道*杜博阿*可能是你的名字。你需要确认这一点。

En: Have another look at that folder.
Zh: 再看一眼那个文件夹。

En: Kim suspects something.
Zh: 金在怀疑一些事情。

En: Read shipping folders before.
Zh: 之前看过航运文件。

En: Words flow like a river of honey from his lips.
Zh: 言语仿佛一条甜蜜的河流，从他的嘴唇里流淌出来。

En: You *must* have questions, Harry. C'mon on now... He makes a come-hither motion. "Don't be shy. Let's hear them."
Zh: “你*肯定*有些疑问，哈里。来吧……”他做了一个勾引的动作。“别害羞。说来听听。”

En: Asking too many questions will make you look weak. You should maybe focus on the folder.
Zh: 问太多问题会让你显得很脆弱。也许你应该集中在文件夹上。

En: You feel like a *Du Bois*, but you don't feel like a *Harry*. Strange.
Zh: 你感觉自己可能是*杜博阿*，不过*哈里*感觉又不太像。奇怪。

En: You don't feel like either one of these things -- you know what your name is. You have a sophisticated name. Like that of a count. Or a beautiful man.
Zh: 你感觉这些都不太对——你知道自己的名字叫什么。你的名字很精致又世故。就像一个伯爵。或者是一个美丽的人。

En: You don't feel like any of these things -- you know what your name is. You have a sophisticated name. Like that of a count. Or a beautiful man.
Zh: 你感觉这些都不太对——你知道自己的名字叫什么。你的名字很精致又世故。就像一个伯爵。或者是一个美丽的人。

En: Wait, I thought *you* are Harry Du Bois.
Zh: “等等，我以为*你*是哈里·杜博阿。”

En: That's not my name, my name is Raphael Costeau.
Zh: “那不是我的名字，我叫拉斐尔·库斯托。”

En: I am Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我叫龙舌兰日落。”

En: I feel like a Du Bois, but not quite like a Harry. Something longer.
Zh: “我觉得杜博阿是的，不过哈里不太像。应该更长一点。”

En: I don't really like it.
Zh: “我真的不太喜欢。”

En: Okay, I like it. I can work with that.
Zh: “好吧，我喜欢它，可以接受。”

En: Don't be silly, Harry. He smiles broadly. "Harry Du Bois -- That's a real man's name. A Union man's."
Zh: “别傻了，哈里。”他笑的更欢了。“哈里·杜博阿——这是一个真男人的名字。一个工会男人的名字。”

En: And *I* can work with you, Harry! He raises his index finger. "Now, what else can I do for you?"
Zh: “而且*我*也可以接受跟你一起工作，哈里！”他竖起食指。“现在，还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: Sure, okay. You're Harold. Harman. Haroldemus. But that's not what the record says. The record says 'Harry Du Bois'. He taps on the folder. "A real *man's* name. "
Zh: “当然了，好吧。你是哈罗德。哈曼。哈罗德蒙斯。不过记录里不是这么写的。记录里写的是‘哈里·杜博阿’。”他拍拍文件夹。“一个*真男人*的名字。”

En: Excellent! I know you RCM people like your call signs. Harry 'Tequila Sunset' Du Bois -- I can see that. But to me -- and the census bureau -- you will always be little Harry.
Zh: “太棒了！我知道你们RCM喜欢叫外号。哈里‘龙舌兰日落’杜博阿——我能看出来。不过在我这里——还有人口统计局那里——你永远都会是小哈里。”

En: Fine, Harry. He waves you off. "You can even be Harry Raphael Du Bois De Costeau -- or whatever you *choose* to be."
Zh: “好吧，哈里。”他朝你摆摆手。“你甚至可以叫哈里·拉斐尔·杜博阿·库斯托——或者随便你*选择*什么名字。”

En: No. I'm really, really not. *You* are, Harry.
Zh: “不。我真的，真的不是。*你*才是，哈里。”

En: The lieutenant covers his face and sighs audibly.
Zh: 警督捂住脸，清晰地叹了一口气。

En: Mr. Kitsuragi doesn't seem even slightly empathetic to your memory loss, Harry. He frowns at the lieutenant. "I, however, wish to help you any way I can."
Zh: “曷城先生好像一点都不同情你失忆的事，哈里。”他朝警督皱起了眉头。“不过，我，很乐意尽我所能的去帮助你。”

En: He doesn't really seem to know any more about it.
Zh: 他好像真的不知道了。

En: Whether you're a family man or not is unimportant right now, officer. We are here for a different reason. In fact, we should probably move on to the murder.
Zh: “不管你是不是个居家型男人，现在都不重要，警官。我们来这里是为了别的理由。事实上，也许我们应该继续讨论凶杀案的事情。”

En: You're right, I don't.
Zh: “你说得对，我不是。”

En: I think I do. I'd be a wonderful father.
Zh: “我想我是。我肯定会是个很棒的父亲。”

En: Never mind the family.
Zh: “家庭不重要。”

En: Family, Harry... He glances lovingly at the framed photo in front of him. "... is the most important thing in the world."
Zh: “家庭，哈里……”他深情地瞥了一眼面前的相框。“……是这个世界上最重要的东西。”

En: That's why I like you, Harry! His fat finger is pointing at you. "A good man knows both his strengths and his weaknesses and you my friend... you are one of the *all time greats*."
Zh: “这就是我喜欢你的原因，哈里！”他用肥胖的手指指向了你。“一个好男人应该明白自己的优点和缺点，而你，我的朋友……你是*有史以来最伟大的*那一个。”

En: Well... yes. He looks around, uninterested. "I'm sure you're gonna make one little boy or a girl very happy and proud one day, Harry."
Zh: “啊……是的。”他冷漠地看了看周围。“我肯定总有一天，你会给一个小男孩或者小女孩带去很多欢乐和骄傲，哈里。”

En: I find that very suspicious. May I have a look?
Zh: “我觉得那个有点可疑。能看一眼吗？”

En: I'm afraid this is meant for Union eyes only, Mr. Kitsuragi. I'm sure you understand. He turns back to you. "Please continue, Harry."
Zh: “恐怕只有工会的人能看，曷城先生。我肯定你能理解。”他又转向了你。“请继续吧，哈里。”

En: As you look at the folder, Evrart covers it with his hand and pets it. He's hiding it from you, because it's not a real RCM folder. It's just another one of those brown folders you saw in the file cabinet.\n
Zh: 当你看向文件夹，艾弗拉特立即把手盖在上面，轻轻抚摸着。他不想让你看见，因为它并不是真正的RCM文件夹。只是你在他的文件柜里看见的普通棕色文件夹。

En: As you look at the folder, Evrart covers it with his hand and pets it. Is he trying to hide that it's not a real RCM folder? It certainly doesn't have the RCM stamp on it.
Zh: 当你看向文件夹，艾弗拉特立即把手盖在上面，轻轻抚摸着。他是想掩饰这不是真的RCM文件夹吗？那上面肯定没有RCM的印章。

En: Evrart's large hands are covering the folder, but the look on his face says *I know everything about you, Harry*.
Zh: 艾弗拉特的大手盖在文件夹上，不过他脸上的表情写着*我知道你的一切，哈里*。

En: That's not an RCM folder.
Zh: “那个不是RCM的文件夹。”

En: Okay, Harry, you got me, he says, grinning. "This is from the Census Bureau, not the RCM. Those Census Bureau people are absolutely *corrupt*. You should do something about them."
Zh: “好吧，哈里，被你抓到了，”他笑着说道。“这是人口统计局的，不是RCM的。人口统计局那些家伙当然是*腐败*的。你应该对他们做点什么。”

En: He got the name from the Census Bureau and everything else from your actions here in Martinaise.
Zh: “他从人口统计局知道这个名字的，其他一切都是从你在马丁内斯的行动得知的。”

En: He got the name from the Census Bureau and everything else from your behaviour here in Martinaise.
Zh: 他从人口统计局知道这个名字的，其他一切都是从您在马丁内斯的行动得知的。

En: Yes, yes, Mr. Kitsuragi, from the Census Bureau, like I said. He looks annoyed. "Now I'm actually a very busy man, so is there anything else I can do for you, Harry?"
Zh: “是的，没错，曷城先生，就像我说的，是从人口统计局拿到的。”他看起来有些恼火。“其实我是个公务繁忙的人，所以我还能为你做些什么呢，哈里？”

En: But I really got you good there for a moment, didn't I, Harry? He laughs like he just pulled off a successful prank. "Now what can I do for you?"
Zh: “不过我刚才是不是真的唬住你了，哈里？”他笑的就像自己刚刚完成了一起成功的恶作剧。“现在，有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: He knows everything about me.
Zh: “他知道我的一切信息。”

En: I've got nothing to hide.
Zh: “我没什么好隐藏的。”

En: I'm sure it's not that bad, the lieutenant whispers. "At worst he has an old RCM folder, and I very much doubt even that."
Zh: “我肯定并没有那么糟糕，”警督低语到。“最坏的可能就是他拿到了RCM的旧档案，不过连这一点我都很怀疑。”

En: Harry... he says gently. "I just know the things that matter to our friendship. And I want you to feel free to ask me about those things."
Zh: “哈里……”他柔声说道。“我只知道关乎我们彼此友谊的信息。而且我希望你可以自由地问我那些事。”

En: Oh Harry... He peeks into the folder -- as if it's hard to turn away from the mysteries inside. "Oh wow... this is really something."
Zh: “哦，哈里……”他偷瞄了一眼文件夹——就好像很难把注意力从里面的秘密上移开。“哇哦……这还真是很不得了呢。”

En: I'm sure it's not that bad, the lieutenant whispers. "At worst he has an old RCM folder, and I very much doubt even that."
Zh: “我肯定并没有那么糟糕，”警督低语到。“最坏的可能就是他拿到了RCM的旧文件夹，不过连这一点我都很怀疑。”

En: So how about it, Harry? He leans back in the chair. "You need assistance, I presume."
Zh: “所以你觉得怎么样，哈里？”他向后靠在椅子上。“我推测，你需要帮助。”

En: It says... He looks in the folder. "Oh yes... very interesting. It says you're more like a mad prophet than a cop. Always rambling about the end of the world... I'm sure these stories are exaggerated."
Zh: “上面说……”他看了一眼文件夹。“哎呀……真的很有趣。上面说你更像是个疯狂的预言家，而不像是个警察。总是嘀咕着世界末日什么的……我肯定这些故事有些夸张了。”

En: Well, Harry...  He turns a couple of pages in the folder. "If I were to sum you up in one word, it would be *apologetic*."
Zh: “这个嘛，哈里……”他在文件夹里翻了几页。“如果用一个词来概括的话，那就是*谦卑*。”

En: Oh Harry... He opens the folder and leafs through its contents. "You are the most regular, traditional, by-the-book law official imaginable."
Zh: “哦，哈里……”他打开文件夹，迅速扫了一眼上面的内容。“你是所有人能想象到的最普通、最传统、最喜欢照章办事的执法人员。”

En: Harry, you're not simply a cop, you're a star! A bright shining star in the drab law enforcement sky, outshining all other stars. You're a *super* star.
Zh: “哈里，你不是一个简单的警察，你是个明星！一颗在灰暗单调的执法天空里闪耀的明星，远比其他星星更加闪亮。你是一个*超级*明星。”

En: Well, Harry... He opens the folder and leafs through its contents. "It looks like you like to party -- hard. And you like disco music. I guess you're a disco-cop."
Zh: “哦，哈里……”他打开文件夹，迅速扫了一眼上面的内容。“看来你很喜欢派对狂欢——而且很疯狂。而且你还喜欢迪斯科音乐。我猜你是个迪斯科警察。”

En: Exaggerated?! Evrart, the time is now! We are looking at a *catastrophic global* loss of life.
Zh: “夸张？！艾弗拉特，就是现在！我们正面临着*灾难性的全球*生命损失。”

En: Very much exaggerated. I'm not a mad prophet. I'm just a cop.
Zh: “确实很夸张。我不是一个疯狂的预言家。我只是个警察。”

En: What? I'm not apologetic. I'm confident.
Zh: “什么？我没有谦卑。我很自信。”

En: I'm... sorry?
Zh: “我……很谦卑？”

En: Hey, I'm not *that* ordinary!
Zh: “喂，我才没有*那么*普通！”

En: I just want to do my job, that's all.
Zh: “我只想做好我的工作，就这样。”

En: No-no-no-no... NO. I'm definitely not a star.
Zh: “不不不……不对。我肯定不是个明星。”

En: That's what I like about you, Evrart. You get me.
Zh: “我就是喜欢你这一点，艾弗拉特。你懂我。”

En: I'm planning on changing a lot of things about myself.
Zh: “我打算改变自己身上的很多东西。”

En: I *do* like me some disco.
Zh: “我*确实*喜欢迪斯科。”

En: Of course, Harry. He nods along. "Sin, blood, death, catastrophic loss of life... all those things. And that's why you and I must be prepared, so let's talk about how we can help each other."
Zh: “当然了，哈里。”他点点头。“罪恶，鲜血，死亡，灾难性的生命损失……所有那些事情。这就是我和你为什么要准备万全的原因，所以我们来聊聊怎么互相帮助的事吧。”

En: I know that, Harry. He smiles broadly. "I *know* you. And rest assured, your secret is safe with me."
Zh: “我知道的，哈里。”他笑的很灿烂。“我*知道*你。而且你放心，你的秘密在我这里很安全。”

En: He's probably just making a guess based on your recent activity in Martinaise.
Zh: 他可能只是基于你最近在马丁内斯的行为做出了猜测。

En: Well you sure come off as very confident in all our interactions, Harry. He chuckles. "You're a real man's man."
Zh: “呃，在我们的所有互动中，你确实表现的很有自信，哈里。”他轻笑着。“你是条真汉子。”

En: Yes. He inspects you over his giant bug-eyed glasses. "You seem to be. A lot of the time. But right now there's no reason to be. Let loose a little. Be *you*."
Zh: “是的。”他透过把眼睛放得很大的镜片观察着你。“你好像是。大部分时候是。不过现在没理由这样。我们放松一点。做回*你自己*。”

En: Of course you're not, Harry, he exclaims. "You're my special boy! Big friend of the Union. This is *very* non-ordinary."
Zh: “你当然不是了，哈里，”他感叹到。“你是最特别的！工会的好朋友。这是*非常*不普通的。”

En: As do we all, Harry. He nods, a sympathetic look in his eyes. "We're all just trying to do our best, aren't we? I'm gonna help you do yours."
Zh: “我们都一样，哈里。”他点点头，眼里有种惺惺相惜的感觉。“我们全都在拼尽全力，不是吗？我也会全力帮你的。”

En: Nonsense, Harry. He shakes his head. "You lit up this container, when you walked in here. You're my golden boy."
Zh: “胡说，哈里。”他摇摇头。“当你走进这里来的时候，你点燃了整个集装箱。你是我的黄金小子。”

En: Of course I do, Harry. He smiles a sly smile. "And I'm gonna help you shine. I'm gonna put you on all the big stages. Your name in giant neon letters: 'HARRY DU BOIS.'"
Zh: “我当然是了，哈里。”他狡猾地笑了。“而且我会帮你大放异彩。我要让你登上所有大舞台。你的名字会以巨大的霓虹字母显示出来：‘哈里·杜博阿’。”

En: I'm going to stand 100% behind you through all these changes, Harry. He reaches his arms out. "You need to understand that all I want to do is to help you."
Zh: “在你的改变过程中，我会100%站在你身后的，哈里。”他伸出自己的胳膊。“你需要理解，我唯一想做的就是帮助你。”

En: Harry, sometimes I think we're so similar it frightens me! He holds his giant head between his hands. "I too love... regular, normal music!"
Zh: “哈里，有时候我觉得我们真的太相似，甚至让我觉得有些可怕！”他用手抱住自己的大脑袋。“我也喜欢……普通，寻常的音乐！”

En: Hey, I'm not that regular, check out my tie.
Zh: “喂，我才没那么普通，看看我的领带。”

En: Now that is *funky*, Harry. He rubs his big hands together. "You are a colourful fellow, not a boring stiff like this deceitful folder here implies."
Zh: “这就有点*时髦*了，哈里。”他摩擦着两只大手。“你是一个鲜艳生动的人，不像这个虚伪的文件夹里暗示的——一个很无趣的人。”

En: We are charming his pants off, *bratan*! Great job!
Zh: 我们的魅力把他的裤子都要吓掉了，*兄弟*！干得好！

En: I think the odds of that... He looks at you. "Are very low."
Zh: “我觉得那个几率……”他看着你。“非常非常低。”

En: I have a big fat folder on you, Harry. I'm sure you have a lot of questions to ask -- maybe I can help you out?
Zh: “我这里有个厚厚的大文件夹，都是关于你的，哈里。我肯定你有很多问题想问——也许我能帮到你？”

En: No, no. Don't interrupt him. He looks the lieutenant with reproach. "Me and Harry are having a moment here. Go on, Harry."
Zh: “不不。别打断他。”他用责备的眼神看着警督。“我和哈里正聊的很开心呢。你继续，哈里。”

En: The giant neon sign reading "HARRY DU BOIS" hanging from the Kvalsund crane can be seen all the way to Jamrock.
Zh: 写着“哈里·杜博阿”的巨大霓虹灯悬挂在库瓦桑德起重机上，从这里到加姆洛克一路都能看见。

En: Don't just jump to the folder. That's not smart, shows you're on the edge. Do some probing first.
Zh: 先不要直接跳到文件夹的话题。这样不太明智，会显示出您的坐立不安。先仔细观察一下。

En: I'm very glad to hear it. Some of my associates here in Martinaise told me you had trouble remembering things -- I am relieved they were mistaken.
Zh: “听你这么说我很高兴。我在马丁内斯的一些同事告诉我说你的记忆出了点问题——我很庆幸是他们搞错了。”

En: I'm sure you had some concerns you thought I might be able to address. He pauses to look at a brown folder on his desk. "And you were probably right."
Zh: “我肯定你有一些担忧，而且你觉得我也许能帮忙解决，”他停下来，看了一眼桌子上的棕色文件夹。“而且你也许是对的。”

En: I guess the stress of peace-keeping and order-maintaining can get even to the best of us. He scratches his earlobe.
Zh: “我猜维护和平和维持秩序的压力让我们都有些喘不过气来。”他抓了抓耳垂。

En: Don't be too hard on yourself, officer. And if there's *anything* I can help you with, please don't hesitate to ask.
Zh: “别对自己太苛刻了，警官。如果有*什么*我可以帮你的话，请毫不犹豫地问吧。”

En: So Harry, what can I help you with today?
Zh: “所以，哈里，今天有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: For a fraction of a second the big man looks at you inquisitively. "Experiencing partial amnesia, are we, Harry?"
Zh: 有那么一眨眼的功夫，大块头男人好奇地打量着你。“经历了部分失忆的状况，是吗，哈里？”

En: It might be a good idea to hide your confusion, see what his game is first.
Zh: 隐藏你的*困惑*可能是个好主意……我的意思是，先看看他在玩什么把戏。

En: To be honest, my memory is a bit hazy right now, yes.
Zh: “老实说，现在我的记忆是有一点模糊，没错。”

En: Nah, my memory is fine -- I'm just testing you.
Zh: “不，我的记忆没问题——我只是在测试你。”

En: Fine by me.
Zh: “我没什么问题。”

En: Of course, let us dispatch with the formalities, you call me Evrart, I call you Harry!
Zh: “当然了，我们先从礼节开始吧。你可以叫我艾弗拉特，我叫你哈里！”

En: Is that my name?
Zh: “那是我的名字吗？”

En: The huge block letters on the folder read "HARRIER DU BOIS". There's something in small writing just above that...
Zh: 文件夹封面用巨大的大写字母写着“哈里尔·杜博阿”几个字。在那上面还有些小字……

En: I see you have gotten hold of some of our old documents. The lieutenant turns to Evrart. "They should have been destroyed some time ago."
Zh: “我看你弄到了一些我们之前的旧文件。”警督转向艾弗拉特。“它们早就该销毁了。”

En: Mr. Kitsuragi... He leaves the rest of this sentence hanging in the air and lets his smug smile deliver the rest of the message.
Zh: “曷城先生……”他把后半句话吊在半空中，让他那副洋洋得意的笑脸传达了剩下的信息。

En: Don't you worry, I've got plenty on you too, my rigid friend.
Zh: “不用担心，我这里也有很多你的信息，这位死板的朋友。”

En: Do *not* trust him. For all you know, it might be *his* name by which he is addressing you. You need confirmation.
Zh: *不要*相信他。据你所知，他称呼你的也许是*他自己*的名字。你需要确认。

En: Revachol Citizens Militia.
Zh: “瑞瓦肖公民武装。”

En: Another question.
Zh: “还有个问题。”

En: Absolutely Harry, absolutely. Sock it to me.
Zh: “当然可以，哈里，当然可以。说吧。”

En: There is no way to sway this man in any direction. He is un-suggestable and un-swayable. Just tell the truth.
Zh: 根本没有办法让这个男人动摇。他的耳根子不软，也不会摇摆不定。直接告诉他真相吧。

En: I did go inside. He had the glorious flag of *Revachol the Suzerain* on his wall.
Zh: “我确实进去了。他在墙上挂了*瑞瓦肖宗主国*的光荣旗帜。”

En: The *weasel* is no true patriot, Harry. He shakes his head. "He prays every night for the downfall of the Union -- and spits upon the name of the king."
Zh: “那只*黄鼠狼*不是真正的爱国者，哈里。”他摇摇头。“他每晚都在祈求工会垮台——而且还侮辱国王的名字。”

En: Oddly, it seems to be true.
Zh: 奇怪的是，这好像是真的。

En: But he *does* live nearby... maybe it's a pedantic weasel? Fascists are neat freaks, if you don't mind me saying so. I feel like a real detective right now, Harry! Am I getting this right? He imitates bashing something with an imaginary baton.
Zh: “不过他*的确*住在附近……也许它是一只迂腐的黄鼠狼？法西斯主义者都有洁癖，如果你不介意我这么说的话。现在我感觉就像是个真正的警探了，哈里！我说得对吗？”他用想象出来的警棍模仿着击打的动作。

En: With a police baton. Like a crook or someone.
Zh: 带着一只警棍。像个坏蛋或者什么的。

En: That's all he's going to say on this subject.
Zh: 关于这个话题，他只会说这么多了。

En: He performs a motion, as if spraying bullets from a machine gun.
Zh: 他做了一个动作，就好像用机关枪在扫射子弹。

En: Those Señorita Pineapple people are scary motherfuckers, decimating your state if you don't give them your pineapples.
Zh: 菠萝小姐公司的人都是些可怕的混球，如果你不给他们菠萝的话，他们就会屠杀你的国家。

En: An entire neighbourhood of... killers.
Zh: 一整个街区都是……杀手。

En: If he's just boasting then it sure doesn't feel like that to you. He's not worried.
Zh: 如果他只是在吹牛，那你肯定不会有这种感觉。他并不担心。

En: He places a lot of faith in that *lawyergirl*. Perhaps this is a tactical error? Anyway.
Zh: 他对这个*女律师*很有信心。也许这是个策略上的错误？管它呢。

En: How odd. The man shrugs. "I don't know what to say, lieutenant. They told me they hanged him. A hanged man is what I saw when I took a look into that yard..."
Zh: “真奇怪。”男人耸耸肩。“我不知道该说什么，警督。他们告诉我说把他吊死了。我往院子里看的时候，就只看到了一个吊人……”

En: It's impossible to say if he's telling the truth, sire.
Zh: 大人，很难说他说的到底是不是实话。

En: That does sound quite unlikely, yes. The big guy leading the scabs at the gates is *colossal*.
Zh: 确实听起来不太可能，没错。大门边的那个工贼头目真的很*魁梧*。

En: He's not scared of them. If anything, he *likes* them being here. Strange...
Zh: 他不害怕那些他们。如果有什么的话，他*喜欢*他们在这里。奇怪……

En: (Better not to mention it.)
Zh: （最好别提。）

En: Oh... He smiles -- it's another big grin. "And she also really likes *gardening* I hear. In March. Strange. Anyway, let's move on."
Zh: “哦……”他笑了——又是特别开心。“而且我听说她真的很喜欢*园艺*。在三月份搞园艺。挺奇怪的。不管怎么说，我们还是继续吧。”

En: Don't give him the pleasure.
Zh: 别让他高兴。

En: Better not to.
Zh: 最好不要。

En: Or what -- he'll give you info? Fine, don't ask it then.
Zh: 或者什么——他会给你信息？好吧，那就别问了。

En: That's him *allowing* the police to ask his men questions.
Zh: 那是他*允许*警察找他的手下问问题。

En: He's betting on them being useless to you.
Zh: 他打赌他们对你没用。

En: Needless to say, this is another *move*. Don't take it.
Zh: 不用说，这又是一个*动作*。不要搭理。

En: How many micro-bribes would this guy take?
Zh: 这家伙到底拿了多少小赃物？

En: A man of the left? So you have to be a social democrat?
Zh: 左派？所以你必须是个社会民主主义者了？

En: He's been *hurt* too much in the past -- by men who aren't *social democrats*.
Zh: 他过去被*伤害*地太深了——被那些不是*社会民主主义者*的人。

En: Kingsman? Yes! Please, let's be that.
Zh: 战士？好耶！来吧，我们就当那个。

En: But, he thinks, it's your call.
Zh: 但是，他想到，这是你的决定。

En: Rollerskating -- not drugs, Harry. You like this.
Zh: 旱冰鞋——不是毒品，哈里。你喜欢这个。

En: Water drips from the eaves. A woman looks at her freshly tarred skiff. There's a pair of cavalry boots under the fish in the box and the wind howls like a vicious spirit...
Zh: 水从屋檐上哗哗流下。一个女人看着自己刚刷过焦油的小艇。盒子里的鱼下面有一双骑兵靴，风像邪恶的精灵一样在咆哮……

En: You're already pretty deep into this. What's a little more? No one can see you here in Martinaise...
Zh: 你已经深陷其中了。再多深一些又能怎么样？反正也没人看见你在马丁内斯……

En: You can now go and tell Titus about this. See what he has to say.
Zh: 现在你可以去找提图斯，把这件事告诉他。看看他有什么好说的。

En: No, no, Evrart. I really did open the door.
Zh: “不，不，艾弗拉特。我真的把门打开了。”

En: Failed to hide your panic
Zh: 没能隐藏你的恐慌

En: Hasn't even taken the key from Mañana.
Zh: 甚至没从马列拉那里拿钥匙。

En: Took the key from Mañana.
Zh: 从马列拉那里拿到钥匙

En: You're right, I was just testing you.
Zh: “你说得对，我只是在试探你。”

En: Actually, I need to get back to you on this door thing.
Zh: “其实，门的那件事我要跟你回复一下。”

En: Of course, Harry! He exclaims. "What are friends for if not for measuring the falsehood one can pass in the disguise of truth."
Zh: “当然了，哈里！”他大喊到。“如果不是为了衡量那些隐藏在事实之下谎言，那朋友还有什么用呢？”

En: Oh, Harry... He shakes his head, smiling. "You don't need to get back to me. You need to open the door. Very simple, really."
Zh: “哦，哈里……”他笑着摇摇头。“你不需要向我回报。你需要把门打开。挺简单的，真的。”

En: Good, now take it a bit further. Take it home.
Zh: 很好，现在再进一步。好好考虑该怎么说。

En: You didn't say it with nearly enough confidence.
Zh: 您说的不够自信。

En: And not only did I open it, I went inside too. It was a real weasel's den, Evrart.
Zh: “我不仅是打开了，我还进去了。那里真是个黄鼠狼窝，艾弗拉特。”

En: I bet it was, Harry. He says with a grimace -- then the smile dissipates. "But seriously, what did you see in his apartment?"
Zh: “我敢打赌它就是，哈里。”他扮了个鬼脸——然后笑容消失了。“不过说真的，你在公寓里看到了什么？”

En: A guy who has antagonized the Union in a Union-run town? Maybe it's political?
Zh: 一个在工会掌控的城市里跟工会对抗的人？也许这跟政治有关？

En: I saw lot of anti-socialist, anti-union stuff. Pamphlets saying: *Get the unions in check*.
Zh: “我看见很多反工人主义，反工会的东西。还有小册子上写着：*约束工会*。”

En: (Shake your head.) "Fascist insignia everywhere. Memorabilia calling for the *Return to the Golden Age*."
Zh: （摇摇头。）“到处都是法西斯主义的标志。还有呼唤*重回黄金年代*的纪念品。”

En: Misogynist posters, racist literature and a signed petition to 'euthanise the invalids'.
Zh: “厌女海报，种族主义文献，还有一份‘让残疾人安乐死’的签名请愿书。”

En: That's exactly what I thought, Harry! He slams his fist on the table. "What a weasel... And for the record, I was only curious, not testing you."
Zh: “跟我想得几乎一模一样，哈里！”他用拳头砸向桌面。“好一只黄鼠狼……还有郑重声明一下，我只是好奇，并不是在试探你。”

En: Thank you, Harry. You have shown me that the Débardeurs' Union and the Citizens Militia can indeed work together. He nods solemnly. "Now, let's cut down to brass tacks."
Zh: “谢谢你，哈里。你让我看到了装卸工会和公民武装的确可以共同协作。”他严肃地点点头。“现在，让我们切入正题吧。”

En: He was testing you. You succeeded.
Zh: 他在试探您。您成功了。

En: Be it far from me to ever question your integrity, Harry, but you must have opened the wrong door, he says with a wry smile. "Next time, please be certain you get the right one."
Zh: “我绝对不会怀疑你的正直，哈里，但你肯定是开错门了，”他笑着揶揄到。“下一次，请务必保证找到正确的那一扇。”

En: You know, what, Harry? He points both index fingers at you. "I got a feeling they're gonna show up in full force *tomorrow*."
Zh: “你知道吗，哈里？”他把两只食指都指向了你。“我有一种感觉，他们*明天*就会全力出动。”

En: He's trying to figure out if you're lying.
Zh: 他是想弄明白你有没有撒谎。

En: I *may have* gone inside and seen a collection of racist mugs.
Zh: “我*可能*进去过，然后看到了一组种族歧视的马克杯。”

En: I did go inside. Weasel had the flag of the Old Revachol on his wall.
Zh: “我确实进去了。黄鼠狼在墙上挂了旧瑞瓦肖的旗帜。”

En: The deal wasn't for me to go inside, so I didn't.
Zh: “这个委托并没有让我进去，所以我没有。”

En: Exactly the kind of fascist memorabilia I was expecting. He shakes his head. "Weasel probably prays to it every night for the downfall of the Union."
Zh: “就是我期待的那种法西斯主义纪念品。”他摇摇头。“黄鼠狼可能每晚都在向它祈求工会快点垮台。”

En: Just as I thought. Culturally antiquated mug collection. What a weasel... He shakes his head. "*Pissing* on Evrart's Rainbow Coalition."
Zh: “跟我想的一样。从文化上来看已经过时的马克杯藏品。真是只黄鼠狼……”他摇摇头。“*在艾弗拉特的彩虹联盟上*撒尿*。”

En: You're right, Harry. You only had to unlock the door. He gives you a clever look. "Which you did, so we're all good here."
Zh: “你说得对，哈里。你确实只是打开了门。”他露出一副机灵的样子。“你做到了，所以我们现在都很好。”

En: He *was* testing you. And you succeeded.
Zh: 他*确实*是在试探您。而您成功了。

En: Now let's get down to brass tacks. It's time for men like me and you to figure out who's killed who and why. He pretends to roll up his sleeves. "Real police work is gonna start happening now. I promise you, Harry, this is gonna be good."
Zh: “现在我们切入正题吧。是时候让你和我这样的人来查清楚到底是谁杀了那个人，而且为什么要这么做。”他假装卷起袖子。“现在要开始真正的警察工作了。我向你保证，哈里，一定会很不错的。”

En: I really went in there. Whose place is it? I just need to know.
Zh: “我真的进去过了。那个地方是谁的？我只需要知道这个。”

En: The mug collection I mentioned was in the apartment... I found a similar mug in the trash with the hanged man's clothes.
Zh: “我提到过在公寓里发现一些马克杯藏品……跟我在垃圾箱里找到的一模一样，而且吊人的衣服也是在那里发现的。”

En: There was a collection of colonial mugs there, and I found a similar mug in the trash with the hanged man's clothes.
Zh: “那里有一系列殖民风格的马克杯，跟我在垃圾箱里找到的一个马克杯一模一样，而且吊人的衣服也是在那里发现的。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: What if it's connected to my case?
Zh: “如果它能我的案子有关呢？”

En: Okay, you're right. It's probably not important.
Zh: “好吧，你说得对。它可能并不重要。”

En: Believe me, he's not a killer. He's a nobody. Just a basement-variety armchair fascist, who comes up with needlessly complex conspiracy theories.
Zh: “相信我，他不是个杀人犯。他谁也不是。只是一个缩在地下室扶手椅里的法西斯主义者，提出了一些没用又复杂的阴谋理论。”

En: What if one of those *complex* theories is right?
Zh: 如果有一条*复杂的*理论是对的呢？

En: Did one of his *complex* theories turn out to be true?
Zh: “有没有哪条*复杂的*理论最后证明是对的呢？

En: Racist mugs in the trash AND in the apartment?! He grabs his head with both hands. "You guys are just light-years ahead of me."
Zh: “垃圾堆和公寓里有种族歧视马克杯？！”他双手抱头。“你们这些家伙已经领先我几光年了。”

En: I have *so much* confidence in the ability of your organization. I'm relieved you're doing this and leaving me to do what *I* do best -- helping people. With the power of *politics*.
Zh: “我对你的组织能力拥有*很大的*信心。你这么做让我很宽慰，让我能去做*自己*最擅长的事——帮助大家。用*政治*的力量。”

En: Yes-yes... Do you think this 'weasel' is somehow connected to the murder?
Zh: “是的……你觉得这个‘黄鼠狼’会不会跟凶杀案有什么联系呢？”

En: Did one of his theories turn out to be true?
Zh: “他有没有哪条理论最后证明是对的呢？

En: Do you think this weasel has something to do with my case?
Zh: “你觉得这只黄鼠狼跟我的案子有关系吗？”

En: Oh, no, no, no, no. I don't cross paths like that. He shakes his head, laughing. "All I want is for you to succeed in your investigation. I would never *complicate* things for you."
Zh: “哦，不不不不，我不喜欢那样横生枝节。”他摇摇头，笑了。“我只希望你的调查能够成功。我永远不会让你的事情*复杂化*。”

En: But this weasel might have cleaned up after the killers.
Zh: “但是这个黄鼠狼可能在凶杀之后清理过现场。”

En: Believe me, Harry, he's a nobody. Just your basement-variety nobody... Can't imagine him being connected to a high-calibre case like this.
Zh: “相信我，哈里，他什么人都不是。只是那种缩在地下室里的无名小卒……根本无法想象会跟这种高水平的案件有什么关联。”

En: But he *does* live nearby -- maybe it's a pedantic weasel? Fascists are known to be neat freaks... I feel like a real detective right now, Harry! Am I getting this right? He imitates bashing something with an imaginary baton.
Zh: “但是他*的确*住在附近——也许是一只迂腐的黄鼠狼？法西斯主义者就是因为洁癖而出名的……现在我感觉就像是个真正的警探了，哈里！我说得对吗？”他用想象出来的警棍模仿着击打的动作。

En: Great technique. You'd make a great sergeant.
Zh: “很棒的技巧。你会成为一个很棒的警长。”

En: There's more to police work than whacking an imaginary baton.
Zh: “警察工作要比挥舞一只想象出来的警棍要复杂多了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Of course, of course, Harry. I'm not a real police officer. *You* are!
Zh: “当然，当然了，哈里。我不是真正的警官。*你*才是！”

En: Of course, Harry. Stoic silence. I like that. Very befitting a police officer. He bows slightly in his chair. "I'm not a real detective. You are."
Zh: “当然了，哈里。斯多葛派般禁欲的沉默。我喜欢。非常适合一个警官。”他在椅子里微微鞠了一躬。“我不是真正的警官。你才是！”

En: Oh, you're too kind, Harry! Way too kind. He chuckles. "I know I'm not a real police officer. *You* are!"
Zh: “哦，你真是太善良了，哈里！太过善良。”他轻笑出声。“我知道我不是个真正的警官。*你*才是！”

En: Would he? I'm not so sure about it.
Zh: “他会吗？我就不那么确定了。”

En: What if it did? What if he took a whack in the dark? Started telling people the strike is funded by a protection racket that drove local shops out of business?
Zh: “如果就是呢？如果他在黑暗中挨了一下子呢？开始告诉别人说罢工是一个勒索保护费的组织资助的，为的就是赶走当地的店铺？”

En: What if he planned to write a hack-piece to the local newspaper regarding these crazy theories he had developed. Just to stir up trouble. Wouldn't that be annoying?
Zh: “如果他打算给本地报社写一篇关于自己那些疯狂理论的文章呢？只是为了制造麻烦。难道这还不够烦人的吗？”

En: I see.
Zh: “我明白了。”

En: That'd be awful if I intervened in something like that.
Zh: “如果我插手这种事，那才叫糟糕呢。”

En: That's not a big deal. Certainly not big enough to do something insidious.
Zh: “其实不是什么大事。肯定没有大到要做一些出阴险的事。”

En: That'd be cool. I live for shutting these journalist fucks down.
Zh: “那挺酷的啊。我活着就是为了让那些该死的记者闭嘴。”

En: Journalism, Harry -- I love it, you love it, and you know why? Because we've got nothing to hide. Let's keep it that way.
Zh: “新闻业，哈里——我喜欢它，你也喜欢它，你知道为什么吗？因为我们没什么好隐瞒的。所以就让它保持现状吧。”

En: Truly awful. He shakes his head. "It's great it didn't happen then. I personally love journalism. And I know you do too, Harry. That's because we've got nothing to hide, right?"
Zh: “真的很可怕。”他摇摇头。“很高兴它并没有发生。我个人很热爱新闻业。而且我知道你也一样，哈里。这是因为我们没有什么好隐瞒的，不是吗？”

En: Insidious? Would I ever ask you to do something insidious? Of course not! He shakes his head. "I personally love journalism. And I know you do too. That's because we've got nothing to hide, right?"
Zh: “阴险？我会让你去做阴险的事吗？当然不会！”他摇摇头。“我个人很热爱新闻业。而且我知道你也一样，哈里。这是因为我们没有什么好隐瞒的，不是吗？”

En: So funny, Harry. I, of course, love journalism. And I know secretly you do too. And you know why? Because we've got nothing to hide.
Zh: “真有意思，哈里，当然了，我很爱新闻业。而且我背地里知道你也一样，哈里。你知道为什么吗？这是因为我们没有什么好隐瞒的。”

En: Now please, let's get back to the good stuff, the police stuff, Harry! I just see myself as one of you guys. Think of me as a sergeant or something. He smiles broadly. "Let's *crack* this, Harry."
Zh: “现在，我们说点好事吧，警察的事，哈里！我把自己看成是你们的一员。觉得自己是个警长或者什么的。”他灿烂地笑了。“让我们*解决*这起案子吧，哈里。”

En: See, Harry? Many questions become irrelevant once we ask ourselves *why* we're even asking them. He winks at you. "But I'm sure you have plenty more where that came from."
Zh: “看见没，哈里？一旦我们询问自己*为什么*要问的时候，很多问题就会变得不想干了。”他朝你眨眨眼。“不过我很肯定，说到这里的话你肯定还会有更多问题。”

En: Harry, this strike is the culmination of many *many*, mistakes made by the Wild Pines Group. They tried to shut the strike down by sending in armed mercenaries.
Zh: “哈里，罢工就像野松公司犯下的很多、*很多*的错误达到了一个顶点。他们想通过派遣武装雇佣兵来制止罢工。”

En: Everything they did there, they brought over here. They want to turn Revachol into a Third World slum. Honestly, the only thing they didn't do, is kill the village elephant.
Zh: “他们做的一切，他们带给这里的一切。他们想把瑞瓦肖变成一个第三世界的贫民窟。老实说，他们唯一没做的就是杀掉村庄大象了。”

En: Wait, they move the container?
Zh: “等等，他们会移动集装箱的吗？”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: Yes! I'm an old man, Harry. My legs aren't what they used to be. They lift my office with that big crane. It's actually very fun, you should try it.
Zh: “是的！我是个老人家了，哈里。我的腿脚已经不像之前的样子了。他们用那台大起重机把我的办公室抬起来。其实还挺有意思的，你应该试一下。”

En: But enough about me and my fun container. His face turns serious. "The killers the company hired... I think there were three of them. All hardened commando-types."
Zh: “关于我和这个有趣的集装箱的事，已经说的差不多了。”他的面色变得严肃起来。“公司雇佣的那些杀手……我觉得一共有三个人。全是那种久经沙场的突击队员。”

En: Harry, he says, ignoring the lieutenant, "what you need to realize is -- we dockworkers are not pushovers."
Zh: “哈里，”他说道，完全忽视了警督的存在，“你需要意识到——我们码头工人可不是容易打败的对手。”

En: We got grit, Harry -- this whole neighbourhood does. Push us hard enough and we push back -- and when we do... He raises his finger. "We push to kill."
Zh: “我们有勇气，哈里——整个街区都有。如果把我们逼得够紧，我们就会反噬回去——而且，当我们这么做的时候……”他举起手指。“我们会被迫杀人。”

En: Who exactly did the *pushing*?
Zh: “那到底是谁在*被迫*杀人呢？”

En: Wait, the whole *neighbourhood* is in on it?
Zh: “等等，整个*街区*都参与其中了？”

En: Potentially, Harry, potentially. We got arm wrestling champions, rowing club people, ex-coal miners -- tough guys, all ready to spring into action for their home base.
Zh: “可能是吧，哈里，可能吧。我们有掰手腕冠军，划船俱乐部成员，前矿工——都是硬汉，随时准备为了自己的家园行动起来。”

En: Again: that sounds like organized crime.
Zh: 又来了：听起来很像有组织犯罪。

En: So these *idealists* killed our victim?
Zh: “所以这些*理想主义者*杀了我们的被害人？”

En: You believe they killed the hanged man?
Zh: “你相信是他们杀了吊人吗？”

En: Did they kill the hanged man?
Zh: “是他们杀了吊人吗？”

En: Aren't you worried we might arrest them for this?
Zh: “你不担心我们可能会逮捕他们吗？”

En: Are you not afraid that we're going to arrest them?
Zh: “你不害怕我们会逮捕他们吗？”

En: Oh, I'm not *at all* worried about that. These are not the kind of men who get arrested. They're Martinaise boys, tough and gritty. I'd like to see the man who takes them in. He chuckles. "Besides I sent my lawyergirl to look after them."
Zh: “哦，我*一点*也不担心。他们都不是会被逮捕的那种人。他们全都是马丁内斯汉子，强壮又坚韧。我倒是想看看谁能把他们逮捕起来。”他轻笑出声。“再说了，我派了手下的女律师去打点他们。”

En: Despite his boastful tone, he's having doubts about something -- but what?
Zh: 虽然他的口气十分自负，不过他确实对某些事心存疑问——是什么呢？

En: I think you're sharing information with the police, because you don't think they *actually* did it.
Zh: “我觉得你跟警方分享这些信息，是因为你觉得*其实*并不是他们做的。”

En: Okay. Another question.
Zh: “好吧。还有个问题。”

En: Certainly, Harry.
Zh: “当然可以，哈里。”

En: That's very clever, Harry. Yes. They might have said it just to impress some girls or something. He rubs his chin. "But Titus has a bad temper, so the chances are 50-50."
Zh: “你很聪明，哈里。没错。他们也许是为了哄女孩或者什么的这么说过。”他摸了摸下巴。“但是提图斯是个暴脾气，所以是他的可能性是55开。”

En: He thinks it's closer to 60-40. 60 they didn't do it.
Zh: 他觉得更接近于64开。60%不是他们做的。

En: Guess what? Not only was the hanging a cover-up -- it was orchestrated by a woman named Ruby.
Zh: “你猜怎么着？不仅绞刑是掩饰——这是一个叫露比的女人精心安排的。”

En: I've talked to the boys and it turns out the hanging was a cover-up. They were helping a woman named Klaasje.
Zh: “我跟小伙子们聊过了，结果把他吊起来只是为了掩护。他们是在帮忙一个名叫卡拉洁的女人。”

En: Of course, they just blew smoke up my ass... There's disappointment in his voice. "I guess they're not as tough as they say they are. Or there's more to this than meets the eye."
Zh: “当然，他们把烂摊子甩到我这里来了……”他的声音里透露出失望的情绪。“我猜他们并不像自己嘴里说的那么强悍。或者其实背地里还隐藏着更多东西。”

En: You're disappointed.
Zh: “你很失望。”

En: Do you know Ruby?
Zh: “你认识露比吗？”

En: Do you know Klaasje?
Zh: “你认识卡拉洁吗？”

En: No, he says looking at the swordfish-clock inattentively. "I don't remember hiring any Rubies to the Hardie boys."
Zh: “不，”他心不在焉地看着剑鱼钟说道。“我不记得为哈迪兄弟招募过一个叫露比的人。”

En: Oh, the chick Titus and the boys have been hanging around all winter? He chuckles. "She has them wrapped around her finger. They're paying her rent, bringing her stuff..."
Zh: “哦，跟提图斯和他的兄弟厮混了整个冬天的那个小妞？”他轻笑到。“他们全都任由她摆布。他们帮她付了租金，给她送东西……”

En: Maybe that's connected too? He seems excited by the possibility. "I'm not some genius judge of character. What happened here is up to you to figure out, Harry."
Zh: “也许这也有关联？”他似乎因为这种可能性兴奋起来。“我不是什么天才的法官角色。里面发生的事只能靠你去查清楚了，哈里。”

En: There's more to this.
Zh: 事情绝对不止这么简单。

En: He *remains* honestly disappointed they didn't kill him.
Zh: 他*还是*真心为他们没有杀他感到失望。

En: This woman may be running drugs for him. There's no way he doesn't know her.
Zh: 这个女人也许在为他贩毒。他不可能不知道她。

En: So this Ruby is *not* running a drug operation for you, Evrart?
Zh: “所以这个露比*没有*帮你贩毒了，艾弗拉特？”

En: All right.
Zh: “好吧。”

En: Indeed, my friend. All *is* right.
Zh: “确实，我的朋友。一切*都*挺好的。”

En: His smile is so wide it could blanket the universe.
Zh: 他的嘴角拉扯地十分宽广，好像可以将整个宇宙包裹其中。

En: I'm gonna say *no*, he says decisively. "I would say I don't have a drug operation, and if I did, I wouldn't have some Ruby, whom I don't even know, running it. I'd hire cops or... my mom. I don't even know, Harry."
Zh: “我会说*不*，”他果断说道。“我会说我没有贩毒，就算我有，我也不会找露比，一个我甚至都不知道是谁的人去运营。我会雇佣警察，或者是……我的妈妈。我真的不知道，哈里。”

En: I'm not a drug dealer. Suddenly he starts laughing. "For a moment it seemed like you were on the track, had a good theory there, but now it's getting *convoluted*. My suggestion is to keep it *simple*. Truth is always simple."
Zh: “我不是一个毒贩。”他突然笑了起来。“有时候感觉你好像很上道，有一套很好的理论，但是现在就有点*复杂*了。我的建议是*简单*一点。真相总是简单的。”

En: You're disappointed. Why did you want them to have killed him?
Zh: “你很失望。你为什么希望他们杀了他？”

En: There were other things I was wondering about.
Zh: “我还在想其他事情。”

En: Why was I disappointed that they didn't start a war? I'm not a floozy, Harry. He flashes you a sly smile. "You don't just get to second base with Evrart like this. We're at first base, Harry."
Zh: “他们没能开战，我为什么要失望？我不是一个浪荡子，哈里。”他朝你露出狡猾的笑容。“你不可能像这样直接带着艾弗拉特我上二垒的。我们还在一垒呢，哈里。”

En: Of course there were. He nods.
Zh: “当然有了。”他点点头。

En: There was a bullet in the hanged man's head.
Zh: “吊人的脑袋里有一颗子弹。”

En: So they shot him? He sounds pleasantly surprised.
Zh: “所以他们射杀他了？”他听起来很惊喜。

En: He was shot in the head *before* he was hanged.
Zh: “在他被吊起来*之前*，他的脑袋就已经中枪了。”

En: Yes. Someone shot him in the head before they hanged him.
Zh: “是的。在他们把他吊起来之前，有人朝他的脑袋开了一枪。”

En: How odd. The man shrugs. "I don't know what to say, Harry. They told me they hanged him. A hanged man is what I saw when I took a look into that yard..."
Zh: “真奇怪。”男人耸耸肩。“我不知道该说什么，哈里。他们告诉我说把他吊死了。我往院子里看的时候，就只看到了一个吊人。”

En: What I *do* know is -- the case is in safe hands. If anyone can get to the bottom of this shot-and-hanged-man, it's my two little policemen. Godspeed, policemen!\n
Zh: “我*知道*的是——这起案子在可靠的人手里。如果说还有人能彻底查清这起‘枪杀吊尸’的案子，那就只能是我这两位小警官。祝成功，警官们！”

En: What I *do* know is -- the case is in safe hands. If anyone can get to the bottom of this shot-and-hanged man, it's my favourite policeman -- Harry.
Zh: “我*知道*的是——这起案子在可靠的人手里。如果说还有人能彻底查清这起‘枪杀吊尸’的案子，那就只能是我最喜欢的警官——哈里。”

En: How do you know the mercenaries were hired by the shipping company?
Zh: “你怎么知道雇佣兵是航运公司雇佣的？”

En: How do I know? Let me tell you about these people. He slams his fist on the desk. "That's their MO. It's what they do."
Zh: “我怎么知道？我来给你们讲讲这些人吧。”他用拳头砸向桌面。“那是他们的做事方式。他们都是这么做的。”

En: Last winter some poor workers in Terminal E went on a little strike. The company sent in *Sediment* -- a security contractor. The strike was over the workers' right to wear protective footwear, Harry.
Zh: “去年冬天E中转站有些可怜的工人进行了一次小罢工。公司派来了*赛迪蒙特*——一个安全服务商。那次罢工是为了给工人争取穿防护鞋的权利，哈里。”

En: These guys turn up and start beating people. Tell you what, Harry, I wouldn't be surprised if we got the same mercenary company -- after a little *rebranding*. And I'm sure as hell not surprised to see an army of scabs under my gates.
Zh: “这些家伙出现了，开始殴打群众。我告诉你，哈里，如果他们派了同一个雇佣兵公司，我一点也不会奇怪——在稍微做了点*品牌更名*之后。而且大门下面出现一支工贼大军，我也不会觉得奇怪。”

En: So you think the security contractor organized the scabs?
Zh: “所以你觉得是安全服务商组织了那帮工贼？”

En: So you believe the scabs were organized by the security contractor?
Zh: “所以你相信工贼是安全服务商组织的？”

En: You said it. Hell... The fist slams on the desk again. "... one of those guys looks big enough to take down that proverbial elephant! Boys like that don't just *happen* to show up during strikes."
Zh: “这可是你说的。该死——”拳头再次落在了桌面上。“……里面有个大块头看起来能够打倒那只众所周知的大象！像他那种人是不会*偶然*出现在罢工现场的。”

En: You said it, Harry! Hell -- the fist lands on the desk again -- "one of those guys looks big enough to take down that proverbial elephant! Boys like that don't just *happen* to show up during strikes."
Zh: “这可是你说的，哈里！该死——”拳头再次落在了桌面上——“里面有个大块头看起来能够打倒那只众所周知的大象！像他那种人是不会*偶然*出现在罢工现场的。”
