En: The name of the company is Krenel this time. It might have been Sediment before.
Zh: “这次的公司名称叫科奈尔，它之前可能叫赛迪蒙特。”

En: Of course, you're always one step ahead of me, Harry. I'm no genius. I'm in this position because people *like* me.
Zh: “当然，你总是领先我一步，哈里。我不是天才。我坐在这个位置上只是因为大家*喜欢*我。”

En: The remaining mercenaries are organizing a tribunal to take on the Hardies.
Zh: “其余的雇佣兵正在组织一场针对哈迪兄弟的审判。”

En: Tribunal? He appears aghast. "That sounds *serious* Harry. We Union men should be *shitting* ourselves..." He rubs his chin and smiles suddenly: "I wish you hadn't told me that. I'm gonna lose *sleep* over this. Let's change the subject."
Zh: “审判？”他好像惊呆了。“听起来很*严重*啊，哈里。我们工会的人都要吓得*屁滚尿流*了……”他摸了摸下巴，突然笑了：“真希望你没告诉我这件事。我会因此而*失眠*的。我们换个话题吧。

En: He's clearly happy about the tribunal.
Zh: 他显然对审判的事很开心。

En: Let's change the subject.
Zh: “我们换个话题。”

En: By all means, Harry. What's on your mind?
Zh: “务必，哈里。你在想什么呢？”

En: You don't *seem* too worried about it.
Zh: “你*好像*并不是太担心。”

En: Okay. Yes.
Zh: “好吧。可以。”

En: Oh, Harry, what do I *really* think about the tribunal? You're trying to climb to second base with old Evrart before you've even courted him properly.
Zh: “哦，哈里，我*到底*怎么看待审判？你甚至都还没恰当地奉承老艾弗拉特，就想直接上二垒吗。”

En: He wants you to do more *things* for him before.
Zh: 他希望你可以先为他多做一些*事情*。

En: You're right, you're right. My sleep isn't your concern -- tell me, Harry, what else can I help you with?
Zh: “你说得对，说的没错。我的睡眠问题不应该由你担心——告诉我，哈里，我还能帮你什么吗？”

En: You mentioned a *lawyergirl*?
Zh: “你提到一个*女律师*？”

En: Oh, Liz is a bright one! He grins broadly. "I paid for that law degree myself, thinking it'll probably turn her all fancy, but hell, Harry -- she came back a firebrand socialist! Sometimes she scares *me* with her zeal."
Zh: “哦，莉齐可聪明了！”他大大地咧开嘴笑了。“我自己供她读下了法律学位，觉得这样可能会让她变成一个上等人，结果呢，哈里——她回来之后变成了一个煽风点火的工人主义者！有时候她的热情还真是有些吓到*我*了。”

En: Did you send her to spy on me disguised as a gardener?
Zh: “是你让她伪装成园丁来监视我的吗？”

En: I did that, didn't I? He snickers. "She thinks of herself as a guerrilla fighter. These middle-class kids and the books they read are crazy, Harry. I think she would rather be an *insurgent* than a lawyer. I hope it's a phase."
Zh: “我做过，是吗？”他偷偷笑了。“她觉得自己是个游击队战士。这些中产阶级小孩，还有他们看的那些书都疯狂的不得了，哈里。我觉得她更愿意做个*起义者*，而不是一个律师。我希望这只是一个阶段的想法。”

En: Evrart, I met these Hardies. Can you ask them to cooperate with me?
Zh: “艾弗拉特，我见过哈迪兄弟了。你能让他们跟我配合吗？”

En: In the future, I could use your backing. Can you ask the Hardie boys to cooperate?
Zh: “以后我也许会需要你的支持。你能让哈迪兄弟配合我吗？”

En: Old Theo used to run them, but things really *kicked into gear* when Titus took the reins and named the group after himself. He starts laughing. "Gotta love his initiative."
Zh: “他们曾经是老特奥的手下，不过提图斯接手而且还以自己的名字命名之后，事情就真的*开始运转*起来了。”他笑了起来。“我真的很喜欢他的主动性。”

En: Separate one from the herd.
Zh: 从群体里面分一个出来。

En: Where can I find them? The Union booth in the Whirling was empty.
Zh: “我能在哪里找到他们？飞旋旅社里的工会包厢没人。”

En: Where can I find them?
Zh: “我能去哪里找到他们？”

En: What if I were to pin this on someone and just quietly...
Zh: “如果我把这件事归咎到某一个人身上，然后静静地……”

En: But of course! It's the least I can do for my good friend, Harry. I'll do it right after we've concluded this talk.
Zh: “当然了！这是我能为好朋友做的最起码的事情，哈里。等这次谈话结束之后，我马上就办。”

En: Also, Harry, here's five reál. He holds out a banknote.
Zh: “还有，哈里，这里是5雷亚尔。”他拿出一张钞票。

En: Wait, why are you giving it to me?
Zh: “等等，你为什么要给我这个？”

En: Take it.
Zh: 收下。

En: I don't need it, I only wanted you to help me with the Hardie boys.
Zh: “我用不着，我只想你帮我安排一下哈迪兄弟的事。”

En: I'm not giving you anything. I'm just holding out five reál.
Zh: “我什么也没有给你。我只是拿出来5雷亚尔。”

En: Oh, I wasn't offering it to you, just holding it out there. He pockets the bill. "But I am willing to share *information*. Was there anything else?"
Zh: “哦，我没想给你，只是拿出来罢了。”他把钞票装进口袋。“但是我愿意分享*信息*。还有别的事吗？”

En: Good boy, a real team player. He rubs his hands together. "Now -- do you have any more questions?"
Zh: “好家伙，确实是个很好的合作伙伴。”他搓搓双手。“现在——你还有其他问题吗？”

En: The lieutenant watches you pocket the bank note. He looks a little puzzled.
Zh: 警督看着你把钞票装进口袋，表情有些困惑。

En: *Was* it a good talk? He leans back, suddenly worried. "I'm not sure we made much headway here. I was hoping we'd bust the case wide open, heck, I even wanted to tell you what I *really* want to achieve with the strike..."
Zh: “这*算是*一次不错的谈话吗？”他向后靠了靠，突然有些担心。“我不确定我们在这方面取得了多大的进步。我希望我们打开天窗说亮话，见鬼，我甚至想告诉你我*到底*希望通过这次罢工得到些什么……”

En: I don't know what happened, Harry. I wanted you to feel like Mr. Martinaise! And, of course -- I also wanted you to *find your gun*. Great sadness comes over him. "But... it's like I can't completely trust you. Yet."
Zh: “我不知道发生了什么，哈里。我想让你感觉像是马丁内斯先生一样！而且，当然了——我也希望你能*找到你的枪*。”他突然被一阵巨大的悲伤包围了。“不过……好像我并不能完全相信你。暂时不能。”

En: Yet?
Zh: “暂时？”

En: Yes, Harry. It's like I can't *fully* trust you if you're not a man of the left, he says, slowly shaking his head. "I *want* to, but I just can't..."
Zh: “是的，哈里。如果你不是左派的话，那我就不能*完全*相信你，”他一边说，一边慢慢摇头。“我*想*相信你，但我真的不能……”

En: I am not a 'man of the left'. I'm a patriot of Revachol.
Zh: “我不是‘左派’。我是瑞瓦肖爱国者。”

En: You're right not to trust me. I take care of *me*. I'm a hustler. I grind. I'm a money engineer.
Zh: “你不相信我是对的。我只在乎*我自己*。我是一个实干家。我辛苦工作。我是一个造钱工程师。”

En: What's that supposed to mean?! I'm more left than *you* are.
Zh: “那又是什么意思？！我比*你*还要更左派一些。”

En: This is another corrupt scheme, isn't it? I'm neither left nor right. I do what my *heart* tells me to do.
Zh: “又是一个腐败的计划，对吗？我不是左派，也不是右派。我做事只凭自己的一颗*心*。”

En: Just between us, I don't care for those lefty dinks either. He immediately changes course. "It's all about *power* and here's how we're gonna get it -- I need you to be my kingsman, Harry."
Zh: “这话仅限于你我之间，我也不在乎那些什么左派呆子。”他立马转变了方向。“其实这都是为了*权利*，而这就是如何得到它的方法——我需要你成为我的战士，哈里。”

En: Perfect, Harry! That's perfect! he claps his hands. "My version of the left is not against the companies. It's *with* the companies. Honestly, what I have in mind is a business proposal. A left-wing business proposal, but still..."
Zh: “完美，哈里！真是太完美了！”他拍拍手。“我的左派观点并不是反对公司。而且跟公司*一起*。老实说，我脑子里有一个商业计划。一个左派商业计划，不过……”

En: You're saying it, but I don't believe you. You know how it is -- company snitches, *agent provocateurs* everywhere... I'm barricaded in this fortress of mine, and I need to get a message out. Will you help me?
Zh: “你是这么说，但我不能相信你。你知道是怎么回事——公司的告密者，*内奸*，到处都是……我把自己封锁在这个堡垒里面，而且我需要把消息送出去。你能帮我吗？”

En: What does your heart tell you about your lost gun, Harry? Does it tell you to forget about it? Or do you think it wants to be found? He picks something from his teeth. "I think it's lonely and cold. I think it wants to be found and I have a proposal for you."
Zh: “那你的心对那把失枪是什么感受呢，哈里？有没有告诉你要忘了它？还是你希望能找到它？”他从牙齿里剔出来什么东西。“我觉得它现在孤零零，冷冰冰，希望被找到。而我这里，有一个提案给你。”

En: It's time for men like me and you to figure out who's killed who and why. He nods. "Real police work is gonna start happening now. I promise you, Harry, this is gonna be good."
Zh: “是时候让你和我这样的人来查清楚到底是谁杀了那个人，而且为什么要这么做。”他点点头。“现在要开始真正的警察工作了。我向你保证，哈里，一定会不错。”

En: You see, Harry, it only benefits me if you open the right door. If you open the wrong door, then it makes things harder for me, 'cause now you're scaring random people in my district.
Zh: “你也看见了，哈里，只有你打开正确的那扇门，我才会从中受益。如果开错了门，那我的处境只会变得更加艰难。因为那样的话，你就是在随便吓唬我地盘里的人。”

En: I don't want them to be scared. I want them to think, 'Wow, I feel so safe. I like Evrart.' I only want weasels to be scared.
Zh: “我不想让他们受到惊吓。我希望他们觉得，‘哇哦，我感觉很安全。我喜欢艾弗拉特。’我只想让黄鼠狼受到惊吓。”

En: The lieutenant shrugs.
Zh: 警督耸耸肩。

En: He means: "Fuck it, let's open the door then."
Zh: 他的意思是：“去他妈的，那我们就去开门吧。”

En: You need to get signatures from Isobel Sadie and Lilienne Carter. The cul-de-sac is right past the pawnshop and across the canal. I heard there was some trouble with the water lock, but it should be fixed now.
Zh: “你需要拿到伊泽贝尔·莎蒂和莉莉恩·卡特的签名。经过当铺，死胡同就在运河对面。我听说水闸好像有点问题，不过现在应该已经修好了。”

En: Once you have the signatures, mail this to 13022 La Rocca in La Delta. *Then* I'll know you're a real kingsman and a patriot. He runs his fingers through his thin hair.\n
Zh: “等你拿到签名之后，就把这个寄到德尔塔区的拉罗卡路13022号。*然后*我就会知道你是一个真正的战士，一个爱国者。”他的手指梳理着稀疏的头发。

En: Once you have the signatures, mail this to 13022 La Rocca in La Delta. *Then* I'll know you're a solid socialist. He runs his fingers through his thin hair.\n
Zh: “等你拿到签名之后，就把这个寄到德尔塔区的拉罗卡路13022号。*然后*我就会知道你是一个坚定的工人主义者。”他的手指梳理着稀疏的头发。

En: Once you have the signatures, mail this to 13022 La Rocca in La Delta. *Then*  I know we can do business together. He runs his fingers through his thin hair.\n
Zh: “等你拿到签名之后，就把这个寄到德尔塔区的拉罗卡路13022号。*然后*我就会知道我们可以一起做生意。”他的手指梳理着稀疏的头发。

En: Once you have the signatures, mail this to 13022 La Rocca in La Delta. *Then* we can talk about your gun. He runs his fingers through his thin hair.\n
Zh: “等你拿到签名之后，就把这个寄到德尔塔区的拉罗卡路13022号。*然后*我们可以聊聊你那把失枪的事。”他的手指梳理着稀疏的头发。

En: Once you have the signatures, mail this to 13022 La Rocca, district of La Delta. Then we can talk. He runs his fingers through his thin hair.
Zh: “等你拿到签名之后，就把这个寄到德尔塔区的拉罗卡路13022号。然后我们再说。”他的手指梳理着稀疏的头发。

En: Once again I require nothing unethical or illegal of you. You just need to get two little signatures on this piece of paper. He pulls out an envelope. "And then mail it to my accountant in La Delta."
Zh: “再声明一次，我没有让你做任何不道德或者是非法的事。你只需要让这张纸上出现两个签名。”他抽出一张信封。“然后把他寄给我在德尔塔的会计师。”

En: What are the signatures for?
Zh: “签名是用来做什么的？”

En: What will happen to the current occupants?
Zh: “现在的居民会怎么样？”

En: They're just gonna have to deal with the construction noise for six months and then they'll be living like kings -- right next to a fancy new youth centre, designed by the best architects from Stella Maris.
Zh: “他们只需要忍受六个月的工程噪音，然后就会过上国王一般的生活——就生活在一个高档的青少年活动中心旁边，而它的设计者将会是斯特拉里斯最棒的建筑师！”

En: There's a nameless little street on the coast with some old houses around it. Most people have already signed. I just need *two* more signatures to get this mission off the ground, Harry.
Zh: “海岸上有一条无名小街，周围有些旧房子。大部分人都已经签过了。我只需要再多*两个*签名就能完成这次任务了，哈里。”

En: No, I'm not going to do this. (Reject the task.)
Zh: “不，我不会这么做的。”（拒绝任务。）

En: Fine, if I happen to be there, I can ask them. (Accept the task.)
Zh: “好吧，如果我碰巧过去的话，可以去问问。”（接受任务。）

En: You bring joy to my heart, Harry --  such a pleasure to be working with you. Here... He hands you an open white envelope.
Zh: “你给我的心带来了欢乐，哈里——跟你一起共事真是太开心了。给你……”他把一个打开的白色信封交给你。

En: Perfectly acceptable. He smiles. "No one's gonna force you into anything, but I don't know about that gun of yours... You see, I'm a vengeful man, Harry. Part of how I got where I am is that, well -- I can be quite nasty sometimes."
Zh: “完全可以接受。”他笑了。“没人会强迫你做任何事，只是我不知道你那把失枪……你也看到了，我是个很有报复心的人，哈里。我能坐在今天这个位置，部分原因也在于此——有时候我这个人真的挺难对付的。”

En: I'm glad you asked, Harry -- the Union is going to build a modern youth centre in Martinaise! He grins broadly. "It will be *righteous*. We're gonna get those teenagers off drugs -- and *on* roller skates!"
Zh: “很高兴你能这么问，哈里——工会要在马丁内斯建一座现代化的青少年中心！”他的笑容更灿烂了。“它将会是*正义的*。我们会让那些青少年远离毒品——*穿上*旱冰鞋！”

En: That's not how you baton someone! The technique is way off. You strike with your whole body not just the baton...
Zh: 警棍可不是这么用的！技术实在是太不靠谱了。你要用全身的力量去击打，而不仅仅是靠警棍……

En: By now I'm *sure* you've figured out who the dead man was working for -- the bad guys. Wild Pines. Sent to scare us. Another *violent measure* of the top hats against us flat caps.
Zh: “到了现在，我*肯定*你已经查清楚那个死人是为谁工作的了——坏人。野松公司。专门派来吓唬我们的。又是那帮有钱人对付我们平民的一种*暴力手段*。”

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我在听。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: You mean our victim?
Zh: “你是说我们的被害人？”

En: Our victim?
Zh: “我们的被害人？”

En: About my fun container? He chuckles. "It's a hoot, Harry. Who knows, maybe you'll be in here the next time they move it. It will be very fun, I promise."
Zh: “关于我这个有趣的集装箱？”他轻笑出声。“它是挺有意思的，哈里。谁知道呢，也许下次我们移动它的时候，你也会在现场。我保证，一定会很有意思的。”

En: What makes you think the Wild Pines negotiator can't control them?
Zh: “你为什么觉得野松公司的交涉人控制不了他们呢？”

En: How did he end up hanging from that tree?
Zh: “他是怎么被吊在树上的？”

En: By *negotiator* you mean Joyce?
Zh: “你说的那个*交涉人*是指乔伊斯吗？”

En: Harry, what you need to realize is -- we dockworkers are not pushovers.
Zh: “哈里，你需要意识到一点——我们码头工人可不是好惹的。”

En: Maybe he doesn't believe the boys *really* had it in them. They're his boys after all.
Zh: 也许他并不相信哈迪兄弟*真的*有那个胆。毕竟在他眼里那只是一群小伙子。

En: He's thinking: *Damn, what a mess. Good thing this isn't gonna come back to me.*
Zh: 他在想：“该死，这什么乱七八糟的。还好不会转回我这里来。”

En: What more can you tell me? Who's second in command? Who's the most violent?
Zh: “你还有什么能告诉我的？谁是二把手？谁是最暴力的？”

En: Interesting. Who's second in command?
Zh: “有意思。谁是二把手？”

En: The lieutenant marks something in his notebook.
Zh: 警督在笔记本上做了记号。

En: What would this entail?
Zh: “需要什么条件？”

En: And what would this entail?
Zh: “那需要什么条件？”

En: Kim, what do you think of this?
Zh: “金，你对这个怎么看？”

En: It depends. I don't think what we just got from Mr. Claire was very useful. He studies Evrart.
Zh: “看情况。我不觉得克莱尔先生给的消息会很有用。”他打量着艾弗拉特。

En: As I said, it weighs on me heavily... He bows his head in shame, then looks up and smiles: "But once we get *really* talking... well, I'm gonna hand you the keys to Martinaise! And maybe even help you figure out who's behind this killing."
Zh: “我也说过了，它给我带来了很大的负担……”他羞愧地低下头，然后抬头笑了笑：“不过等我们*真的*开始谈话的时候……我会把马丁内斯的钥匙交给你！甚至可以帮你查出杀他的凶手是谁。”

En: You mean the fishing village?
Zh: “你是说那个渔村？”

En: Where is this *place* exactly?
Zh: “那这个*地方*到底在哪里？”

En: Yes, yes, the little cul-de-sac on the coast. Where all the men have drowned -- in either the sea or the bottle. A gloomy place, doesn't have that Union attitude.
Zh: “是的，是的，海岸上有一条死胡同。那里的所有人都淹死了——不是在海里，就是在酒瓶子里。一个很阴郁的地方，没有那种团结的态度。”

En: On the coast, Harry. Across the canal. There's a cul-de-sac there -- a little *village* they're calling it. A gloomy place. You'll find it. I trust your detective skills, Harry.
Zh: “在海岸上，哈里。就在运河对面。那里有一条死胡同——他们把它叫做一个小*村子*。一个很阴郁的地方。你会找到的。我相信你的侦察技巧，哈里。”

En: Is he absolutely sure the tenants won't be thrown out in the street?
Zh: 他真能确定那些住户不会被扔到街上去吗？

En: Are you 100% sure no one's going to end up homeless?
Zh: “你100%保证没人会变得无家可归吗？”

En: Sounds like everybody wins.
Zh: “听起来大家都能获利。”

En: Am I...? The big man shakes his head in disbelief. "Harry, these people... Martinaise is the most important thing in my life. I would never let anything bad happen to them."
Zh: “我能不能保证……？”大块头男人难以置信地摇摇头。“哈里，这些人……马丁内斯是我一生中最重要的东西。我永远不会让任何坏事发生在他们头上。”

En: The people win, Harry. He nods solemnly and repeats: "The people win."
Zh: “大家都是赢家，哈里。”他严肃地点头重复到。“大家会赢。”

En: You need to get signatures from Isobel Sadie and Lilienne Carter. The cul-de-sac is right past the pawnshop and across the canal. I hear there is some trouble with the water lock, but they should fix it by Wednesday morning.
Zh: “你需要拿到伊泽贝尔·莎蒂和莉莉恩·卡特的签名。经过当铺，死胡同就在运河对面。我听说水闸好像有点问题，不过周三早晨应该就能修好。”

En: He's saying as little as possible as vaguely as he can. Deliberately omitting things.
Zh: 他说的话极尽可能的少而模糊。故意回避了一些东西。

En: We're gonna build a youth centre there. The value of their properties goes up and kids have a place to play in. I'm looking out for these people, not pulling the rug from under them, Harry. I'm looking out for all of Martinaise, not just the harbour.
Zh: “我们会在那里建一座青少年活动中心。他们的财产价值会提升，孩子们也能有个玩耍的地方。我是在照顾这些人，而不是釜底抽薪，哈里。我要照看整个马丁内斯，而不仅仅是港口。”

En: He means it.
Zh: 他是认真的。

En: But the good news is, the moment you change your mind and want to look into this matter -- just tell me, and we'll be buddies again. He smiles pleasantly.
Zh: “但是好消息是，只要你一改变主意，打算调查这件事的话——只要告诉我，然后我们又会成为好兄弟的。”他高兴地笑了。

En: The lieutenant's eyes meet yours and he shrugs.
Zh: 你和警督对视了一下，他耸了耸肩。

En: Okay. Fine.
Zh: 好吧。那就这样吧。

En: Let's change the topic.
Zh: “我们换个话题。”

En: By all means, Harry! He nods excitedly. "What's on your mind?"
Zh: “务必，哈里！”他兴奋地点点头。“你在想什么呢？”

En: No. You have to put your weight behind it if you wanna do real damage.
Zh: “不。如果你想真正造成伤害的话，那就应该用尽全力。”

En: Oh, Harry, he bursts out laughing. "Thanks for the tip. I'll leave doing the 'real damage' to you -- you are the real police officer after all, not me."
Zh: “哦，哈里，”他大笑起来。“感谢你的提示。制造‘真正伤害’的事就留给你了——毕竟你才是真警察，我不是。”

En: Harry, the big man says with a tragic look on his face. "Had you asked me earlier, I could have used your help in dealing with a certain weasel. It would have really *solidified* our friendship."
Zh: “哈里，”大块头男人说着，脸上流露出悲痛的表情。“如果你早点来问我，我就能在你的帮助下去对付一只黄鼠狼。那样真的可以*巩固*我们的友谊。”

En: Sadly the moment has passed and the weasel's back in its den. Nothing to do about it now, so get this -- He points at you. "-- I'm just going to *give* you the information, because I like you, Harry."
Zh: “可悲的是那个时刻已经过去了，黄鼠狼也溜回了它的老巢。现在已经没什么可以做的了，所以不如这样吧——”他指向你。“——我会直接把这条信息*告诉*你，因为我喜欢你，哈里。”

En: There's probably more to that.
Zh: 可能远远不止这些。

En: Wait, who was the 'weasel'?
Zh: “等等，那个‘黄鼠狼’是谁？”

En: Yes, let's talk about the murder.
Zh: “好吧，我们说说凶杀案的事吧。”

En: The time has passed, Harry. Were you not listening, when I just said that? Pay attention please. He wiggles his chubby index finger at you. "Now, do you want to hear about the hanging or not?"
Zh: “那个时机已经过去了，哈里。我刚才说话的时候，难道你没有听见吗？请集中一点。”他朝你挥舞着胖胖的食指。“现在，你还想不想听吊死的那个男人的事了？”

En: Fantastic! Time for men like me and you to figure out who's killed who and why. His fist lands on the desk. "Real police work is gonna start happening now. I promise you, Harry, this is gonna be good."
Zh: “太棒了！是时候让你和我这样的人来查清楚到底是谁杀了那个人，而且为什么要这么做。”他一拳砸在了桌面上。“现在要开始真正的警察工作了。我想你保证，哈里，一定会不错。”

En: The weasel-thing sounds fun, wish I could have done that.
Zh: “那个黄鼠狼的事听起来好像挺有意思的，要是我能参与就好了。”

En: Yes, that would have been the interesting way to do things. For both of us really. But you were too slow. His expression turns to compassion. "But do not despair over this minor hiccup, Harry. I *will* tell you about the murder."
Zh: “是的，那肯定会是一种有趣的做事方式。真的，对于我们俩来说都是。不过你动作太慢了。”他的表情变为同情。“不过不要因为这个小挫折而绝望，哈里。我*会*告诉你凶杀案的事。”

En: But enough about me and my container. His face turns serious. "The killers the company hired... I think there were three of them. All hardened commando-types."
Zh: “关于我和这个有趣的集装箱的事，已经说的差不多了。”他的面色变得严肃起来。“公司雇佣的那些杀手……我觉得一共有三个人。全是那种久经沙场的突击队员。”

En: You fucked up. You waited too long, the weasel came back, and now you can't open the door I asked you to open... The big man looks you straight in the eye.
Zh: “你搞砸了。你等得太久，黄鼠狼回来了，现在你不能再打开我要求的那扇门……”大块头男人直勾勾地盯着你。

En: Evrart forgives, Harry. A wide smile crosses his face. "Don't cry, my boy. It's gonna be alright. I'm *still* gonna tell you about the murder. That's just the way I am. Benevolent."
Zh: “艾弗拉特会原谅你，哈里。”他摆出一个大大的笑脸。“别哭，我的孩子。没事的。我*还是*会把凶杀案的事告诉你。我就是这种人。仁慈。”

En: I thought I had more time...
Zh: “我以为时间还很多……”

En: I don't deserve that information, I didn't earn it...
Zh: “我不配知道这些消息，不是我自己挣来的……”

En: Fucking weasel...
Zh: “该死的黄鼠狼……”

En: Wait, the girl by the Whirling, who was keeping an eye on you -- is he talking about her?
Zh: 等等，飞旋旅社旁边那个女孩，一直监视你的那个——他说的是她吗？

En: When you meet this *Titus*, tell him about this. See what he has to say.
Zh: 当你见到这个*提图斯*的时候，跟他说说这个。看看他有什么反应。

En: Uncertainty belies his boasting. In the end, though, he believes it -- or at least, he *wants to believe*.
Zh: 犹豫掩饰了他的吹嘘。不过最后，他还是相信了——或者至少，他*想要*相信。

En: So he doesn't want to counter with a narrative that exonerates the Union. But why?
Zh: 所以他不想编个故事为工会开脱。但是，为什么呢？

En: I'm not, he says with a widest of smiles. "But I guess you need to talk to this Klaasje person now. What a job!"
Zh: “我没有，”他一边说，一边露出了最灿烂的笑容。“不过，我猜你现在要跟这位叫卡拉洁的人好好聊聊吧。这工作真辛苦！”

En: Ruby? His belly rolls as he laughs to himself. "You come across some wild characters, Harry, let me tell you! Ruby, huh? I guess you need to discuss her too?"
Zh: “露比？”他笑了，肚子也跟着上下起伏。“你遇到了一些狠角色啊，哈里，我跟你讲！露比，是吗？我猜你也需要找她聊聊？”

En: An excellent opportunity presents itself, sire! You could win the trust of the arch-liar, pretend to play into his hand -- then, should you wish, bend his efforts towards your own!
Zh: 这是个绝佳的机会，大人！你可以赢得那个大骗子的信任，假装被他玩弄于股掌之间。然后，如果您愿意，就可以操纵他为您服务！

En: Does this jiggling ooze think he's going to *use* you? He's got another thing coming. Play his game, son, with your eyes peeled. He's going to slip up, and when he does -- you're going to come out on top.
Zh: 这个蠢货以为他能利用你吗？他又要耍其他花招了。就和他玩玩，小子，眼睛睁大了。他一定会出错，一旦出错——你就会占据上风。

En: Keep it cool, baby boy. You can use this, groove with this. Play along, let him throw his weight around -- and when he throws it too much, and you've got him off guard, that's when your trip him up.
Zh: 保持冷静，小子。你可以利用局势，顺势起舞。配合他，让他发挥自己的力量……等他用力过猛了，趁他不备就能给他下绊子了。

En: The stars aligned into a cosmic frown here. He has your fate decided. Bide your time, however, and let the stars continue their course -- and that frown shall turn into a smile. Only if you play along...
Zh: 群星在这里排列成宇宙紧皱的眉头。他已经决定了你的命运。然而，等待时机吧，让群星继续走它们的路 -- 那皱眉将变成微笑。只要你配合他……

En: He thinks he can force you into his scheme. You can turn this around on him if you accept.
Zh: 他认为他可以强迫你加入他的计划。如果你接受的话，你就可以把这一切推到他身上。

En: If you can stomach agreeing, you can turn this around on Evrart. And physically speaking -- you *can* stomach it.
Zh: 如果你能忍受，你就能把这一切转嫁到艾弗拉特身上。而且从身体上说，你*可以*忍受。

En: Agreeing to take the contracts does not equate to you agreeing to actually help him...
Zh: 同意接受合同并不等于你真的同意帮助他……

En: Take the documents off him and you take his power.
Zh: 拿走他的文件，你就夺走了他的权力。

En: Are you shitting me, Harry? Did you not really open the door and are now just telling me you did? His lively eyes are mapping your face. "You're a wild one, Harry!"
Zh: “你在骗我吗，哈里？你其实并没有真的打开门，现在却来告诉我说你已经打开了？”他那双生机勃勃的眼睛扫视着你的脸。“你真是够狂野的，哈里！”

En: A fantastic change of heart, Harry! He rubs his nose. "Go talk to Mañana down by the gates. He'll brief you and give you the key."
Zh: “哈里，你能改变心意真是太好了！”他摸了摸鼻子。“去找大门下面的马列拉聊聊。他会向你简单介绍一下，然后把钥匙交给你。”

En: I'm very glad to hear that, Harry, he says with a smile. "One question: you didn't actually happen to stumble in and see what's inside the apartment, did you?"
Zh: “我很高兴听到这个消息，哈里，”他笑着说道。“一个问题：你不会碰巧摔了进去，然后看到公寓里面有什么了吧？”

En: I've heard about a connection between the lynching and the strike. I'd like to hear what you know about it.
Zh: “我听说私刑和罢工之间有关联。我想听听你有什么看法。”

En: I want to hear again about how the strike and lynching are connected.
Zh: “我想再听一遍，罢工和私刑是怎么关联起来的。”

En: You said the lynching and the strike are related. How?
Zh: “你说私刑和罢工是有关联的。怎么回事？”

En: Tell me about Titus Hardie and his crew.
Zh: “跟我说说提图斯·哈迪和他那帮人。”

En: He nods gravely. "A security contractor. Can you imagine that? Workers standing in peaceful protest -- united in the spirit of fellowship! -- and they send hired killers to *mow* us down with machine gun fire."
Zh: 他郑重地点点头。“一个安全服务商。你能想象到吗？工人们和平地站在那里抗议——因为友谊精神团结在了一起！——结果他们派来了雇来的杀手，想用机关枪的火力把我们像草一样*割倒*。”

En: I'm talking *beasts*, hardened killers from proxy wars in Yeesut, Semenine, Saramiriza -- you name it, they've done it. Raping, killing, burning villages -- killing little children for the Señorita Pineapple company, Harry...
Zh: “我说的可是*野兽*，参加过伊苏特，塞美尼，萨拉米尔泽代理人战争、久经沙场的杀手——凡是你能说出来的，他们都干过。掠夺、残杀、烧毁村庄——为了菠萝小姐公司杀害小孩，哈里……”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: I think I've heard enough.
Zh: “我想我已经听够了。”

En: Hold on, you have a village elephant?
Zh: “等等，你有村庄大象吗？”

En: No, you haven't, Harry. He waves at you with a jolly smile. "I haven't told you anything about the crime yet."
Zh: “不，你没有，哈里。”他朝你挥挥手，脸上带着愉悦的微笑。“我还没跟你说过任何跟犯罪有关的事呢。”

En: No, Harry, the elephant is metaphorical and so is the village, but the mercs -- and their brutality -- are very real.
Zh: “不，哈里，大象是一种比喻，村庄也是，不过雇佣兵——还有他们的残暴无情——是真真切切的。”

En: Now, I haven't *personally* witnessed the brutalities out there. I have the luxury of staying in my container, you see -- if I need to go somewhere, they just move my container. He laughs.
Zh: “哎呀，我*自己*没有亲眼目睹过那些暴行。我有幸能够待在我的集装箱里，你也看到了——如果我需要去什么地方，他们只要移动我的集装箱就好了。”他笑了。

En: There's a militant wing inside the Union. A group of people whose duties don't involve manual labour, but peacekeeping in the neighbourhood. Making sure everything runs smoothly.
Zh: “工会里面也有一批好战份子。他们的职责不包括体力劳动，而是维护街坊邻居的安全。保证一切都能井然有序地进行。”

En: That sounds a bit like organized crime.
Zh: 听起来有点像有组织犯罪。

En: They're like you -- idealistic people who want to make sure that bad things don't happen. And if they already have... well, punishment must follow.
Zh: “他们和你很像——理想主义者，想要保证坏事不会发生。如果已经发生……呃，那就需要惩罚了。”

En: They're like you guys, he nods to you and the lieutenant. "Idealistic people who want to make sure bad things don't happen. And if they already have... well, punishment must follow."
Zh: “他们很像你们这些人，”他朝着你和警督点点头。“理想主义者，想要保证坏事不会发生。如果已经发生……呃，那就需要惩罚了。”

En: One of them got downright suicidal. Getting drunk, violent, a little rapey... He shakes his head. "Even their own negotiator couldn't control him. That's your boy, the one who likes *hanging out* and trees."
Zh: “其中一个人完全有自杀倾向了。酗酒，暴力，还有强暴……”他摇摇头。“甚至他们自己的交涉人都控制不了他。那就是你要找的人，那个喜欢*挂在*树上的人。”

En: Mhmh. One day Titus Hardie -- leader of this peacekeeping faction -- comes up to me and says: 'Boss, socialist-democratic fervour drove us to take it upon ourselves to kill this beast that was burdening the land.'
Zh: “嗯。有一天，提图斯·哈迪——这个维护和平的小帮派首领——来找我，然后说：‘老大，工人主义的热情驱使我们想要亲自杀死这头掠夺我们土地的野兽。’”

En: He probably worded it differently, but that was the idea. Sure sounded to me like they killed him. He chuckles. "I gave them two weeks paid leave and told them to lay low to avoid retaliation."
Zh: “他可能措辞不太一样，不过话是这个意思。在我听来当然就是他们杀了他。”他咯咯地笑了。“我给了他们两个星期的薪水让他们离开，告诉他们保持低调，以免遭人报复。”

En: Oh, they are simply fine young men -- all seven of them! Exemplary Union members. Always working to advance their position in the local socialist-democratic movement. Core members.
Zh: “哦，他们都是些很好的年轻人——所有7个都是！工会成员的模范。总是努力想要提高自己在这次工人主义运动中的地位。核心成员。”

En: Let's change the subject.
Zh: “我们换个话题。”

En: Good talk. Let's conclude for now.
Zh: “这次谈话挺愉快的。暂时先到这里吧。”

En: By all means, Harry. What's on your mind?
Zh: “务必，哈里。你在想什么呢？”

En: They're almost all of them *great* guys, born leaders. Whatever happened, I'm sure they only had the best interests of Martinaise and Revachol in mind. \n
Zh: “不过可以说他们全都是*很好*的人，天生就是领袖。不管发生什么，我肯定他们时刻都会把瑞瓦肖的最大利益放在心里。”

En: Harry, they're almost all of them *great* guys, born leaders. Whatever happened, I'm sure they only had the best interests of Revachol in mind.
Zh: “哈里，差不多可以说他们全都是*很好*的人，天生就是领袖。不管发生什么——我肯定他们时刻都会把瑞瓦肖的最大利益放在心里。”

En: Work with them -- hell, interview them! But don't fight them. They really are just like you -- men who like beer, women, and some *order* on the streets.
Zh: “跟他们合作——该死的，去走访他们吧！不过别跟他们打架。他们真的跟你一样——喜欢啤酒，女人，还有街道上的*秩序*。”

En: So let me ask you this... Which one of Hardie's boys is your least favourite?
Zh: “我想问问你……哈迪兄弟里谁是你最不喜欢的？”

En: Oh, that would definitely be Fat Angus. His feet smell from a city-block away and he's always having noisy stomach troubles. Horrible, revolting guy.
Zh: “哦，那肯定是胖子安格斯了。他的臭脚在一街区之外都能闻到，而且他的胃总是发出很吵闹的声音。他是个可怕又让人讨厌的家伙。”

En: So let's say something happens to Fat Angus... let's say a citizen's arrest...
Zh: “假如说，有什么事情发生在胖安格斯身上……比如说对公民实施拘捕……”

En: You would die, Harry, he says, grinning. "You would die and in the process start a bloody and completely unnecessary war between the Débardeurs' Union and the Citizens Militia."
Zh: “你会死的，哈里，”他笑着说道。“你会死，而且在这个过程中，装卸工会和公民武装之间会发生一场完全没有必要的、血腥的全面战争。”

En: Angus, his ever-growling stomach, and his smelly feet are all part of the Union. You have as much right to *arrest* him as he has to arrest you...
Zh: “安格斯，他那个永远叫唤不停的胃，还有他的臭脚都是工会的一部分。你有多少权力*逮捕*他，他同样就有多少权利逮捕你……”

En: Angus, his ever-growling stomach, and his smelly feet are all part of the Union. You have as much right to *arrest* him as I have to arrest your partner here. He points to the lieutenant.
Zh: “安格斯，他那个永远叫唤不停的胃，还有他的臭脚都是工会的一部分。你有多少权力*逮捕*他，我就同样有多少权力逮捕你这位搭档。”他指向警督。

En: Actually less, because this is my home town, my territory and my backyard. You are a guest here, Harry. Please remember that.
Zh: “事实上远不止如此，因为这里我的家乡，我的领土，我的后花园。你在这里是客人，哈里。请记住这一点。”

En: ...actually less, because it's his home and his backyard. You are a guest here, Harry. Please remember that.
Zh: “……实际上更少，因为这里是他的家乡，他的后花园。你在这里是客人，哈里。请记住这一点。”

En: Oh Harry... He starts laughing. "This is getting real grim and there's no need for that. We are friends." He sits back and looks you in the eye with a wide smile.
Zh: “哦，哈里……”他笑了起来。“形势还真是变得越来越严峻了，不过真的没有这个必要。我们是朋友。”他靠在椅背上，微笑着看着你。

En: Most certainly, Harry. The big man nods merrily. "Nothing brightens my day like brainstorming these things with you."
Zh: “当然可以了，哈里。”大块头男人愉快地点点头。“能跟你就这些问题一起头脑风暴，没有什么比这事更能让我开心的了。”

En: Oh, of course. That's your main thing here. That's *why* you're in Martinaise. He nods. "I know everything that goes on around here. And I would *love* to help you, like I'm helping you with the body and your lost gun..."
Zh: “哦，当然。你在这里的主要任务就是这个。这就是你来马丁内斯的*原因*。”他点点头。“我知道这里发生的一切，而且也很*乐意*帮你，比如说那具尸体，还有你的失枪……”

En: Oh, of course. That's your main thing here. That's *why* you're in Martinaise. He nods. "I know everything that goes on around here. And I would *love* to help, like I'm helping you with your lost gun..."\n
Zh: “哦，当然。你在这里的主要任务就是这个。这就是你来马丁内斯的*原因*。”他点点头。“我知道这里发生的一切，而且也很*乐意*帮你，比如说你的失枪……”

En: Oh, of course. That's your main thing here. That's *why* you're in Martinaise. He nods. "I know everything that goes on around here. And I would *love* to help you, like I'm helping you with the body..."\n
Zh: “哦，当然。你在这里的主要任务就是这个。这就是你来马丁内斯的*原因*。”他点点头。“我知道这里发生的一切，而且也很*乐意*帮你，比如说那具尸体……”

En: Oh, of course. That's your main thing here. That's *why* you're in Martinaise. He nods. "I know everything that goes on around here and I would *love* to discuss it with you."
Zh: “哦，当然。你在这里的主要任务就是这个。这就是你来马丁内斯的*原因*。”他点点头。“我知道这里发生的一切，而且也很*乐意*跟你一起讨论。”

En: I mean, it's no secret that the lynching is connected to the strike -- so much to talk about! Honestly, it's been weighing on me so heavily. I understand -- you need to *interview* me...
Zh: “我的意思是，私刑跟罢工有关已经不是什么秘密了——里面有很多可以聊的东西！老实说，它给我带来了沉重的负担。我理解——你需要*走访*我……”

En: I sense there's a *but*.
Zh: “我感觉到了一个*但是*。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: ...but there's a *thing* that's been keeping me up at night. I *want* to talk about the hanging. I mean... if we could just calmly talk, exchange information, we could blow this thing wide open!
Zh: “……但是有*一件事*让我夜不能寐。我*想*说说吊人的事。我的意思是……如果我们可以冷静地交谈，彼此交换信息，我们就能把这件事情彻底搞明白！”

En: But I *can't* think straight with this thing weighing on me... Suddenly, he slaps himself on the forehead. "You're police officers, aren't you? I have a crazy idea. You guys are basically door-opening machines. Incredibly talented at opening doors."\n
Zh: “但是有这件事压着我，我*无法*清晰地思考……”他突然拍了拍前额。“你是一个警官，对吗？我有个疯狂的主意。你们基本上可以说是开门机器。在开门这方面特别有天赋。”

En: But I *can't* think straight with this thing weighing on me... He slaps himself on the forehead. "You're a police officer, aren't you? I have a crazy idea. You guys are basically door-opening machines. Incredibly talented at opening doors."
Zh: “但是有这么一件事一直困扰我，使我*无法*清晰地思考……”他拍了拍前额。“你是一个警官，对吗？我有个疯狂的主意。你们基本上可以说是开门机器，在开门这方面特别有天赋。”

En: Kim, is that true? Are we door-opening machines?
Zh: “金，是真的吗？我们真是开门机器吗？”

En: I've opened a few doors in my life.
Zh: “我这辈子就开了不少门。”

En: You've heard wrong Evrart. We're not.
Zh: “你的消息有误，艾弗拉特。我们没有。”

En: Oh, you're being too modest, my friend. He chuckles. "But don't worry. This annoying thing I have is completely legal. I just need you to open a door."
Zh: “哎呀，你这就太谦虚了，我的朋友。”他轻笑到。“别担心。我烦恼的这件事完全是合法的。我只需要你帮我开一扇门。”

En: And I'm sure you're gonna open this one with flying colours, Harry. He chuckles. "This really is very simple and there's nothing shady about it."
Zh: “而且我肯定你能很出色地打开这扇门，哈里。”他咯咯地笑了。“这件事真的很简单，完全没有什么见不得人的。”

En: I'm not sure I understand. He looks to the Union boss. "If you're asking us to break down someone's door, it's not going to happen."
Zh: “我不确定自己理解的对不对。”他看着工会老大。“如果你是想要我们闯入别人家里，那是不可能的。”

En: Come now. I just need you to go open a *little* door for me -- and leave it unlocked. A simple thing. Absolutely nothing shady about it.
Zh: “拜托，我只需要你去帮我开一扇*小*门——然后别锁上。很简单一件事。绝对没有什么见不得人的地方。”

En: Oh, no one's. It's just a weasel. A weasel lives there. Nothing for you to worry about.
Zh: “哦，没什么。只是一只黄鼠狼。那里住了一只黄鼠狼。你没什么好担心的。”

En: Whose door is it?
Zh: “那是谁家的门？”

En: Damnit, fine, I'll look into it, we need to talk about that murder. (Accept the task.)
Zh: “该死，好吧。我会去看看，我们需要讨论一下凶杀案的事。”（接受任务。）

En: I can't accept this thing. (Refuse the task -- for now.)
Zh: “我不能接受这件事。”（暂时拒绝任务。）

En: Why don't you just open it yourself?
Zh: “你为什么不自己去开呢？”

En: Harry, I'm a very busy man and, more importantly,  I don't have that extraordinary physique you do. He slams his fists together. "You look like you could run around all day!"
Zh: “哈里，我是个很忙的人，而且更重要的是，我没有你那种非凡的体格。”他紧握双拳。“你看起来像可以跑上一整天的样子！”

En: You want to send someone a message that the police are working for you.
Zh: “你想向某人传达一条信息：警察在为你做事。”

En: He wants to send a message: "Even the police are working for me."
Zh: 他想要传达一条信息：“就连警察也在为我做事。”

En: I repeat, I'm a very, *very* busy man, Mr. Kitsuragi, and therefore I must occasionally enlist... outside help. He turns back to you. "So what will it be, Harry?"
Zh: “我再重复一次，我是一个非常，*非常*忙的人，曷城先生，因此我偶尔必须谋求……外部的帮助。”他又转向了你。“所以你觉得怎么样，哈里？”

En: You can get the key from Mañana -- he's down by the gates. Mañana's like a free agent in the Union. Special operations. Hardened socialist. A real free-thinker too. He'll tell you precisely where the door is. He smiles, obviously satisfied with how well he planned it all out. "One last thing, Harry."
Zh: “你可以从马列拉那里拿到钥匙——他就在大门下边。马列拉就像工会的自由人，特别行动小组。他是个老练的工人主义者，也是一个真正的自由思想家。他会告诉你那扇门的准确位置。”他笑着，显然对自己的计划很满意。“还有最后一件事，哈里。”

En: Of course, Harry, I understand. But if that's the case, I don't think we'll *ever* find your gun. Even worse -- we won't be able to speak like equals about the murder...
Zh: “当然了，哈里，我能理解。不过如果是这么回事的话，我想我们可能*永远*也找不到你的枪了。更糟糕的是——我们就不能平等地谈论凶杀案的事了……”

En: Perhaps this was just bad timing for you? Know that you can *always* come back to me. I really hope you do. He winks at you. "For your sake, my sake -- and for your gun's sake too."
Zh: “也许对你来说这个时机不太对？你*随时*可以回到我这里。我真的希望你可以这么做。”他朝你眨眨眼。“为了你好，为了我——也为了你的枪。”

En: I bet you don't even know anything about the hanging.
Zh: “我打赌你根本就不知道吊人的事。”

En: Harry, my dear friend. He sinks deeper into the chair. "I am what people call a *local big wig*. I know everything that goes on in Martinaise."
Zh: “哈里，我亲爱的朋友。”他深深陷入自己的椅子里。“我就是别人口中的那种*地头蛇*。我知道马丁内斯发生的所有事。”

En: Fantastic, my friend! Just let me know when it's done and we can take our friendship to the next level. He flicks his fingers.
Zh: “太棒了，我的朋友！事成之后记得告诉我，然后我们的友谊就能继续推进到下一个阶段了。”他打了个响指。

En: Sure. I understand. But if that's the case, I don't think we'll *ever* get to speak like equals. Beautiful friendship unrealized. I really hope you'll change your mind, Harry...
Zh: “当然，我理解。不过如果是这么回事的话，我想我们可能*永远*也无法平等地对话了。美丽的友谊小船也会被打翻。我真心希望你能改变主意，哈里……”

En: What do you mean by a 'weasel'?
Zh: “你说‘黄鼠狼’是什么意思？”

En: Just go there, unlock the door and leave it open. It's been such a burden on me, Harry. I just want this to be over so I can discuss business with you. He puts his glasses back on.
Zh: “你只要过去，把门打开，然后让它就这么开着。这对我来说真是压在心头的一块石头，哈里。我是想这件事快点过去，这样我就能跟你谈正经事了。”他又把眼镜戴上了。

En: A loud blabbering weasel. When weasels feel no one is watching, they start acting *foolishly...* He removes his glasses and rubs his nose.
Zh: “一只喜欢乱说话的大嗓门黄鼠狼。当黄鼠狼觉得没人看着它的时候，行为就会变得很*愚蠢……*”他取下眼镜，摸了摸自己的鼻子。

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Yes -- we both understand what you meant."
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“是的——我们都理解你是什么意思。”

En: And just like that, it's happening -- the roller coaster is moving. Too late to take it back now.
Zh: 就像这样，它发生了——过山車开始动了。现在想要回头已经太迟。

En: Just open *one little door*. You don't need to go in or anything. I just want that weasel to come home and see the unlocked door. That's it. Anything else we should discuss?
Zh: “只是开*一扇小门*。你不需要进去或者什么的。我只想让黄鼠狼回家之后看到没有上锁的门。仅此而已。我们还有什么需要讨论的？”

En: Yes, let's blow it open.
Zh: “好吧，我们把它炸开吧。”

En: Yes. That sounds good, the lieutenant says with a slow nod. "Let's do that."
Zh: “是的。听起来不错，”警督一边说着，一边慢慢点了点头。“我们就这么做吧。”

En: Just open the door. You don't need to go in or anything. I just want that weasel to come home and see the unlocked door.
Zh: “你只需要把门打开。不需要进去或者什么的。我只想让黄鼠狼回家之后看到没有上锁的门。”

En: This may be the only way, he thinks. I won't hold it against you. In fact, we  probably *should* reconsider later.
Zh: 这可能是唯一的办法了，他想到。我不会怪你的。事实上，我们之后也许*应该*重新考虑一下。

En: This must be the woman who bought the gun from Roy, the one he described as "terrifying."
Zh: 这肯定就是从罗伊那里买走枪的那个女人，他形容的很*可怕*的那个人。

En: So the gun's still with the woman who bought it from Roy...
Zh: “所以那个从罗伊店里买走枪的女人，东西还在她身上……”

En: Yes, the same one -- I see you've done your research. The pawnshop made the gun easy to track... He smiles and shakes his head in wonderment. "Crazy stuff, Harry. Selling your gun like that! Wild. Anyway..."
Zh: “是的，同一个人——我看你自己也做了点调查。当铺让枪变得更容易追踪了……”他惊叹地笑着摇摇头。“真是疯了，哈里。居然就这么把你的枪卖掉！疯了。总而言之……”

En: Is this the same woman who bought the gun from Roy?
Zh: 这跟从罗伊那里买走枪的是同一个女人吗？

En: Is she the person who bought the gun from Roy?
Zh: “就是她从罗伊那里买走枪的吗？”

En: Uhm, I think I needed money so I pawned my sidearm.
Zh: “呃，我想我是因为缺钱把配枪当了。”

En: Stay silent.
Zh: 保持沉默。

En: It was my fault. I pawned it off. I'm a terrible cop.
Zh: “是我的错。我把它当掉了。我是个糟糕的警察……”

En: Wild stuff, Harry. Absolutely *wild*. I'm not judging you. We all have our dark days. What matters is: Union men are gonna help you fix this. He smiles. "Anyway..."
Zh: “太疯狂了，哈里。绝对的*疯狂*。我没有评判你。我们都有属于自己的苦难岁月。重要的是：工会的人会帮你解决这个问题。”他笑了。“总之……”

En: ...the neighbours of this old woman contacted my men, because they trust me and the Débardeurs Union. Apparently she was waving it around at the entrance to her building.
Zh: “……这个老妇人的邻居联系了我的人，因为他们相信我和这个装卸工会。她好像是拿着枪在自家门口到处挥舞。”

En: Better not to gloat, sire. 'Tis *arrogance* that gives the play away. Be subtle.
Zh: 最好不要幸灾乐祸，大人。*傲慢*会泄露你的秘密。要狡猾一些。

En: Judging from how happy he is, it looks like you did it. He doesn't *appear* to suspect trickery.
Zh: 从他高兴的程度判断，看起来的确是你做的。他*似乎*并没有怀疑有诈。

En: You don't know his full plan. Perhaps he expected trickery? Perhaps the signatures weren't important? The point is: do not think you're ahead of him.
Zh: 你不知道他的全盘计划。也许他在期待被骗？也许签名并不重要？关键在于：不要以为你走在了他的前面。

En: Hear that? You did it the honest way. You got the *real* signatures and now he's happy. Well done.
Zh: 听见没？你选择了诚实坦率的方式。你拿到了*真正*的签名，现在他很高兴。干得好。

En: We would've preferred trickery, but the choice was yours, sire, as always. You are the liege. And you're *in*.
Zh: 我们会更偏向于诡计，但是一如既往，这是您的选择，大人。你才是君主。而且你已经陷入*其中*了。

En: Finally -- answers. Ask away, son.
Zh: 总算是——有答案了。问吧，孩子。

En: I don't know... your dirty forgery-fingers aren't sure if playing this one straight was the right thing to do.
Zh: 我不知道……你那肮脏的伪造手指也不确定这是不是正确的事。

En: Oops... yes, the noise would do the trick. It'll be terrible.
Zh: 哎呀……是的，噪音就能达到效果。这会很吓人的。

En: He proudly spreads his hands to demonstrate the size of the palaces. They're very large.
Zh: 他骄傲地张开双手，展示着殿堂的尺寸。真的非常大。

En: There is true indignation in his voice when he speaks about the state of things. And even a touch of pain.
Zh: 当他说到事情现状的时候，声音里夹杂着货真价实的愤怒。甚至还有一丝痛苦。

En: The pain is true. He's seen the kids do worse than that.
Zh: 这份痛苦是真的。他见过孩子们做过更糟糕的事情。

En: Wait, but -- will you erect a statue to yourself?
Zh: “等等，但是——你会为自己立一座雕像吗？”

En: I'm not a *symbolist*, Harry. I'm a realist. My statue will be Martinaise rebuilt: five story building complexes, workers on welfare and landowners in Ozonne *hating* me. That will be my statue -- *and* yours. We're doing this together.
Zh: “我不是一个*象征主义者*，哈里。我是一个现实主义者。我的雕像将会是重建后的马丁内斯：5层高的综合大楼，工人的福利，还有欧佐纳尔那些*痛恨*我的地主。它们将会成为的雕像——*还有*你的。我们携手并进。”

En: You would look nice as a statue...
Zh: 你当雕像应该挺好看的……

En: And then there will be a giant statue of him, towering above it all?
Zh: 然后还会有一座他的雕像，耸立在这一切之上？

En: You would look nice as a statue...
Zh: 你当雕像应该挺好看的……

En: I'm not a *symbolist*, Harry. I'm a realist. My statue will be Martinaise rebuilt: five story building complexes, kids off speed and landowners in Ozonne *hating* me. That will be my statue -- *and* yours. We're doing this together.
Zh: “我不是一个*象征主义者*，哈里。我是一个现实主义者。我的雕像将会是重建后的马丁内斯：5层高的综合大楼，戒掉思必得的孩子们，还有欧佐纳尔那些*痛恨*我的地主。它们将会成为的雕像——*还有*你的。我们携手并进。”

En: Wait, but -- will you erect a statue to yourself?
Zh: “等等，但是——你会为自己立一座雕像吗？”

En: Oh I do, Harry, I really do. He crosses his arms on his chest. "I'm gonna make the working man richer than *Joyce Messier* with that vote of yours. That's my job. Just like yours is to keep the peace."
Zh: “哦，我会的，哈里，我真的会。”他把双臂交叉在胸口。“有了你支持的一票，我会让工人们变得跟*乔伊斯·梅西耶*一样富有。这就是我的工作。就好像你的工作是维护和平一样。”

En: Damn right I'm up to something, Harry! The fist lands on the table again. "I'm gonna make the working man as rich as *Joyce Messier*. That's my job. Just like yours is to keep the peace."
Zh: “该死的没错，我是有自己的打算，哈里！”他的拳头又落到了桌面上。“总有一天，我会让工人变得像*乔伊斯·梅西耶*一样富有。这就是我的工作。就好像你的工作是维护和平一样。”

En: Sounds like you got this. You can rely on my vote.
Zh: “听起来你能行。我会为你投上支持的一票。”

En: I knew you were up to something.
Zh: “我就知道你有自己的打算。”

En: A true flash of anger in him -- as he thinks of her.
Zh: 当他想到她的时候——怒火真的爆发了。

En: A true flash of anger in him -- as he thinks of her.
Zh: 当他想到她的时候——怒火真的爆发了。

En: That *is* true. People don't trust pretty people.
Zh: 那*的确*是真的。大家都不相信长得漂亮的人。

En: He crosses his hands, contently, thinking of the interior temperature of the wasp rising.
Zh: 他满足地交叉双手，想着大黄蜂内部的温度正在上升。

En: They cook it alive in its exoskeleton.
Zh: 他们带着外骨骼把它活活煮了。

En: The word *fuck* rings like a gunshot from his mouth. He doesn't swear often.
Zh: 他嘴里的那句*去他妈的*就像一声枪响。他不经常骂人的。

En: He looks at the swordfish clock and nods: "It's already *happening*."
Zh: 他看着剑鱼钟，点了点头：“它已经*发生*了。”

En: And they're all well motivated. At least the ones you've seen.
Zh: 他们的积极性都很高。至少你见过的是这样。

En: 2,373 is a sizeable contingent for a labour organization in Revachol.
Zh: 对于瑞瓦肖的工人组织来说，2,373是一个相当大的数字。

En: Diggin' it, Evrart. I have to say -- I'm diggin' this part!
Zh: “我喜欢，艾弗拉特。我必须说——我喜欢这部分！”

En: Wow, a neurochemical, psychoactive labour uprising / hostile takeover.
Zh: 哇哦，一种神经化学品，影响心理状态的工人起义/恶意接收。

En: So am I. The company thinks transporting these chemicals in bulk 'looks bad,' he makes air quotes, "'has bad optics,' 'may be illegal in some countries.' The Débardeurs' Union, however... we're all about the large volume column."
Zh: “我也是。公司认为大量运送这些化学制品‘看起来不太好’，”他用手比了个双引号，“‘影响不好啦’，‘在某些国家可能是违法的’。然而，装卸工会……我们本身就是大体积、大容量的。”

En: In bulk shipping, *large volume column* is a major buyer. A shark.
Zh: 在散货运输中，*大批量*是一个主要买家。一头巨鲨。

En: He smiles. "But if I *were* to supply ingredients for some sort of rainbow party, I would make sure the Union took a fantastic share -- and I'd keep that stuff far away from Martinaise."
Zh: 他笑了。“不过如果我要给某种彩虹派对供应原料，我肯定会保证工会获得丰厚的利润——而且我会让那种东西远离马丁内斯。”

En: Uhm... because you *pawned off* your gun?
Zh: 嗯……因为你*当掉*了你的枪？

En: You pawned it off for liquor.
Zh: 你把它当掉换酒了。

En: You pawned it, so you wouldn't *shoot* yourself in the head.
Zh: 你把它当掉就不会朝自己的脑袋*开枪*了。

En: It's good I sold it. I would've shot myself with it.
Zh: “还好我把它卖了，不然我会开枪自杀的。”

En: Union boys are gonna help you *fix* it, he winks at you. Don't worry Harry.
Zh: 工会的兄弟会帮你*处理*好的，他朝你眨眨眼。别担心，哈里。

En: Waving the gun around doesn't sound good. None of this does.
Zh: 拿着枪到处乱挥听起来不太妙啊。这一切都不太妙。

En: Waving the gun around. Doesn't sound good.
Zh: 拿着枪到处乱挥。听起来不太妙啊。

En: It sounds like a very disturbed and desperate individual.
Zh: 听起来像是一个非常不安又绝望的人。

En: We might be looking at someone with a medical history here. Watch out.
Zh: 我们要找的可能是个有病史的人。要小心。

En: Unstable is good, actually. She will be easier to disarm... no wait, actually unstable is pretty bad.
Zh: 其实吧，不稳定挺好的。解除武装会容易一些……不对，等等，其实不稳定挺糟糕的。

En: There it is again -- *the pigs*, like Roy said. Not good at all.
Zh: 又来了——*猪猪女侠*，罗伊也说过。一点都不好。

En: It actually sounds extremely bad, but let's not give him the satisfaction.
Zh: 其实听起来挺糟糕的，不过我们不能让他得意。

En: She will be there. From 22.00 till 02.00.
Zh: 她会在那里的。从22点到2点。

En: More fun stuff... He looks at you. "Seems like we already have fun stuff to do."
Zh: “更多有趣的事情……”他看着你。“我们好像已经有很有趣的事要做了。”

En: It's done. I mailed the signatures you asked me to mail.
Zh: “完事了。你让我寄的签名，我已经寄出去了。”

En: My friend, the mail-man, confirms the letter is on its way. You've done a great thing today. You've given the children of Martinaise a real future, Harry, and I feel I can finally trust you now.
Zh: “我的朋友，那个邮递员确认信已经在运送途中了。你今天做了一件大事。你给了马丁内斯的孩子一个真正的未来。我感觉自己总算是可以相信你了，哈里。”

En: My friend, the mail-man, confirms the letter is on its way. You've done a great thing today. You've given the children of Martinaise a future and proven to be a true man of the left. I can finally trust you now.
Zh: “我的朋友，那个邮递员确认信已经在运送途中了。你今天做了一件大事。你给了马丁内斯的孩子一个真正的未来。我感觉自己总算是可以相信你了，哈里。”

En: My friend, the mail-man, confirms the letter is on its way. You've done a great thing today. You've given the children of Martinaise a bright future, and you've proven yourself someone I can trust, someone I can really do *business* with.
Zh: “我的朋友，那个邮递员确认信已经在运送途中了。你今天做了一件大事。你给了马丁内斯的孩子一个真正的未来，而且你证明了自己是我可以信任的人，一个真正可以跟我*合作*的人。”

En: My friend, the mail-man confirms the letter is on its way. You've done a great thing today. You've given the children of Martinaise a real future, Harry, and proven yourself a real kingsman who's not afraid to make the tough decisions. I know I can trust you now.
Zh: “我的朋友，那个邮递员确认信已经在运送途中了。你今天做了一件大事。你给了马丁内斯的孩子一个真正的未来，而且证明了你自己是一个真正的战士，不怕做出艰难的决定。现在我知道自己可以相信你了。”

En: My friend, the mail-man, confirms the letter is on its way. You've done a great thing today. You've given the children of Martinaise a real future. I feel I can finally trust you, Harry.
Zh: “我的朋友，那个邮递员确认信已经在运送途中了。你今天做了一件大事。你给了马丁内斯的孩子一个真正的未来。我感觉自己总算是可以相信你了，哈里。”

En: You're in my inner circle. You too, Mr. Kitsuragi, he nods to the lieutenant, smiling broadly. "We can talk about anything: the strike, the murder, your lost gun -- *nothing* is off the table."
Zh: “你已经进入我的核心集团了。还有你，曷城先生，”他朝着警督点点头，脸上笑开了花。“我们可以谈论任何事情：罢工，凶杀案，你的失枪——*没什么*不能谈的。”

En: He leans forward and looks straight into your eyes with the warmest of smiles. "You're in my inner circle. We can talk about anything: the strike, the murder, your lost gun -- *nothing* is off the table."
Zh: 他靠近了一些，直直地看着你的眼睛，脸上挂着最温暖的笑容。“你已经进入我的核心集团了。我们可以谈论任何事情：罢工，凶杀案，你的失枪——*没什么*不能谈的。”

En: See, forging that signature really paid off.
Zh: 看见没，伪造签名真的见成效了。

En: Well done, sire. By guile and deceit, you're in.
Zh: 做得好，大人。通过诡计和谎言，您成功了。

En: What was the fine print on the document I mailed? What was the catch?
Zh: “我寄的文件上那行小字是什么？有什么意图？”

En: There was no fine print, Harry. He leans forward. "But I do hope all that construction noise and limited street access *encourages* some people to move. After a year of that ruckus, who wouldn't?"
Zh: “没有什么小字，哈里。”他靠向前方。“不过我确实希望所有那些建筑噪音和封禁的街道会*鼓励*一些人搬走吧。吵了一年之后，谁不会呢？”

En: And when they do... He holds out his index finger. "They will have a *good* place to go. I'm currently working on renovating the buildings near the roundabout into affordable *workers palaces*."
Zh: “而且，当他们这么做的时候……”他伸出食指。“他们会去一个*好*地方。我正在翻新环岛附近的一些建筑，把那里变成物美价廉的*工人殿堂*。”

En: The signatures I got -- I know you plan to force them out with the construction noise.
Zh: “我拿到的签名——我知道你的计划是用建筑噪音强迫他们离开。”

En: So the village is doomed -- because I helped you.
Zh: “所以整个村子都在劫难逃——因为我帮了你。”

En: Harry... He shakes his head. "By now you should know I would never do anything tricky like that. However, if the construction noise and limited street access makes *some* people consider moving..."
Zh: “哈里……”他摇摇头。“到了现在，你应该知道我绝对不会做出这种事情。不过，如果建筑噪音和封禁的街道能让*某些*人考虑搬走……”

En: Well, let's just say there'll be freshly renovated buildings near the roundabout where those poor people can finally enjoy a significant uptick in quality of life. I'm talking real affordable *workers palaces*.
Zh: “呃，这么说吧，海岸附近会有一些新近翻修好的建筑，那些穷人终于可以在那里享受更好的生活质量了。我说的可是他们可以负担的*工人文化宫*。”

En: Did *you* order the hanged man killed?
Zh: “是*你*下令杀掉吊人的吗？”

En: Can I get my gun now?
Zh: “现在能把枪给我了吗？”

En: You were there, you saw the place. A waste land -- there's nothing left. But mark my words, officers. His slams his fist on the table, causing some of the coffee to spill. "We are going to *reset* it."
Zh: “你去过那里，亲眼见到那个地方了。一片废土——什么也没剩下。不过记住我的话，警官。”他用拳头砸向桌子，弄洒了杯中的几滴咖啡。“我们会*重置*它的。”

En: You were there, you saw the place. A waste land -- there's nothing left. But mark my words, Harry. His slams his fist on the table, causing some of the coffee to spill. "We are going to *reset* it."
Zh: “你去过那里，亲眼见到那个地方了。一片废土——什么也没剩下。不过记住我的话，哈里。”他用拳头砸向桌子，弄洒了杯中的几滴咖啡。“我们会*重置*它的。”

En: So the village is doomed, the lieutenant says grimly.
Zh: “所以那个村庄在劫难逃，”警督的口气很冷酷。

En: *Re-set*, he repeats. "I have big plans for Martinaise. And they do *not* include humans living in those pig sheds on the coast. That land will be used for municipal buildings and commerce."
Zh: “*重置*，”他重复到。“我为马丁内斯准备了一个大计划。但是*并不*包括海岸边那些住在猪圈里的人。那片土地将会用于市政建筑和商业中心。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你什么意思？”

En: Harry, imagine a Youth Centre-Supermarket-Church complex! Employing hundreds, no, thousands of people. The coast will be lit up with enterprise -- and *life*! All those ruins out there turned into *low-income housing*...
Zh: “哈里，想象一个有着青年中心、超级市场和教堂的复合区域！那里能够雇佣上百，不，上千的人。整个海岸都会被企业活动点亮——充满*生机*。所有的废墟都会变成*低收入住房*…… ”

En: Youth-Supermarket-Church complex? Do you really expect me to believe that?
Zh: “青少年活动中心——超级市场——教堂综合设施？你真指望我会相信你？”

En: Low-income housing? If even part of this is true, then why not.
Zh: “低收入住房？哪怕只有一部分是真的，那就没有反对的理由。”

En: And *life*... (Nod.) "I knew you were a man of the people, Evrart."
Zh: “还有*生活*……”（点点头。）“我知道你是人民的公仆，艾弗拉特。”

En: Yes, I do. I got the centre, I got room for a retail complex, and in four years I'll get the church too. The wheels are already turning, Harry. The wheels of progress. This post-war limbo -- I won't stand for it. There are kids practically playing with their own *faeces* out there... It cannot go on.
Zh: “是的，没错。我规划了中心区，规划了零售综合设施的空间，四年之内还会修建一座教堂。車轮已经开始转动，哈里。进步的車轮。这片战后的失落之地——我不会再忍受下去了。这里的孩子们甚至只能玩自己的*粪便*……不能再这样下去了。”

En: I accept your salute, Harry, he nods. "*All* of it is true. I've got the centre, I've got room for a retail complex, and in four years I'll get the church too. The wheels are turning, Harry. The wheels of progress. This post-war limbo -- I won't stand for it. There are kids practically playing with their own *faeces* out there... It cannot go on."
Zh: “我接受你的敬意，哈里，”他点点头。“*一切*都是真的。我规划了中心区，规划了零售综合设施的空间，四年之内还会修建一座教堂。車轮已经开始转动，哈里。进步的車轮。这片战后的迷失之境——我无法忍受。事实上，我们的孩子在外面玩自己的*粪便*……不能再这样继续下去了。”

En: And I knew you're one too, Harry! Right when you rolled into town. I've got the centre, I've got room for a retail complex, and In four years I'll get the church too. The wheels are turning, Harry. The wheels of progress. This post-war limbo -- I won't stand for it. There are kids practically playing with their own *faeces* out there... It cannot go on.
Zh: “而且我知道你也一样，哈里！从你进城的那一刻起，我就知道了。我规划了中心区，规划了零售综合设施的空间，四年之内还会修建一座教堂。車轮已经开始转动，哈里。进步的車轮。这片战后的迷失之境——我无法忍受。事实上，我们的孩子在外面玩自己的*粪便*……不能再这样继续下去了。”

En: But will there be giant statues of the Claire brothers towering above it all? Joyce knew he was up to something.
Zh: 在那一切之上，会不会矗立着克莱尔兄弟的巨大雕像？乔伊斯知道他有自己的打算。

En: Joyce was right when she said you were up to something...
Zh: “乔伊斯说你有自己的打算，她说的没错……”

En: Sounds like you got this. You can rely on my vote.
Zh: “听起来你能行。我会为你投上支持的一票。”

En: Oh I do, Harry, I really do. He crosses his arms on his chest. "I'm gonna make the working man as rich as *Joyce Messier* with that vote of yours. That's my job. Just like yours is to keep the peace."
Zh: “哦，我会的，哈里，我真的会。”他把双臂交叉在胸口。“有了你支持的一票，我会让工人们变得跟*乔伊斯·梅西耶*一样富有。这就是我的工作。就好像你的工作是维护和平一样。”

En: Damn right I am, Harry! The fist lands on the table again. "I'm gonna make the working man as rich as she is one day. That's my job. Just like yours is to keep the peace."
Zh: “该死的，就是这样没错，哈里！”他的拳头又落到了桌面上。“总有一天，我会让工人变得和她一样富有。这就是我的工作。就好像你的工作是维护和平一样。”

En: Order it? You know my men didn't kill him. They told you -- it was a happy accident. You know how it is, no one takes the *initiative*. If I wanted him dead I would have had to do it myself. And I'm too fat for that.
Zh: “命令？你知道我的人没杀他。他们告诉过你——那不过是一起愉快的意外。你知道是怎么回事，没有人*主动*过。如果我想让他死，我必须自己下手。不过我太胖了，做不到。”

En: What do you gain from him being dead?
Zh: “他死了你有什么好处？”

En: Okay, another question.
Zh: “好吧，还有个问题。”

En: I'm listening, Harry.
Zh: “我在听，哈里。”

En: Victory, Mr. Kitsuragi. I have *victory* to gain. We are going to start a war with the Wild Pines Group -- and win before they even realize there *is* a war.
Zh: “胜利，曷城先生。我要赢得*胜利*。我们会跟野松公司开战——而且在他们还没意识到战争*已经*打响之前就取得胜利。”

En: They're trained military people. Aren't you afraid for your men?
Zh: “他们是受过训练的军人。难道你不为自己的手下感到害怕吗？”

En: Have you ever heard what two Giant Seraise Hornets can do to an entire colony of bees? They destroy it.
Zh: “你有没有听说过两只巨大的萨弗里黄蜂会对整个蜂群造成什么影响？它们会毁了它。”

En: I have. It's a great story, Harry. He nods. "Did you also know how the bee colony kills the giant hornet? They swarm and blanket it entirely, until it suffers a *massive heat stroke* and dies."
Zh: “是的。这是个很棒的故事，哈里。”他点点头。“那你知道蜂群是怎么杀死大黄蜂的吗？它们蜂拥而上，把它完全包裹起来，直到它*严重中暑*而死去。”

En: Harry, we outnumber them fifteen hundred to one. And that's just Martinaise. With all the unions in Revachol -- and with public opinion on our side -- we can hold off two men. Or fifteen men. Or even fifty men.
Zh: “哈里，我们对他们是1500比1。这还只是马丁内斯。算上瑞瓦肖的所有工会——还有公众舆论站在我们这一边——我们可以挡住两个人。或者是15个人。或者甚至是50个人。”

En: How is this connected to the strike?
Zh: “这跟罢工有什么关系？”

En: Harry, there is no strike, only war. Class war. Or, in business terms: a *dawn raid*. Or wait... He pauses to rub his chin. "Is that when you still *pay* them something? Because we won't do that."
Zh: “哈里，没有罢工，只有战争。阶级战争。或者，用商业术语来讲：一次*开盘抢购*。或者，等等……”他停下来，摸了摸下巴。“那代表着你还是需要付钱的对吧？因为我们可不会给他们任何东西。”

En: We're not gonna give nothing. We're gonna *take* Terminal B away from them: the roads, the gates, the containers, that big crane... even the damn coffee maker. We're gonna take all of it for the people -- and *fuck* Wild Pines.
Zh: “我们什么也不会给他们的。我们会从他们手里*拿走*中转站B：道路，大门，集装箱，大起重机……甚至是那个该死的咖啡机。我们会为了大家把一切全部拿走——还有，*去他妈的*野松公司吧。”

En: Is that why you haven't let Joyce in here?
Zh: “这就是你不放乔伊斯进来的原因吗？”

En: So that's why you haven't let Joyce in?
Zh: “这就是你不放乔伊斯进来的原因吗？”

En: Yes. It's also why I let that midget Gaumont go. He's too nice. I can't put him through this. Plus, he knows how to get in here. That woman can't tell her tits from her asshole. She has no chance.
Zh: “是的。这也是我为什么让那个小矮子戈蒙离开的原因。他人太好了。我不能让他经历这一切。再说了，他知道怎么在这里安身立命。那个女人根本牛头不对马嘴。她没有机会的。”

En: How are you going to fund your new independent harbour?
Zh: “你打算怎么为你的新独立港口提供资金呢？”

En: Oh, you mean what sort of *goods* are gonna be flowing through? How am I gonna replace all the contacts we'll lose once the poo-poo hits the fan? The clients who'll ditch us? Harry, we've thought of everything.
Zh: “哦，你的意思是什么样的*货物*会从这里流通？一旦捅了娄子，我怎么替换掉所有联系人？还有那些会抛弃我们的客户？哈里，我们已经全都想过了。”

En: Drugs are a no go for me. I'll *report* this.
Zh: “毒品在我这里就不行。我会*汇报*情况的。”

En: I have to admit: that's a well put-together plan. And far-removed from you.
Zh: “我必须承认：这是一个精心策划的计划。而且会让你脱胎换骨。”

En: Interesting stuff. I just want to solve this murder, okay?
Zh: “有意思。我只想解决这起凶杀案，好吗？”

En: Makes sense to regulate the drug trade like this. Keeps it out of more dangerous hands.
Zh: “这样控制毒品贸易是有道理的。能让它远离那些更危险的人。”

En: Yes, yes, of course, and while you're doing that, please go ahead and also tell the Wild Pines. He snickers. "I wanna hear them squeal from the indignity."
Zh: “是的，是的，当然，你这么做的时候，麻烦也顺便告诉一下野松公司。”他窃笑着。“我想听听他们因为屈辱而尖叫的声音。”

En: He smiles slyly. "It's also far-removed from my men and the people of Martinaise, who've put their trust in me."
Zh: 他狡猾地笑了。“他也会让我的人，还有那些信任我的马丁内斯人脱胎换骨。”

En: Anyway, let's not focus on the sensationalism of the drug trade. This hypothetical drug trade is all anyone ever seems to be interested in. It would only be a small part of the Harbour's turnover -- just like the harbour is but a small part of Martinaise.
Zh: “总之，我们先不要关注毒品贸易带来的轰动效应了。所有人似乎都对这个假想中的毒品贸易很感兴趣。它只会是港口营业额的很小一部分——就像港口也不过是马丁内斯的一小部分而已。”

En: You know why you're such a good detective, Harry? You don't get *sidetracked*. You care about the people you're supposed to protect, not some systems that may or may not be unethical.
Zh: “你知道自己为什么是个好警探吗，哈里？你不会*分心*。你在乎自己应该保护的人，而不是那些不确定道德与否的系统。”

En: Oh, Harry, you've misunderstood. I have no drugs in my hands. He raises both hands, palms open. "It's all far-removed from me like some half-remembered dream."
Zh: “哦，哈里，你误会了。我手上没有毒品。”他举起两只手，掌心向外。“那一切就像模糊的梦境，离我非常遥远。”

En: Is Ruby helping you secure this fantastic share?
Zh: “露比在帮你保护这份好到爆的收益吗？”

En: Harry, if I *was* supplying raw materials to drug manufacturers, I would need an army of Rubies.
Zh: “哈里，如果我*曾经*为毒品制造商供应过原材料的话，那我会需要一大群露比。”

En: The lieutenant nods slowly. "Understood."
Zh: 警督慢慢点头。“明白了。”

En: Okay, so that's basically his system here.
Zh: 好吧，他的系统大致就是这样了。

En: I'm gonna unite them all into one economic body. We're gonna incorporate this place to kingdom come. Everyone's gonna be in on the wealth. And *everyone's* gonna pull their weight.
Zh: “我要把他们全部纳入一个经济主体。我们要把这个地方合并成一个天国。每个人都会得到一份财富。而且*每个人*都会贡献自己的一份力量。”

En: Well, I mean, if it has the word *incorporate* in it, then I like it. I'm a money-guy.
Zh: “呃，我的意思是，如果里面出现了*合并*这个词，那我就挺喜欢的。我是那种金钱至上的人。”

En: That's very ambitious. I love what you're doing for the working man.
Zh: “你还真是很有野心啊。我喜欢你为工人做的一切。”

En: I'm not feeling a whole lot of *Revachol* here. Not enough flags or kings.
Zh: “我在这里并没有感受到太多的*瑞瓦肖*精神。旗帜不够，国王也不够。”

En: Honestly, it's not my place to judge or express an opinion.
Zh: “老实说，我没有资格去评判或者表达一个意见。”

En: Yes, if you start thinking about it like *that*, the socialist municipal body sort of is like a corporation, isn't it? It uses corporate law -- we're incorporated. I like to think we're using the best parts of *all* the ideologies here, Harry.
Zh: “是的，如果你也能开始*那么*想的话，民主社会的市政机构有点类似一个公司，不是吗？它实施公司法——我们都是法人。我认为我们在这里使用了*所有*意识形态最精华的部分，哈里。”

En: Thank you, Harry, thank you. He bows courteously in his chair. "You have no idea how much it means to me, because in many ways *you* are that working man. You have already done so much work."
Zh: “谢谢你，哈里，谢谢。”他坐在椅子上礼貌地鞠了一躬。“你不知道这对我来说有多大的意义，因为在很多方面，*你*也是一个工人。你已经做了这么多工作。”

En: Harry, the future I have in store for Martinaise will have all the *macho* aesthetics you love so much. The crowns, the guns... I will even slip in a skull and bones for you, my friend.
Zh: “哈里，我为马丁内斯计划的未来拥有很多你喜欢的*男子汉*美学。王冠，枪支……我甚至可以为你偷偷塞一个骷髅头和骨头进去，我的朋友。”

En: Harry, the length you're willing to go to keep your nose clean is *remarkable*. He stares at you lovingly. "You will always have a warm bed in Mr. Claire's household, my friend, and a special place in the future of Martinaise."
Zh: “哈里，这么长时间你一直保持这么的洁身自爱真的很*了不起*。”他亲切地凝望着你。“克莱尔先生的家里永远会为你保留一张温暖的床位，我的朋友，在未来的马丁内斯，也会有属于你的一个特殊位置。”

En: That's it for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “暂时先这样吧。”（总结。）

En: How many of you guys are there in the Union?
Zh: “你们工会有多少人？”

En: You can bother Leonard with that. He points to the door. "He loves to run his mouth on such matters. But I'm in Big-Time mode, Harry."
Zh: “这种事情你可以去麻烦利奥纳德。”他指向门口。“他很喜欢对这种事喋喋不休。不过我现在更像是处于大时代模式，哈里。”

En: 2,372, he replies like a whip. "Plus yours truly, of course."
Zh: “2,372，”他立刻回复到。“当然了，再加上你。”

En: Can I ask you about specific union members?
Zh: “我能问下某些特定的工会成员吗？”

En: A few more questions about the harbour.
Zh: “还有一些关于港口的问题。”

En: I'm always happy to educate and entertain you, my friend. He takes a sip from his coffee. "So, what's on your mind?"
Zh: “我总是很乐意培养和娱乐你的，我的朋友。”他抿了一口咖啡。“所以，你在想什么呢？”

En: Harry, I've got to be honest with you. He turns solemn. "Your gun was found two days ago. Withholding this information weighed heavily on me. But it had to be done."
Zh: “哈里，我可以跟你说实话。”他突然变得严肃起来。“你的枪在两天以前找到了。隐瞒这条消息给我带来了很沉重的负担。不过我必须要这么做。”

En: You bastard!
Zh: “你这个混账！”

En: Fine, where is it?
Zh: “好吧，它在哪？”

En: Your gun is with an old woman, he says, absolutely unperturbed by your outburst. "I hear she's a character, so watch out."
Zh: “你的枪在一个老妇人手里，”他说着，完全没有受到你爆发的情绪干扰。“我听说她性格很怪，所以要小心。”

En: An old woman has it and, let me tell you, Harry, word on the street is she's a character. So watch out.
Zh: “枪在一个老妇人手里，我可以告诉你，哈里，街上有人传说她性格很怪，所以要小心。”

En: How did this woman come to be in possession of the firearm?
Zh: “这个女人怎么会有武器呢？”

En: How did this old woman get a hold of my gun?
Zh: “我的枪为什么会在这个女人手里呢？”

En: Turns out she bought the gun from Roy, the local pawnbroker. And honestly, Harry... He winks at you three times in quick succession. "I have *no idea* how your gun ended up in Roy's hands."
Zh: “结果她是从本地的当铺老板罗伊那里买来的。说实话，哈里……”他飞快地朝你连眨了三下眼。“我*不知道*你的枪怎么会落在罗伊手里的。”

En: Someone must have *stolen* it from you while you were sleeping. He smiles. It's really friendly. "Anyway, the neighbours of this old lady contacted my men because they trust me and the Débardeurs Union. Apparently she was waving it around at the entrance to her building."
Zh: “肯定是有人在你睡觉的时候*偷走*的。”他笑了。真的很友好。“不管怎么说，这个老妇人的邻居联系了我的人，因为他们相信我和这个装卸工会。她好像是拿着枪在自家门口到处挥舞。”

En: She was waving it around at people?
Zh: “她朝着别人挥枪吗？”

En: As I said she's a character. I didn't have time for details. He smiles. "It sounds like she's unstable, but don't worry. No one got hurt."
Zh: “我说过，她性格很怪。我没时间了解太多细节。”他笑了。“听起来她不太稳定，不过别担心。没有人会受伤的。”

En: Who *is* this old woman?
Zh: “这个老女人*是*谁？”

En: Unfortunately, I don't know any more. You're gonna have to go in blind, Harry. But she's an old lady -- how dangerous can she possibly be? Oh, and she calls herself the Pigs.
Zh: “很可惜，这我就不知道了。你只能这么去见她了，哈里。不过她是个老妇人——所以能有多危险呢？哦，还有她称呼自己是猪猪女侠。”

En: I, for one, find it refreshing. Finally someone calls *themselves* a pig. A smile flickers in the corner of his mouth.
Zh: “举例来说，我，觉得这挺让人神清气爽的。总算是有人称呼*自己*是猪猪女侠了。”他的嘴角闪过一丝微笑。

En: Can you set up a meeting? (Conclude.)
Zh: “你能安排见一面吗？”（总结。）

En: I already have! He holds out his index finger. "Tonight, starting 22.00. Near the old fish market on the coast -- the one on the boardwalk, a little past the fishing village. Be careful, Harry. I would never set you up for anything dangerous, but you *did* ask for this. Now..." He claps his hands. "Back to the fun stuff."
Zh: “已经安排好了！”他竖起食指。“今晚22点，在海岸边的旧鱼市附近——就是木板道附近那个，离渔村不远。要小心，哈里。我宁愿你不去做任何冒险的事，不过你*确实*是自己要求的。现在……”他拍拍手。“说回有意思的事吧。”

En: That's it? He raises his eyebrows. "But you haven't asked about your gun yet. We have so much more to talk about."
Zh: “就这些？”他扬起眉头。“但是你还没有问我枪的事呢。我们还有很多可以聊的。”

En: Great, Harry, great! I think we have truly built a bridge between Martinaise and Jamrock today. We have united the RCM and the Débardeurs' Union... Suddenly there's sadness in his tone.
Zh: “很好，哈里，太好了！看来今天我们真的在马丁内斯和加姆洛克之间搭建了一座桥梁。我们让RCM和装卸工会联合起来了……”突然间，他的口气有些悲伤。

En: This... He points to you, then himself. "... has been *so* great. I'm sorry we don't have more fun things to do together, but if you ever feel like bouncing something off me, my door is always open."
Zh: “这个……”他指向你，然后指指自己。“……这一切真是*太*棒了。很抱歉，我们没能在一起做更多有趣的事情，不过如果你想从我这里得到些什么意见的话，我的大门随时为你敞开。”

En: Very nice, Harry. Is there anything else?
Zh: “非常好，哈里。还有别的事吗？”

En: He leans forward. "Harry, enough is enough! We're taking this district back. The war was fifty years ago, for god's sake. It's time to move on."
Zh: 他向后靠了靠。“哈里，够了！我们要把这片区域夺回来。看在老天爷的份上，战争已经是50年前的事了。是时候该向前看了。”

En: Why *are* you so fat?
Zh: “你*为什么*这么胖？”

En: Glad you asked. I've got Type 2 diabetes because sugar and fat was all my mother had to give me and my brother Edgar when we were kids.
Zh: “很高兴你这么问了。我得了二型糖尿病，因为我和我的兄弟埃德加小的时候，妈妈能给我们的只有糖和脂肪。”

En: He wags his finger at you. "Good job too, as it made me ugly. And ugly people, Harry, are *much* better at politics."
Zh: 他朝你摆摆手指。“其实这样挺好的，让我变丑了。而丑陋的人呢，哈里，要*更擅长*政治一些。”

En: Why, a war, of course.
Zh: “为什么这么问，当然是一场战争了。”

En: And what do you have to gain from a war?
Zh: “那你能从战争里得到什么好处呢？”

En: Yes, but what do you have to gain from a war?
Zh: “是的，但你要通过战争获得什么？”

En: Victory, Harry. I have *victory* to gain. We are going to start a war with the Wild Pines Group -- and win before they even realize there *is* a war.
Zh: “胜利，哈里。我要赢得*胜利*。我们会跟野松公司开战——而且在他们还没意识到战争*已经*打响之前就取得胜利。”

En: Why are you telling me your plans?
Zh: “你为什么要把自己的计划告诉我？”

En: Because we're friends, Harry! Besides, it doesn't matter now. You can go tell her, if you want -- it won't change the course of events. We have a significant head start.
Zh: “因为我们是朋友，哈里！再说了，现在已经不重要了。如果你愿意，你可以告诉她——这样也不会改变事情的进程。我们取得了一个良好的开端。”

En: The more they send, the worse it's going to look for them. They made a *huge* mistake hiring those guys. *No one* likes foreign mercenaries. The leftists hate them, the fascists hate them, even the moralists think they're *in bad taste*.
Zh: “他们派的人越多，他们的样子就会越难看。雇佣那些人是他们犯下的一个*巨大的*错误。*没人*喜欢外国雇佣兵。左翼分子痛恨他们，法西斯分子痛恨他们，甚至连道德家都觉得他们是*粗俗*的。”

En: Yeah, everyone hates them. Except ultraliberals maybe. Which happens to be me.
Zh: “是啊，所有人都恨他们。也许只有极端自由主义者除外。而我正好就是。”

En: You're not an ultraliberal, Harry. Get the fuck out of here. Don't be a retard. He smiles warmly.
Zh: “你不是极端自由主义者，哈里。赶紧离开这里吧。别当个窝囊废。”他亲切地笑了。

En: What?
Zh: “什么？”

En: Tits from her asshole -- it's a local saying.
Zh: “牛头不对马嘴——这是本地的一种说法。”

En: Actually, no, it's not.
Zh: 其实呢，不是，不是这样的。

En: I did not, Harry. He shakes his head energetically. "Although I am very, very glad he's dead."
Zh: “我没有，哈里。”他用力摇摇头。“虽然他死了我真的非常、非常高兴。”

En: Who killed the hanged man?
Zh: “谁杀了吊人？”

En: No idea. Could have been his own mother for all I know. If you ever find the guy, give him a big fat kiss from Evrart Claire. Couldn't have done it without him.
Zh: “不知道。据我所知，说不定是他自己的母亲。不过如果你找到那个家伙，记得代大胖艾弗拉特·克莱尔亲他一口。要是没有他，我们也做不到这一切。”

En: He really doesn't know.
Zh: 他真的不知道。

En: How do you know it's a guy?
Zh: “你怎么知道是一个男人？”

En: A guy, huh?
Zh: 一个男人，哈？

En: I don't. I told you it could have been his own mother... I'm pretty sure it wasn't anyone from the Union. Maybe it was the mob... or maybe he killed himself 'cause he was a closet socialist? Truth is, I simply don't know.
Zh: “我不知道。我告诉过你可能是他自己的母亲……我肯定不是工会的人。也许是暴徒……或者也许他是自杀的，‘因为他背地里是个工人主义者’？事实是，我真的不知道。”

En: He really doesn't.
Zh: 他真的不知道。

En: We're way past *specific* Union members now. This is the Big Time. His eyes are shining. "We're talking about the future of Revachol here, Harry."
Zh: “我们现在已经远远超过了*特定的*工会成员。现在是一个大时代。”他的眼睛在闪烁。“我们讨论的可是瑞瓦肖的未来，哈里。”

En: There's something different about him now. He's more vibrant. More alive in his 'Big-Time mode'.
Zh: 现在他有些不太一样了，变得更有活力，更像是活在了他的‘大时代’里。

En: Calls herself *the Pigs?* That sounds bad.
Zh: 叫自己*猪猪女侠*？听起来有点糟糕。

En: Clients would take a well-known multinational conglomerate over a local mobster any day.
Zh: 任何时候，客户都会选择一个声名远播的跨国企业，而不是一个本地暴徒。

En: Everything?
Zh: “所有事？”

En: You can't possibly hope to continue like you have. Clients will leave en masse.
Zh: “你不可能希望一切会像你希望的那样继续下去。客户会一起离开的。”

En: We've been running back channel negotiations with all the major clients. I think the company will be *unpleasantly* surprised to see how many of them stay loyal to Martinaise. And to the new, competitive contracts we can offer.
Zh: “我们一直在跟所有主要客户进行渠道谈判。我想公司会得到一个*不太愉快*的惊喜，特别是看到有多少人愿意对马丁内斯保持忠诚的时候。而且还有我们提供的新的、更有竞争力的合同。”

En: Sure, some will go, but mark my words: the company will be *unpleasantly* surprised to see how many of them stay loyal to Martinaise. And to the new, competitive contracts we can offer.
Zh: “当然，有些人会离开，但是记住我说的话：公司会*郁闷*而又惊讶地看到，有多少人愿意对马丁内斯保持忠诚。而且还有我们提供的新的、更有竞争力的合同。”

En: With renewed zeal sparked by communal ownership, the men will be shipping those containers double time. You'll be surprised to see how fast things go without parasites latching on. We'll have our hands free to pursue bold, exotic new revenue streams.
Zh: “当公社所有制点燃了人们新的狂热后，他们会以双倍的速度去运送那些集装箱。你会惊讶地看到没有了那些寄生虫，事情进展地会有多么地迅速。我们也可以放手自由地去追求更大胆、更新的收入来源。”

En: That's drugs!
Zh: 那就是毒品！

En: By bold new revenues you mean the drug trade?
Zh: “你说的大胆的新收入来源是贩毒吗？”

En: Drug trafficking.
Zh: “贩毒。”

En: Drug trafficking? Don't be stupid, Mr. Kitsuragi. There are perfectly legal, 100% ethical chemical factories on the Samaran isola. You don't need to be *colonialist* about it.
Zh: “贩毒？别傻了，曷城先生。它们完全是合法的，来自萨马拉洲100%符合道德标准的化学工厂。你不用为此成为*殖民主义者*的。”

En: All they do is produce components to keep the pharmaceutical industry running. That's people's health we're talking about. Old grannies, little babes, people with disabilities...
Zh: “他们所做的只是生产部件，来维持制药业的运转。人们的健康问题可不能拿来说笑啊。老奶奶，小婴儿，残障人士……”

En: Drug trade? Now you're being stupid, Harry. There are perfectly legal, 100% ethical chemical factories on the Samaran isola.
Zh: “贩毒？看你又傻了吧，哈里。它们完全是合法的，来自萨马拉洲100%符合道德标准的化学工厂。”

En: The company thinks transporting these chemicals in bulk 'looks bad,' he makes air quotes, "'has bad optics,' 'may be illegal in some countries.' The Débardeurs' Union, however... we're all about the large volume column."
Zh: “公司认为大量运送这些化学制品‘看起来不太好’，”他用手比了个双引号，“‘影响不好啦’，‘在某些国家可能是违法的’。然而，装卸工会……我们只关心最重要的货物体量。”

En: That's just the top of the iceberg, though, isn't it?
Zh: “尽管这不过是冰山一角，不是吗？”

En: God, that sounds shady...
Zh: “天啦，听起来真的有些见不得人……”

En: We're gonna transport the living daylights out of those materials, Harry. His slams his fist on the desk once more. "So your sick kid can get his *benafed* and your wacky uncle doesn't have to come off *risperizole*!"
Zh: “我们要从这些材料中运送出鲜活的生命之光，哈里。”他再次用拳头砸向桌面。“这样你那生病的孩子可以拿到*苯呐啡得*，还有你那位古怪的叔叔也不用戒掉*利培唑*了！”

En: *Benafed* is children's cold medicine, usually apricot flavoured, and *risperizole* is used to treat severe psychosis.
Zh: *苯呐啡得*是小孩子用的感冒药，一般是杏子口味的。*利培唑*是用来治疗严重精神错乱的。

En: And the kids on the street don't have to give up on speed and pyrholidon?
Zh: “而且街上的孩子也不用戒掉思必得和吡嚯浣酮了？”

En: And the kids on the street can get speed and pyrholidon?
Zh: “那样街上的孩子就能拿到思必得和吡嚯浣酮了？”

En: I'm an old-fashioned guy, Mr. Kitsuragi. I sometimes grab a beer with the boys, but I have no idea about the things you just mentioned. He smiles.
Zh: “我是个老派的人，曷城先生。有时候我会跟小伙子们一起喝杯啤酒，不过我真的不知道你刚说的是什么东西。”他笑了。

En: I'm an old-fashioned guy, Harry. I sometimes grab a beer with the boys, but I have no idea about the things you just mentioned.
Zh: “我是个老派的人，哈里。有时候我会跟小伙子们一起喝杯啤酒，不过我真的不知道你刚说的是什么东西。”

En: He's basically admitting to it.
Zh: 他基本算是承认了。

En: Nothing in his features betrays any concern.
Zh: 他的脸上没有流露出任何担忧的神情。

En: It would still be illegal.
Zh: “那还是非法的。”

En: So is there a trade or isn't there?
Zh: “所以到底是有还是没有？”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: Let's look at the big picture. Martinaise as a whole. There are little girls out there with dreams of making music. Young mothers who want to start businesses. Models who want to walk catwalks and steel welders who want to weld steel.
Zh: “让我们看看大局。看看整个马丁内斯。有些小姑娘拥有音乐梦想。年轻母亲想要自己创业。模特想要走上伸展台，电焊工想要焊接钢铁。”

En: Let's keep focusing on the drug trade. He was almost admitting to it.
Zh: 我们还是专注毒品贸易吧。他几乎算是承认了。

En: Hold on, I don't want to look at the big picture. I want to look at the drug trade you almost admitted to.
Zh: “等等，我不想看什么大局。我想看看你差不多已经承认的毒品交易。”

En: No, no, Harry. That's boring. He sighs. "Alright, it's gone! The hypothetical raw materials trade is off the table. It's such a small and insignificant slice of revenue, I'm cutting it."
Zh: “不不，哈里。那就太无聊了。”他叹了口气。“好吧，它没了！假想的原材料贸易已经离开台面了。那份利润实在是太小、太没价值，我已经开始削减了。”

En: Boys... He looks around the container. "Harry felt queasy about it. We're not doing it. Can we talk about my beautiful incorporated Martinaise and its many-sided business ventures now? This bold new vision of incorporated socialism I'm offering?"
Zh: “小伙子们……”他环顾着集装箱四周。“哈里感觉到有些反胃。我们不会这么做的。现在能不能聊聊我心中美丽的马丁内斯法人组织，还有各种各样的附加产业呢？谈谈我提出的这个工人主义法人的新愿景呢？”

En: Oh yeah, skull and bones! Skull and bones on the Revacholian flag. The Revacholian Death Squad. Oh yeah! The visual image has completely bedazzled you. You're 100% with Evrart now.
Zh: 哦耶，骷髅头和骨头！在瑞瓦肖的旗帜上画上骷髅头和骨头。瑞瓦肖敢死队。哦耶！这幅景象已经彻底让你眼花缭乱了。现在你100%站到了艾弗拉特这一边。

En: Did he just deflate slightly? Was there something he wanted you to ask about? Maybe you should come back if you learn something relevant and ask again.
Zh: 他刚才是不是稍稍有些泄气？他是不是有话要问你？如果了解到相关信息的话，也许你应该回来再问一遍。

En: Do you remember you said you'd fund your new independent harbour with bold, new exotic revenue streams?
Zh: “还记得你说过要用大胆的、外来的新收益流为你的新独立港口提供资金吗？”

En: I sure do, Harry. He smiles at you idly twirling his thumbs. "One of our many great conversations. Heck, one of my favourites! What about it?"
Zh: “当然记得了，哈里。”他懒洋洋地捻弄着大拇指，朝你笑了。“我们有很多段伟大的对话，这是其中之一。见鬼，这可是我最喜欢的一个！怎么了？”

En: By revenue -- did you mean *drug trade*?
Zh: “你说的收益——是*贩毒*吗？”

En: Cool. I'm glad you remember. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “不错。你还记得，我很高兴。我们聊点别的吧。”

En: He nods. "Sure thing, champ. Lead the way!"
Zh: 他点点头。“当然了，冠军。你带路吧！”

En: What kinds of bold, new exotic revenue streams are we talking about?
Zh: “我们说的哪种大胆的、外来的新收益流？”

En: You should definitely come back here and ask again if you learn more about the Union's dealings. There's something he's not telling you.
Zh: 等你对工会的交易有更多了解之后，一定要回来再问一遍。他有些话没有告诉你。

En: Haven't you seen the crates outside, Harry? There's all sorts of fun stuff inside them! He pushes up his glasses as he laughs. "I mean, heck -- this one has you and the lieutenant, me, and my novelty swordfish clock!"
Zh: “你没看见外面的箱子吗，哈里？那里面有各种各样有趣的东西！”他推了推眼镜，笑了。“我是说，嗨——这一个有你，警督，我，还有我这个新奇的剑鱼钟！”

En: Haven't you seen the crates outside, Harry? There's all sorts of fun stuff inside them! He pushes up his glasses as he laughs. "I mean, heck -- this one has you, me, and my novelty swordfish clock!"
Zh: “你没看见外面的箱子吗，哈里？那里面有各种各样有趣的东西！”他推了推眼镜，笑了。“我是说，嗨——这一个有你，我，还有我这个新奇的剑鱼钟！”

En: The golden boy returns once more! Wonderful -- simply wonderful, Harry. He claps his hands together like a child who's just been offered cotton candy. "Of course, I already knew this."
Zh: “黄金小子又回来了！太棒了——真是太棒了，哈里。”他像收到棉花糖的孩子一样开心地拍了拍手。“当然，我已经知道了。”

En: Oh, *bratan*, you played the old man like a three-string banjo. He has no idea.
Zh: 哦，*兄弟*，你像拨弄三弦班卓琴一样玩弄这个老家伙，而他并不知情。

En: *If* she actually wants to see me, she will find a way. Any good negotiator would. And I just don't have anything to discuss with a bad negotiator.
Zh: “*如果*她真想见我，她会想出办法的。任何一个称职的交涉人都能做到。而且我跟一个不称职的交涉人真的没什么好说的。”

En: And I'm sure you two had a hell of a time. He throws a couple of playful punches in your direction. "But I couldn't care less about the bitch. Let's talk about the *real* stuff, Harry. The stuff that matters."
Zh: “而且我肯定你们俩一定会度过一段愉快的时光。”他朝你的方向开玩笑似的挥了几拳。“但是我一点都不在意那个贱人。我们聊点*真事*吧，哈里。那些真正重要的事。”

En: Okay then. Positive emotions it is! You like positive emotions.
Zh: 那好吧。那就积极情绪吧！你喜欢积极情绪。

En: Hmh... The lieutenant seems incredulous.
Zh: “嗯……”警督似乎有些怀疑。

En: Sure, he thinks. It's about *transparency*.
Zh: 当然，他想到。这是关于*透明度*的。

En: It's hard to say if he really lost his temper or if this is another one of his tricks. This man... almost never angers visibly.
Zh: 很难说他是不是真的发脾气了，或者这又是他的一个诡计。这个男人……几乎从来看不出明显的生气。

En: That much is true. His heart *truly* is in it. Though you wouldn't think so by looking at him.
Zh: 那倒是真的。他的心*真的*在这里。虽然看着他的你不会这样想。

En: This particular brand of humour he has... it makes for a fine distraction.
Zh: 他的这种独特的幽默感……是一种很不错的消遣。

En: If it's spilled blood you're looking for then there certainly isn't any in his expression, or demeanour now.
Zh: 如果你看到是暴力杀人犯，那现在他的表情或者举止都没有任何异样。

En: His expression makes it clear: this is childish and irresponsible behaviour.
Zh: 他的表情表示地很清楚：这是幼稚又不负责任的行为。

En: It's a fortress. He knows she can't get in here.
Zh: 这里是一座堡垒。他知道她是进不来的。

En: He doesn't *want* to see her. It's simple as that.
Zh: 他*不想*见她。就这么简单。

En: It *is* rather interesting to tell people things about each other isn't it? It was nice telling him about her right now...
Zh: 告诉人们彼此的事情*其实*挺有意思的，不是吗？现在把她的事情告诉他就挺好的……

En: Tell her about *all* of it. My brother's picture, my singing swordfish clock. He looks around. "Tell her how overweight I am and how I've helped you with your lost gun. Tell her everything -- Evrart doesn't mind."
Zh: “*全都*告诉她吧。我兄弟的相片，我这个会唱歌的剑鱼钟。”他看了看周围。“告诉她我的负担有多重，还有我是怎么帮你寻找失枪的。把一切都告诉她吧——艾弗拉特不介意的。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I'm helping you find your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，而且还在帮你找失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是个爱妒忌的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I helped you find your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，而且帮你找过失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是个爱妒忌的人。”

En: Yes, we're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you shouldn't work with her just because you and I are such good friends. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “是的，我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得因为我和你是这么好的朋友，你就不应该跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I tried to help you with the body... and *helped* you find your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，帮你放下那具尸体……而且还*帮你*找到了失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I tried to help you with the body... *and* finding your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，帮你放下那具尸体……*而且*还在帮你找失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I helped you get the body down... *and* I'm helping you find your gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，帮你放下了那具尸体……*而且*还在帮你找失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I helped you get the body down... *and* to find your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，帮你放下了那具尸体……*而且*还在帮你寻找失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I helped you get that nasty body down from the tree. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，而且还帮你把那具恶心的尸体从树上弄了下来，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I tried to help you with the body... I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，而且还试着想帮你搞定那具尸体，你就不能跟她合作……我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I'm helping you get that nasty body down from the tree. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，而且还想帮你把那具恶心的尸体从树上弄下来，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I'm helping you get that nasty body down from the tree... *and* with finding your lost gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，想帮你把那具恶心的尸体从树上弄下来，*而且*还帮你寻找失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: We're all trying to do what's best for Martinaise here. His smile widens. "Don't feel like you can't cooperate with her, because you and I are such good friends and I'm helping you get that nasty body down... *and* I helped you find your gun. I'm not a jealous guy."
Zh: “我们都在竭尽全力为马丁内斯做最好的打算。”他的笑容很灿烂。“不要觉得我和你是这么好的朋友，想帮你把那具恶心的尸体从树上弄下来，*而且*还帮你找到了失枪，你就不能跟她合作。我不是一个好嫉妒的人。”

En: Oh, that's very nice. I haven't gotten around to her yet -- I'm very, very busy, you see. He adjusts a button on his sleeve. "I hope you're getting along."
Zh: “哦，那很好啊。我还没考虑过她的事情呢——你也看到了，我是个非常、非常忙的人。”他调整了一下袖子上的纽扣。“我希望你们能够和睦相处。”

En: Great! You wanna bounce something off your old friend, Mr. Claire, right? He seems genuinely pleased. "Well, let's hear it, Harry!"
Zh: “太棒了！你想征求一下你的老朋友，克莱尔先生的意见，对吗？”他好像真的很开心。“那就说来听听吧，哈里！”

En: One thing I wanna make very clear, Harry, is that this is not some kind of Union *versus* Corporation situation. Everyone is just pals here.
Zh: “有一件事我需要讲明白，哈里，并不存在什么工会*对抗*公司的情况。在这里大家都是兄弟。”

En: Just pals?
Zh: “都是兄弟？”

En: Whoa... that's so nice of him. Suspiciously nice.
Zh: 哇哦……他还真的挺好的，好的有些可疑。

En: Are you sure? I find it a little odd.
Zh: “你确定吗？我觉得有点奇怪。”
