En: I told Joyce that I met you.
Zh: “我跟乔伊斯说我见过你。”

En: What happened to the previous negotiator, Mr. Gaumont?
Zh: “之前的那个交涉人，戈蒙先生怎么样了？”

En: Joyce said the previous Union leader vanished under suspicious circumstances.
Zh: “乔伊斯说之前的工会领袖在可疑的情况下消失了。”

En: Evrart, Joyce seems to think the Union is low-balling her.
Zh: “艾弗拉特，乔伊斯好像觉得你在跟她虚报低价。”

En: Why haven't you let her in to see you?
Zh: “你为什么不让她进来见你？”

En: Okay, let's talk about something else. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好吧，我们说说别的。”（总结。）

En: Of course, Harry. Let me just assure you one more time -- it's perfectly okay to share anything we discuss here with this... *Joyce*. This is a completely transparent organization.
Zh: “当然，哈里。我再向你保证一次——把我们聊的一切都分享给这位……*乔伊斯*是完全没有问题的。我们是一个完全透明的组织。”

En: This is weird. Not *nice* weird, but okay.
Zh: 有点不可思议。不是*好的*那种，不过还行。

En: It's *perfectly* okay. Even if you've told her everything we've talked about, it's *absolutely* fine.
Zh: “真的*完全*没问题。就算你把我们聊的一切都告诉她，都*完全*没问题。”

En: He seems happy about the prospect of you telling Joyce about your conversations.
Zh: 你打算把谈话内容告诉乔伊斯的前景，似乎让他感觉很开心。

En: You cannot but notice the prospect of you telling Joyce about your conversations is actually pleasing Evrart.
Zh: 你不禁注意到一点：计划把谈话内容告诉乔伊斯让艾弗拉特真的很高兴。

En: It sounds like you *want* me to tell her about our conversations.
Zh: “听起来你*想*让我把我们的谈话内容告诉她。”

En: No, no, Harry. I'm perfectly neutral *and* one hundred percent for transparency. I know people say a lot of bad things about the Débardeurs Union, but we are actually, -- he squeaks his chair -- "squeaky clean."
Zh: “不不不，哈里。我完全中立，*而且*这样100%是为了公开透明。我知道大家说了很多装卸工会的坏话，不过实际上，”——他把椅子弄得吱吱响——-“我们极其干净。”

En: What do you mean, Harry? The big man sounds annoyed. "Nothing. I let him go."
Zh: “你是什么意思，哈里？”大块头男人好像有些恼火。“没什么。我让他走的。”

En: He made concessions for the company in previous negotiations. Why would you let an ally like that go?
Zh: “在之前的谈判中，他对公司做出了让步。你为什么会放走一个这样的盟友呢？”

En: Okay, another question?
Zh: “好吧，再问个问题？”

En: Empty all your barrels on me, Harry.
Zh: “还有什么就尽管说吧，哈里。”

En: He's an old man, Harry. I wanted him to spend more time with his family. He looks down and sighs. "God knows how long he's got left."
Zh: “他年纪大了，哈里。我想让他能有更多时间跟家人待在一起。”他低下头，叹了口气。“天知道他还剩下多少日子。”

En: You called him a midget.
Zh: “你称呼他小矮子。”

En: Harry! he exclaims, indignant. "I have *little people* in my organization. I would never call someone a midget. What is this?"
Zh: “哈里！”他愤愤不平地呼喊。“我的组织里有一些*小个子*。我永远也不会称呼别人是小矮子的。你这是干什么？”

En: *Vanished*?! Harry, the woman left her casserole in the oven and couldn't make it here in time for the voting.
Zh: “*消失*？！哈里，那个女人把炖菜忘在烤箱里了，结果没来得及参加这里的选举。”

En: 'Oh, did I leave my casserole on? Better go home and check. The election can wait!' The man frowns, disapprovingly. "When she got back the whole thing was over."
Zh: “‘哦，我的炖菜是不是没关火？最好还是回家看看去。选举可以等的！’”男人不以为然地皱起眉头。“等她回来的时候，整件事都结束了。”

En: Wait... there was no mention of a casserole from Joyce.
Zh: 等等……乔伊斯没有提过炖菜的事。

En: Yes, yes -- *low-balling*. Of course... He's suddenly very serious. "This isn't a casino, Harry. Real people, real livelihoods are at stake here. But everything's a casino for those rich types..."
Zh: “是啊，是啊——*虚报低价*。当然……”他突然变得严肃起来。“这不是在赌博，哈里。实实在在的人，实实在在的生活正处于紧要关头。但是对于那些有钱人来说，一切都是赌博……”

En: Funny, Joyce didn't mention any casserole.
Zh: “有意思，乔伊斯没提过炖菜的事。”

En: Another question then?
Zh: “那再问个问题？”

En: Yes, please, Harry! The big man nods slowly. "Feel free to speak your mind or ask me anything."
Zh: “可以的，请问吧，哈里！”大块头男人慢慢点头。“随意跟我说说你的想法，或者问我任何问题。”

En: Harry, Harry, Harry! He flicks his fingers. "Do not fixate on this little matter. Maybe it was a rabbit stew... or a hair dryer, or an iron. The point is, her heart wasn't in it. Mine *was*."
Zh: “哈里，哈里，哈里！”他打了个响指。“不要老是纠结这种小事嘛。也许是兔肉煲……或者吹风机，或者是电熨斗。问题在于，她的心不在这里。我的*在*。”

En: Okay, that's cool.
Zh: “好吧，那挺酷的。”

En: Very cool, Harry. He nods enthusiastically. "It's very cool."
Zh: “非常酷，哈里。”他热情地点点头。“非常酷。”

En: Absolutely, Harry! What's on your mind?
Zh: “绝对可以，哈里！你在想什么呢？”

En: I have no interest in what she is doing, but I myself have *nothing* to hide. Your business is your business and I respect your privacy. Just remember, none of this... He makes an all-encompassing gesture. "... is secret."
Zh: “我对她在做什么完全没兴趣，但是我个人没有*任何*隐瞒。你的事是你的事，我尊重你的隐私。只要记住，这一切……”他比划了一个包容一切的手势。“……都不是秘密。”

En: I'm just a nice guy, Harry. I wouldn't be where I am now if I wasn't nice. He slams his fist into his hand. "Politics is all about *emotions*, and I want you to have positive emotions when you think of me."
Zh: “我只是个好人，哈里。如果我不好，那就不会坐到现在这个位置。”他用拳头砸向自己的手掌。“政治是完全关乎于*情感*的，我希望你想到我的时候能有积极的情绪。”

En: Yes, he really doesn't seem to mind.
Zh: 是的，他好像真的不介意。

En: Yes, he really doesn't seem to mind.
Zh: 是的，他好像真的不介意。

En: Honestly, I'm beginning to think *you're* a midget, Harry. Abruptly, he smiles and changes his tone. "I'm only kidding, Harry. You're not a midget. No one is. We're pals."
Zh: “老实说，我开始觉得*你*是个小矮子了，哈里。”他突然笑了起来，换了种口气。“我只是在开玩笑，哈里。你不是小矮子。没有人是。大家都是兄弟。”

En: Tell her about all of it. My brother's picture, my singing swordfish clock. He looks around. "Tell her how overweight I am and how I'm helping you find your lost gun. Tell her about everything -- Evrart doesn't mind."
Zh: “全都告诉她吧。我兄弟的相片，我这个会唱歌的剑鱼钟。”他看了看周围。“告诉她我的负担有多重，还有我是怎么帮你寻找失枪的。把一切都告诉她吧——艾弗拉特不介意的。”

En: (Sings.) "Container, container, container!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“集装箱，集装箱，集装箱！”

En: (Sings.) "Soon, oh soon the green is gone and everything is re-eeed!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“很快，哦很快，绿色就不见了，一切都会变成红色！”

En: (Sings.) "Plants are happy, boots are shining, every man is fed!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“植物在欢笑，靴子在闪耀，所有人都吃饱饱！”

En: (Sings.) "Leo, Leo, Leo -- Evrart will be pleased!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“利奥，利奥，利奥——艾弗拉特会很高兴！”

En: (Sings.) Leo, Leo, Easy Leo -- nothing he can't do-ooooo..."
Zh: （唱歌。）“利奥，利奥，老好人利奥——没有什么他做不到……”

En: (Sings.) "Container, container, container!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“集装箱，集装箱，集装箱！”

En: (Sings.) "Soon, oh soon the green is gone and everything is re-eeed!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“很快，哦很快，绿色就不见了，一切都会变成红色！”

En: (Sings.) "Leo, Leo, Leo -- Evrart will be pleased!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“利奥，利奥，利奥——艾弗拉特会很高兴！”

En: (Sings.) "Plants are happy, boots are shining, every man is fed!"
Zh: （唱歌。）“植物在欢笑，靴子在闪耀，所有人都吃饱饱！”

En: (Sings.) Leo, Leo, Easy Leo -- nothing he can't do-ooooo..."
Zh: （唱歌。）“利奥，利奥，老好人利奥——没有什么他做不到……”

En: Squint.
Zh: 眯起眼睛看。

En: Cover your eyes...
Zh: 捂住你的眼睛。

En: Actually -- at the level this guy is, it takes several generations to do that, but all right.
Zh: 其实——要想达到这人的水平，需要好几代人的共同努力，不过好吧。

En: Exactly! He agrees. "A world of slavery and violence. Which brings us back to the essential truth of modernism..."
Zh: “没错！”他表示赞同。“一个充满奴役和暴力的世界。这就把我们都带回到现代主义的本质特征……”

En: Yeah, that last one. Ile-de-Casherbrume.
Zh: “是的，最后一个。雾金岛。”

En: Maybe I should? Sadly, I've never been there. I'm sure it's nice but in the end -- does it matter?
Zh: “也许我应该这样？可悲的是，我从来没有去过那里。我肯定它挺好的，不过最后——这重要吗？”

En: That's not good -- for you.
Zh: 这不太好——对于你来说。

En: A man this *chill* is at least a triple-digit billionaire.
Zh: 一个这么*冷漠*的人，至少是个三位数的亿万富翁。

En: In the very, very early days of colonizing this archipelago, the Kingdom of Suresne, a precursor of modern Sur-La-Clef, used to own the city of Revachol. An obscure detail in the bigger picture, but still -- worth dropping.
Zh: 在殖民这个群岛的早期，苏雷恩王国，也就是现在苏拉菲的前身，曾经占领过瑞瓦肖城。从更大的全局来看，这不过是一个模糊的细节，不过——依然值得一提。

En: Wow.
Zh: “哇哦。”

En: Yes. I imagine that *does* look strange to you. My container.
Zh: “是的。我能想象到，在你来看*的确*很奇怪。我的集装箱。”

En: Okay. Well. Maybe I was wrong. We've all learned something today.
Zh: 好吧。也许我错了。今天我们都学到了一些东西。

En: Stop embarrassing yourself. Pop a magnesium and calm down.
Zh: 别再让自己尴尬了。吃点镁，冷静一下。

En: Have you considered breaking into the local art market?
Zh: “你有考虑过进军当地艺术市场吗？”

En: We've hit the big time! This is the man who's going to make you *rich*. Always rely on your friendly neighbourhood swindler.
Zh: 我们撞了大运！他就是那个会让你*发财*的人。永远依靠你友好的骗子邻居。

En: You better explain that you aren't Savvy, by the way. Can't have you nicking this identity after working hard to gain such a prodigious rep.
Zh: 顺便说一句，你最好解释一下，你不是鬼鬼。好不容易才获得了这么好的名声，怎么能让你盗用这个身份。

En: There it is. I knew it as soon as you did the Milton 32-8 knock. A fellow entrepreneur!
Zh: “就是这样。你一做米尔顿32-8式敲门，我就知道了。是同行的企业家！”

En: The art market? Intriguing. You've already convinced me to invest in you, so please, go on -- tell me what you're thinking.
Zh: “艺术市场？有趣。你已经说服我投资你了，所以请你继续——告诉我你的打算。”

En: The art market? Intriguing. Well, as I said, I don't carry much money on my person. But do go on, what are you thinking?
Zh: “艺术市场？有趣。好吧，我说过，我身上带的钱不多。不过，请继续说，你打算怎么做？”

En: Ah, a fellow entrepreneur. Tremendous! The art market? Intriguing. Sure, I'm keen. What are you thinking?
Zh: “啊，一位企业家同行。好极了！艺术品市场？有趣。当然，我很感兴趣。你打算怎么做？”

En: The art market, you say? Sure. 'Hit me with it', as they say. I'm keen to hear what you have in mind.
Zh: “你是说，艺术市场？当然。‘打动我吧’，就像他们说的那样。我很想听听你有什么打算。”

En: The art market, you say? Possibly. As I said, I don't carry much money on my person, but do go on -- what are you thinking?
Zh: “你是说，艺术市场？可能吧。我说过，我身上带的钱不多，但继续吧——你有什么打算？”

En: Well, you've already convinced me to invest in you, so please, go on -- tell me about this opportunity. I'm keen.
Zh: “好吧你已经说服我投资你了，所以请你继续——告诉我这个机会。我很感兴趣”

En: Wait, the Milton 32-8 knock?
Zh: “等等，米尔顿32-8式敲门？”

En: Yes, the special knock that helps us ultras identify each other. I almost forgot it myself.
Zh: “是的，这种特殊的敲门声可以帮助我们这种超级富豪认识彼此。我自己都差点忘了。”

En: But I'm getting side-tracked. Tell me about your business proposal. I am all *ears*. His face seems to distort in the blinding light, pulling and twisting at his sides.
Zh: “不过我跑偏了。跟我说说你的商业计划书吧。我*洗耳恭听*。”在眩目的光线下，他的脸似乎扭曲了，身体的两侧不停地拉扯扭动着。

En: The Milton 32-8 knock was created through data sonification. It's based on the time series data of the world's most profitable bank during its prosperous years in the Thirties. The peak points were turned into the rhythm that makes up the knock.
Zh: 米尔顿32-8式敲门声是通过数据超声化制作的。基于世界上最赚钱的银行在三十年代繁荣时期的时间序列数据。其峰值点构成了敲击的节奏。

En: I've recently discovered a local painter with enormous potential. (Show him Cindy's *chef d'oeuvre*.)
Zh: “我最近发现了一位潜力巨大的本地画家。”（给他看辛迪的*名作*。）

En: However, there are a few *complications*.... I don't have the cash on me right now to pay for art of this calibre. What I can instead offer are shares in Mauer-Koshlai Mercury Group.
Zh: “不过，有一些*复杂的问题*……我现在身上没有现金来支付这种级别的艺术品。我可以提供玛瓦-科什莱水银集团股份。”

En: Shares -- that's even *better*. You can do a lot with them if you play your cards right.
Zh: 股票--那就*更好*了。如果你处理得当，你可以用来做很多事情。

En: What are these shares worth?
Zh: “这些股票值多少钱？”

En: We have a deal. (Accept offer.)
Zh: “达成协议。” (接受报价。)

En: Thanks, but I need some time to think about it. (Decline for now.)
Zh: “谢谢，但我需要一些时间来考虑。”（暂时拒绝）

En: I bought these shares many years ago for a steal. As predicted, Mauer-Koshlai Mercury's operating profit has increased year on year, and there's no signs of it slowing down. People *always* need mercury.
Zh: “我很多年前就便宜买下了这些股票。正如我预料，玛瓦-科什莱水银集团的营业利润持续增长，而且没有放缓的迹象。人总是*需要*水银的。”

En: Which means you could net yourself a considerable profit if you need some immediate liquidity. But if you don't mind waiting I expect the value of these shares to double within the next five years.
Zh: “也就是说，如果你需要一些即时的流动资金，你可以给自己净赚一笔可观的利润。不过，如果你不介意等待的话，我预计这些股票的价值会在未来五年内翻一番。”

En: I've been thinking about those shares you offered me.
Zh: “我一直在考虑你给我的那些股票。”

En: They could set you up for a *long time*. Mauer-Koshlai Mercury is just getting started. Mark my words.
Zh: “它们可以让你玩上好一段时间。玛瓦-科什莱水银集团才刚刚起步。记住我的话。”

En: Not a problem. The offer stands for as long as I'm around these parts.
Zh: “没问题。只要我在这附近，这个提议就有效。”

En: Splendid. You hear the sound of expensive fabrics brushing against each other. When it stops, a roll of paper emerges from the radiance.
Zh: “秒极。”你听到了昂贵的织物互相摩擦的声音。当声音停止时，一卷纸从光芒中浮现。

En: What's that?
Zh: “那是什么？”

En: (Take the scroll.) "Thanks."
Zh: (接过纸卷) “谢谢。”

En: This is a stock certificate. Well, a photocopy of one. Nevertheless, it's solid proof that you own shares in Mauer-Koshlai Mercury Group.
Zh: “这是一张认股书。嗯，是复印件。不过，这可是你拥有玛瓦-科什莱水银集团股份的确凿证明。”

En: The light around the individual in front of you changes somehow as you take the scroll. You can't figure out what's different -- you just know that it is.
Zh: 当你接过纸卷时，你面前的个人周围的光线不知为何发生了某种变化。你搞不清楚有什么不同——你只知道发生了这样的事情。

En: After you hand over the artwork, the voice from the light says: "Another deal complete. Welcome to the big leagues! If you haven't already, I'd advise you to consider hiring a Personal Brand Strategist."
Zh: 在你交出作品后，光中的声音说：“又一笔交易完成。欢迎加入大联盟! 如果你还没有，我建议你考虑聘请一位个人品牌战略师。”

En: What does a Personal Brand Strategist do?
Zh: “个人品牌战略师是做什么的？”

En: Could you hook me up with one?
Zh: “你能帮我介绍一个吗？”

En: What *don't* they do? The shiny magnate chuckles. "They practically do everything -- from spotting good investments and analysing design trends, to booking a lunchtime table at the prestigious L'Ambroisie."
Zh: “他们*不会*做什么？”闪亮的大亨笑着说。“他们实际上什么都做——从发现好的投资项目和分析设计趋势，到在著名的神之筵席餐厅预订一张午餐桌。”

En: Unfortunately not. I only know the ones who work with multi-millionaires and beyond. But take a look around and I'm sure you'll find one. Personal Brand Strategists are attracted to money like *sharks* to blood. You'll find having net worth opens many new doors for you.
Zh: “恐怕不行。我只认识那些和千万富翁打交道的人。不过，四处看看，我相信你会找到一个。个人品牌战略师对金钱的吸引力就像血液对*鲨鱼*一样。你会发现拥有净资产会为你打开许多新的大门。”

En: Personal Brand Strategist, huh? Not the kind of person you're likely to find around here. We'll have to ask anyone who seems like they might understand marketing, reputation management... someone who dreams big.
Zh: 个人品牌战略师，嗯？你在这里不可能找到这种人。我们得问问那些看起来像懂市场营销、声誉管理的人……一个有远大理想的人。

En: Actually, Savvy *isn't* my name. Hope you'll excuse me. Let me introduce myself again...
Zh: “其实，*鬼鬼*不是我的名字。希望你能多包涵。让我再自我介绍一下……”

En: Oh, and we should see what this net worth can do. Might score a discount, or better yet, get you some free goods. That's the kind of thing that really makes you feel like a hotshot. Ask the local merchants about it if you get the chance.
Zh: 哦，我们应该看看这个净资产能做什么。说不定能在买东西的时候拿到优惠价，或者更好的是，给你弄点免费的商品。这样的事情，才会让你真正感觉到自己是个大人物。有机会的话，问问当地的商家吧。

En: They are the very *unit* of capital. You can buy them, sell them, even sit on them if you think it wise. Trust me when I say this -- becoming a shareholder means you're part of the *big leagues*.
Zh: “它们是资本的*单位。你可以买进它们，卖出它们，如果你明智的话，甚至能坐拥它们。相信我说的话——成为股东就意味着你是*大联盟*的一员。”

En: It's a Gottwaldian-Iilmaraan company that specialises in extracting mercury. It's seen massive growth lately due to the discovery of rödfärg mercury.
Zh: “这是一家戈特伍德-伊尔玛公司，专门提取汞。最近由于发现了红色水银，它有了很大的发展。”

En: Rehm's using it in their next model. I can't wait for mine to turn up! My predictions say its improved speed and azimuthal range should increase my trade efficiency by a whopping 46%. You really should order one if you don't want to be left behind.
Zh: “下个批次的雷姆将会装配它。我迫不及待地等着我的出货了！我的预测说，它改进后的速度和方位角范围，将使我的交易效率提高46%。如果你不想落后的话，你真的应该订购一台。”

En: A step forward for technology, yes -- but only for those who can afford it.
Zh: 技术的进步，是的——但仅限于那些负担得起的人。

En: Before I invest, I need to know more about the work. How do I know this asset is going to appreciate with time?
Zh: “在我投资之前，我需要了解更多关于作品的信息。我怎么知道这个资产能随着时间的推移而升值？”

En: Remember, you're not just selling a painting, you're selling a *story*. Give the man a reason to throw down that paper.
Zh: 记住，你不仅仅是在卖一幅画，你是在兜售一个*故事*。给他一个扔下那张纸的理由。

En: Emerging? That's a word with plenty of appeal. What is it that defines this style exactly?
Zh: “新兴的？这是一个很有吸引力的词。到底是什么定义了这种风格？”

En: The obvious answer is to be illustrative. However, he would be much more interested in art that directly tackles social issues.
Zh: 答案显而易见，就是要有说明性。不过，他对直接处理社会问题的艺术会更感兴趣。

En: Just tell him the truth. Why does no one ever want to tell the truth?
Zh: 就告诉他真相吧。为什么从来没有人愿意说实话？

En: Why are you letting this opportunity slip away? Give him a story, an experience, make him want to run away with this painting and marry it in secret.
Zh: 为什么你要让这个机会溜走？给他一个故事，一段经历，让他想带着这幅画跑掉，然后秘密地跟它结婚。

En: Running your fingertips across the splatters reveals frequencies used by finance companies for long-distance communication.
Zh: 用指尖划过这些飞溅印迹，就会发现金融公司用于远距离通讯的频率。

En: That's not very *art*. Tell him it contains an invisible pigment used by corporate spies. Own the palette and you can unveil a whole world of concealed communications.
Zh: 这不是很*艺术*。告诉他这里面有商业间谍使用的隐形颜料。拥有这个调色板，你就能揭开整个隐蔽通信世界的面纱。

En: Now is the time to champion the power of the sinuses!
Zh: 现在是捍卫鼻窦的力量的时候了！

En: I've found it's much better for my health just to throw money at those kinds of problems and hope they go away. That way I don't have to feel guilty about avoiding them.
Zh: “我发现，把钱花在解决那种问题上，希望它们消失，这对我的健康更好。这样我就不用为逃避它们而感到内疚了。”

En: Looks like he's about to throw some of his money our way...
Zh: 看来他要把他的钱投给我们了……

En: It's the organs. She hardly has anything left.
Zh: “是卖器官。她几乎什么都不剩了。”

En: Actually, I lied. She's from a working class background, but seems to be doing okay.
Zh: “其实，我撒谎了。她是工人阶级出身，但看起来还不错。”

En: Oh. He sounds disappointed. "Well, I'm sure her work represents some very interesting *internal* struggle. Probably with mental illness. Yes, that sounds right."
Zh: “哦。”他听起来很失望。“嗯，我相信她的作品表达了一些非常有趣的*内心*斗争。可能是与精神疾病有关。是的，听起来没错。”

En: Pray tell. The human shape within the light appears to lean even closer. Tiny dots flood your retina as the shipping container gets too bright.
Zh: “请说吧。”光线内的人形似乎更加靠拢了。你的视网膜上泛起细小的光点，因为集装箱变得太亮了。

En: It's the groceries. The poor thing has to go to Frittte every day.
Zh: “是杂货店。可怜的家伙每天都要去弗利多。”

En: *Mesque bangers*? No! Have I told you I've stopped reading the news? It's too negative, can't let it affect my mojo.
Zh: “*梅斯克帮*？不！我有跟你说过我已经不看新闻了吗？太消极了，不能让它影响我的气场。”

En: This guy clearly gets off on macabre stories. You've got just the one -- an absolute skullfuckery.
Zh: 这家伙显然对恐怖的故事很感兴趣。你刚好有一个——正儿八经的骷髅头混混。

En: An artist's value doubles if they come with a good backstory. I thought you were an experienced art dealer, you should know this.
Zh: “如果一个艺术家有一个好的背景故事，身价会翻倍。我想你是个有经验的艺术品商人，你应该知道这一点。”

En: Seems like he's not actually that interested in hearing the details. Too bad, you had a good story cooked up...
Zh: 看来他对细节没那么感兴趣。太糟糕了，你编了个好故事……

En: What a sad, sad story. I should ask Rhéa to look into this, maybe the foundation can set up some scholarships for cases like these.
Zh: “真是个悲伤的故事。我应该让雷哈去调查一下，也许基金会可以为这样的情况设立一些奖学金。”

En: It's something even worse. See, she got involved with Mesque bangers...
Zh: “比这更糟糕的事情。你看，她和梅斯克帮的人在一起厮混……”

En: *Every day*? The blinding light becomes almost melancholy. "But how does she find time to do any work?"
Zh: “*每一天*？” 刺眼的光线变得近乎忧郁。“可她哪来时间干活呢？”

En: That's disgusting and thoroughly unethical. You should refuse to engage with this.
Zh: 这真是太恶心了，太不道德了。你应该拒绝参与这件事。

En: You still have an option to not go along with this senseless sensationalism.
Zh: 你还可以选择不参与这种毫无意义的煽情。

En: It's the artist herself who becomes a commodity, not merely their work.
Zh: 变成商品的是艺术家自己，而不仅仅是他们的作品。

En: Just a hollow husk... The blinding light becomes almost melancholy.
Zh: “只是一具空洞的躯壳……”刺眼的光芒变得近乎忧郁。

En: At least she still has her heart intact, unlike some people here.
Zh: 至少她的心还在，不像这里的某些人。

En: Actually, she seemed to be doing more than okay. If anyone suffered then it's me.
Zh: “其实，她似乎做得还不错。如果有人受苦，那么就是我了。”

En: Wait, why does it matter?
Zh: “等等，为什么要这样做？”

En: Oh. He sounds disappointed. "Well, I'm sure her work represents some very interesting *internal* struggle. Probably with mental illness. Yes, that sounds right."
Zh: “哦。”他听起来很失望。“嗯，我相信她的作品表达了一些非常有趣的*内心*斗争。可能是与精神疾病有关。是的，听起来没错。”

En: You? The light appears to blink, as if someone switched off the light source for a second. "But why should we care about *you*?!"
Zh: “你？”光线似乎闪烁了一下，好像有人关掉了光源一秒钟。“可是我们为什么要关心*你*？！”

En: Any details? I've heard some struggling families have to sell their organs to pay the rent. There is also the terrible misfortune of having to go get your own groceries when you're poor. Which one is it?
Zh: “有什么细节吗？我听说有些困难家庭要卖掉自己的器官来支付房租。还有一种可怕的不幸，就是穷的时候要自己去买杂货。是哪一种呢？”

En: Her socio-economic conditions were meagre, but her spirit remained strong.
Zh: “她的社会经济条件虽然很差，但她的精神依然很坚强。”

En: (Nod solemnly.) "She suffered *terribly*."
Zh: (郑重地点点头。) “她遭受了*莫大的痛苦*。”

En: (Nod solemnly.) "Terribly."
Zh: (郑重地点点头。)“可怕。”

En: In any case, is there anything *else* you can tell me about this piece?
Zh: “不管怎么说，关于这篇作品，你还有什么*其它东西*可以告诉我吗？”

En: Please, if there's something I'm missing, I'd love to know what it is.
Zh: “请说，如果我遗漏了什么东西，我很想知道。”

En: It's art that tackles issues. Art plus issues -- Art-ishoo.
Zh: “这是处理问题的艺术。艺术加问题——问题艺术。”

En: I see -- very good. What kinds of issues are we talking about? I've really got into the housing crisis recently.
Zh: “我明白了——很好。我们说的是什么样的问题呢？我最近陷入了房地产危机。”

En: Poor thing! In that case, buying this will help me fulfil my *noblesse oblige* for the month.
Zh: “可怜啊！这样的话，买下这个作品可以帮我完成这个月的*高贵义务*。”

En: I see. What is so special about it then?
Zh: “原来如此。那它有什么特别之处呢？”

En: Oh yeah, this is all about the housing crisis. The artist doesn't even have a home.
Zh: “哦，是的，这都是关于房地产危机的。作者连房子都没有。”

En: Nah, it's just another fad. But there's something else special about this painting.
Zh: “不，那只是另一种时尚。不过这幅画还有一些特别之处。”

En: Oh yes, I see it now. Very *graphic*, isn't it? Really delivers insight into what it's like being that... unruly.
Zh: “哦，是的，我现在看到了。很*生动*，是吗？真能把那种……桀骜不驯的感觉深入人心。”

En: It's art that is produced by the trigeminal nerve.
Zh: “这是由三叉神经产生的艺术。”

En: It's art that is produced by way of sneezing.
Zh: “这是通过打喷嚏的方式产生的艺术。”

En: But perhaps I judge too quickly. Is there an intelligent reasoning behind the method?
Zh: “但也许我有点武断了。这种方法背后有什么靠谱的说法吗？”

En: Hm. Seems unhygienic. Is that what's *in* these days?
Zh: “嗯，好像不太卫生。这就是现在的*潮流*吗？”

En: That's it, lap it up, buddy. That might have done it.
Zh: 就这样，尽情享受吧，哥们。这可能已经成功了。

En: It's a real spit-and-sawdust affair. See those splatters? That's *poor* people.
Zh: “这是真正肮脏破败的主题。看到那些飞溅的痕迹了吗？那是*穷人*。”

En: He only wants to hear about struggles he isn't familiar with.
Zh: 他只想听他不熟悉的困境。

En: His scintillating face-shape flashes with an expression that resembles disgust.
Zh: 他那闪烁着光泽的脸型闪烁着类似厌恶的表情。

En: On second thought, not the kinds of issues you'd be interested in.
Zh: “仔细想想，不是你感兴趣的那种问题。”

En: By stimulating the nerve, the artist enters a state of no-mindedness and lets the body take over.
Zh: “通过刺激神经，艺术家进入了一种无我状态，让肉体接管一切。”

En: The artist enters a state of no-mindedness and lets the body take over.
Zh: “艺术家进入一种无我状态，让肉体来接管一切。”

En: Splatter, disarray, chaos -- it's anti-design.
Zh: “飞溅，无序，混沌——这是反设计的。”

En: But design is the very signature of humanity. Everything we do is design. Text columns, urban architecture, the precise shape of a stapler... what are we without any of that?
Zh: “但设计是人性的标志。我们所做的一切都是设计。文本栏、城市建筑、订书机的精确形状——如果没有这些，我们会是什么？”

En: Challenge him on this. Cause a scene.
Zh: 挑战他这一点。引起一场争论。

En: Don't you dare! This isn't the time. Put your personal feelings aside -- you're a salesman!
Zh: 你敢！现在不是时候。把你的个人感情放在一边……你是个推销员!

En: Design is nothing. Let it go. It's holding you back.
Zh: “设计本身什么也不是。不要管它。它在拖你的后腿。”

En: Exactly. Don't you want to find out what *else* we could be?
Zh: “正是如此。难道你不想知道我们*还能*成为什么吗？”

En: He swills it around in his brain. "So it's an éxtrasomatic painting. An effort carried out by physical instinct -- not the mind."
Zh: 他在脑子里转了一圈你的观点。“所以这是一种体外作画。由身体本能——而不是头脑——进行的创作。”

En: Perhaps I *have* been neglecting the triumphs of anatomical perspicacity lately... He sinks into thought.
Zh: “也许我*一直*忽视了最近解剖学洞察力的成就……”他陷入了思考。

En: That's a shame. Is there anything else to this painting, or is that it?
Zh: “那真是太可惜了。这幅画还有什么其他的特点吗，或者说就只有这样？”

En: Hmm, I do cling to it rather a lot. Perhaps that is one of my shortcomings -- I just haven't realised it before now.
Zh: “嗯，我确实对它相当感兴趣。也许这是我的一个缺点——只是我现在还没有意识到这一点。”

En: Tell him it was used to mix together an invisible pigment. Own it and he opens up a whole world of concealed communications.
Zh: 告诉他这是用来混合一种看不见的颜料。拥有它，他就会打开整个隐蔽通信的世界。

En: This is a new type of design. You'll work it out if you spend more time with it.
Zh: “这是一种新型的设计。如果你花更多的时间去研究它，就会明白。”

En: So close to envisioning what's beyond. Just needs a little push over the edge...
Zh: 已经很靠近前面的景象了。只是需要再推过界一点……

En: Hm. I hadn't thought about it that way before. You might have just opened up a new door in my mind.
Zh: “嗯，我以前没有这么想过。你可能为我的思想打开了一扇新的大门。”

En: That prophet-for-hire did say I should identify weakness and remove it from my life...
Zh: “那个受雇预言家确实说过，我应该找出弱点，并将它从我的生活中去除……”

En: It contains a unique invisible paint. You can use it to hide important messages.
Zh: “它含有一种独特的隐形涂料。你可以用它来隐藏重要的信息。”

En: Certainly not in its material form. But there's more to it than that.
Zh: “当然不是以物质形态。但它的作用不止于此。”

En: Yeah-yeah, you're doing a good deed here, really giving back to society. Just give us the money already.
Zh: 是啊是啊，你这是在做善事，真的是在回馈社会。把钱给我们好不。

En: Doors, windows, toilet seats -- open whatever it is you need, buddy. Just get on with making a purchase already!
Zh: 门、窗、马桶座……需要什么就开什么，哥们。给我赶紧买下这东西吧！

En: Here's a prophecy for you, pal. Buying this painting will make your life better (and ours).
Zh: 给你预言一下，伙计。买下这幅画会让你的生活变得更好（还有我们的）。

En: That changes everything.
Zh: “这改变了一切。”

En: Perfect! I've been looking for a way to communicate without the information being intercepted or stolen by rivals.
Zh: “完美！我一直在寻找一种不被对手截获或窃取信息的交流方式。”

En: Go on. I'm listening...
Zh: “继续，我在听……”

En: Perhaps I could be the one to 'discover' it? To share it with the wider world...
Zh: “也许我可以成为那个‘发现'它的人？与更广阔的世界分享它……”

En: That's not how discovery works, but let him think whatever he wants if it means he'll buy it.
Zh: 发现不是这样的，但只要能让他买账，他怎么想都可以。

En: You're actually the first person to see this painting after the artist.
Zh: “其实你是继作者之后第一个看到这幅画的人。”

En: You mean even *you* haven't looked at it?
Zh: “你是说连*你*都没看过？”

En: Still, only two people have seen it thus far. That makes it pretty *exclusive* and therefore potentially valuable.
Zh: “不过，到目前为止，只有两个人看过它。这使它具有相当的*独特性*，因此具有潜在的价值。”

En: Oh yeah. Duh!
Zh: “哦，是吗。啧！”

En: (Lie.) "I haven't so much as squinted at it. It's the exclusivity that gives it value."
Zh: (撒谎。) “我甚至连看都没看一眼。正是这种独特性赋予了它价值。”

En: Wow, he worked it out all by himself. Such a clever boy. But is he gonna pay up?
Zh: 哇，他都自己想明白了。真是个聪明的孩子。不过，他到底买不买？

En: I see, so by artificially constraining supply you're also stimulating demand. I have to say, it's a brilliant strategy...
Zh: “我明白了，通过人为地限制供应，也就刺激了需求。我不得不说，这是一个聪明的策略……”

En: Great work. I think you've got him. Let's see what he says...
Zh: 干得好，我想你已经搞定他了。让我们看看他怎么说……

En: And if I don't buy it now, more people will see it -- ergo its value will drop.
Zh: “如果我现在不买的话，会有更多的人看到它——也就是说它的价值会降低。”

En: It's now or never!
Zh: 机不可失，时不再来！

En: What he wants to know is how exclusive it is. The more untouched it is, the better.
Zh: 他想知道的是它有多独家。越是没人看过就越好。

En: Bit of corporate espionage? He's straight in there. Great job.
Zh: 一点商业间谍活动？他直接就想到了。干得好。

En: Sounds like a winner. Good pitch.
Zh: 看来你赢了。推销得不错。

En: He ate that right up. Pretty sure you've done it.
Zh: 他很吃这一套。很明显你已经成功了。

En: This transaction has changed the physical relationship between you. You are now more alike somehow.
Zh: 这笔交易改变了你们之间的物理关系。你们现在在某种程度上更相似了。

En: There! The blinding light dimmed just long enough for you to see his true form! He looked like a regular middle-aged man!
Zh: 好了！眩目的光线逐渐变得黯淡，足以让你看清他的真面目！他看起来就像一个普通的中年男人！

En: Something's rattling within you. It feels like the first penny dropped into a piggy bank.
Zh: 你的内心有什么东西在响动。感觉就像第一笔钱掉进了存钱罐里。

En: Lying will get you nowhere. You can't outrun the truth -- it will catch up to you one day.
Zh: 撒谎是没有用的。你无法逃脱真相——总有一天它会追上你。

En: The comfortably wealthy want their views challenged when making a purchase. Don't hold back.
Zh: 安逸的富人在购买时希望自己的观点受到挑战。不要退缩。

En: He's so close to envisioning what's beyond. Just needs a little push over the edge...
Zh: 他就差一点就能设想到前面的景象了。只是需要再推过界一点……

En: It turns the artistic process into the art itself.
Zh: “它把艺术过程变成了艺术本身。”

En: What an original idea! By drawing our focus away from the final piece we gain a new appreciation for the craft behind it. An art in its own right that is usually hidden from us.
Zh: “多么新颖的想法！通过将我们的注意力从最终的作品上转移开来，我们对背后的工艺有了新的认识。这是一种平常我们会忽视的艺术本身的权利。”

En: These people will find a way to justify anything. Looks like you've reeled him.
Zh: 这些人总会找到一个方法来证明任何事情。看来你已经把他套牢了。

En: You've reeled him right in. That's got to have done it.
Zh: 你已经把他套牢了。这一定会成功。

En: The Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient for you and this individual has now dropped to .9989 repeating.
Zh: 你和这个人的韦斯·韦斯曼系数曲线现在已经降到了0.9989恒值了。

En: Wouldn't say it's exactly *safe* now -- but financially secure individuals are known adrenaline junkies.
Zh: 现在还不能说它真的很*安全*——不过经济上有保障的个体都是以嗑肾上腺素出名的。

En: Now, was there anything else I could help you gentlemen with?
Zh: “现在，我还有什么能帮你们的吗？”

En: Now, was there anything else I could help you with?
Zh: “现在，我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: It's a secret handshake of the ultra rich, distributed though private-access yacht clubs and interisolary balls for the heirs and heiresses of the old money. Where you picked it up is a mystery.
Zh: 这是超级富豪之间的秘密握手仪式，只通过私人游艇俱乐部和为老富豪的子女继承人举办的跨界舞会里传播。你在哪里学会的是个谜。

En: A painter, eh? I am always looking to expand into new asset classes. Who's the artist?
Zh: “一幅画作，嗯？我一直在寻找拓展新的资产类别。作者是谁？”

En: Grotbags. Actually, make that Granny Grotbags.
Zh: “绿女巫。实际上，应该是绿女巫奶奶”

En: Let's go with... Lucinda Schweinehack.
Zh: “让我们叫她……露辛达·施魏因哈克。”

En: Cindy the SKULL.
Zh: “骷髅头辛迪。”

En: I'd prefer to keep it a secret.
Zh: “我更希望保密。”

En: A-ha. And the name of the piece? He leans in to take a good look at the rectangular plastic board coated thick with emulsion.
Zh: “啊哈。作品的名字呢？”他俯身仔细看了看那块涂了厚厚乳剂的长方形塑料板。

En: Morve sur toile.
Zh: “《画布上的鼻涕》”

En: The Arduous Slaughter of the Eighth Pinniped.
Zh: “《第八类鳍足生物的艰辛屠杀》。”

En: Gross Profit.
Keith.
Not even a pseudonym? The light appears to vibrate with surprise.
Zh: “连艺名都没有吗？”光线似乎因惊讶而震动。

En: Wonderful things aren't they -- names? They can completely change your reading of a person. Or, in this case, a painting.
Zh: “奇妙的东西，不是吗——名字？它们可以完全改变你对一个人的解读。或者，在这种情况下，一幅画作的解读。”

En: Well, I think that settles it. You've found a buyer, my friend. A pleasure transacting with you. The light around him warms you like a friendly embrace.
Zh: “好吧，我想这就解决了。你已经找到了一个买家，我的朋友。很高兴与你交易。”他周围的光让你感到暖心，就像一个友好的拥抱。

En: No, a complete mystery. You can call her Anonymous.
Zh: “不，完全是个谜。你可以叫她无名氏。”

En: And the name of the piece? He leans in to take a good look at the rectangular plastic board coated thick with emulsion.
Zh: “作品的名字呢？”他俯身仔细看了看那块涂了厚厚乳剂的长方形塑料板。

En: Original. I don't think any of my friends have any pieces from anonymous artists. Or maybe only Luca, the son-in-law of the Comte de L'Ys.
Zh: “新颖。我想我的朋友中没有任何一个人有匿名艺术家的作品。或者，也许只有L'Y伯爵家的女婿卢卡拥有。”

En: No, not even him. He might have some Moelles and de Vignys, the *authenticity* of which historians are debating, but I don't think that counts. Perfect.
Zh: “不，连他也没有。他可能有一些莫雷时期和维戈尼时期的作品，历史学家们正在争论其*真伪*，但我认为这不算。完美。”

En: The Aloïs-Lorblanchet family is known to have one of the biggest and most impressive private art collections in the world.
Zh: 众所周知，阿里奥斯-罗布兰切特家族拥有世界上最大、最令人印象深刻的私人艺术收藏之一。

En: What are 'shares' and what can I do with them?
Zh: “什么是'股份'，我可以用来做什么？”

En: What is Mauer-Koshlai Mercury Group?
Zh: “什么是玛瓦-科什莱水银集团？”

En: This is the only question that matters. Fail to come up with an interesting hook and he's out.
Zh: 这是唯一重要的问题。讲不出有趣的故事，他就会被淘汰出局。

En: There's no sign of a reaction for a good three seconds. Then...
Zh: 足足有三秒钟没有反应。然后……

En: Keith? Just *Keith*?
Yes, Keith. Stick with it.
Zh: 是的，《基思》。坚持口径。

En: What were you thinking -- *KEITH*??!! You're not starting a boy band here, you're naming a future masterpiece!
Zh: 你在想什么呢——《基思》？？！！你不是在组男孩乐队，你是在给未来的杰作取名！

En: (Nod.) "Keith."
Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Keith, he repeats.
Zh: “《基思》，”他重复道。

En: Even the lieutenant looks tense, waiting to see where this is going...
Zh: 连警督都显得很紧张，等着看事态的发展……

En: Keith, I like it. One of my nephews is named Keith. We haven't spoken in years, since I declined to pay for his most recent trip to rehab. But perhaps this will help patch things up between us...
Zh: “《基思》，我喜欢它。我的一个侄子就叫基思。自从我拒绝支付他最近一次去戒毒所的费用后，我们已经好几年没有说话了。但也许这能帮助我们和解……”

En: Sweat pools up in your clavicles, a rancid smell of *fear*.
Zh: 汗水在你的锁骨上积聚，一股*恐惧*的臭味。

En: There's no sign of a reaction for a good three seconds. Then...
Zh: 足足有三秒钟没有反应。然后……

En: Nod confidently.
Zh: 自信地点点头。

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: Joyce might be able to help?
Zh: 乔伊斯也许能帮上忙？

En: Evrart seems to know a thing or two...
Zh: 艾弗拉特也许略知一二……

En: Garte seems to have his life together.
Zh: 加尔特似乎生活很美满。

En: No one else comes to mind...
Zh: 想不起还有谁……

En: No one comes to mind...
Zh: 想不起有谁……

En: She *could*, but she represents the Wild Pines, which is the top of that particular game. No way she's stepping down from there. We need someone lower down.
Zh: 她*可以*，但她代表着野松集团，那是那个领域的顶端。她不可能从那里辞职。我们需要一个级别更低人。

En: That old communist?! The only thing he'll strategize is a plan to *redistribute* your hard-earned money to losers and moochers!
Zh: 那个老康米主义者？他唯一会制定的战略就是把你们辛苦赚来的钱*再分配*给失败者和占便宜者！

En: He's nothing more than a middle manager living a middle-class life -- your aspirations are *much* more ambitious than that.
Zh: 他不过是一个过着中产阶级生活的中层管理者——你的蓝图比这*更*远大。

En: We'll stumble upon someone, someone with the right amount of *can-do* attitude... I'll let you know when we do. Just keep your eyes open!
Zh: 我们会遇到那个人的，一个有适合的*“我可以”*态度的人……找到了我会告诉你的。睁大你的眼睛吧!

En: So! What's next on the agenda?
Zh: “那么！接下来的议程是什么？”

En: Here's an *avant-garde* proposal... What about Idiot Doom Spiral? He used to hustle and hang with the sales hawks and trend pushers of the marketing world. Let's ask him if he knows any Personal Brand Strategists.
Zh: 我有个很*前卫*的提议……白痴厄运漩涡怎么样？他过去经常与营销界的精英和潮流推手们厮混。让我们问他是否认识一些个人品牌战略师。

En: Seems like a clever investment. Definitely worth thinking about if you care about your future.
Zh: 似乎是个明智的投资。如果你关心自己的未来，绝对值得考虑。

En: Yes, but chances are you can't use them to buy anything *right now*. No cigarettes, no alcohol, no funky little colourful pills...
Zh: 是的，但你有可能*现在*无法用它们来买任何东西。没有烟，没有酒，没有时髦的彩色小药丸……

En: He's right, the combination of Rehm's solid engineering and the new rödfärg mercury valves is huge.
Zh: 他是对的，雷姆的扎实的做工和新型红色水银阀的结合是空前的。

En: It looks almost ethereal -- is it even real?
Zh: 它看起来几乎是虚无缥缈的——它是真的吗？

En: Have you heard about it? At the moment, it's trading at more than 1.5x the price of gold. That's because it's an essential component in the next generation of radiocomputers...
Zh: “你听说过它吗？目前，它的交易价格是黄金价格的1.5倍以上。那是因为它是下一代无线电脑的重要组成部分……”

En: That concludes *our part* of the transaction. Once you've turned over the asset, my people will get the legal ball rolling.
Zh: “这次交易中*我们的部分*就到此为止了。一旦你交出资产，我的人就会启动法律程序。”

En: It's actually just a mixing palette.
Zh: “这其实只是一个调色板。”

En: It's an early entry in an emerging style known as Art-ishoo.
Zh: “这是一种新兴风格的早期作品，被称为问题艺术。”

En: The artist was struggling. *Really* struggling.
Zh: “这位艺术家很困难。*真的*很困难。”

En: Value is something you should *discover* for yourself. What do you see?
Zh: “价值应该是你自己去*发现*的。你看到了什么？”

En: I see... The light appears to concentrate...
Zh: “我看到……”光线似乎集中起来了……

En: Staring at it long enough lets you immerse yourself in *karperie*.
Zh: “盯着它看的时间越长，你就越能沉浸在*卡佩里*的学说里。”

En: Just a mixing palette? But where does the value lie then?
Zh: “只是一个调色板？但是，价值在哪里呢？”

En: That's what I wanted to ask you about. Tell me, what's their *story*? Did they *suffer*?
Zh: “这就是我想问的。告诉我，他们的*故事*是什么？他们有没有*受苦*？”

En: I'll be honest, all I see are splatters that lack any true intention.
Zh: “说实话，我只能看到一块缺乏任何真实意图的胡乱涂鸦。”

En: Great work -- that might have done it.
Zh: 干得好——可能已经成功了。

En: It lets you *immerse yourself* in a modern school of thought? Are you sure?
Zh: “它会让*你专心于*在某种现代思想流派中？你确定吗？”

En: Ten seconds -- borders dissipate. Twenty seconds -- loss of self. Thirty seconds -- everything dissimilates into raw unreality.
Zh: 10秒——边界消散。20秒——失去自我。30秒——一切都消融在原始的虚幻中。

En: That's just one of the many dangers of getting too deep into karperie.
Zh: 这只是沉迷于卡帕里的诸多危险之一。

En: Yes. Spend time with it and the whole world becomes defined by karperie.
Zh: “是的，在它上面花费越多的时间，整个世界都会被卡佩里定义。”

En: Actually, forget what I said. It doesn't really make sense.
Zh: “其实，忘了我说的话吧。它并没有什么意义。”

En: Complete nonsense -- both 'karperie' and what you just said. Can't bathe in thoughts!
Zh: 完全是胡说八道——无论是‘卡佩里’还是你刚才说的话。不能沉浸在思绪中!

En: Mhmh. Karperie is *dangerous*. It spreads like a virus, infecting entire universities.
Zh: “嗯嗯，卡佩里是*危险的*。它像病毒一样传播，感染了所有大学。”

En: *Dangerous*? I like things that are dangerous. You could even say I'm one of these 'adrenaline-junkies'. The light emanating from him seems to intensify.
Zh: “*危险的*？我喜欢危险的东西。你甚至可以说我是一个‘肾上腺素迷'。 ”他身上散发的光芒似乎更加强烈了。

En: Sounds like a hell of a trip. I really must experience it for myself. The light emanating from him seems to intensify.
Zh: “听起来像是一次地狱之旅。我真得亲自体验一下。”他身上散发的光似乎更加强烈了。

En: I appreciate you saving us from wasting time. Is there anything else I should know about the painting?
Zh: “感谢你让我们免于浪费时间。关于这幅画还有什么我应该知道的吗？”

En: This is a cryptograph used by circuit benders to bring down banks.
Zh: “这是电路干扰者用来搞垮银行的解码器。”

En: The man stands at the far end of the shipping container. It's hard to say anything more about him. You cannot make out any of his details, but you do feel the overwhelming presence of... capital.
Zh: 男人站在船运集装箱较远的另一端。关于他的情况，很难再多说些什么。你无法了解他的任何细节，不过你确实感觉到了一种压倒性的存在……资本的存在。

En: Who are you?
Zh: “你是谁？”

En: Welcome! Savvy, was it? Make yourself at home. Sorry I'm not better able to receive you -- I wasn't expecting visitors today.
Zh: “欢迎！鬼鬼，是吧？请进，别拘束。抱歉，我招待不周——没想到今天会有访客。”

En: Welcome! Come in, make yourself at home. Sorry I'm not better able to receive you -- I wasn't expecting visitors today.
Zh: “欢迎！请进，别拘束。抱歉，我招待不周——没想到今天会有访客。”

En: Now. He claps his hands together. "What can I do for you gentlemen?" What you can see of his body appears composed. In a sharp summer suit. And yacht shoes.
Zh: “现在。”他合起双手。“有什么能帮你的吗，先生？”你能看出他的身体似乎很镇定。穿着一套笔挺的夏季西装。还有游艇鞋。

En: Now. He claps his hands together. "What can I do for you, good sir?" What you can see of his body appears composed. In a sharp summer suit. And yacht shoes.
Zh: “现在。”他合起双手。“有什么能帮你的吗，好先生？”你能看出他的身体似乎很镇定。穿着一套笔挺的夏季西装。还有帆船鞋。

En: Who *am* I? Oh, I haven't been asked that question for such a long time. There's genuine surprise in his voice. "I don't meet a lot of people outside my circle these days..."
Zh: “我*是*谁？哦，已经很久没有人问过我这个问题了。”他是真心感到惊讶。“最近我都没怎么见过圈子之外的人……”

En: Anyhow, my name is Roustame Diodore -- investor, licence holder, and extremely high-net-worth individual. And your name is Savvy, right?
Zh: “总之，我的名字叫鲁斯塔姆·狄奥多——投资人，许可授权人，而且是个超高资产净值人士。而你叫鬼鬼，对吧？”

En: Anyhow, my name is Roustame Diodore -- investor, licence holder, and extremely high-net-worth individual. And you are?
Zh: “总之，我的名字叫鲁斯塔姆·狄奥多——投资人，许可证持有者，而且是个超高资产净值人士。请问你是？”

En: My name is Harry, and I'm an alcoholic.
Zh: “我的名字叫哈里，是一个酒鬼。”

En: What are you doing in this container?
Zh: “你在这个集装箱里做什么？”

En: My name is Harry, and I'm a detective.
Zh: “我的名字叫哈里，是一个警探。”

En: My name is Harry, and I'm an alcoholic detective.
Zh: “我的名字叫哈里，是一个酗酒的警探。”

En: I'm an alcoholic.
Zh: “我是一个酒鬼。”

En: I'm a detective.
Zh: “我是一个警探。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, Harry, he says warmly. "Though I'm sorry to hear a good man like yourself is in servitude to the drink. But then, nobody's perfect."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，哈里，”他亲切地笑了。“虽然我很遗憾听到一个像你这么好的人会沦为酒精的奴隶。不过，人无完人嘛。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, Harry, he says warmly. "Though I'm sorry to hear a good public servant like yourself is in servitude to *the drink*. A man can't serve two masters, in my experience."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，哈里，”他亲切地笑了。“虽然我很遗憾听到一个像你这么好的公仆会沦为*酒精*的奴隶。以我的经验来看，一个人不可能同时侍奉两个主人。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, Harry, he says warmly. "Public service is a noble cause."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，哈里，”他亲切地笑了。“公共服务是一项崇高的事业。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, he says warmly. "Though I'm sorry to hear a good man like yourself is in servitude to the drink. But then, nobody's perfect."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，”他亲切地笑了。“虽然我很遗憾听到一个像你这么好的人会沦为酒精的奴隶。不过，人无完人嘛。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, he says warmly. "Public service is a noble cause."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，”他亲切地笑了。“公共服务是一项崇高的事业。”

En: I'm an alcoholic detective.
Zh: “我是一个酗酒的警探。”

En: We should get back to our investigation. Thanks for your time. [Leave.]\n
Zh: “我们该继续回去调查了。感谢你能抽出宝贵的时间。”[离开。]

En: I should get back to my investigation. Thanks for your time. [Leave.]
Zh: “我该继续回去调查了。感谢你能抽出宝贵的时间。”[离开。]

En: I can assure you, Mr. Diodore, that despite his vices he is a competent detective.
Zh: “我可以向你保证，狄奥多先生，除开他那些恶习，他确实是个很能干的警探。”

En: I can assure you, Mr. Diodore, that despite his vices he is a competent detective.
Zh: “我可以向你保证，狄奥多先生，除开他那些恶习，他确实是个很能干的警探。”

En: How did you become so rich?
Zh: “你是怎么变得这么有钱的？”

En: Oh, lord, not this again.
Zh: “哦，天啦，别又来了。”

En: Mr. Diodore, I think there's been some confusion. His name is, in fact, *not* Savvy. He glares at you. "Let's start over. I am Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi of the RCM, and this is my partner..."
Zh: “狄奥多先生，我想这里有点误会。其实，他的名字*不叫*鬼鬼。”他瞪了你一眼。“我们重新自我介绍一下吧。我是RCM的曷城警督，这位是我的搭档……”

En: Mr. Diodore, I am Lieutenant Kim Kitsuragi of the RCM, and this is my partner...
Zh: “狄奥多先生，我是RCM的曷城警督，这位是我的搭档……”

En: Something's amiss. The light beams bend around his face and scatter in a thousand directions. It seems the laws of physics do not apply here. They are suspended, distorted, an echo.
Zh: 有什么不太对劲。光线在他脸部周围弯曲了，向几千个方向散发。物理定律在这里似乎不起作用。他们被隔断、扭曲了，成了一个回音。

En: In the general stillness, only your tongue moves, flickering as you utter...
Zh: 在万物沉寂之中，只有你的舌头在说话时微微颤动……

En: Hello!
Zh: “你好！”

En: What's going on in here?
Zh: “里面出什么事了？”

En: Welcome, Savvy, welcome! Not too much, actually, just pleasantly surprised to have company today.
Zh: “欢迎，鬼鬼，欢迎！其实也没什么，只是今天能有客人来拜访，真是个惊喜。”

En: Welcome, welcome! Not too much, actually, just pleasantly surprised to have company today.
Zh: “欢迎，欢迎！其实也没什么，只是今天能有客人来拜访，我感觉有些惊喜。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, he says warmly. "Though I'm sorry to hear that a good public servant like yourself is in thrall to the drink. A man can't serve two masters, in my experience."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，”他亲切地笑了。“虽然我很遗憾听到一个像你这么好的公仆会沦为酒精的奴隶。以我的经验来看，一个人不可能同时侍奉两个主人的。”

En: The feeling causes all the hairs on your body to stand at attention like soldiers preparing for review.
Zh: 这种感觉让你全身的毛发都竖了起来，就像立正等候检阅的士兵。

En: You can't *hear* him, exactly, yet you're able to understand every word he says. It is very strange. An overwhelming hum covers everything -- voice doesn't escape from him.
Zh: 确切的说，你*听不见*他的声音，但你仍然能够理解他说的每一个字。真的很奇怪。一股压倒性的嗡嗡声掩盖了一切——声音无法从他身边逃脱。

En: What's the matter, Kim?
Zh: “出什么事了，金？”

En: The man chuckles. "To be perfectly blunt, I inherited my fortune from my grandmother, who, herself, was an extremely high-net-worth individual back in Graad..."
Zh: 男人轻笑出声。“坦率地讲，我继承了祖母的财富，她自己在格拉德就是个超高资产净值人士……”

En: Oh, nothing. It's just that we've got this *murder* to solve, and yet you go around asking everyone about *money*. And every time I ask, 'Are you sure this is related to the case?', you say, 'Sure, Kim. I think it is'...
Zh: “哦，没什么。只是我们有一起*凶杀案*要调查，而你却在到处找别人要*钱*。而且每次我问你，‘你确定这跟案子有关系吗？’你就会说，‘当然了，金。我觉得有……'”

En: The man chuckles. "It's quite alright. I'm used to the question by now. To be blunt, I inherited my fortune from my grandmother, who, herself, was an extremely high-net-worth individual back in Graad..."
Zh: 男人轻笑出声。“说的很对。现在我已经习惯这个问题了。坦率地讲，我继承了祖母的财富，她自己在格拉德就是个超高资产净值人士……”

En: Cool. But I want to ask you about something else.
Zh: “厉害。不过我还想点别的问题。”

En: You're a rich investor, right? Can I have some money?
Zh: “你是一个富有的投资人，对吧？那能给我一点钱吗？”

En: What do you mean? His... essence seems to signify actual surprise.
Zh: “你什么意思？”他的……实质似乎真的很惊讶。

En: There's something strange about you.
Zh: “你身上有些奇怪的地方。”

En: Well, I don't know how to put it... you look... somehow... a little different.
Zh: “呃，我不知道该怎么说……你看起来……不知怎么的……有些不太一样。”

En: It's actually nothing. Forget I said anything.
Zh: “其实真的没什么。还是忘了我说的吧。”

En: What's it like being an extremely high-net-worth individual?
Zh: “做一个超高资产净值人士是什么感觉？”

En: Go right ahead.
Zh: “那就问吧。”

En: The man exhales with a whistle. "I gotta tell you, at first, being rich is a lot of work. You've got to work hard because everything's so darn expensive. You know, prices increase exponentially at this income level..."
Zh: 男人吹了声口哨。“我得告诉你，首先，变成有钱人需要做大量的工作。你要努力工作，因为一切东西都贵的要命。你知道的，以我这个收入水平，价格都是呈指数增长的……”

En: But then, once you've reached my position, it's nearly impossible for me *not* to make money. My assets are so diversified that I'm bound to come out ahead no matter what...
Zh: “不过呢，一旦你达到我的位置，几乎就不太可能*赚不到*钱了。我的资产是如此地多样化，不管怎么样，我都一定会获利……”

En: Hey, HEY. All this talk about money has made you lose the thread. What is going on with the light in this place? *That's* what you need to ask him about!
Zh: 喂，我说。所有这些关于钱的话题让你有些摸不着头绪。这个地方的光线是怎么回事？*那*才是你要问他的问题！

En: Man, being a high-net-worth individual sounds great.
Zh: “老兄，当个高净值人士听起来太棒了。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: You're right. Capital accumulation is its own reward.
Zh: “你说得对。资本累积本身就是一种奖励。”

En: Don't you think that you should use your great wealth for the glory of your fatherland?
Zh: “难道你不觉得应该用自己那笔巨大的财富来光耀自己的祖国吗？”

En: You're a thief. People out there are working their asses off while you *chill* here.
Zh: “你是个小偷。外面那些人都在累死累活的工作，而你却在这里*凉快*。”

En: It's great that you've done so well for yourself, but don't you think you owe some of that wealth to the rest of society?
Zh: “你为自己所做的一切真的很棒，但你不觉得自己应该把一些财富分给其他人吗？”

En: Listen, Mr. Sunset, if capital and nationalism went hand in hand, there'd be no need to ask the question...
Zh: “听着，日落先生，如果资本和民族主义是息息相关的，那根本就没必要问这个问题了……”

En: Listen, Mr. Du Bois, if capital and nationalism went hand in hand, there'd be no need to ask the question...
Zh: “听着，杜博阿先生，如果资本和民族主义是息息相关的，那就没必要问这个问题了……”

En: Listen, Mr. Costeau -- Raphaël, if I may -- if capital and nationalism went hand in hand, there'd be no need to ask the question...
Zh: “听着，库斯托先生——拉斐尔，恕我冒昧——如果资本和民族主义是息息相关的，那就没必要问这个问题了……”

En: Listen, Harry, if capital and nationalism went hand in hand, there'd be no need to ask the question...
Zh: “听着，哈里，如果资本和民族主义是息息相关的，那就没必要问这个问题了……”

En: Listen, if capital and nationalism went hand in hand, there'd be no need to ask the question...
Zh: “听着，如果资本和民族主义是息息相关的，那就没必要问这个问题了……”

En: States are akin to boulders in a river, slowing the free flow of ideas. Given enough time, the water will wear those boulders down.
Zh: “国家就像河里的大岩石，延缓了思想的自由流动。如果有足够的时间，水会把这些石头冲垮的。”

En: Precisely, Mr. Sunset! You know, I hear people talk sometimes about *economic equality* and *fairness* and all those things. But tell me, where would we be without liberalism? In caves! Whacking each other with bones!
Zh: “没错！日落先生！你知道吗，我有时候会听到别人谈论*经济平等*和*公平*。但是告诉我，要是没有自由主义，我们又会在哪里？在山洞里！用骨头互相攻击！”

En: Precisely, Mr. Du Bois! You know, I hear people talk sometimes about *economic equality* and *fairness*. But tell me, where would we be without liberalism? In caves! Whacking each other with bones!
Zh: “没错！杜博阿先生！你知道吗，我有时候会听到别人谈论*经济平等*和*公平*。但是告诉我，要是没有自由主义，我们又会在哪里？在山洞里！用骨头互相攻击！”

En: Precisely, Mr. Costeau -- may I call you Raphaël?! You know, I hear people talk sometimes about *economic equality* and *fairness* and all that kind of stuff. But tell me, where would we be without liberalism? In caves! Whacking each other with bones!
Zh: “没错，库斯托先生——我能叫你拉斐尔吗！？！你知道吗，我有时候会听到别人谈论*经济平等*还有*公平*之类的话题。但是告诉我，要是没有自由主义，我们又会在哪里？在山洞里！用骨头互相攻击！”

En: Precisely, Harry! You know, I hear people talk sometimes about *economic equality* and *fairness* and all that kind of stuff. But tell me, where would we be without liberalism? In caves! Whacking each other with bones!
Zh: “没错，哈里！你知道吗，我有时候会听到别人谈论*经济平等*还有*公平*之类的话题。但是告诉我，要是没有自由主义，我们又会在哪里？在山洞里！用骨头互相攻击！”

En: Precisely! You know, I hear people talk sometimes about *economic equality* and *fairness* and all that kind of stuff. But tell me, where would we be without liberalism? In caves! Whacking each other with bones!
Zh: “没错！你知道吗，我有时候会听到别人谈论*经济平等*还有*公平*之类的话题。但是，你告诉我，要是没有自由主义，我们又会在哪里？在山洞里！用骨头互相攻击！”

En: Listen, Tequila, I used to be an idealist, just like you. But the truth is that we have no objective system by which to measure someone's value other than the market. We should just embrace that, rather than resist it.\n
Zh: “听着，龙舌兰，我曾经是个理想主义者，就像你一样。但事实是，除了市场，我们并没有什么目标系统能够更好地衡量一个人的价值。我们应该拥抱这一点，而不是抵触它。”

En: Listen, Mr. Du Bois, I used to be an idealist, just like you. But the truth is that we have no objective system by which to measure someone's value other than the market. We should just embrace that, rather than resist it.\n
Zh: “听着，杜博阿先生，我曾经是个理想主义者，就像你一样。但事实是，除了市场，我们并没有什么目标系统能够更好地衡量一个人的价值。我们应该拥抱这一点，而不是抵触它。”

En: Listen, Mr. Costeau. He pauses. "Raphaël. I used to be an idealist, just like you. But the truth is that we have no objective system by which to measure someone's value other than the market. We should just embrace that, rather than resist it."\n
Zh: “听着，库斯托先生。”他停顿了一下。“拉斐尔。我曾经是个理想主义者，就像你一样。但事实是，除了市场，我们并没有什么目标系统能够更好地衡量一个人的价值。我们应该拥抱这一点，而不是抵触它。”

En: Listen, Harry, I used to be an idealist, just like you. But the truth is that we have no objective system by which to measure someone's value other than the market. We should just embrace that, rather than resist it.\n
Zh: “听着，哈里。我曾经是个理想主义者，就像你一样。但事实是，除了市场，我们并没有什么目标系统能够更好地衡量一个人的价值。我们应该拥抱这一点，而不是抵触它。”

En: Listen, I used to be an idealist, just like you. But the truth is that we have no objective system by which to measure someone's value other than the market. We should just embrace that, rather than resist it...
Zh: “听着，我曾经是个理想主义者，就像你一样。但事实是，除了市场，我们并没有什么目标系统能够更好地衡量一个人的价值。我们应该拥抱这一点，而不是抵触它……”

En: The hand that allocates resources among men is invisible and cruel, but it is *steady*, *measured*, indeed it is *just*.
Zh: “人与人之间那只分配资源的手是无形而残酷的，不过它是*不变的*、*慎重的*，它的的确确是*公正的*。”

En: Sure, and they benefit when I buy things to stimulate the economy. Do you know how many jobs it takes to build and maintain a racing yacht?
Zh: “当然，而且当我通过购物来刺激经济的时候，他们也会因此受益。你知道建造和维修一艘赛艇能提供多少岗位吗？”

En: Go right ahead, don't be shy.
Zh: “那就直接说吧，不要害羞。”

En: It's as if something is happening to the light.
Zh: “感觉好像光线出了什么问题。”

En: Oh, *that's* what you mean. Yes, I've heard of this effect, though I've never witnessed it myself, of course. It has something to do with our Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient.
Zh: “哦，原来你是*那个*意思。是的，我听说过这种效果，不过当然了，我自己从来没有亲眼见过。也许是跟我们的韦斯·韦斯曼系数有关吧。”

En: Oh, don't you worry, lieutenant. I can tell a good man when I see one.
Zh: “哦，别担心，警督。当我看到一个好人的时候，我能分辨出来的。”

En: Are you talking about my chin?
Zh: “你是在说我的下巴吗？”

En: No-no, I mean, I can't even see... *you*, it's as if something is happening to the light.
Zh: “不不，我是说，我甚至看不见……*你*，就好像光线出了什么问题。”

En: Yeah, it's the chin.
Zh: “是啊，是下巴的事。”

En: The lieutenant leans in and whispers to you: "Please, behave yourself."
Zh: 警督靠了过来，低声说道：“拜托，注意自己的行为。”

En: Hm. I've never heard anybody say anything about my chin. Yet I know that people often tell each other half-truths, things they think the other party would wish to hear...
Zh: “嗯。我从没听过别人说我下巴的事。不过我知道大家经常传些半真半假的话，那些他们以为别人会愿意听的话。”

En: Being rich does not make one impervious to the human condition. Maybe I do have a strange chin and you are the first person to call me out on this. Not that you should. But you did. For this -- I am grateful.
Zh: “富有并不能让人不受人类社会影响。也许我有个奇怪的下巴，而你是第一个说出来的人。并不是说你应该这么做。但你还是做了。为此——我很感激。”

En: I see nothing of the sort. To be frank, all I see is a gentleman who's unusually well dressed for Martinaise. In a cargo container. Which, I admit, is odd.
Zh: “我没看见那种东西。说实在的，我只看见了一个为马丁内斯特别精心打扮过的绅士。在一个货物集装箱里面。这一点，我必须承认，挺奇怪的。”

En: Are you telling me that you are so rich that light literally bends around your face?
Zh: “你是想告诉我，因为你很有钱，所以光线都在你脸上扭曲了吗？”

En: Travelling! This is a great way to get around -- it's fun, it's safe, and it gives me lots of time to think...
Zh: “旅行！这是一种很好的出行方式——有趣，安全，而且还能给我很多思考的时间……”

En: By the way, let me now ask *you* a question: Where are we, exactly?
Zh: “话说回来，现在让我来问*你*一个问题：我们到底是在哪里？”

En: We're in Revachol, formerly a colony of the Kingdom of Suresne.
Zh: “我们在瑞瓦肖，苏雷恩王国以前的一个殖民地。”

En: We're in Martinaise. There's a pretty good hostel here called the Whirling-in-Rags.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯。这里有一个很不错的旅社，叫褴褛飞旋。”

En: We're in Martinaise, a district of Revachol -- the former capital of the world.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯，瑞瓦肖的一个区——世界的前首都。”

En: Ummm... I really don't know.
Zh: “嗯……我真的不知道。”

En: What the detective means is that we're in Martinaise, a subdistrict of Jamrock in the capital city of Revachol.
Zh: “警探的意思是我们在马丁内斯，隶属于首都瑞瓦肖加姆洛克区的一片分区。”

En: Among other things... but calm down, I'm but a lowly single-digit billionaire.
Zh: “的确如此……但是冷静一点，我只不过是一个只有几亿资产的富翁而已。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: I'm calm.
Zh: “我很淡定。”

En: No, not really. There are actually quite many digits.
Zh: “不，不是几亿。其实有好几个位数的亿。”

En: It's a shame I can't get out and explore myself. One of the downsides of being an extremely high-net-worth individual is that mobs of low-net-worth individuals are constantly banding together to ask for money.
Zh: “很可惜我不能出去自己探索一番。做个超高资产净值人士有一个缺点，那就是很多低净值人士经常团结起来要钱。”

En: So you travel from place to place via shipping container?
Zh: “所以你通过船运集装箱从一个地方去往下一个地方？”

En: He smiles. "Never mind then. We'll just have to be on equal footing, geographically speaking."
Zh: 他笑了。“不要紧的。从地理的角度来讲，我们都要处在平等的基础之上。”

En: Wait -- why don't you help them? You've got so much money it can't make a difference to you.
Zh: “等等——你为什么不帮他们呢？你这么有钱，对你来说要没多大区别。”

En: Smart, no? It also provides a means to hide from all the targeted advertising we extremely high-net-worth individuals are constantly subjected to...
Zh: “很聪明，不是吗？它还提供了一种手段，让我们可以避开那些面向超高资产净值人士的定向广告……”

En: Luxury yachts, high-fidelity portable radio systems, pale-proof outerwear, and so on. It just gets a bit *middle class* after a while. A bit *bourgeois.*
Zh: “豪华游艇，高保真便携无线电系统，防灰域外套，等等之类的。不久之后就会变得有些*中产阶级*。有一点*小资*了。”

En: Those things all sound pretty nice.
Zh: “那些东西听起来都挺好的啊。”

En: Don't get me wrong: They're nice things, but once you achieve a certain level of wealth your time and mental space become *much* more important than material goods.
Zh: “不要误解我的意思：它们都是些很好的东西，不过一旦财富达到一定的水准之后，你的时间和心理空间就会变得比物质商品*更加*重要。”

En: There simply aren't enough hours a day to hand out all the handouts. It's like feeding seagulls. There are always more, and they never seem to do anything interesting with it -- except more seagulls.
Zh: “每天根本没有足够的时间去施舍所有救济品。感觉就像在喂海鸥。总会有更多的出现，而且它们似乎从来没用它做过什么有趣的事情——除了更多的海鸥。”

En: Spending money is a matter of desire, I'm sure you agree. I don't have the desire for spending it like that.
Zh: “花钱是一种欲望，我肯定你也同意。我并没有花那种钱的欲望。”

En: We're in Martinaise. There's a cool church here.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯。这里有一座很酷的教堂。”

En: We're in Martinaise. There's a creepy abandoned commercial area here.
Zh: “我们在马丁内斯。这里有一个很吓人的废弃商业区。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: And that's, what? Unjust? I think it's perfectly just. His tone is ever-so-slightly less agreeable than before.
Zh: “那又怎么了？不公平？我觉得这样非常公平。”他的口吻变得没有之前那么亲切了。

En: For almighty Revachol, of course!
Zh: “当然是为了全能的瑞瓦肖！”

En: Which one? Revachol, where I nominally reside? Or Graad, where my father emigrated from? Or Ile-de-Casherbrume where I've registered my shell companies?
Zh: “哪一个？瑞瓦肖，我名义上居住的地方？或者是格拉德，我父亲移居的地方？或者是雾金岛，我注册贝壳公司的地方？”

En: That's good.
Zh: “那很好啊。”

En: Present a sound investment plan.
Zh: 提出一个可靠的投资计划。

En: Know about money.
Zh: 知道钱的事。

En: Dresscode: Business casual.
Zh: 着装要求：商务便装。

En: Dresscode: Interisolar business class.
Zh: 着装要求：跨岛商务舱。

En: Art degree useless.
Zh: 艺术学位无用。

En: Well, as we say in my circle -- one person's crushing business failure is someone else's development opportunity.
Zh: “呃，我的圈子里流传着这样一句话——一个人惨重的商业失败就是另一个人的发展契机。”

En: Ah, a fellow history buff! I, myself, am currently reading up on Franconigerian-era trains. Very interesting stuff.
Zh: “啊，一个历史迷！我自己，目前正在研究弗兰考格斯时代的火車。非常有趣的东西。”

En: Was it Kras Mazov that said 'religion is pyrholidon for the masses'?
Zh: “那句‘宗教信仰是大众的吡嚯浣酮’是卡拉斯·马佐夫说的吗？”

En: Ah, interesting. I've made quite a few real-estate plays over the years.
Zh: “啊，有意思。这几年我做了不少房地产生意。”

En: Ah, Revachol. I remember walking its streets as a teenager. There used to be a bowling alley in Stella Maris... I wonder if it's still there?
Zh: “啊，瑞瓦肖。我记得十几岁的时候在那里的街道上走过。斯特拉里斯曾经有一个保龄球馆……不知道还在不在那里？”

En: He may have been a communard, but we're in agreement about that. Still, I imagine the architecture is quite lovely.
Zh: “他可能是一个公社社员，不过我们已经达成了一致。不过，我能想象那个建筑风格一定很不错。”

En: He speaks from the heart. He has very different problems compared to low-net-worth individuals such as yourself. For example, no problems at all.
Zh: 他说的话是真心的。相比你这种低净值人士来说，他遇到的问题完全不同。比如说，根本就没有问题。

En: Could you please stop asking *everyone* for money? It does not reflect well on the RCM and to be perfectly frank we can't afford to look worse than we already do.
Zh: “你能不能别再找*每一个人*要钱了？这对RCM影响不好，而且坦白地讲，我们不能让自己看上去比现在更糟糕了。”

En: But Kim! I also can't *afford* to look any better than I do now. That's why I need the money!
Zh: “但是，金！我也*没钱*让自己看起来比现在更好啊。所以我才需要钱啊！”

En: You're right. That was extremely unprofessional. I apologize.
Zh: “你说得对。这样真的太不专业了。我道歉。”

En: Sure! Let me check my pockets.
Zh: “当然！让我翻翻口袋。”

En: It's perfectly alright. Based on your appearance I can tell I'm dealing with a smart man...
Zh: “真的没什么。根据你的外表，我能看出自己在跟一个聪明人打交道……”

En: As you may know, us high-net-worth individuals do not have a lot of cash on hand. Investments and liquidity are enemies of one another -- I think I only have coins for coffee machines.
Zh: “你可能知道，我们高净值人士手上一般都没什么钱。投资和流动资产彼此是敌人——我想我的钱可能只够买咖啡机。”

En: Here's three reál, how much can you get for this?
Zh: “我有3雷亚尔，靠这些你能得到些什么？”

En: It gets me almost nothing.
Zh: “这点钱干什么都不够。”

En: Thank you for your kindness.
Zh: “感谢你的一片好心。”

En: Come on, man. He's *got* to have more than that. See if you can score a little more of that *lucre*.
Zh: 拜托，老兄。他*肯定*不只有这些。看看你能不能再多挣一些*收益*。

En: You're welcome. You know... His eyes narrow, the light seems to bend more *aggressively*.
Zh: “不客气。你知道的……”他眯起眼睛，光线似乎扭曲得更*激烈 *了。

En: That's the idea, my friend! You've got to work for the rest.
Zh: “就是这么回事，我的朋友！其他的就要靠你自己努力了。”

En: Maybe you can make that money grow? Come up with an investment plan? How's that sound?
Zh: “也许你可以让钱生钱？想出一个投资计划？听起来怎么样？”

En: This is a proposal, not a question.
Zh: 这是一个提议，不是一个问题。

En: It's *really* nothing.
Zh: “*真的*没什么。”

En: The lieutenant seems *very* relieved.
Zh: 警督看起来*非常*宽慰。

En: Oh, alright then. Another question.
Zh: “哦，那好吧。再来个问题。”

En: Deploying high-concept buzzword generator. All systems functional: Ready to engage in 3, 2, 1...
Zh: 部署高概念流行语生成器。所有系统功能：准备启动，3,2,1……

En: If business planning were really your strong suit, you probably wouldn't be a cop.
Zh: 如果商业计划真是你的特长，你可能就不会当警察了。

En: You should invest in a youth centre.
Zh: “你应该投资一个青少年活动中心。”

En: You should invest in the RCM!
Zh: “你应该投资RCM！”

En: Okay, so I've got this idea for a board game.
Zh: “好吧，我有个关于桌游的点子。”

En: A youth centre, huh? What *kind* of youth centre?
Zh: “一个青少年活动中心？哪种*类型*的青少年活动中心？”

En: Go on.
Zh: “继续。”

En: The volunteer police force? And why would I do that?
Zh: “自愿警察组织？我为什么要这么做？”

En: Are you sure you don't have any more? I thought you were a billionaire?
Zh: “你确定没有多的了吗？你不是个亿万富翁吗？”

En: Yes, I'm sure. You know... His eyes narrow, the light seems to bend more *aggressively*.
Zh: “是的。我很肯定。你知道的……”他眯起眼睛，光线似乎扭曲得更*激烈 *了。

En: Reminds you of that Joyce woman, doesn't he? Can't get rich by giving out free money.
Zh: 让你想到了那个叫乔伊斯的女人，不是吗？白给钱是不会变成有钱人的。

En: And yet it never seems to get us any closer to solving the case.
Zh: “而且好像也并不能让我们离破案更近一步。”

En: Playing the pity card, are we? Whatever gets us over the moon, baby, I'm your hand to be dealt.
Zh: 在打同情牌，是吗？不管怎么样，只要能带我们飞上月球，宝贝，我怎么都能接受。

En: These ultraliberal types love losing huge sums of money on ludicrous proposals. Ergo, you should come up with a plan that's TOTALLY dead in the water.
Zh: 这些极端自由主义者喜欢在荒唐的提案上花一大笔钱。因此，你应该想出一个完全行不通的计划。

En: Present an investment plan that is sure to fail.
Zh: 提出一个注定失败的投资计划。

En: Know about money.
Zh: 知道钱的事。

En: Dresscode: Business casual.
Zh: 着装要求：商务便装。

En: Dresscode: Interisolar business class.
Zh: 着装要求：跨岛商务舱。

En: Art degree useless.
Zh: 艺术学位无用。

En: Congratulations, you've somehow managed to fail at failing, which means, in a strange way, that you've succeeded?
Zh: 恭喜，你莫名其妙因为失败而失败了，这就意味着，以一种奇怪的方式来看，你成功了？

En: What's worse than throwing money at something that's already failed?
Zh: 还有什么能比往已经失败的东西里砸钱更糟糕的呢？

En: Steal the Union boss's idea, add a twist, and present it as your own? Very capitalistic!
Zh: 偷窃工会老大的点子，加点转折，然后当成你自己的呈现出来？这还真是有够资本主义的！

En: One dedicated to instilling liberal economic values in children from low-net-worth families.
Zh: “致力于向低净值家庭的孩子灌输自由经济的价值观。”

En: A place to teach them practical skills like teamwork and self-discipline.
Zh: 一个教会他们实用技能的地方，比如团队合作和自律。

En: Brilliant! Without children who'll be there to buy stuff in the future?
Zh: “太棒了！如果没有孩子，谁还会在未来买东西呢？”

En: Yes. And if it doesn't work out, we can always re-purpose the centre as a shopping mall or private equity firm.
Zh: “是的。而且如果没有成功，我们总是可以重新规划那个中心，把它变成一个购物中心，或者是私人股本公司。”

En: Very impressive. You've got a natural eye for unusual investment opportunities.
Zh: “确实很激动人心。你天生就对与众不同的投资机会很有眼光。”

En: When life closes a door, it opens a window, yes? What's the expected return on this?
Zh: “当生活关上一扇门，就会为你打开一扇窗，是吗？那这个的预期回报是多少？”

En: With human beings there's always a risk associated. Which is why we've got to hold onto the centre itself as a fallback.
Zh: “人类总是跟风险联系在一起的。所以我们要把这个中心本身当作是一个储备物。”

En: Highly educated, work-ready, human capital ready to be directed toward any number of your vast interests.
Zh: “受过高等教育，做好工作准备的人力资本，随时准备为你巨大的利益而服务。”

En: Thank you.
Zh: “谢谢。”

En: I know.
Zh: “我知道。”

En: I don't normally do this without a formal pitch deck, but to hell with it, what's the point of being rich if you have to follow all the rules?
Zh: “一般来说，没有正式的融资演讲稿我是不会这么做的，但是让它见鬼去吧，如果必须遵守所有规则的话，那当有钱人还有什么意思呢？”

En: Here's a round of seed funding. This should be enough to prove out the concept and get things off the ground.
Zh: “这是第一轮的种子资金。应该足够证明这个概念，然后让计划正式起步了。”

En: Thank you for placing your unwavering *trust* in me.
Zh: “感谢你对我坚定不移的*信任*。”

En: Thanks for the handout.
Zh: “感谢你的施舍。”

En: Remember: it's not a *handout*, it's an investment. And I expect to see returns.
Zh: “记住：这不是*施舍*，这是一项投资。而且我期望能看到回报。”

En: The lieutenant stands there, dumbfounded. His mouth opens slightly, then closes again.
Zh: 警督目瞪口呆地站在原地。他的嘴巴微微张开，然后又合上了。

En: Is he having a stroke?
Zh: 他是不是中风了？

En: What do you think, Kim? Not bad, huh?
Zh: “你觉得怎么样，金？不赖吧，是不是？”

En: Kim, are you alright?
Zh: “金，你还好吗？”

En: Kim, are you having a stroke?
Zh: “金，你中风了吗？”

En: I am not *un*-impressed. Let's leave it at that.
Zh: “我并不是*没有*被打动。这个话题先到此为止吧。”

En: Yes. I'm alright. He pauses to compose himself. "Can we get going now?"
Zh: “是的。我很好。”他停下来调整了一下情绪。“现在我们可以走了吗？”

En: No, I am *not* having a stroke. You're just... still full of surprises. Most of them bad, but some good...
Zh: “不，我*没有*中风。你还真是……充满了惊喜啊。大部分时候这都是坏事，不过也有好的…… ”

En: The lieutenant has granted you an aura of legitimacy. Bathe in it, but don't let your satisfaction show. Play it cool.
Zh: 警督赋予了你一个合法的光环。沐浴其中吧，但是不要让你的满足感显露出来。要装的酷一点。

En: It's a pen-and-paper role-playing game -- only the players are connected with radiocomputers!
Zh: “是个纸笔角色扮演游戏——只是玩家是通过无线电脑连接的！”

En: CHA-CHING. What'll it be? Speed? Vodka? Cigarettes?
Zh: 叮咚。会是什么呢？思必得？伏特加？香烟？

En: *Bratan*, now's your chance to take some time off. Spend it with your good buddy, and get absolutely *wrecked* in the process.
Zh: *兄弟*，现在你有机会稍微放松一下了。跟你的好兄弟一起享受，在过程中彻底*迷幻陶醉*吧。

En: Hmm. Drugs do go well with money, I agree.
Zh: “嗯。毒品确实跟钱很配，我同意。”

En: I'm sorry, man, I'm an investor now -- I have to stay sober to calculate risks.
Zh: “很抱歉，老兄，现在我是个投资人了——我要清醒地计算风险。”

En: What is this shit?!? CALCULATING RISKS? *BRATAN*. THE RISKS YOU CANT CALCULATE ARE THE ONLY ONES WORTH TAKING!
Zh: 这他妈都是什么鬼？！？计算风险？*兄弟*。你无法计算的风险才是那些值得冒的风险！

En: Nothing less than total annihilation will suffice.
Zh: 只有完全地毁灭才能够格。

En: The lieutenant looks at you with horror. You've been mumbling to your necktie in a daze for several minutes.
Zh: 警督惊恐地看着你。你已经迷迷糊糊地对着自己的领带念叨了好几分钟了。

En: Ah, yes. Now you're displaying it... the *eccentricity* that becomes a wealthy individual. If the money-saint's visage weren't wrapped in physics-defying light, you would see his approval.
Zh: “啊，是的。现在你展示出来了……成为一个有钱人士的*怪癖*。”如果这位钱圣的面容没有被违背物理原理的光线所包裹的话，你会看到他赞同的样子。

En: Hello, darkness.
Zh: “你好啊，黑暗。”

En: I'm sorry. I just don't see the ROI in that.
Zh: “很抱歉。我并没有看出里面的投资回报率。”

En: Thanks for stopping by. If you ever have an idea you want to pitch me, I'm all ears.
Zh: “感谢你的来访。如果你有什么点子想推销给我的话，我洗耳恭听。”

En: Brace yourself. It is *very* high concept.
Zh: “准备好。这是一个*非常*高层次的设想。”

En: I'm ready.
Zh: “我准备好了。”

En: Hold on. Have we met before?
Zh: “等等。我们之前是不是见过？”

En: Its a pen-and-paper game where people all over the world can play with each other -- using radios!
Zh: “是一个纸笔游戏，全世界的人都可以一起玩耍——使用无线电！”

En: Nicking an idea that wasn't worth anything in the first place and trying to pass it off as your own? How very *entrepreneurial*. I love it.
Zh: 窃取一个从开始就毫无价值的想法，还要试着把它包装成自己的？还真是很有*企业家精神*呢。我喜欢。

En: Well, at first everything was rosy, the ideas were solid, but they were lacking in... how do I put it...
Zh: “呃，一开始一切都是乐观的，点子很可靠，不过他们缺少……我该怎么说呢……”

En: I don't think so.
Zh: “我不这么认为。”

En: Maybe? I wouldn't really know.
Zh: “也许？我也不知道。”

En: No, on second thought, it couldn't have been you.
Zh: “不，仔细一想，应该不可能是你。”

En: I guess the inordinate amount of time they poured into drawing mythical creatures did not generate a return on investment.
Zh: “我猜他们在描绘神秘生物上花费了过多的时间，但是却并没有得到投资上的回报。”

En: Your idea reminds me of a group of young men who came to me a long time ago, calling themselves 'Fortress Accident' -- the name should've been a red flag.
Zh: “你的想法让我想起一群年轻人，他们很早之前来找过我，称呼自己是‘要塞事故’——这个名字本身就是个危险信号。”

En: Yeah, on second thought, it couldn't have been you.
Zh: “是啊，仔细一想，应该不可能是你。”

En: They pitched me and a handful of other investors on an idea for a role-playing game that would, in their words, change the world.
Zh: “他们向我和其他一些投资人推销了一个角色扮演游戏的主意，用他们的话来说，这个游戏可以改变世界。”

En: What happened?
Zh: “出什么事了？”

En: They lacked the *will* to get things done. As their financial situation became more desperate, their ideas devolved from realistic to absolute insanity.
Zh: “他们缺少完成计划的*意志力*。当他们的财政状况变得越发绝望时，他们的主意从现实退化成了纯粹的狂想。”

En: We lost all of our money. High-art types never deliver. They're only good for peddling welkins or whatever creeps they come up with.
Zh: “我们失去了所有的钱。玩高级艺术那类人从来都不能履行诺言。他们只擅长兜售精灵或者是他们能够想象出来的那些小怪物。”

En: Did you try to salvage the project somehow?
Zh: “你有没有尝试过挽救那个项目呢？”

En: Wait. Where are they now?
Zh: “等等。他们现在在哪？”

En: Nobody knows for sure, but the place can't be pretty.
Zh: “没人能肯定，但是这个地方肯定不太美妙。”

En: What I want to tell you is this: it's a very bad idea. Dead in the water. You seem like a reasonable man, but that is not a reasonable plan.
Zh: “我想告诉你的是：这个是很坏的主意。完全行不通。你看起来像是个理智的人，但这并不是一个理智的计划。”

En: Sadly, when we got there, it was too late. The concept had run out of steam, only dust remained.
Zh: “可悲的是，当我们过去的时候，已经太迟了。这个设想已经燃烧殆尽，只余灰尘了。”

En: Thank you for the advice.
Zh: “感谢你的建议。”

En: Are you *sure* you don't want to give those little welkins a second go?
Zh: “你*确定*不想给那些小精灵第二次机会吗？”

En: Absolutely not.
Zh: “绝对不想。”

En: Because they have guns!
Zh: “因为我们有枪！”

En: Because it's chronically mismanaged, deeply corrupt, and completely outmatched.
Zh: “因为它长期管理不善，极其腐败，而且完全技不如人。”

En: You should make your money work for you.
Zh: “你应该让金钱为你工作。”

En: Intriguing. Go on.
Zh: “很吸引人。继续说。”

En: Like they say in investment circles, you gotta spend money to make money. Money's the name of the game here.
Zh: “就像我们投资圈有句话，你需要通过花钱来挣钱。钱才是这里最重要的东西。”

En: I think I follow you. You're saying I should invest in exotic financial derivatives? Or maybe some kind of real-estate play?
Zh: “我想我明白了。你是说我应该投资外国金融衍生品？或者也许是某种房地产生意？”

En: I'm very impressed. You have a deep understanding of the world that few men ever attain.
Zh: “我很受触动。你对这个世界有很深的理解，只有极少数人能达到你的水平。”

En: It's a twist on an already established, working idea.
Zh: “这是一个对已经完善、且行之有效的主意的转折。”

En: Yes?
Zh: “什么？”

En: It's time to disrupt the future. You've got to stay lean, innovative, and focus on what matters most: *the children*.
Zh: 是时候干涉未来了。你必须须保持高效、创新，集中在最重要的问题上：* 孩子*。

En: Welcome back, gentlemen. What can I do for you? Although you cannot see his face, you can tell he's smiling warmly.\n
Zh: “欢迎回来，先生。我能为你做什么呢？”虽然你看不见他的脸，但是你能感觉到他那张亲切的笑脸。

En: Welcome back, friend. What can I do for you? Although you cannot see his face, you can tell he's smiling warmly.
Zh: “欢迎回来，我的朋友。我能为你做什么呢？”虽然你看不见他的脸，但是你能感觉到他那张亲切的笑脸。

En: Actually, I'm not so sure about that.
Zh: “其实呢，我也不是很确定。”

En: I'll give it some thought.
Zh: “我会考虑一下的。”

En: It was a real pleasure. Sadly, though, my current lifestyle prevents me from remaining more than a brief time in any one place. Perhaps we'll meet again, somewhere far away...
Zh: “我真的挺高兴的。不过可悲的是，我现在的生活状态不能让我在任何一个地方停留太长的时间。也许我们还会再见，在某个遥远的地方……”

En: The thing about investing is that the less work you have to do, the better. Don't over-think. That's what return on investment means.
Zh: “投资这种事就是你做的越少，效果就越好。不要想太多。这就是投资回报的含义。”

En: It's perfectly alright. Based on your appearance I can tell I'm dealing with a civilized man...
Zh: “其实真的没关系。根据你的外表，我知道自己在跟一个文明人打交道……”

En: Could you please stop asking people for money? It does not reflect well on the RCM and to be perfectly frank we can't afford to look worse than we already do.
Zh: “你能不能别找别人要钱了吗？这对RCM影响不好，而且坦白地讲，我们不能让自己看上去比现在更糟糕了。”

En: I don't see how it's appropriate for a representative of the law to ask a wealthy person for money. This shines a... bad light on the RCM, if you catch my meaning.
Zh: “我不觉得一个执法人员找富人要钱是合适的。这样会给……RCM造成不好的影响，如果你明白我的意思。”

En: Come now, there is not even a scent of corruption here. I am merely being polite... so let me check my pockets.
Zh: “拜托，这里连一丝腐败的味道都没有。我只不过是出于礼貌……我来翻翻口袋。”

En: The name's Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau. Most likely.
Zh: “我的名字叫拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托，也许吧。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, Raphaël, he says warmly. "I am surprised -- in a positive manner, do understand -- about your exceedingly high-net-worth name."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，拉斐尔，”他亲切地说道。“我很惊讶——请理解，是以一种积极的态度——你拥有一个非常高净值范的名字。”

En: It's Harrier Du Bois, but I prefer to be called Tequila Sunset.
Zh: “我叫哈里尔·杜博阿，不过我更喜欢别人叫我龙舌兰日落。”

En: The name's Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: “名字是哈里尔·杜博阿。”

En: Harrier Du Bois.
Zh: “哈里尔·杜博阿。”

En: Pleasure to meet you, Harrier Du Bois, he says warmly. "I must admit, the name suits you very well."
Zh: “很高兴见到你，哈里尔·杜博阿，”他亲切地说道。“我必须承认，这个名字非常适合你。”

En: Pleasure to meet you Harr... I mean, Tequila Sunset, he says warmly. "It is an odd name, to be sure. There's something... rather grim about it."
Zh: “请高兴见到你，哈……我是说，龙舌兰日落，”他亲切地说道。“老实说，这是个奇怪的名字。给人有一种……特别冷酷的感觉。”

En: It is not... The lieutenant stops to rub his temples. "Never mind."
Zh: “不是的……”警督停下来揉了揉太阳穴。“没事了。”

En: You would not even believe, the lieutenant mutters to himself.
Zh: “你根本不会相信，”警督自己嘀咕着。

En: Ignore him.
Zh: 无视他。

En: We have a *murder* to solve, and yet you go around bothering everyone about *money*, arguing that you think it's 'somehow related to the case.'
Zh: “我们有一起*凶杀案*要调查，结果你还到处找每一个人*要钱*，还狡辩说你觉得‘不知怎么地就是与案子有关联。’”

En: Cool. I don't even have any interesting replies.
Zh: “厉害。我甚至都想不出什么有意思的回复。”

En: One would imagine living such a high-net-worth existence is overwhelmingly exciting, but yes, as you now see... it can sometimes just stun you with its boredom.
Zh: “人们会想象，过着这种高净值生活一定会出奇地兴奋，不过没错，现在你也看到了……有时候也会这么的朴实无华且枯燥，让人惊讶。”

En: It is, truly. It's almost entirely care free. He nods. "It really leaves you time to better yourself spiritually."
Zh: “没错，是真的，的确是可以无忧无虑。”他点点头。“真的留给你很多时间，在精神层面上提升自己。”

En: Capital. He nods. "Makes one speechless, does it not? Blinds like the sun that rises from beyond the horizon after a gloomy winter."
Zh: “资本家。”他点点头。“让人无言以对，不是吗？就像阴郁冬季过后从地平线上升起的太阳一般让人盲目。”

En: A million?!
Zh: “一百万个？！”

En: You know, a regular youth centre, with basketball courts and stuff. To *really* develop their physical proficiency.
Zh: “你知道的，一个普通的青少年活动中心，里面有篮球场啊，什么之类的。可以*真正*提升他们的身体素质。”

En: You know, a regular youth centre, with basketball courts and stuff. To teach teamwork and other emotional skills.
Zh: “你知道的，一个普通的青少年活动中心，里面有篮球场啊，什么之类的。可以教会他们团队协作以及其他社会情感能力。”

En: The *blaasma gun* that shoots periwinkle blue high energy plasma! This is your chance...
Zh: 可以发射浅紫蓝色高能等离子体的*离子枪*！这是你的机会……

En: Let me tell you about an idea that combines bigger guns with the latest and greatest in technology progress.
Zh: “我来给你说说一个想法，它把威力更强的枪支跟最新、最伟大的技术进步组合在一起了。”

En: I'm listening.
Zh: “我正在听。”

En: Imagine, a massive gun that uses a magnetic accelerator to accelerate highly energized hydrogen-based plasma to near light speed. At contact with anything it detonates with the *power of a dying star*, erasing it from existence in a flash of periwinkle blue!
Zh: “想象一下，一支巨大的枪支，能够使用磁力加速器将高能氢基等离子体提升至接近光速。只要接触到任何东西，都能爆发出*一颗陨星的能量*，一片浅紫蓝色闪过之后，就能将任何存在彻底抹去。”

En: The shape stands in stunned silence.
Zh: 那个人影惊愕地站在一片寂静之中。

En: Not the blaasma gun again. The lieutenant takes off the glasses and rubs his eyes with his fingers. "It's not even *possible*."
Zh: “别再提什么离子枪的事了。”警督取下眼镜，用手指揉了揉眼睛。“这根本就*不可能*。的”

En: It is not possible. But what a dream... He shakes his head. "Either way, even if I could do it, I certainly wouldn't give an incompetent and corrupt police force *WORLD-ENDING* weaponry."
Zh: “的确不可能。不过真是个伟大的梦想……”他摇摇头。“不管怎样，就算我能做到，我肯定也不会把能够*毁灭世界*的大杀器交给一支无能又腐败的警察队伍。”

En: But I appreciate the *grandiosity* of your ideas. Thus you might understand -- the entire org structure of the RCM needs to be redefined. You need a unified chain of command, an end to petty spats between precincts, new metrics for measuring performance that reward real *police work*. Does that sound right?
Zh: “但是我很欣赏你这个*宏伟*的想法。不过你也许能理解——RCM的整个组织结构都需要重新定义。你需要一条统一的指挥链，结束各分局之间的小争端，还有衡量真正的*警察工作*并给予奖励的新标准。你说对不对吧？”

En: Trying to visualize the physics at play is liable to give you an aneurysm. Don't think about it too hard!
Zh: 尝试将物理学作用形象化的话，你会很容易患上动脉瘤的。千万别想太多了！

En: The Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient for you and this individual appears to be...
Zh: 你和这个人之间的韦斯·韦斯曼系数好像是……

En: The Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient is a ratio designed to reflect the difference in net worth between individuals. When the coefficient is close to 1 (or 100%) it means one person possesses all the net worth among that group of individuals. It's been observed that when the Weiss-Wiesemann coefficient reaches about .96 or so, the laws of physics begin to bend around the high-net-worth individual.
Zh: 韦斯·韦斯曼系数是一个比率，旨在反应个体之间的净资产差异。从本质上看，当这个系数接近1（或者100%）的时候，就意味着一个人占有了一群人的所有资本净值。通过观察结果发现：当韦斯·韦斯曼系数达到0.96左右的时候，高净值人士周围的物理定律就会开始扭曲。

En: So what is our coefficient?
Zh: 那我们的系数呢？

En: .9998 repeating.
If you ask me, he's only serving one of them thus far, the lieutenant mutters, half to himself.
Zh: “如果你问我，目前为止他只为其中一个服务了，”警督嘟囔着，有一半是对自己说的。

En: If you ask me, he's only serving one of them thus far, the lieutenant mutters, half to himself.
Zh: “如果你问我，目前为止他只服务了其中一个，”警督嘟囔着，有一半是对自己说的。

En: All I did was take her fortune and invest it prudently. Believe it or not, it takes more than a bit of skill not to blow a vast fortune on sailing boats, bad choices, and *unsupervised* state policy.
Zh: “我唯一做的就是接管了她的财富，然后谨慎投资。不管你信不信，要想不在游艇、糟糕的选择和*无人监督的*国家政策上挥霍大笔资金的话，需要的不仅仅是技巧这么简单。”

En: And blow.
Zh: 还有挫折。

En: Some of my lower-net-worth friends say to me, 'But doesn't that take all the fun out of it?' and I tell them, 'Not really.'
Zh: “我有一些低净值朋友告诉我，‘但是那不就失去所有乐趣了吗？’，然后我就会告诉他们，‘不见得。’”

En: Hundreds?
Zh: “几百个？”

En: Thousands?
Zh: “几千个？”

En: *Dozens*, at least. Of course, in the future it'll all be automated. But my point is this... he says, jabbing his finger into the air a bit...
Zh: “至少*几十个*。当然了，未来全部会变成自动化的。不过我的观点是……”他一边说，一边用手指在空气中戳了一下……

En: Every man gets what he earns. It's the height of tyranny to take that from him.
Zh: “每个人能挣得多少就是多少。如果连这个都要夺走的话，那就是暴政的顶点了。”

En: Where we are right now, the freedom of mankind can only be derived from the free flow of capital.
Zh: “我们现在所处的地方，人类只能从资本的自由流动中得到自由。”

En: Just a bunch of evil apes dukin' it out on a giant ball.
Zh: “只不过是一群邪恶的大猩猩在太空中的一个巨大的球上决一雌雄。”

En: That would suck.
Zh: “那就太糟糕了。”

En: Oh, probably Graad, in that case.
Zh: “哦，如果是这样的话，那可能是格拉德。”

En: You're using your money as its own justification.
Zh: “你在用你的钱为它自己辩护。”

En: A nightmare scenario. A world of slavery and violence. Which brings us back to the essential truth of modernism...
Zh: “一个噩梦般的场景。一个充满奴役和暴力的世界。这就把我们都带回到现代主义的本质特征……”

En: Our what?
Zh: “我们的什么？”

En: It's some statistical thing. In essence, it says that when an extremely *low*-net-worth individual meets an extremely *high*-net-worth individual, some of the laws of physics cease to apply.
Zh: “这是一种统计学上的东西。大体上，它是说当一个极低净值人士遇到一个极高净值人士时，某些物理定律就不再适用了。”

En: Kim, are you seeing this weird stuff?
Zh: “金，你看到这个奇怪的东西了吗？”

En: Ah, so you're saying being rich isn't worth the hassle.
Zh: “啊，所以你是说有钱不值得这些麻烦。”

En: What? No, I didn't say that at all. Being rich is great. Just don't tell anyone I told you that. The bending light appears to *wink*.
Zh: “什么？不，那可不是我说的。有钱的感觉很棒。只是不要跟别人讲是我说的。”扭曲的光线似乎*眨了眨眼*。

En: (Yawn.) "Yeah, that does sound pretty tiresome."
Zh: （打个哈欠。）“是啊，听起来就挺烦人的了。”

En: Good. Now that we've sorted that out, let me ask you something else.
Zh: “很好。既然这个已经说清楚了，那我问你点别的问题。”

En: Go right ahead.
Zh: “那就问吧。”

En: You know, a regular youth centre, with basketball courts and stuff.
Zh: “你知道的，一个普通的青少年活动中心，里面有篮球场啊，什么之类的。”

En: A place to train *buff kids*.
Zh: 一个训练那些*小屁孩*的地方。

En: One to inspire the future leaders of tomorrow to public service.
Zh: “一个激励未来的领导者投身于公共事业的地方。”

En: Come on. Tell him what he wants to hear.
Zh: 拜托。说点他想听的。

En: Hmmm, the man thinks for a moment.
Zh: “嗯，”男人想了一会儿。

En: I like that you're thinking about the future, but couldn't the centre have a more... economic focus?
Zh: “我喜欢你考虑到了未来这一点，不过这个中心就不能再聚焦在……经济上一点吗？”

En: No, no, no. You've got to think *bigger* than that.
Zh: “不不不。你要想的再*大*一点。”

En: You're deep into ultraliberal territory now. Good work.
Zh: 现在你已经深深陷入极端自由主义者的领域了。干得好。

En: I'm saying it's fun to ride the lion until it's time to get off.
Zh: “我是说骑在狮子身上是挺好玩的，直到到时候要下来的那一刻。”

En: I'm saying there's no sense throwing good money after bad.
Zh: “我是说把钱花在不该花的地方是没有意义的。”

En: Some people say money can't buy happiness, but those people just didn't have enough money.
Zh: “有些人说钱买不来快乐，但是那些人只是钱不够而已。”

En: But what do you say to those who claim that the assets underlying those derivatives are overvalued? Does that pose a systemic risk to the wider capital markets?
Zh: “但是对于那些声称衍生品的潜在价值被高估的人，你是怎么看的？它会给更广泛的资本市场带来系统性风险吗？”

En: But real-estate is an illiquid asset. What happens if you need a short-term capital infusion and all your holdings are tied up?
Zh: “但是房地产是一种非现金资产。如果你需要注入短期资金，但是所有财产都被套牢了呢？”

En: You're in deep now, but don't lose your nerve. You can do this.
Zh: 现在你已经深陷其中，但是不要紧张。你可以做到的。

En: Look man, markets are always going like this. (Make an up-and-down motion with your hand.)
Zh: “你看，老兄，市场总是这样运作的。”（用你的手做出上下起伏的动作。）

En: You've got to spend money to make money.
Zh: “你必须通过花钱来赚钱。”

En: Money literally makes the world go round.
Zh: “可以毫不夸张的说，钱才是让这个世界转动的东西。”

En: A wise man once told me that money is all about trust.
Zh: “一个智者告诉我金钱完全是关乎于信任的。”

En: You said that already.
Zh: “你已经说过了。”

En: Yes, yes, but the question is how do we take advantage of those fluctuations?
Zh: “是的，是的，但问题是我们该怎么利用那些波动？”

En: Yes, every school child hears that. The question is how do *we* get more of that money?
Zh: “是的，每个上过学的孩子都听说过。问题是*我们*怎么才能赚到更多的钱？”

En: The man pauses, considering your words. The light bends and swims around his face. You cannot tell what he's thinking...
Zh: 男人停顿下来，思考着你的话语。光线在他的脸上扭曲、游走。你不知道他在想什么……

En: Trust... yes...
Zh: “信任……是的……”

En: Who'd have thought you'd remember that nugget of wisdom after nearly frying your brain like you have?
Zh: 谁会想到在你的大脑差一点爆炸之后，你还能记起这样的至理名言呢？

En: Such a simple truth, yet so easily forgotten. What is money, anyway, but an idea in our heads we all *trust* one another to believe in?
Zh: “如此简单的一句信任，但是却那么容易被遗忘。不管怎么说，什么是金钱，不过是我们全都想让彼此*信服*的一个脑海里的概念？”

En: You're welcome. Now, was there something else I could help you with?
Zh: “不客气。现在，你还有什么需要我帮忙的吗？”

En: Because if you don't, who will?
Zh: “因为如果你不做，谁还会呢？”

En: That's why I *should* invest? Are you trying to say that the RCM's bad reputation makes it an undervalued asset?
Zh: “这就是我*应该*投资的原因？你是想说RCM的坏名声让它变成了一项估值过低的资产？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”
