En: No, I think it's exactly as valuable as it looks.
Zh: “不，我觉得它就跟看起来一样有价值。”

En: I'm already on the board of two major arms manufacturers. I've got no shortage of guns, if I wanted such things...
Zh: “我已经是两家大型军火商的董事会成员了。如果我想要枪的话，绝对不会缺的……”

En: I don't believe in handouts, especially for people who *volunteered* for their positions...
Zh: “我不相信施舍，特别是施舍给那些*自愿*承担某种职务的人……”

En: Still, the idea of a privatized police force is *extremely* forward thinking. You could even say it's *ultra* ultraliberal...
Zh: “不过，警局私有化的点子*的确*属于前瞻思维。你甚至可以说它是*究极的*极端自由主义……”

En: Tell me, if you could invest in the RCM, where would you direct your resources?
Zh: “告诉我，如果我投资RCM，你会把资源用在什么地方？”

En: Bigger guns! Large-calibre motherfuckers that leave exit wounds the size of grapefruits.
Zh: 更猛的枪！大口径的上等货，能留下葡萄柚大小的出口伤。

En: The latest technological wonders.
Zh: 最新的技术奇迹。

En: Take care of the people who protect the people.
Zh: 照顾那些保护群众的人。

En: Radiocomputers for every precinct.
Zh: “给每个分局配无线电脑。”

En: Pay raises for officers and generous benefits.
Zh: “增加警官的薪水还有丰厚的福利。”

En: Large-calibre weapons for patrol officers.
Zh: “给巡警配备大口径武器。”

En: Hmm. I don't believe in giving anyone money for nothing, but maybe there's something to using *market incentives* to improve performance...
Zh: “嗯。我不相信免费给人送钱这一套，不过也许利用*市场激励*来提高绩效是可行的……”

En: The RCM's problems aren't the kind you can solve with *radiocomputers*. The rot goes much deeper than that...
Zh: “RCM的问题不是*无线电脑*就能解决的。根源要比那个更深……”

En: You want to take an incompetent and corrupt police force and give them *deadlier* weapons? No, no, that won't work...
Zh: “你想接手一支无能又腐败的警察队伍，还要给他们*致命*武器？不，不行，这样行不通的……”

En: You need to think *bigger*. The entire org structure of the RCM needs to be redefined. You need a unified chain of command, an end to petty spats between precincts, new metrics for measuring performance that reward real *police work*. Does that sound right?
Zh: “你需要想的再*大*一些。RCM的整个组织结构都需要重新定义。你需要一条统一的指挥链，结束各分局之间的小争端，还有衡量真正的*警察工作*并给予奖励的新标准。你说对不对吧？”

En: Hell no! Can you imagine how much work that would take? Why would I do that when I can just speculate on exotic financial derivatives from the comfort of this shipping container?
Zh: “当然不会！你能想象这样需要付出多少努力吗？当我可以舒舒服服地待在这个船运集装箱里考虑外国金融衍生品的时候，为什么还要这么做呢？”

En: Yes, yes! That's *precisely* what we need.
Zh: “是的，没错！这*正是*我们需要的。”

En:
So you'll do it?	“所以你会这么做吗？”
0x0100007000003C70	Dialogue Text	CONTAINERYARD / LIGHT BENDING GUY	You	That sounds like a lot of work."
Zh: “听起来确实挺费力的。”

En: Precisely, which is why I'm not going to do it. Why would I do that when I can just speculate on exotic financial derivatives from the comfort of this shipping container?
Zh: “没错，所以我不会这么做。当我可以舒舒服服地待在这个船运集装箱里考虑外国金融衍生品的时候，为什么还要这么做呢？”

En: The lieutenant's disappointment is palpable. It's like someone just shot his dog.
Zh: 警督的失望是溢于言表的。感觉好像有人刚刚枪杀了他的爱犬。

En: Now, was there anything else I could help you gentlemen with?
Zh: “现在，我还有什么能帮你们的吗？”

En: Now, was there anything else I could help you with?
Zh: “现在，我还有什么能帮你的吗？”

En: He should what?
Zh: “他应该怎么？”

En: Just a minute. The RCM has its difficulties, but generally speaking--
Zh: “等一下。RCM是有它的困难，但是总的来说……”

En: You know, that's *exactly* what I've heard. From what I understand, the RCM as currently constituted is essentially a lost cause...
Zh: “你知道的，这*正是*我听到的。按照我的理解，当前构建的这个RCM本来就是一个注定会失败的事业……”

En: Hmmmm, a tried-and-true strategy for increasing the perception of value without improving the underlying asset. Not bad. But still...
Zh: “嗯，在不提高标的资产的情况下提升感知价值确实是一个可靠的策略。还不赖。不过……”

En: Uhhh... I'd pay some designers to come up with a new logo and tagline?
Zh: “呃……我请一些设计师想出一个新标志和标语？”

En: If I could interject: the RCM's problems are mainly *structural*. We're hamstrung by a lack of clear lines of authority, and our system of performance reviews strongly *disincentivizes* inter-precinct cooperation.
Zh: “请允许我插一句：RCM的问题主要在于*结构*。因为缺乏一条清晰的权力链导致现在这种力不从心的局面，而且我们的工作表现衡量体系严重*抑制*了各分局之间的配合。”

En: I think the lieutenant's right. Your problems run much deeper than equipment or funding. The entire org structure of the RCM needs to be redefined. You need a unified chain of command, new metrics for measuring performance that reward real *police work*. Does that sound right?
Zh: “我觉得警督说得没错。你们的问题远比设备或者资金要深远的多。RCM的整个组织结构都需要重新定义。你需要一条统一的指挥链，结束各分局之间的小争端，还有衡量真正的*警察工作*并给予奖励的新标准。你说对不对吧？”

En: The pleasure was mine. Unfortunately, I must be away soon. The next time we meet, I'll be expecting an update on my investment!
Zh: “这是我的荣幸。不幸的是，我很快就要离开了。下次见面的时候，期望能听到我这笔投资的新信息。”

En: Farewell, friend, and may your peace of mind guide you to happiness.
Zh: “再见了，朋友，愿你内心的宁静指引你走向幸福。”

En: Why go so soon? I feel we've so much more to talk about. Ah, well. Until next time!
Zh: “这么快就要走了吗？我感觉我们还有很多东西可以聊呢。啊，好吧。那就下次再见吧！”

En: Unbelievable. You manage to meet a man who's so rich light *literally bends around him* and all you can get out of him is three reál? What's wrong with you?
Zh: 难以置信。你遇到了一个有钱到能*切实扭曲周身光线*的人，结果你只从他那里拿到了可怜的3雷亚尔？你有什么毛病吗？

En: I can't see how that was worth the ruckus... He looks at the crate. "Except for seeing the crane in action. Which, I admit, was satisfying."
Zh: “我看不出怎么就值得搞出那么大的骚动……”他看着集装箱。“不过能看见运转中的起重机。这一点，我必须承认，挺让人满意的。”

En: With a loud grind the crane shifts overhead, moving a massive metal container through the air...
Zh: 随着一声巨响，头顶的起重机开动了，吊起一个巨大的金属集装箱经过半空……

En: A rusting control panel with several knobs. Two buttons, marked *Marche* and *Arrêt*, are faded with use. It seems to control the large crane above -- a container is attached to its hook block.
Zh: 一个生锈的控制面板，上面有几个开关。两个标注着*启动*和*停机*的按钮，被用得褪了色。它好像控制着上面那台大起重机——它的带钩滑車上连着一个集装箱。

En: Press *Arrêt*.
Zh: 按下*停机*按钮。

En: Press *Marche*.
Zh: 按下*启动*按钮。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: The crane stands tall: proud, erect and *still*.
Zh: 起重机高高地矗立着，骄傲，笔直，*纹丝不动*。

En: No screech of metal when you push the button this time. Only silence, as the crane remains in its state of dormancy.
Zh: 这次当你按下按钮的时候，没有发出金属刺耳的尖叫声。只有沉默，而起重机依然处于休眠状态。

En: The crane does not return to its original position. It does not move at all.
Zh: 起重机没有回到原来的位置。它完全没动。

En: ... and with a surprisingly quiet thunk, the crane places the container down.
Zh: ……随着安静地惊人的*铛*得一声，起重机把集装箱放下了。

En: The harbour sleeps as the strike rages in the distance. The crane can rest again, now that its purpose has been fulfilled.
Zh: 罢工在远处进行得如火如荼，而港口则陷入了沉睡。起重机的目的已经实现，现在它又可以休息了。

En: Moving *this* container, of course. For this purpose it was built; for this purpose it has acted. And now it will rest.
Zh: 当然，是为了移动*这个*集装箱。这就是建造它的目的；而这个目的已经实现。所以现在它要休息了。

En: Its purpose? What do you mean?
Zh: 它的目的？你是什么意思？

En: Understood. Let's get back to the panel.
Zh: 明白。我们回到控制面板上吧。

En: The rusty control panel grows warm to the touch. Then grows cold. It is finished.
Zh: 生锈的控制面板摸起来有些温暖。然后又变冷了。它已经完工了。

En: A faint smell of burning rubber reaches your nostrils. A short-circuit.
Zh: 一股淡淡的烧焦的橡胶味钻进你的鼻孔，是短路的味道。

En: A rusting control panel with several knobs. The words *Marche* and *Arrêt* are -- very, very slightly -- even more faded with use.
Zh: 一个生锈的控制面板，上面有几个开关。上面写着*启动*和*停机*——字迹非常、非常轻——因为经常使用有些褪色。

En: *Marche* -- On. *Arrêt* -- Off.
Zh: *启动*——开启。*停机*——关闭。

En: What's inside the container?
Zh: 集装箱里有什么？

En: Understood. Let's get back to the panel.
Zh: 明白了。我们回到控制面板上吧。

En: Who can say? All you know is -- it's special.
Zh: 谁知道呢？你只知道——它很特别。

En: If only you knew...
Zh: 如果你能知道……

En: This crane was built with a purpose which has now been fulfilled -- to move *this* container.
Zh: 建造这台起重机的目的现在已经实现了——移动*这个*集装箱。

En: You're right, it's probably nothing.
Zh: “你说得对，可能什么也没有。”

En: Damn it, now you've got *me* curious... this is *not* what we came to the harbour for.
Zh: “见鬼，现在你搞得*我*都开始好奇了……我们来港口可*不是*为了这个的。”

En: We're not here to interact with containers -- we're here to get the body down from the tree.
Zh: “我们来这里可不是为了跟集装箱互动——我们来这里是为了把尸体从树上弄下来的。”

En: We're not here to interact with containers -- we're here to interview the Union boss.
Zh: “我们来这里可不是为了跟集装箱互动——我们来这里是为了走访工会老大的。”

En: We're not here to interact with containers -- we're here to talk to the Union, right?
Zh: “我们来这里可不是为了跟集装箱互动——我们来这里是为了跟工会谈谈的，对吗？”

En: To your left, the lieutenant considers your actions -- with some puzzlement.
Zh: 警督站在你的左边，思考着你的行动——带着一些费解。

En: Mark my words -- there's someone rich buzzing away inside!
Zh: “记住我说的——里面有个有钱人在嗡嗡声响！”

En: I don't hear anything. The lieutenant cups one of his ears.
Zh: “我什么也没听到。”警督捂住他的一只耳朵。

En: That's because you're not *tuned in*. Perks of being a hustler. You'll see -- I'll be rolling in money soon.
Zh: “那是因为你没有*同调*，这可是骗子的特长。你会明白的——我很快就会发大财。”

En: Step aside, rank and file, Savvy's at the top of the VIP list here. That's right -- out of the way!
Zh: 靠边站，排好队，鬼鬼在这里的VIP名单中名列前茅。没错——让开!

En: Khm-khm... (Clear your throat, knock on the door.) "Hi, it's Savvy here, Savvy with two v's, I'm on the list. The guest list."
Zh: “咳-咳…”(清清嗓子，敲敲门。) “嗨，我是鬼鬼，有两个V的鬼鬼，我在名单上。客人名单上的。”

En: Thank you, thank you. Now, are you ready? It's time to *make our entrance*.
Zh: 谢谢，谢谢。现在，你准备好了吗？是时候*华丽入场*了。

En: Whoa! Watch it!
Zh: 哇！小心！

En: How'd he get in front? Little slimeball...
Zh: 他是怎么走到前面来的？小滑头……

En: That's a very good question considering you have Savvy around -- the ultimate VIP.
Zh: 这是个很好的问题，考虑到鬼鬼就在你身边——那位终极VIP。

En: Seems like it. You're still hanging with this fool? Something isn't right...
Zh: 好像是这样。你还和这个傻瓜在一起？有些不对劲……

En: At first your knock rings hollow on the door -- and then...
Zh: 起初你的敲门声在门上空洞回响——然后……

En: Before you stands a cargo container. Just one of many in the yard.
Zh: 你面前矗立着一只货物集装箱。不过是院子里众多集装箱中的一个。

En: Lieutenant, I think there's something *special* about this container...
Zh: “警督，我觉得这个集装箱有些不一样。”

En: Open the door.
Zh: 打开门。

En: Nothing more to do here for now. [Leave.]
Zh: 这里暂时没有什么可以做的了。[离开。]

En: You attempt to turn the handle, to no avail. The doors seem to be mechanically locked.
Zh: 你打算转动把手，但是没有用。这扇门似乎是用机械装置锁上的。

En: You do? Because I don't.
Zh: “是吗？我不觉得。”

En: There are a million containers here. Why are you fixating on this one?
Zh: “这里有几百万个集装箱。你为什么要盯着这一个？”

En: What, why not?
Zh: “什么，为什么不呢？”

En: I don't know, Kim, it just *feels* special.
Zh: “我不知道，金，我只是*感觉*到它很特别。”

En: Persuade the door to open.
Zh: 劝说门自己打开。

En: Erratic, yet thorough.
Zh: 古怪，但是又很彻底。

En: Been in the world for two days.
Zh: 在这个世界上待了两天。

En: Been in this world for many days.
Zh: 在这个世界上待了很多天。

En: Precarious world.
Zh: 危险的世界。

En: One more door.
Zh: 又是一扇门。

En: Money is all about trust!
Zh: 金钱完全是关乎于信任的！

En: Icosahedral Dice Set "Sirens".
Zh: 二十面体骰子套组“塞壬”。

En: Savvy's here!
Zh: 是鬼鬼！

En: (Inspect the container.) "Maybe there's contraband in there?"
Zh: （调查集装箱）“也许里面有违禁品？”

En: The lieutenant considers this for a moment. "There may very well be, but we're not here to look for that."
Zh: 警督考虑了一下。“很可能有，不过我们不是为调查这个来的。”

En: Hey there, sugar.
Zh: “你好啊，甜心。”

En: It's a cargo container, detective. Just like all the others. He doesn't even look at it.
Zh: “这是个货物集装箱，警探。就像其他这些一样。”他甚至看都没看。

En: The container door appears unmoved by your attempt at flattery.
Zh: 你的努力奉承似乎没有打动集装箱的门。

En: You're a fine piece of engineering, do you know that?
Zh: “你真是工程学上的一件杰作，你知道吗？”

En: Why are you even trying to open a door with rhetoric?
Zh: 你为什么要尝试用花言巧语打开一扇门呢？

En: Yeah, why *am* I?
Zh: “是啊，我为什么要*这样做*？”

En: Why are you what? The lieutenant looks startled.
Zh: “为什么要什么？”警督看起来受了惊吓。

En: Anything at all.
Zh: “没什么。”

En: Despite the dirt that surrounds and trails you, a beacon of light emerges from deep within you.
Zh: 尽管你的周围和身后都是尘埃，一束光还是从你的内心深处照射出来。

En: Hello, is there anybody in there?
Zh: “喂，里面有人吗？”

En: If there's someone in there, I'd like to talk to you.
Zh: “如果里面有人的话，我想跟你谈谈。”

En: From deep within the container, a voice: "Ahoy! Come on in!"
Zh: 集装箱最深处响起一个声音：“啊嗬！进来吧！”

En: Never mind.
Zh: “算了，没事了。”

En: The door stands silent.
Zh: 门沉默地站着。

En: Nevermind, I have better things to do. [Leave.]
Zh: “没事了，我还有更重要的事要做。”[离开。]

En: Knock on the door.
Zh: 敲敲门。

En: No reply.
Zh: 没有回应。

En: The knock produces a hollow ring of metal. Doesn't sound like there's anything inside the container.
Zh: 你的敲击发出一个空洞的金属声。听起来集装箱里面好像什么也没有。

En: Just like that, you hear a click. Then a rattle. Some mechanism unlocks itself inside the door.
Zh: 就这样，你听见一个咔哒声。然后是咯咯声。某种机械装置在门里面自动解锁了。

En: The door seems to be locked. Conclusively.
Zh: 门好像上锁了，决定性的。

En: Who understands anything you do?
Zh: 谁能理解你做的一切？

En: The lieutenant sighs. "Is this like your thing with that wall again?"
Zh: 警督叹了口气。“你又要玩之前墙壁那一套了吗？”

En: No, it's totally different. I don't want to paint it. I'm just *drawn* to it.
Zh: “不，这完全不一样。我不想在上面刷油漆。我只是被它*吸引*了。”

En: Maybe? I can't tell. I think we should investigate further.
Zh: “也许？我也说不出来。我觉得我们应该做进一步调查。”

En: A tough nut to crack, for sure. But the strongest containers often have the softest hearts.
Zh: 这肯定是个棘手的问题。但是最牢固的集装箱往往也有一颗最柔软的心。

En: C'mon, don't shut me out. Let me in.
Zh: “拜托，别把我关在外面。让我进去。”

En: The door does not respond to your advances. Is it possible that it seems somehow *more* closed than before?
Zh: 门对你的示爱好像没有反应。有没有可能它变得比以前*更加*封闭了？

En: Why does this situation feel so familiar? Do you have a history of propositioning inanimate objects?
Zh: 为什么这种状况感觉这么熟悉？你有过向非生命物体求爱的前科吗？

En: I wonder what's in there.
Zh: “不知道里面是什么。”

En: What's the matter, honey? You shy?
Zh: “出什么事了，亲爱的？你在害羞吗？”

En: The lieutenant nods. "Are you satisfied, detective?"
Zh: 警督点点头。“你满意了吗，警探？”

En: Now is not the time to get philosophical, detective. You can do that after hours.
Zh: “现在不是探讨哲学问题的时候，警探。你可以几小时之后再做。”

En: Because getting physical wasn't an option!
Zh: “因为动粗不在选择范围之内！”

En: Have I been summoned?
Zh: 你在召唤我吗？

En: No one needs you. Go away.
Zh: 没人需要你。走开。

En: Because what wasn't an option? The lieutenant looks startled.
Zh: “因为什么不在选择范围之内？”警督看起来好像受惊了。

En: You've got to dance with the one that brought you, detective.
Zh: 你应该对关照你的人表现自己的忠诚，警探。

En: Using my body over my wits.
Zh: “使用我的肉体多于我的智慧。”

En: Try again.
Zh: 再试一次。

En: Satisfied, detective? A wry smile crosses the lieutenant's face.
Zh: “满意了吗，警探？”警督的脸上露出一个揶揄的笑容。

En: The lieutenant's smile disappears. "You can't be serious."
Zh: 警督的笑容消失了。“你不是认真的吧。”

En: The smile disappears. "You can't be serious."
Zh: 笑容消失了。“这不是真的吧。”

En: You're back before the cargo container. Its draw has not lessened since you were last here. If anything, it seems to have grown slightly.
Zh: 你又回到了货物集装箱前。自从上次来这里，它的吸引力丝毫没有减弱。如果说有什么的话，它的吸引力反而更大了。

En: [Leave the container alone.]
Zh: [不管这个集装箱。]

En: It's just another day, another cop and another container.
Zh: “又是一天，又是一个警察和又一个集装箱。”

En: It was hanging from the crane.
Zh: “它吊在起重机上了。”

En: You just picked one out, because you wanted to interact with a cargo container.
Zh: “你只是随便挑了一个，因为你想跟货物集装箱互动。”

En: Aaaaand as it's always been -- it's impossible to open a container with rhetoric. Maybe you're losing your mind?
Zh: 而且总是这样——想用花言巧语打开集装箱是不可能的。也许是你疯了？

En: The mundaneness of the situation does not seem to affect the container in the slightest.
Zh: 这种世俗的状况似乎丝毫没有影响到这个集装箱。

En: The door does not respond to your advances. Is it possible that it seems somehow *more* closed than before?
Zh: 门对你的示爱好像没有反应。有没有可能它变得比以前*更加*封闭了？
